    --query "Certificate.DomainValidationOptions[*].ResourceRecord"
Output: [ { "Name": "_abc123def456.anihpj.com.", "Type": "CNAME", "Value": "_xyz789abc123.acm-validations.aws." } ]
Django Context: # Add this CNAME to Route 53: # Validates domain ownership # Certificate issued after validation # Then attach to ALB for Django HTTPS
Example 4: List all certificates

List certificates for the account.

aws acm list-certificates \
    --certificate-statuses ISSUED \
    --query "CertificateSummaryList[?contains(DomainName, 'anihpj')]"
Output: [ { "CertificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123-def456-ghi789", "DomainName": "anihpj.com", "SubjectAlternativeNameSummaries": [ "anihpj.com", "*.anihpj.com" ], "Status": "ISSUED", "Type": "AMAZON_ISSUED", "KeyAlgorithm": "RSA-2048", "NotAfter": "2026-01-17T23:59:59.000Z", "RenewalEligibility": "ELIGIBLE" } ]
Django Context: # Active certificates for Django: # - Main wildcard cert for all services # - Covers JobPost API endpoints # - Skills search endpoints # - Author authentication endpoints
Example 5: Attach certificate to ALB

Configure ALB listener with SSL certificate.

# Create HTTPS listener on ALB
aws elbv2 create-listener \
    --load-balancer-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789012:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-lb/abc123 \
    --protocol HTTPS \
    --port 443 \
    --certificates CertificateArn=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123-def456-ghi789 \
    --ssl-policy ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06 \
    --default-actions Type=forward,TargetGroupArn=arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789012:targetgroup/anihpj-tg/xyz789
Output: { "Listeners": [ { "ListenerArn": "arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:listener/app/anihpj-lb/abc123/def456", "LoadBalancerArn": "arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-lb/abc123", "Port": 443, "Protocol": "HTTPS", "Certificates": [ { "CertificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123-def456-ghi789" } ], "SslPolicy": "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06" } ] }
Django Context: # Django now served over HTTPS: # - TLS 1.3 for best security # - All JobPost API calls encrypted # - User credentials protected # - Set SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT=True in settings.py

AWS Secrets Manager

Secure storage and rotation of secrets and credentials

📋 Secrets Manager Overview

AWS Secrets Manager helps protect access to applications, services, and IT resources by securely storing secrets with automatic rotation. For the anihpj Django application, Secrets Manager stores database credentials, API keys, Django SECRET_KEY, and third-party service credentials needed for JobPost, Skills, and Author functionality.

Core Secrets Manager Commands

create-secret - Create a secret
get-secret-value - Retrieve secret
update-secret - Update secret value
rotate-secret - Trigger rotation
list-secrets - List all secrets
delete-secret - Delete a secret

create-secret

Creates a new secret to store sensitive data.

Syntax
aws secretsmanager create-secret \
    --name <secret-name> \
    --secret-string '<secret-value>' \
    [--description <description>] \
    [--tags Key=<key>,Value=<value>]
Example 1: Store database credentials

Create secret for Django database connection.

aws secretsmanager create-secret \
    --name anihpj/production/database \
    --description "RDS PostgreSQL credentials for anihpj Django" \
    --secret-string '{
        "username": "anihpj_admin",
        "password": "SecureDbPassword123!",
        "engine": "postgres",
        "host": "anihpj-db.abc123.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com",
        "port": 5432,
        "dbname": "anihpj"
    }' \
    --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production
Output: { "ARN": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:123456789012:secret:anihpj/production/database-abc123", "Name": "anihpj/production/database", "VersionId": "version-uuid-here" }
Django Context: # In settings.py: # import boto3 # client = boto3.client('secretsmanager') # secret = client.get_secret_value(SecretId='anihpj/production/database') # db_creds = json.loads(secret['SecretString']) # DATABASES['default'] = {...db_creds}

get-secret-value

Retrieves the current secret value.

Example 2: Retrieve Django secret key

Get the Django SECRET_KEY from Secrets Manager.

aws secretsmanager get-secret-value \
    --secret-id anihpj/production/django-secret-key
Output: { "ARN": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:123456789012:secret:anihpj/production/django-secret-key-xyz789", "Name": "anihpj/production/django-secret-key", "VersionId": "version-uuid-here", "SecretString": "django-insecure-abc123def456ghi789jkl012mno345pqr678stu901", "CreatedDate": "2025-01-17T10:00:00.000Z" }
Django Context: # Django uses SECRET_KEY for: # - Session encryption # - CSRF tokens # - Password reset tokens # - Cryptographic signing
Example 3: Store all Django secrets

Create a consolidated secret for Django configuration.

aws secretsmanager create-secret \
    --name anihpj/production/django-config \
    --description "All Django configuration secrets" \
    --secret-string '{
        "SECRET_KEY": "django-secret-key-value-here",
        "DATABASE_PASSWORD": "SecureDbPassword123!",
        "EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD": "smtp-password-here",
        "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID": "AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE",
        "AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY": "wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY",
        "STRIPE_SECRET_KEY": "sk_live_abc123...",
        "SENTRY_DSN": "https://abc123@sentry.io/123456"
    }'
Output: { "ARN": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:123456789012:secret:anihpj/production/django-config-abc123", "Name": "anihpj/production/django-config", "VersionId": "version-uuid-here" }
Django Context: # Secrets for Django services: # - Email for Author notifications # - Stripe for premium JobPost listings # - Sentry for error tracking # - AWS keys for S3 media storage
Example 4: List all secrets

List secrets for the anihpj application.

aws secretsmanager list-secrets \
    --filters Key=name,Values=anihpj
Output: { "SecretList": [ { "ARN": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:...:secret:anihpj/production/database-abc123", "Name": "anihpj/production/database", "Description": "RDS PostgreSQL credentials for anihpj Django", "LastChangedDate": "2025-01-17T10:00:00.000Z", "Tags": [ {"Key": "Project", "Value": "anihpj"}, {"Key": "Environment", "Value": "production"} ] }, { "ARN": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:...:secret:anihpj/production/django-config-def456", "Name": "anihpj/production/django-config", "Description": "All Django configuration secrets", "LastChangedDate": "2025-01-17T10:05:00.000Z" }, { "ARN": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:...:secret:anihpj/staging/database-ghi789", "Name": "anihpj/staging/database", "Description": "Staging database credentials" } ] }
Django Context: # Secrets organized by environment: # - /production/ for live JobPost data # - /staging/ for testing Skills updates # - Separate DB credentials per environment
Example 5: Rotate database password

Trigger automatic rotation of database credentials.

# Enable rotation with Lambda
aws secretsmanager rotate-secret \
    --secret-id anihpj/production/database \
    --rotation-lambda-arn arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:SecretsManagerRDSPostgreSQLRotation \
    --rotation-rules AutomaticallyAfterDays=30
Output: { "ARN": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:123456789012:secret:anihpj/production/database-abc123", "Name": "anihpj/production/database", "VersionId": "new-version-uuid" }
Django Context: # Password rotation ensures security: # - Auto-rotates every 30 days # - Django fetches new credentials dynamically # - No downtime for JobPost queries # - Skills, Author, Location queries unaffected

AWS KMS

Key Management Service for encryption key management

📋 KMS Overview

AWS KMS (Key Management Service) makes it easy to create and manage cryptographic keys for encrypting data. For the anihpj Django application, KMS protects sensitive JobPost data, encrypts Author credentials, secures Skills information at rest, and manages encryption keys for S3, RDS, and EBS resources.

Core KMS Commands

create-key - Create CMK
create-alias - Create key alias
encrypt - Encrypt data
decrypt - Decrypt data
list-keys - List all keys
enable-key-rotation - Enable rotation

create-key

Creates a new customer managed key (CMK).

Syntax
aws kms create-key \
    [--description <description>] \
    [--key-usage ENCRYPT_DECRYPT|SIGN_VERIFY] \
    [--key-spec SYMMETRIC_DEFAULT|RSA_2048|...] \
    [--tags Key=<key>,Value=<value>]
Example 1: Create key for Django data encryption

Create a CMK for encrypting sensitive JobPost data.

aws kms create-key \
    --description "CMK for anihpj Django application data encryption" \
    --key-usage ENCRYPT_DECRYPT \
    --key-spec SYMMETRIC_DEFAULT \
    --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Framework,Value=Django
Output: { "KeyMetadata": { "AWSAccountId": "123456789012", "KeyId": "1234abcd-12ab-34cd-56ef-1234567890ab", "Arn": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/1234abcd-12ab-34cd-56ef-1234567890ab", "CreationDate": "2025-01-17T10:00:00.000Z", "Enabled": true, "Description": "CMK for anihpj Django application data encryption", "KeyUsage": "ENCRYPT_DECRYPT", "KeyState": "Enabled", "Origin": "AWS_KMS", "KeySpec": "SYMMETRIC_DEFAULT", "MultiRegion": false } }
Django Context: # CMK encrypts Django data: # - JobPost salary information # - Author personal details # - User credentials in transit # - Skills certifications

create-alias

Creates a friendly name for a KMS key.

Example 2: Create alias for the key

Create an easy-to-remember alias for the Django key.

aws kms create-alias \
    --alias-name alias/anihpj-django-key \
    --target-key-id 1234abcd-12ab-34cd-56ef-1234567890ab
Output: # No output on success (HTTP 200)
Django Context: # Use alias in Django code: # KMS_KEY_ID = 'alias/anihpj-django-key' # Easier than using full key ID # Can point to different keys per environment

encrypt

Encrypts plaintext data using a KMS key.

Example 3: Encrypt sensitive JobPost data

Encrypt salary information before storing.

aws kms encrypt \
    --key-id alias/anihpj-django-key \
    --plaintext "salary:150000" \
    --output text \
    --query CiphertextBlob
Output: AQICAHhBSj8xKlmN2b3dY7kP...encrypted_base64_data...==
Django Context: # In Django model: # class JobPost(models.Model): # encrypted_salary = models.TextField() # # def set_salary(self, salary): # self.encrypted_salary = kms_encrypt(f"salary:{salary}") # # def get_salary(self): # return kms_decrypt(self.encrypted_salary)
Example 4: Decrypt data

Decrypt encrypted JobPost salary data.

aws kms decrypt \
    --ciphertext-blob fileb://encrypted_salary.bin \
    --key-id alias/anihpj-django-key \
    --output text \
    --query Plaintext | base64 --decode
Output: salary:150000
Django Context: # Django decrypts on read: # jobpost = JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) # salary = jobpost.get_salary() # Returns 150000 # Only authorized IAM roles can decrypt
Example 5: Enable key rotation

Enable automatic key rotation for security.

# Enable automatic rotation
aws kms enable-key-rotation \
    --key-id 1234abcd-12ab-34cd-56ef-1234567890ab

# Verify rotation is enabled
aws kms get-key-rotation-status \
    --key-id 1234abcd-12ab-34cd-56ef-1234567890ab
Output: { "KeyRotationEnabled": true }
Django Context: # Key rotation benefits: # - Auto-rotates yearly # - Old data still decryptable # - New encryptions use new key # - Meets compliance requirements # - JobPost data always protected

list-keys

Lists all KMS keys in the account.

Example: List KMS keys with aliases

List all encryption keys used by anihpj.

aws kms list-aliases \
    --query "Aliases[?contains(AliasName, 'anihpj')]"
Output: [ { "AliasName": "alias/anihpj-django-key", "AliasArn": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:alias/anihpj-django-key", "TargetKeyId": "1234abcd-12ab-34cd-56ef-1234567890ab", "CreationDate": "2025-01-17T10:05:00.000Z" }, { "AliasName": "alias/anihpj-rds-key", "AliasArn": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:alias/anihpj-rds-key", "TargetKeyId": "5678efgh-56ef-78gh-90ij-567890123456", "CreationDate": "2025-01-15T14:00:00.000Z" } ]
Django Context: # KMS keys for Django: # - anihpj-django-key: App data encryption # - anihpj-rds-key: RDS encryption at rest # All JobPost, Skills, Author data protected

🔗 Application Integration

AWS Application Integration services enable loosely coupled, distributed architectures. This section covers SQS for message queuing, SNS for pub/sub messaging, API Gateway for REST APIs, and Step Functions for serverless workflows.

Amazon SQS (Simple Queue Service)

Fully managed message queuing for microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications

📋 SQS Overview

Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) is a fully managed message queuing service that enables you to decouple and scale microservices, distributed systems, and serverless applications. SQS eliminates the complexity and overhead associated with managing and operating message-oriented middleware, and empowers developers to focus on differentiating work. Using SQS, you can send, store, and receive messages between software components at any volume, without losing messages or requiring other services to be available. For the anihpj Django application, SQS handles async job post processing, skill updates, notifications, and inter-service communication with Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

Core SQS Operations for Django Message Processing

create-queue - Create standard or FIFO queue
send-message - Send message to queue
receive-message - Retrieve messages from queue
delete-message - Remove processed message
send-message-batch - Send multiple messages
delete-message-batch - Delete multiple messages
get-queue-attributes - Get queue configuration
set-queue-attributes - Update queue settings
purge-queue - Delete all messages
list-queues - List all queues
delete-queue - Remove queue entirely
get-queue-url - Get queue URL by name

create-queue

Creates a new standard or FIFO queue for the anihpj Django application message processing.

Syntax
aws sqs create-queue \
    --queue-name <value> \
    [--attributes <value>] \
    [--tags <value>]
Parameters
  • --queue-name (string): Name of queue (FIFO queues must end with .fifo)
  • --attributes (map): Queue attributes like VisibilityTimeout, MessageRetentionPeriod
  • --tags (map): Key-value pairs for resource tagging
Return Values
  • QueueUrl: URL of the created queue
Example 1: Create standard queue for job post processing

Create a queue to handle async job post creation tasks.

#!/bin/bash
aws sqs create-queue \
    --queue-name anihpj-jobpost-queue \
    --attributes '{
        "VisibilityTimeout": "300",
        "MessageRetentionPeriod": "1209600",
        "DelaySeconds": "0"
    }' \
    --tags Project=anihpj Environment=production
Output: { "QueueUrl": "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue" }
Django Context: # Queue created for async job posting # JobPost.objects.filter(status='pending').count() -> Check pending jobs # Can queue tasks for: job creation, skill matching, notification sending
Example 2: Create FIFO queue for ordered skill updates

Create FIFO queue to maintain order for skill update operations.

#!/bin/bash
aws sqs create-queue \
    --queue-name anihpj-skill-updates.fifo \
    --attributes '{
        "FifoQueue": "true",
        "ContentBasedDeduplication": "true",
        "MessageRetentionPeriod": "604800"
    }' \
    --tags Project=anihpj Component=skills
Output: { "QueueUrl": "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo" }
Django Context: # Skills.objects.all().count() -> Track total skills # FIFO ensures skill updates process in exact order # No duplicate skill update messages with ContentBasedDeduplication
Example 3: Create dead letter queue for failed messages

Create DLQ to capture messages that fail processing.

#!/bin/bash
# First create DLQ
aws sqs create-queue \
    --queue-name anihpj-jobpost-dlq \
    --attributes '{
        "MessageRetentionPeriod": "1209600"
    }' \
    --tags Project=anihpj Type=DLQ

# Get DLQ ARN for main queue configuration
DLQ_URL=$(aws sqs get-queue-url --queue-name anihpj-jobpost-dlq --query 'QueueUrl' --output text)
DLQ_ARN=$(aws sqs get-queue-attributes --queue-url $DLQ_URL --attribute-names QueueArn --query 'Attributes.QueueArn' --output text)
Output: { "QueueUrl": "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-dlq" }
Django Context: # DLQ captures failed JobPost creation attempts # JobPost.objects.filter(status='failed') -> Check failed posts # Retry logic can pull from DLQ for manual intervention
Example 4: Create queue with long polling enabled

Enable long polling to reduce empty responses and API costs.

#!/bin/bash
aws sqs create-queue \
    --queue-name anihpj-notification-queue \
    --attributes '{
        "ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds": "20",
        "VisibilityTimeout": "120",
        "MaximumMessageSize": "262144"
    }' \
    --tags Project=anihpj Component=notifications
Output: { "QueueUrl": "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue" }
Django Context: # Queue for sending job application notifications # User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() -> Active users to notify # Long polling reduces costs and improves efficiency
Example 5: Create queue with server-side encryption

Create encrypted queue for sensitive data like user applications.

#!/bin/bash
aws sqs create-queue \
    --queue-name anihpj-applications-queue \
    --attributes '{
        "KmsMasterKeyId": "alias/aws/sqs",
        "KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds": "300",
        "MessageRetentionPeriod": "345600"
    }' \
    --tags Project=anihpj Security=encrypted
Output: { "QueueUrl": "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-applications-queue" }
Django Context: # Encrypted queue for job application data # Author.objects.filter(applications__isnull=False) -> Authors with apps # SSE-KMS protects sensitive applicant information

send-message

Sends a message to the specified SQS queue for processing by anihpj Django workers.

Syntax
aws sqs send-message \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --message-body <value> \
    [--message-attributes <value>] \
    [--delay-seconds <value>]
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the target queue
  • --message-body (string): Message content (max 256KB)
  • --message-attributes (map): Custom attributes for message metadata
  • --delay-seconds (integer): Delay before message becomes available (0-900)
Return Values
  • MessageId: Unique identifier for the message
  • MD5OfMessageBody: MD5 digest of message body
Example 1: Send job post creation message

Queue a new job post for async processing.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs send-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --message-body '{
        "action": "create_jobpost",
        "title": "Senior Django Developer",
        "location_id": 42,
        "author_id": 15,
        "skills": [1, 5, 12]
    }' \
    --message-attributes '{
        "Priority": {"StringValue": "high", "DataType": "String"},
        "PostType": {"StringValue": "full-time", "DataType": "String"}
    }'
Output: { "MessageId": "a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111", "MD5OfMessageBody": "51b0a325...c69e9e79e3a5" }
Django Context: # Message queued for worker to process # Location.objects.get(id=42) -> Get job location # Skills.objects.filter(id__in=[1,5,12]) -> Required skills # Author.objects.get(id=15) -> Post author
Example 2: Send skill update with delay

Schedule skill update to process after 5 minutes.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs send-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --message-body '{
        "action": "update_skill",
        "skill_id": 7,
        "new_name": "Django 5.0"
    }' \
    --delay-seconds 300
Output: { "MessageId": "b2c3d4e5-6789-01bc-defg-EXAMPLE22222", "MD5OfMessageBody": "62c1b436...d70f0f80f4b6" }
Django Context: # Skill.objects.get(id=7).update(name="Django 5.0") # 5-minute delay allows for validation checks # JobPost.objects.filter(skills__id=7).count() -> Affected posts
Example 3: Send batch notification message

Queue notification to multiple users about new job posts.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

aws sqs send-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --message-body '{
        "action": "send_notifications",
        "jobpost_id": 123,
        "user_ids": [10, 25, 30, 45],
        "notification_type": "new_job"
    }' \
    --message-attributes '{
        "NotificationType": {"StringValue": "email", "DataType": "String"},
        "Urgency": {"StringValue": "normal", "DataType": "String"}
    }'
Output: { "MessageId": "c3d4e5f6-7890-12cd-efgh-EXAMPLE33333", "MD5OfMessageBody": "73d2c547...e81g1g91g5c7" }
Django Context: # JobPost.objects.get(id=123) -> Get job details # User.objects.filter(id__in=[10,25,30,45]) -> Recipients # Send email notifications for matching skills
Example 4: Send FIFO message with deduplication

Send message to FIFO queue with message group ID.

#!/bin/bash
FIFO_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"

aws sqs send-message \
    --queue-url "$FIFO_URL" \
    --message-body '{
        "action": "update_skill_category",
        "skill_id": 12,
        "category": "Backend"
    }' \
    --message-group-id "skill-updates" \
    --message-deduplication-id "skill-12-$(date +%s)"
Output: { "MessageId": "d4e5f6g7-8901-23de-fghi-EXAMPLE44444", "MD5OfMessageBody": "84e3d658...f92h2h02h6d8", "SequenceNumber": "18851234567890123456" }
Django Context: # Skills.objects.get(id=12).update(category="Backend") # FIFO ensures ordered processing # MessageGroupId maintains FIFO within group
Example 5: Send message with JSON metadata

Include rich metadata for complex job post operations.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs send-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --message-body '{
        "action": "create_jobpost_bulk",
        "posts": [
            {"title": "Backend Dev", "location_id": 1},
            {"title": "Frontend Dev", "location_id": 2}
        ]
    }' \
    --message-attributes '{
        "Source": {"StringValue": "admin-panel", "DataType": "String"},
        "BatchSize": {"StringValue": "2", "DataType": "Number"},
        "Timestamp": {"StringValue": "'$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)'", "DataType": "String"}
    }'
Output: { "MessageId": "e5f6g7h8-9012-34ef-ghij-EXAMPLE55555", "MD5OfMessageBody": "95f4e769...g03i3i13i7e9" }
Django Context: # Bulk create operation # Location.objects.filter(id__in=[1,2]) -> Job locations # JobPost.objects.bulk_create([...]) -> Efficient bulk insert

receive-message

Retrieves messages from an SQS queue for processing by Django workers.

Syntax
aws sqs receive-message \
    --queue-url <value> \
    [--max-number-of-messages <value>] \
    [--wait-time-seconds <value>] \
    [--visibility-timeout <value>]
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --max-number-of-messages (integer): Max messages to retrieve (1-10)
  • --wait-time-seconds (integer): Long polling wait time (0-20)
  • --visibility-timeout (integer): Message visibility timeout override
Return Values
  • Messages: Array of messages with Body, ReceiptHandle, MessageId
Example 1: Receive single job post message

Pull one message from queue for processing.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs receive-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --max-number-of-messages 1 \
    --wait-time-seconds 10
Output: { "Messages": [ { "MessageId": "a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111", "ReceiptHandle": "AQEBwJnKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0a...", "MD5OfBody": "51b0a325...c69e9e79e3a5", "Body": "{\"action\":\"create_jobpost\",\"title\":\"Senior Django Developer\"}" } ] }
Django Context: # Worker receives message and processes # JobPost.objects.create(title="Senior Django Developer", ...) # Must delete message after successful processing
Example 2: Receive multiple messages with attributes

Fetch up to 10 messages with all attributes.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs receive-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --max-number-of-messages 10 \
    --attribute-names All \
    --message-attribute-names All \
    --wait-time-seconds 20
Output: { "Messages": [ { "MessageId": "msg-001", "Body": "{\"action\":\"create_jobpost\"}", "Attributes": { "SentTimestamp": "1523232000000", "ApproximateReceiveCount": "1" }, "MessageAttributes": { "Priority": {"StringValue": "high", "DataType": "String"} } } ] }
Django Context: # Process batch of 10 messages # for msg in messages: process_jobpost(msg['Body']) # JobPost.objects.bulk_create() for efficiency
Example 3: Receive with custom visibility timeout

Set longer visibility for complex processing tasks.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs receive-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --max-number-of-messages 5 \
    --visibility-timeout 600 \
    --wait-time-seconds 15
Output: { "Messages": [ { "MessageId": "msg-002", "ReceiptHandle": "AQEB1xJm...", "Body": "{\"action\":\"update_skills\"}" } ] }
Django Context: # 10-minute visibility for complex skill updates # Skills.objects.filter(...).update() # Prevents duplicate processing by other workers
Example 4: Receive from FIFO queue

Process messages from FIFO queue in order.

#!/bin/bash
FIFO_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"

aws sqs receive-message \
    --queue-url "$FIFO_URL" \
    --max-number-of-messages 1 \
    --attribute-names All
Output: { "Messages": [ { "MessageId": "fifo-msg-001", "Body": "{\"action\":\"update_skill_category\"}", "Attributes": { "MessageGroupId": "skill-updates", "SequenceNumber": "18851234567890123456" } } ] }
Django Context: # FIFO ensures ordered skill updates # Skills.objects.get(id=X).update(category="Y") # Sequential processing maintains data consistency
Example 5: Receive and parse job notification

Receive notification message and extract data.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

RESPONSE=$(aws sqs receive-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --max-number-of-messages 1 \
    --wait-time-seconds 10)

# Extract message body
echo $RESPONSE | jq -r '.Messages[0].Body'
Output: { "Messages": [ { "MessageId": "notif-001", "Body": "{\"action\":\"send_notifications\",\"jobpost_id\":123,\"user_ids\":[10,25,30]}", "ReceiptHandle": "AQEB2yKn..." } ] } Parsed Body: {"action":"send_notifications","jobpost_id":123,"user_ids":[10,25,30]}
Django Context: # Parse and send notifications # jobpost = JobPost.objects.get(id=123) # users = User.objects.filter(id__in=[10,25,30]) # send_email_notifications(users, jobpost)

delete-message

Deletes a message from the queue after successful processing by Django worker.

Syntax
aws sqs delete-message \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --receipt-handle <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --receipt-handle (string): Receipt handle from receive-message
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Delete processed job post message

Remove message after successfully creating job post.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"
RECEIPT_HANDLE="AQEBwJnKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0a..."

aws sqs delete-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --receipt-handle "$RECEIPT_HANDLE"
Output: # No output - message successfully deleted
Django Context: # After successful JobPost.objects.create(...) # Delete message to prevent reprocessing # Worker pattern: receive -> process -> delete
Example 2: Delete after skill update validation

Remove message after validating skill update.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"
RECEIPT="AQEB1xJmKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0b..."

# Validate and delete
aws sqs delete-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --receipt-handle "$RECEIPT"
Output: # Message deleted successfully
Django Context: # Skills.objects.get(id=7).update(name="Django 5.0") # Validation passed, delete message # Prevents duplicate skill updates
Example 3: Delete notification after sending

Clean up notification message after emails sent.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"
RECEIPT="AQEB2yKnKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0c..."

aws sqs delete-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --receipt-handle "$RECEIPT"
Output: # Notification message deleted
Django Context: # After sending notifications to User.objects.filter(...) # Email sent successfully, remove from queue # Avoids duplicate notifications
Example 4: Delete with error handling

Safely delete message with try-catch pattern.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"
RECEIPT="AQEB3zLnKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0d..."

if aws sqs delete-message \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --receipt-handle "$RECEIPT" 2>&1; then
    echo "Message deleted successfully"
else
    echo "Failed to delete message"
fi
Output: Message deleted successfully
Django Context: # Robust deletion after JobPost processing # Handle edge cases like expired receipt handles # Log deletion status for monitoring
Example 5: Delete from DLQ after manual fix

Remove message from dead letter queue after resolving issue.

#!/bin/bash
DLQ_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-dlq"
RECEIPT="AQEB4aMnKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0e..."

aws sqs delete-message \
    --queue-url "$DLQ_URL" \
    --receipt-handle "$RECEIPT"
Output: # DLQ message removed
Django Context: # Failed JobPost manually fixed # Remove from DLQ after resolution # JobPost.objects.filter(status='failed').update(status='active')

delete-message-batch

Deletes up to 10 messages in a single batch operation for efficiency.

Syntax
aws sqs delete-message-batch \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --entries <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --entries (list): List of messages with Id and ReceiptHandle
Return Values
  • Successful: List of successfully deleted messages
  • Failed: List of failed deletions with error codes
Example 1: Batch delete processed job posts

Delete multiple processed messages efficiently.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs delete-message-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {"Id": "msg1", "ReceiptHandle": "AQEBwJnKyrHigUMZj6..."},
        {"Id": "msg2", "ReceiptHandle": "AQEB1xJmKyrHigUMZj7..."},
        {"Id": "msg3", "ReceiptHandle": "AQEB2yKnKyrHigUMZj8..."}
    ]'
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "msg1"}, {"Id": "msg2"}, {"Id": "msg3"} ] }
Django Context: # After processing 3 job posts # JobPost.objects.bulk_create([post1, post2, post3]) # Batch delete reduces API calls and costs
Example 2: Batch delete with error handling

Handle partial success in batch deletion.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

RESULT=$(aws sqs delete-message-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {"Id": "n1", "ReceiptHandle": "VALID_HANDLE_1"},
        {"Id": "n2", "ReceiptHandle": "INVALID_HANDLE"},
        {"Id": "n3", "ReceiptHandle": "VALID_HANDLE_2"}
    ]')

echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.Failed'
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "n1"}, {"Id": "n3"} ], "Failed": [ { "Id": "n2", "Code": "ReceiptHandleIsInvalid", "Message": "Receipt handle invalid" } ] }
Django Context: # 2 notifications sent successfully # 1 failed - handle expired receipt # Retry failed messages or log for investigation
Example 3: Delete 10 messages maximum batch

Process maximum allowed batch size.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

# Generate entries for 10 messages
ENTRIES='['
for i in {1..10}; do
    ENTRIES+="{\"Id\":\"msg$i\",\"ReceiptHandle\":\"HANDLE_$i\"}"
    [ $i -lt 10 ] && ENTRIES+=","
done
ENTRIES+=']'

aws sqs delete-message-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries "$ENTRIES"
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "msg1"}, {"Id": "msg2"}, {"Id": "msg3"}, {"Id": "msg4"}, {"Id": "msg5"}, {"Id": "msg6"}, {"Id": "msg7"}, {"Id": "msg8"}, {"Id": "msg9"}, {"Id": "msg10"} ] }
Django Context: # Processed 10 job posts in batch # Maximum efficiency with batch operations # JobPost.objects.bulk_create() + batch delete
Example 4: Delete after skill updates batch

Clean up after batch skill processing.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"

aws sqs delete-message-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {"Id": "s1", "ReceiptHandle": "SKILL_HANDLE_1"},
        {"Id": "s2", "ReceiptHandle": "SKILL_HANDLE_2"},
        {"Id": "s3", "ReceiptHandle": "SKILL_HANDLE_3"}
    ]'
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "s1"}, {"Id": "s2"}, {"Id": "s3"} ] }
Django Context: # Skills.objects.filter(id__in=[1,2,3]).update(...) # Batch update completed, clean messages # Maintains FIFO order during processing
Example 5: Delete with JSON file input

Use JSON file for large batch operations.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

# Create entries file
cat > delete_entries.json << 'EOF'
[
    {"Id": "1", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_A"},
    {"Id": "2", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_B"},
    {"Id": "3", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_C"}
]
EOF

aws sqs delete-message-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries file://delete_entries.json
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "1"}, {"Id": "2"}, {"Id": "3"} ] }
Django Context: # Clean batch operations from file # Useful for scheduled cleanup tasks # cron job: process_queue() -> delete_batch()

delete-queue

Deletes an SQS queue and all its messages permanently.

Syntax
aws sqs delete-queue \
    --queue-url <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue to delete
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Delete temporary test queue

Remove queue created for testing.

#!/bin/bash
TEST_QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-test-queue"

aws sqs delete-queue \
    --queue-url "$TEST_QUEUE_URL"
Output: # Queue deleted successfully # Note: 60-second delay before queue name can be reused
Django Context: # Cleanup after integration tests # Remove temporary queues # pytest teardown: delete_test_queue()
Example 2: Delete deprecated notification queue

Remove old queue after migration.

#!/bin/bash
OLD_QUEUE="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-old-notifications"

# Verify queue is empty first
MESSAGE_COUNT=$(aws sqs get-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$OLD_QUEUE" \
    --attribute-names ApproximateNumberOfMessages \
    --query 'Attributes.ApproximateNumberOfMessages' \
    --output text)

if [ "$MESSAGE_COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    aws sqs delete-queue --queue-url "$OLD_QUEUE"
    echo "Old queue deleted"
else
    echo "Queue not empty: $MESSAGE_COUNT messages"
fi
Output: Old queue deleted
Django Context: # Migrated to new notification system # Verify User.objects notifications complete # Safe deletion after migration validation
Example 3: Delete DLQ after resolving issues

Remove dead letter queue when no longer needed.

#!/bin/bash
DLQ_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-temp-dlq"

aws sqs delete-queue \
    --queue-url "$DLQ_URL"

echo "DLQ deleted successfully"
Output: DLQ deleted successfully
Django Context: # All failed JobPost messages resolved # No longer need temporary DLQ # Clean infrastructure after debugging
Example 4: Delete queue with confirmation

Interactive deletion with safety check.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-staging-queue"

read -p "Delete queue $QUEUE_URL? (yes/no): " CONFIRM

if [ "$CONFIRM" == "yes" ]; then
    aws sqs delete-queue --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL"
    echo "Queue deleted"
else
    echo "Deletion cancelled"
fi
Output: Delete queue https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-staging-queue? (yes/no): yes Queue deleted
Django Context: # Safe deletion for staging environment # Cleanup before redeployment # Settings: QUEUE_URL removed from Django settings
Example 5: Delete multiple queues in cleanup script

Batch delete multiple test queues.

#!/bin/bash
# Cleanup script for development queues
QUEUES=(
    "anihpj-dev-queue-1"
    "anihpj-dev-queue-2"
    "anihpj-test-fifo.fifo"
)

for QUEUE_NAME in "${QUEUES[@]}"; do
    QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/$QUEUE_NAME"
    aws sqs delete-queue --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL"
    echo "Deleted: $QUEUE_NAME"
    sleep 1
done
Output: Deleted: anihpj-dev-queue-1 Deleted: anihpj-dev-queue-2 Deleted: anihpj-test-fifo.fifo
Django Context: # Development environment cleanup # Remove all test queues # Fresh start for next development cycle

get-queue-attributes

Retrieves attributes of an SQS queue for monitoring and configuration validation.

Syntax
aws sqs get-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url <value> \
    [--attribute-names <value>]
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --attribute-names (list): Specific attributes to retrieve or "All"
Return Values
  • Attributes: Map of queue attributes and values
Example 1: Get all queue attributes

Retrieve comprehensive queue configuration.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs get-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --attribute-names All
Output: { "Attributes": { "QueueArn": "arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-jobpost-queue", "ApproximateNumberOfMessages": "42", "ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible": "3", "ApproximateNumberOfMessagesDelayed": "0", "CreatedTimestamp": "1642384800", "VisibilityTimeout": "300", "MessageRetentionPeriod": "1209600" } }
Django Context: # Monitor queue health # 42 pending JobPost messages # 3 currently being processed # JobPost.objects.filter(status='queued').count()
Example 2: Get message count for monitoring

Check approximate message count.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

MSG_COUNT=$(aws sqs get-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --attribute-names ApproximateNumberOfMessages \
    --query 'Attributes.ApproximateNumberOfMessages' \
    --output text)

echo "Queue has $MSG_COUNT messages"
Output: Queue has 42 messages
Django Context: # Dashboard metric for pending jobs # Alert if MSG_COUNT > threshold # JobPost.objects.filter(status='pending')
Example 3: Check FIFO queue configuration

Verify FIFO and deduplication settings.

#!/bin/bash
FIFO_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"

aws sqs get-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$FIFO_URL" \
    --attribute-names FifoQueue ContentBasedDeduplication
Output: { "Attributes": { "FifoQueue": "true", "ContentBasedDeduplication": "true" } }
Django Context: # Verify FIFO configuration # Skills updates maintain order # Automatic deduplication enabled
Example 4: Monitor DLQ configuration

Check dead letter queue settings.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs get-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --attribute-names RedrivePolicy
Output: { "Attributes": { "RedrivePolicy": "{\"deadLetterTargetArn\":\"arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-jobpost-dlq\",\"maxReceiveCount\":\"3\"}" } }
Django Context: # Failed messages go to DLQ after 3 attempts # JobPost.objects.filter(retry_count__gte=3) # Monitor DLQ for processing failures
Example 5: Check encryption status

Verify server-side encryption configuration.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-applications-queue"

aws sqs get-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --attribute-names KmsMasterKeyId KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds
Output: { "Attributes": { "KmsMasterKeyId": "alias/aws/sqs", "KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds": "300" } }
Django Context: # Verify encryption for sensitive data # Application data encrypted at rest # Compliance requirement validated

get-queue-url

Gets the URL of a queue by its name.

Syntax
aws sqs get-queue-url \
    --queue-name <value> \
    [--queue-owner-aws-account-id <value>]
Parameters
  • --queue-name (string): Name of the queue
  • --queue-owner-aws-account-id (string): AWS account ID of queue owner
Return Values
  • QueueUrl: URL of the queue
Example 1: Get queue URL by name

Lookup queue URL for configuration.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL=$(aws sqs get-queue-url \
    --queue-name anihpj-jobpost-queue \
    --query 'QueueUrl' \
    --output text)

echo "Queue URL: $QUEUE_URL"
Output: Queue URL: https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue
Django Context: # Store in Django settings # QUEUE_URL = get_queue_url("anihpj-jobpost-queue") # Use for send_message operations
Example 2: Get FIFO queue URL

Retrieve FIFO queue URL for skill updates.

#!/bin/bash
FIFO_URL=$(aws sqs get-queue-url \
    --queue-name anihpj-skill-updates.fifo \
    --query 'QueueUrl' \
    --output text)

echo "$FIFO_URL"
Output: https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo
Django Context: # Skills.objects.all() updates queue # FIFO_QUEUE_URL in settings # Ordered skill processing
Example 3: Get DLQ URL for configuration

Retrieve dead letter queue URL.

#!/bin/bash
DLQ_URL=$(aws sqs get-queue-url \
    --queue-name anihpj-jobpost-dlq \
    --query 'QueueUrl' \
    --output text)

# Get ARN for redrive policy
DLQ_ARN=$(aws sqs get-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$DLQ_URL" \
    --attribute-names QueueArn \
    --query 'Attributes.QueueArn' \
    --output text)

echo "DLQ ARN: $DLQ_ARN"
Output: DLQ ARN: arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-jobpost-dlq
Django Context: # Configure main queue with DLQ # Failed JobPost messages route to DLQ # Use ARN in redrive policy
Example 4: Get cross-account queue URL

Access queue in different AWS account.

#!/bin/bash
SHARED_QUEUE=$(aws sqs get-queue-url \
    --queue-name shared-jobpost-queue \
    --queue-owner-aws-account-id 987654321098 \
    --query 'QueueUrl' \
    --output text)

echo "$SHARED_QUEUE"
Output: https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/987654321098/shared-jobpost-queue
Django Context: # Cross-account queue access # Shared queue for multi-tenant setup # Requires proper IAM permissions
Example 5: Store URL in environment variable

Export queue URL for application use.

#!/bin/bash
export ANIHPJ_QUEUE_URL=$(aws sqs get-queue-url \
    --queue-name anihpj-jobpost-queue \
    --query 'QueueUrl' \
    --output text)

# Use in application
echo "Queue configured: $ANIHPJ_QUEUE_URL"

# Add to .env file
echo "QUEUE_URL=$ANIHPJ_QUEUE_URL" >> .env
Output: Queue configured: https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue
Django Context: # Django settings.py # QUEUE_URL = os.getenv('QUEUE_URL') # Configure queue in environment variables

list-queues

Lists all SQS queues in the account or filtered by prefix.

Syntax
aws sqs list-queues \
    [--queue-name-prefix <value>]
Parameters
  • --queue-name-prefix (string): Filter queues by name prefix
Return Values
  • QueueUrls: List of queue URLs
Example 1: List all queues

Get all SQS queues in the account.

#!/bin/bash
aws sqs list-queues
Output: { "QueueUrls": [ "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue", "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue", "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo", "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-dlq" ] }
Django Context: # Inventory all queues # 4 queues for anihpj project # JobPost, notifications, skills, DLQ
Example 2: List queues with prefix filter

Filter queues by anihpj prefix.

#!/bin/bash
aws sqs list-queues \
    --queue-name-prefix anihpj-
Output: { "QueueUrls": [ "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue", "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue", "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo", "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-dlq" ] }
Django Context: # Filter project-specific queues # All anihpj queues listed # Useful for monitoring dashboard
Example 3: Count total queues

Get count of queues for monitoring.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_COUNT=$(aws sqs list-queues \
    --queue-name-prefix anihpj- \
    --query 'length(QueueUrls)' \
    --output text)

echo "Total anihpj queues: $QUEUE_COUNT"
Output: Total anihpj queues: 4
Django Context: # Infrastructure monitoring # Expected queue count validation # Alert if count deviates from expected
Example 4: List FIFO queues only

Filter for FIFO queues specifically.

#!/bin/bash
aws sqs list-queues \
    --query 'QueueUrls[?contains(@, `.fifo`)]' \
    --output json
Output: [ "https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo" ]
Django Context: # List FIFO queues only # Skills updates queue identified # FIFO for ordered processing
Example 5: Export queue list to file

Save queue inventory for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws sqs list-queues \
    --queue-name-prefix anihpj- \
    --output json > anihpj_queues.json

# Pretty print
cat anihpj_queues.json | jq -r '.QueueUrls[]' > queue_list.txt

echo "Queue list saved to queue_list.txt"
Output: Queue list saved to queue_list.txt Contents of queue_list.txt: https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-dlq
Django Context: # Documentation for deployment # Queue inventory exported # Use in infrastructure as code

purge-queue

Deletes all messages in a queue without deleting the queue itself.

Syntax
aws sqs purge-queue \
    --queue-url <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue to purge
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Purge test queue

Clear all messages from development queue.

#!/bin/bash
TEST_QUEUE="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-test-queue"

aws sqs purge-queue \
    --queue-url "$TEST_QUEUE"

echo "Test queue purged"
Output: Test queue purged
Django Context: # Clear test data before new test run # pytest setup: purge_test_queue() # Fresh start for integration tests
Example 2: Purge with confirmation

Interactive purge with safety check.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

# Check message count first
MSG_COUNT=$(aws sqs get-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --attribute-names ApproximateNumberOfMessages \
    --query 'Attributes.ApproximateNumberOfMessages' \
    --output text)

echo "Queue has $MSG_COUNT messages"
read -p "Purge all messages? (yes/no): " CONFIRM

if [ "$CONFIRM" == "yes" ]; then
    aws sqs purge-queue --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL"
    echo "Queue purged"
fi
Output: Queue has 42 messages Purge all messages? (yes/no): yes Queue purged
Django Context: # Safe purge with confirmation # 42 pending JobPost messages deleted # Use for emergency cleanup
Example 3: Purge DLQ after resolution

Clear dead letter queue after fixing issues.

#!/bin/bash
DLQ_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-dlq"

aws sqs purge-queue --queue-url "$DLQ_URL"

echo "DLQ purged - all failed messages cleared"
Output: DLQ purged - all failed messages cleared
Django Context: # All failed JobPost messages resolved # Clear DLQ for fresh monitoring # JobPost.objects.filter(status='failed').update(status='fixed')
Example 4: Scheduled queue purge

Automated cleanup for staging environment.

#!/bin/bash
# Cron job: 0 2 * * * /path/to/purge_staging.sh

STAGING_QUEUE="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-staging-queue"

aws sqs purge-queue --queue-url "$STAGING_QUEUE"

echo "$(date): Staging queue purged" >> /var/log/queue-purge.log
Output: 2026-01-17 02:00:00: Staging queue purged
Django Context: # Nightly cleanup for staging # Clear test data automatically # Fresh environment each morning
Example 5: Purge before redeployment

Clear queue before application deployment.

#!/bin/bash
# deployment-prep.sh

QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

echo "Preparing for deployment..."

# Purge queue
aws sqs purge-queue --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL"

echo "Queue purged. Ready for deployment."
sleep 60  # Wait for purge to complete
Output: Preparing for deployment... Queue purged. Ready for deployment.
Django Context: # Pre-deployment cleanup # Clear old JobPost messages # Prevent processing with old code version

send-message-batch

Sends up to 10 messages in a single batch operation for efficiency.

Syntax
aws sqs send-message-batch \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --entries <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --entries (list): List of messages with Id and MessageBody
Return Values
  • Successful: List of successfully sent messages
  • Failed: List of failed sends with error codes
Example 1: Batch send job posts

Send multiple job post messages efficiently.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs send-message-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {
            "Id": "1",
            "MessageBody": "{\"action\":\"create_jobpost\",\"title\":\"Backend Dev\",\"location_id\":1}"
        },
        {
            "Id": "2",
            "MessageBody": "{\"action\":\"create_jobpost\",\"title\":\"Frontend Dev\",\"location_id\":2}"
        },
        {
            "Id": "3",
            "MessageBody": "{\"action\":\"create_jobpost\",\"title\":\"DevOps Engineer\",\"location_id\":3}"
        }
    ]'
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "1", "MessageId": "msg-001", "MD5OfMessageBody": "abc123..."}, {"Id": "2", "MessageId": "msg-002", "MD5OfMessageBody": "def456..."}, {"Id": "3", "MessageId": "msg-003", "MD5OfMessageBody": "ghi789..."} ] }
Django Context: # Batch create 3 job posts # Location.objects.filter(id__in=[1,2,3]) # Efficient bulk messaging - 1 API call vs 3
Example 2: Batch send with attributes

Send batch with message attributes.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

aws sqs send-message-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {
            "Id": "n1",
            "MessageBody": "{\"user_id\":10,\"jobpost_id\":100}",
            "MessageAttributes": {
                "Priority": {"StringValue": "high", "DataType": "String"}
            }
        },
        {
            "Id": "n2",
            "MessageBody": "{\"user_id\":20,\"jobpost_id\":100}",
            "MessageAttributes": {
                "Priority": {"StringValue": "normal", "DataType": "String"}
            }
        }
    ]'
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "n1", "MessageId": "notif-001"}, {"Id": "n2", "MessageId": "notif-002"} ] }
Django Context: # Notify multiple users about JobPost # User.objects.filter(id__in=[10,20]) # Priority-based notification processing
Example 3: Batch send to FIFO queue

Send batch to FIFO with message group ID.

#!/bin/bash
FIFO_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"

aws sqs send-message-batch \
    --queue-url "$FIFO_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {
            "Id": "s1",
            "MessageBody": "{\"skill_id\":1,\"action\":\"update\"}",
            "MessageGroupId": "skill-updates",
            "MessageDeduplicationId": "skill-1-'$(date +%s)'"
        },
        {
            "Id": "s2",
            "MessageBody": "{\"skill_id\":2,\"action\":\"update\"}",
            "MessageGroupId": "skill-updates",
            "MessageDeduplicationId": "skill-2-'$(date +%s)'"
        }
    ]'
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "s1", "MessageId": "fifo-001", "SequenceNumber": "001"}, {"Id": "s2", "MessageId": "fifo-002", "SequenceNumber": "002"} ] }
Django Context: # Batch skill updates in FIFO order # Skills.objects.filter(id__in=[1,2]).update(...) # Maintains processing order
Example 4: Handle partial batch failure

Process batch with error handling.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

RESULT=$(aws sqs send-message-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {"Id": "1", "MessageBody": "{\"valid\":\"json\"}"},
        {"Id": "2", "MessageBody": ""},
        {"Id": "3", "MessageBody": "{\"another\":\"valid\"}"}
    ]')

SUCCESS_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.Successful | length')
FAILED_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.Failed | length')

echo "Success: $SUCCESS_COUNT, Failed: $FAILED_COUNT"
Output: Success: 2, Failed: 1 { "Successful": [ {"Id": "1", "MessageId": "msg-001"}, {"Id": "3", "MessageId": "msg-003"} ], "Failed": [ {"Id": "2", "Code": "EmptyBatchRequest", "Message": "Empty message body"} ] }
Django Context: # 2 JobPost messages sent successfully # 1 failed due to empty body # Retry failed messages with valid data
Example 5: Batch send from JSON file

Send large batch from file.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

# Create batch file
cat > batch_messages.json << 'EOF'
[
    {"Id": "1", "MessageBody": "{\"title\":\"Job 1\"}"},
    {"Id": "2", "MessageBody": "{\"title\":\"Job 2\"}"},
    {"Id": "3", "MessageBody": "{\"title\":\"Job 3\"}"},
    {"Id": "4", "MessageBody": "{\"title\":\"Job 4\"}"},
    {"Id": "5", "MessageBody": "{\"title\":\"Job 5\"}"}
]
EOF

aws sqs send-message-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries file://batch_messages.json
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "1", "MessageId": "msg-001"}, {"Id": "2", "MessageId": "msg-002"}, {"Id": "3", "MessageId": "msg-003"}, {"Id": "4", "MessageId": "msg-004"}, {"Id": "5", "MessageId": "msg-005"} ] }
Django Context: # Bulk job post creation from file # jobs = JobPost.objects.bulk_create([...]) # Efficient batch processing

set-queue-attributes

Sets attributes for an SQS queue to modify configuration.

Syntax
aws sqs set-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --attributes <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --attributes (map): Map of attributes to set
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Update visibility timeout

Increase visibility timeout for longer processing.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs set-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --attributes '{
        "VisibilityTimeout": "600"
    }'

echo "Visibility timeout updated to 10 minutes"
Output: Visibility timeout updated to 10 minutes
Django Context: # JobPost processing takes longer # Prevent duplicate processing # 10-minute window for complex operations
Example 2: Configure dead letter queue

Set up redrive policy for failed messages.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"
DLQ_ARN="arn:aws:sqs:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-jobpost-dlq"

aws sqs set-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --attributes '{
        "RedrivePolicy": "{\"deadLetterTargetArn\":\"'$DLQ_ARN'\",\"maxReceiveCount\":\"3\"}"
    }'

echo "DLQ configured with 3 retries"
Output: DLQ configured with 3 retries
Django Context: # Failed JobPost messages to DLQ after 3 attempts # Automatic error handling # Monitor DLQ for persistent failures
Example 3: Enable long polling

Set receive wait time for cost optimization.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

aws sqs set-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --attributes '{
        "ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds": "20"
    }'

echo "Long polling enabled (20 seconds)"
Output: Long polling enabled (20 seconds)
Django Context: # Reduce empty responses # Cost optimization for notification queue # Worker: receive_message with wait_time
Example 4: Update message retention period

Change how long messages are retained.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs set-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --attributes '{
        "MessageRetentionPeriod": "604800"
    }'

echo "Message retention set to 7 days"
Output: Message retention set to 7 days
Django Context: # Keep JobPost messages for 1 week # Balance between retention and storage # Compliance requirement met
Example 5: Set multiple attributes

Update several queue settings at once.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs set-queue-attributes \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --attributes '{
        "VisibilityTimeout": "300",
        "MessageRetentionPeriod": "1209600",
        "ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds": "10",
        "MaximumMessageSize": "262144"
    }'

echo "Multiple attributes updated"
Output: Multiple attributes updated
Django Context: # Comprehensive queue configuration # 5-min visibility, 14-day retention # 10-sec long polling, 256KB messages # Optimized for JobPost processing

change-message-visibility

Changes the visibility timeout of a message being processed.

Syntax
aws sqs change-message-visibility \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --receipt-handle <value> \
    --visibility-timeout <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --receipt-handle (string): Receipt handle from receive-message
  • --visibility-timeout (integer): New visibility timeout (0-43200 seconds)
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Extend processing time

Increase visibility when job takes longer than expected.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"
RECEIPT="AQEBwJnKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0a..."

# Processing taking longer - extend by 5 more minutes
aws sqs change-message-visibility \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --receipt-handle "$RECEIPT" \
    --visibility-timeout 300

echo "Visibility extended by 5 minutes"
Output: Visibility extended by 5 minutes
Django Context: # JobPost creation taking longer # Prevent other workers from picking up # Complex validation: JobPost.objects.filter(...).exists()
Example 2: Reset visibility for immediate retry

Make message visible immediately for retry.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"
RECEIPT="AQEB1xJmKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0b..."

# Processing failed, make available immediately
aws sqs change-message-visibility \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --receipt-handle "$RECEIPT" \
    --visibility-timeout 0

echo "Message available for immediate retry"
Output: Message available for immediate retry
Django Context: # Transient error occurred # Make message available to another worker # Retry JobPost creation immediately
Example 3: Extend for skill processing

Give more time for complex skill updates.

#!/bin/bash
FIFO_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"
RECEIPT="AQEB2yKnKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0c..."

# Skill update requires database migration
aws sqs change-message-visibility \
    --queue-url "$FIFO_URL" \
    --receipt-handle "$RECEIPT" \
    --visibility-timeout 900

echo "Extended to 15 minutes for migration"
Output: Extended to 15 minutes for migration
Django Context: # Complex skill update with migration # Skills.objects.all().update(...) # Need 15 minutes for bulk update
Example 4: Progressive visibility extension

Extend visibility multiple times during long process.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"
RECEIPT="AQEB3zLnKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0d..."

# Every 4 minutes, extend by another 5 minutes
for i in {1..3}; do
    aws sqs change-message-visibility \
        --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
        --receipt-handle "$RECEIPT" \
        --visibility-timeout 300
    
    echo "Extension $i: Processing continues..."
    sleep 240  # Process for 4 minutes
done
Output: Extension 1: Processing continues... Extension 2: Processing continues... Extension 3: Processing continues...
Django Context: # Long-running JobPost batch operation # Keep extending visibility during processing # JobPost.objects.bulk_create(large_list)
Example 5: Conditional visibility change

Extend based on processing status.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"
RECEIPT="AQEB4aMnKyrHigUMZj6rYigCgxlaS3SLy0e..."

PROCESSING_TIME=120  # seconds so far

if [ $PROCESSING_TIME -gt 100 ]; then
    # Need more time
    aws sqs change-message-visibility \
        --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
        --receipt-handle "$RECEIPT" \
        --visibility-timeout 180
    echo "Extended visibility"
fi
Output: Extended visibility
Django Context: # Monitor processing time # Extend if sending many notifications # User.objects.filter(...).count() > threshold

change-message-visibility-batch

Changes visibility timeout for up to 10 messages in a batch.

Syntax
aws sqs change-message-visibility-batch \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --entries <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --entries (list): List with Id, ReceiptHandle, VisibilityTimeout
Return Values
  • Successful: List of successful updates
  • Failed: List of failed updates
Example 1: Batch extend visibility

Extend timeout for multiple messages being processed.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs change-message-visibility-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {"Id": "1", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_1", "VisibilityTimeout": 600},
        {"Id": "2", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_2", "VisibilityTimeout": 600},
        {"Id": "3", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_3", "VisibilityTimeout": 600}
    ]'
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "1"}, {"Id": "2"}, {"Id": "3"} ] }
Django Context: # Batch JobPost processing taking longer # Extend all 3 messages to 10 minutes # Prevents duplicate processing by other workers
Example 2: Batch reset for retry

Make multiple messages available immediately.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

aws sqs change-message-visibility-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {"Id": "n1", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_N1", "VisibilityTimeout": 0},
        {"Id": "n2", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_N2", "VisibilityTimeout": 0}
    ]'
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "n1"}, {"Id": "n2"} ] }
Django Context: # Email service temporarily down # Reset visibility for immediate retry # User notifications will be retried
Example 3: Mixed visibility timeouts

Set different timeouts based on message complexity.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs change-message-visibility-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {"Id": "simple", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_A", "VisibilityTimeout": 300},
        {"Id": "complex", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_B", "VisibilityTimeout": 900},
        {"Id": "medium", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_C", "VisibilityTimeout": 600}
    ]'
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "simple"}, {"Id": "complex"}, {"Id": "medium"} ] }
Django Context: # Different JobPost complexities # Simple: 5 min, Medium: 10 min, Complex: 15 min # Optimize processing time per job type
Example 4: Handle partial batch success

Process results with error handling.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

RESULT=$(aws sqs change-message-visibility-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries '[
        {"Id": "1", "ReceiptHandle": "VALID_HANDLE", "VisibilityTimeout": 300},
        {"Id": "2", "ReceiptHandle": "EXPIRED_HANDLE", "VisibilityTimeout": 300}
    ]')

echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.Failed[]' || echo "All successful"
Output: { "Successful": [{"Id": "1"}], "Failed": [ { "Id": "2", "Code": "ReceiptHandleIsInvalid", "Message": "Receipt handle expired" } ] }
Django Context: # 1 message extended successfully # 1 failed - handle expired, message reprocessed # Handle failures gracefully in worker
Example 5: Batch extend from file

Extend visibility for messages from JSON file.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

cat > visibility_updates.json << 'EOF'
[
    {"Id": "1", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_1", "VisibilityTimeout": 600},
    {"Id": "2", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_2", "VisibilityTimeout": 600},
    {"Id": "3", "ReceiptHandle": "HANDLE_3", "VisibilityTimeout": 600}
]
EOF

aws sqs change-message-visibility-batch \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --entries file://visibility_updates.json
Output: { "Successful": [ {"Id": "1"}, {"Id": "2"}, {"Id": "3"} ] }
Django Context: # Batch visibility extension from file # Useful for scheduled worker operations # JobPost batch processing workflow

add-permission

Adds a permission to a queue for a specified principal.

Syntax
aws sqs add-permission \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --label <value> \
    --aws-account-ids <value> \
    --actions <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --label (string): Unique identifier for the permission
  • --aws-account-ids (list): AWS account IDs to grant permission
  • --actions (list): Actions to allow (SendMessage, ReceiveMessage, etc.)
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Allow SNS to send messages

Grant SNS topic permission to send to queue.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs add-permission \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --label "SNS-SendMessage" \
    --aws-account-ids "123456789012" \
    --actions "SendMessage"

echo "SNS can now send messages to queue"
Output: SNS can now send messages to queue
Django Context: # SNS topic triggers JobPost processing # SNS publishes -> SQS receives -> Worker processes # Event-driven architecture for job posts
Example 2: Cross-account access

Allow another AWS account to send messages.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs add-permission \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --label "PartnerAccount-Send" \
    --aws-account-ids "987654321098" \
    --actions "SendMessage"

echo "Partner account can send job posts"
Output: Partner account can send job posts
Django Context: # Multi-tenant job board # Partner companies send JobPost messages # Shared queue for multiple organizations
Example 3: Grant multiple actions

Allow multiple SQS actions for an account.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

aws sqs add-permission \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --label "AdminAccess" \
    --aws-account-ids "123456789012" \
    --actions "SendMessage" "ReceiveMessage" "DeleteMessage"

echo "Full queue access granted"
Output: Full queue access granted
Django Context: # Admin account full access # Send, receive, delete notifications # User notification management
Example 4: Lambda function access

Allow Lambda to send messages to queue.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs add-permission \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --label "Lambda-JobProcessor" \
    --aws-account-ids "123456789012" \
    --actions "SendMessage" "GetQueueUrl"

echo "Lambda function access configured"
Output: Lambda function access configured
Django Context: # Lambda triggers for JobPost events # Serverless job processing # Lambda -> SQS -> Django worker pipeline
Example 5: S3 event notification access

Allow S3 to send event notifications to queue.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-uploads-queue"

aws sqs add-permission \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --label "S3-EventNotification" \
    --aws-account-ids "123456789012" \
    --actions "SendMessage"

echo "S3 can send upload notifications"
Output: S3 can send upload notifications
Django Context: # S3 uploads trigger processing # User uploads resume -> S3 event -> SQS -> Process # Author.objects.filter(resume_uploaded=True)

remove-permission

Removes a permission from a queue.

Syntax
aws sqs remove-permission \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --label <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --label (string): Label of the permission to remove
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Remove SNS permission

Revoke SNS access to queue.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs remove-permission \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --label "SNS-SendMessage"

echo "SNS permission removed"
Output: SNS permission removed
Django Context: # Disable SNS integration # Direct JobPost creation only # Security audit: remove unnecessary permissions
Example 2: Revoke cross-account access

Remove partner account permission.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs remove-permission \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --label "PartnerAccount-Send"

echo "Partner access revoked"
Output: Partner access revoked
Django Context: # End partnership # Partner can no longer send JobPost messages # Security: revoke access immediately
Example 3: Remove Lambda permission

Revoke Lambda function access.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs remove-permission \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --label "Lambda-JobProcessor"

echo "Lambda access removed"
Output: Lambda access removed
Django Context: # Migrated from Lambda to Django workers # Remove old Lambda permissions # Direct worker processing now
Example 4: Security cleanup

Remove old permissions during security audit.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

# Remove multiple old permissions
for LABEL in "OldService-Access" "TempDev-Access" "ExpiredAPI-Access"; do
    aws sqs remove-permission \
        --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
        --label "$LABEL"
    echo "Removed: $LABEL"
done
Output: Removed: OldService-Access Removed: TempDev-Access Removed: ExpiredAPI-Access
Django Context: # Security audit cleanup # Remove unused User notification permissions # Principle of least privilege
Example 5: Remove S3 event permission

Disable S3 event notifications.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-uploads-queue"

aws sqs remove-permission \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --label "S3-EventNotification"

echo "S3 event notifications disabled"
Output: S3 event notifications disabled
Django Context: # Changed upload processing method # No longer using S3 events # Author resume processing via API now

tag-queue

Adds or overwrites tags for an SQS queue.

Syntax
aws sqs tag-queue \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --tags <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --tags (map): Key-value pairs of tags
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Add environment tags

Tag queue with environment and project info.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs tag-queue \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --tags Environment=production Project=anihpj Component=jobpost

echo "Queue tagged successfully"
Output: Queue tagged successfully
Django Context: # Production JobPost queue tagged # Cost allocation by project # Filter queues: aws resourcegroupstaggingapi
Example 2: Add cost center tags

Tag for billing and cost tracking.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

aws sqs tag-queue \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --tags CostCenter=Engineering Department=Backend Owner=DevTeam

echo "Cost tracking tags added"
Output: Cost tracking tags added
Django Context: # User notification queue cost tracking # Billing reports by CostCenter # Department budget allocation
Example 3: Add compliance tags

Tag queue for compliance and security.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-applications-queue"

aws sqs tag-queue \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --tags DataClassification=Sensitive Compliance=PII Encrypted=true

echo "Compliance tags added"
Output: Compliance tags added
Django Context: # Application data queue - PII # Author applications contain sensitive data # Compliance: GDPR/CCPA tracking
Example 4: Update existing tags

Update tags to reflect queue changes.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs tag-queue \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --tags Version=v2 LastUpdated=2026-01-17 Status=active

echo "Tags updated"
Output: Tags updated
Django Context: # JobPost queue version tracking # Document queue evolution # Status for monitoring systems
Example 5: Batch tag multiple attributes

Add comprehensive tagging strategy.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"

aws sqs tag-queue \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --tags \
        Project=anihpj \
        Environment=production \
        Component=skills \
        Type=FIFO \
        Owner=Backend-Team \
        CostCenter=Engineering \
        ManagedBy=Terraform

echo "Comprehensive tagging completed"
Output: Comprehensive tagging completed
Django Context: # Skills FIFO queue fully tagged # Infrastructure as Code tracking # Skills.objects.all() processing queue # Complete metadata for governance

untag-queue

Removes tags from an SQS queue.

Syntax
aws sqs untag-queue \
    --queue-url <value> \
    --tag-keys <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
  • --tag-keys (list): List of tag keys to remove
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Remove single tag

Remove temporary tag from queue.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs untag-queue \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --tag-keys TempTag

echo "Temporary tag removed"
Output: Temporary tag removed
Django Context: # Remove temporary JobPost queue tag # Cleanup after testing # Maintain clean tag structure
Example 2: Remove multiple tags

Clean up obsolete tags.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

aws sqs untag-queue \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --tag-keys OldOwner DeprecatedTag TestEnvironment

echo "Multiple tags removed"
Output: Multiple tags removed
Django Context: # Clean obsolete User notification tags # Tag governance - remove unused keys # Keep tagging schema current
Example 3: Remove cost allocation tags

Update cost tracking structure.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs untag-queue \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --tag-keys OldCostCenter OldDepartment

echo "Old cost tags removed"
Output: Old cost tags removed
Django Context: # Update cost allocation model # JobPost queue cost tracking updated # New tags will be added separately
Example 4: Cleanup during migration

Remove tags during infrastructure migration.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"

# Remove old infrastructure tags
aws sqs untag-queue \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --tag-keys ManagedBy OldRegion MigrationStatus

echo "Migration tags cleaned up"
Output: Migration tags cleaned up
Django Context: # Skills queue migration complete # Remove temporary migration tags # Fresh tag structure post-migration
Example 5: Security tag removal

Remove sensitive or deprecated security tags.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-applications-queue"

aws sqs untag-queue \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --tag-keys TemporaryAccess ReviewPending

echo "Security review tags removed"
Output: Security review tags removed
Django Context: # Security review completed # Author applications queue approved # Remove temporary security tags

list-queue-tags

Lists all tags on an SQS queue.

Syntax
aws sqs list-queue-tags \
    --queue-url <value>
Parameters
  • --queue-url (string): URL of the queue
Return Values
  • Tags: Map of tag keys and values
Example 1: List all queue tags

View all tags on a queue.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-jobpost-queue"

aws sqs list-queue-tags \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL"
Output: { "Tags": { "Environment": "production", "Project": "anihpj", "Component": "jobpost", "CostCenter": "Engineering", "Owner": "Backend-Team" } }
Django Context: # JobPost queue metadata # Production environment confirmed # Cost allocation to Engineering
Example 2: Extract specific tag value

Get single tag value for automation.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-notification-queue"

ENVIRONMENT=$(aws sqs list-queue-tags \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --query 'Tags.Environment' \
    --output text)

echo "Queue environment: $ENVIRONMENT"
Output: Queue environment: production
Django Context: # Verify User notification queue environment # Use in deployment scripts # Environment-specific configuration
Example 3: Verify compliance tags

Check required tags for compliance.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-applications-queue"

TAGS=$(aws sqs list-queue-tags --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL")

# Check for required compliance tags
echo "$TAGS" | jq -e '.Tags.DataClassification' > /dev/null && echo "✓ Data classification tagged" || echo "✗ Missing data classification"
echo "$TAGS" | jq -e '.Tags.Encrypted' > /dev/null && echo "✓ Encryption status tagged" || echo "✗ Missing encryption tag"
Output: ✓ Data classification tagged ✓ Encryption status tagged Tags: { "Tags": { "DataClassification": "Sensitive", "Encrypted": "true", "Compliance": "PII" } }
Django Context: # Verify Author applications queue compliance # Required tags present # Audit-ready configuration
Example 4: Export tags to file

Save queue tags for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
QUEUE_URL="https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"

aws sqs list-queue-tags \
    --queue-url "$QUEUE_URL" \
    --output json > skill_queue_tags.json

echo "Tags exported to skill_queue_tags.json"
cat skill_queue_tags.json | jq -r '.Tags'
Output: Tags exported to skill_queue_tags.json { "Project": "anihpj", "Component": "skills", "Type": "FIFO", "Environment": "production" }
Django Context: # Skills FIFO queue tag documentation # Infrastructure inventory # Use for IaC templates
Example 5: Compare tags across queues

Verify consistent tagging across queues.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare tags for all anihpj queues

for QUEUE in anihpj-jobpost-queue anihpj-notification-queue anihpj-skill-updates.fifo; do
