Django Context: # User.objects notifications tagged for GDPR compliance # Confidential data classification for user registration events # Security team ownership for compliance audits
Example 4: Batch tag multiple resources

Apply project tags to all topics.

#!/bin/bash
TOPICS=(
    "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-jobpost-notifications"
    "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-skill-updates.fifo"
    "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-location-alerts"
)

for topic in "${TOPICS[@]}"; do
    aws sns tag-resource \
        --resource-arn "$topic" \
        --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=ManagedBy,Value=Terraform
done
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # All notification topics tagged with project identifier # Infrastructure managed by Terraform # Consistent tagging across JobPost, Skills, Location topics
Example 5: Tag with retention policy

Add tags for data retention compliance.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-admin-alerts \
    --tags Key=RetentionPeriod,Value=90days Key=BackupRequired,Value=true Key=CriticalData,Value=true
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Admin alerts tagged with 90-day retention # Critical data requiring backup # Compliance tags for audit trail of system events

untag-resource

Removes tags from an SNS topic.

Syntax
aws sns untag-resource \
    --resource-arn <value> \
    --tag-keys <value>
Parameters
  • --resource-arn (string): ARN of the topic
  • --tag-keys (list): Keys of tags to remove
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Remove temporary tag

Remove test tag from topic.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-jobpost-notifications \
    --tag-keys TestTag TemporaryFlag
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Removed test tags from JobPost notification topic # Clean up after testing phase # Production-ready topic with proper tags only
Example 2: Remove deprecated cost center

Update cost allocation by removing old tag.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-skill-updates.fifo \
    --tag-keys OldCostCenter
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Removed deprecated cost center from Skills topic # Updated to new organizational structure # Skills.objects notifications reallocated to new team budget
Example 3: Bulk remove tags

Remove multiple deprecated tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-user-registrations \
    --tag-keys DevOwner StagingFlag LegacySystem TestPhase
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Cleaned up development tags from User registration topic # Removed all staging and legacy identifiers # Production topic with current tags only
Example 4: Remove tags from all topics

Clean up deprecated tags across all resources.

#!/bin/bash
TOPICS=(
    "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-jobpost-notifications"
    "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-location-alerts"
)

for topic in "${TOPICS[@]}"; do
    aws sns untag-resource \
        --resource-arn "$topic" \
        --tag-keys DeprecatedTag OldVersion
done
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Batch cleanup of deprecated tags # JobPost and Location topics updated # Consistent tagging strategy across all notification topics
Example 5: Remove compliance tag after audit

Clean up temporary compliance tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-admin-alerts \
    --tag-keys AuditInProgress ComplianceReview2025
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Removed temporary audit tags after compliance review # Admin alerts topic audit completed # Clean tag set for ongoing operations

list-tags-for-resource

Lists tags for an SNS topic.

Syntax
aws sns list-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --resource-arn (string): ARN of the topic
Return Values
  • Tags: Array of key-value tag pairs
Example 1: List all topic tags

View all tags on job post topic.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-jobpost-notifications
Output: { "Tags": [ { "Key": "Environment", "Value": "Production" }, { "Key": "Application", "Value": "anihpj" }, { "Key": "CostCenter", "Value": "Engineering" } ] }
Django Context: # JobPost notification topic tags for organization # Production environment, anihpj application # Engineering cost center for budget tracking
Example 2: Export tags to file

Save tag configuration for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-skill-updates.fifo \
    > skill-topic-tags.json
Output: { "Tags": [ { "Key": "Component", "Value": "Skills" }, { "Key": "Team", "Value": "Backend" } ] }
Django Context: # Skills.objects topic tags exported for backup # Component and team ownership documented # Infrastructure as code documentation
Example 3: Check compliance tags

Verify GDPR compliance tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-user-registrations \
    --query 'Tags[?Key==`Compliance`].Value' --output text
Output: GDPR
Django Context: # User registration topic has GDPR compliance tag # User.objects.create() notifications compliant # Audit verification for data protection requirements
Example 4: List tags in table format

Display tags in readable format.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-location-alerts \
    --output table
Output: -------------------------------------- | ListTagsForResource | +----------+-----------------------+ | Key | Value | +----------+-----------------------+ | Project | anihpj | | Feature | LocationAlerts | | Owner | ProductTeam | +----------+-----------------------+
Django Context: # Location alerts topic tags displayed # Project, feature, and owner clearly identified # Easy visual inspection of topic metadata
Example 5: Audit all topic tags

Check tags across all topics for compliance.

#!/bin/bash
TOPICS=$(aws sns list-topics --query 'Topics[*].TopicArn' --output text)

for topic in $TOPICS; do
    echo "Tags for: $topic"
    aws sns list-tags-for-resource --resource-arn "$topic"
    echo "---"
done
Output: Tags for: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-jobpost-notifications { "Tags": [{"Key": "Environment", "Value": "Production"}] } --- Tags for: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-skill-updates.fifo { "Tags": [{"Key": "Component", "Value": "Skills"}] } ---
Django Context: # Complete tag audit across all notification topics # Verifies JobPost, Skills, User, Location topics properly tagged # Compliance check for resource organization

create-platform-application

Creates a platform application object for mobile push notifications.

Syntax
aws sns create-platform-application \
    --name <value> \
    --platform <value> \
    --attributes <value>
Parameters
  • --name (string): Name of the platform application
  • --platform (string): Platform (GCM, APNS, APNS_SANDBOX, FCM, etc.)
  • --attributes (map): Platform-specific credentials
Return Values
  • PlatformApplicationArn: ARN of the created platform application
Example 1: Create FCM application for Android

Setup Firebase Cloud Messaging for Android push.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-platform-application \
    --name anihpj-android-app \
    --platform GCM \
    --attributes PlatformCredential=YOUR_FCM_SERVER_KEY
Output: { "PlatformApplicationArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android-app" }
Django Context: # Android app setup for push notifications # Users receive JobPost alerts on mobile devices # User.objects with Android devices can get real-time notifications
Example 2: Create APNS application for iOS

Setup Apple Push Notification Service for iOS.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-platform-application \
    --name anihpj-ios-app \
    --platform APNS \
    --attributes PlatformCredential=YOUR_APNS_PRIVATE_KEY,PlatformPrincipal=YOUR_APNS_CERTIFICATE
Output: { "PlatformApplicationArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios-app" }
Django Context: # iOS app configured for push notifications # iPhone users receive JobPost and skill update alerts # Integration with User.objects iOS device tokens
Example 3: Create sandbox APNS for testing

Setup iOS sandbox environment for development.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-platform-application \
    --name anihpj-ios-dev \
    --platform APNS_SANDBOX \
    --attributes PlatformCredential=DEV_APNS_KEY,PlatformPrincipal=DEV_APNS_CERT
Output: { "PlatformApplicationArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS_SANDBOX/anihpj-ios-dev" }
Django Context: # iOS development environment for testing push # Test JobPost notifications before production release # Safe testing without affecting production users
Example 4: Create with event endpoints

Configure failure feedback for monitoring.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-platform-application \
    --name anihpj-android-monitored \
    --platform GCM \
    --attributes PlatformCredential=FCM_KEY,EventEndpointCreated=arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:device-created,EventEndpointDeleted=arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:device-deleted
Output: { "PlatformApplicationArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android-monitored" }
Django Context: # Monitored Android app with device lifecycle events # Track when User devices register/unregister # Analytics for mobile user engagement
Example 5: Create multi-region platform app

Setup platform app in different region.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-platform-application \
    --name anihpj-android-eu \
    --platform GCM \
    --attributes PlatformCredential=FCM_KEY \
    --region eu-west-1
Output: { "PlatformApplicationArn": "arn:aws:sns:eu-west-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android-eu" }
Django Context: # EU region platform app for GDPR compliance # European User.objects receive notifications from EU servers # Data residency compliance for Location-based alerts

create-platform-endpoint

Creates an endpoint for mobile device to receive push notifications.

Syntax
aws sns create-platform-endpoint \
    --platform-application-arn <value> \
    --token <value> \
    [--custom-user-data <value>] \
    [--attributes <value>]
Parameters
  • --platform-application-arn (string): ARN of platform application
  • --token (string): Device token from mobile OS
  • --custom-user-data (string): Arbitrary user data
  • --attributes (map): Endpoint attributes
Return Values
  • EndpointArn: ARN of the created endpoint
Example 1: Register Android device

Create endpoint for user's Android device.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-platform-endpoint \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android-app \
    --token "APA91bHun4MxP5egoKMwt2KZFBaFUH-1RYqx..."
Output: { "EndpointArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android-app/a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef" }
Django Context: # User's Android device registered for push notifications # Links to User.objects with device_token field # Can receive JobPost alerts and skill update notifications
Example 2: Register iOS device with custom data

Create endpoint with user metadata.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-platform-endpoint \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios-app \
    --token "740f4707bebcf74f9b7c25d48e3358945f6aa01da5ddb387462c7eaf61bb78ad" \
    --custom-user-data "user_id:12345,preferences:jobpost_alerts"
Output: { "EndpointArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/APNS/anihpj-ios-app/b2c3d4e5-6789-01bc-defg" }
Django Context: # iOS device for User.objects.get(id=12345) # Custom data tracks notification preferences # JobPost alerts enabled for this device
Example 3: Register device with attributes

Enable/disable endpoint notifications.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-platform-endpoint \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android-app \
    --token "device-token-xyz789" \
    --attributes Enabled=true
Output: { "EndpointArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android-app/c3d4e5f6-7890-12cd-efgh" }
Django Context: # Device endpoint created and enabled # User.notification_enabled=True in database # Ready to receive location alerts and job notifications
Example 4: Bulk register devices

Register multiple user devices.

#!/bin/bash
PLATFORM_ARN="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android-app"
TOKENS=("token1" "token2" "token3")

for token in "${TOKENS[@]}"; do
    aws sns create-platform-endpoint \
        --platform-application-arn $PLATFORM_ARN \
        --token "$token"
done
Output: { "EndpointArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android-app/endpoint1" } { "EndpointArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android-app/endpoint2" } { "EndpointArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android-app/endpoint3" }
Django Context: # Multiple User.objects devices registered # Batch onboarding for mobile app users # All devices ready for JobPost push notifications
Example 5: Register with user segmentation

Create endpoint with targeting metadata.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-platform-endpoint \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios-app \
    --token "ios-device-token" \
    --custom-user-data "location:San Francisco,skills:Python,role:developer"
Output: { "EndpointArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/APNS/anihpj-ios-app/d4e5f6g7-8901-23de-fghi" }
Django Context: # Device with targeting metadata for personalized alerts # Location.objects.filter(city='San Francisco') jobs # Skills.objects.filter(name='Python') skill updates # Targeted JobPost notifications based on user profile

delete-platform-application

Deletes a platform application and all its endpoints.

Syntax
aws sns delete-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --platform-application-arn (string): ARN of platform application
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Delete deprecated Android app

Remove old platform application.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns delete-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android-old
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Old Android app removed from notification system # All device endpoints automatically deleted # Users migrated to new app version
Example 2: Delete test platform

Clean up sandbox environment.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns delete-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS_SANDBOX/anihpj-ios-dev
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # iOS sandbox app deleted after testing complete # Production APNS app remains active # Test devices no longer receive notifications
Example 3: Delete with confirmation

Verify before deleting platform app.

#!/bin/bash
PLATFORM_ARN="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-legacy"
aws sns get-platform-application-attributes --platform-application-arn $PLATFORM_ARN &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Deleting platform application"
    aws sns delete-platform-application --platform-application-arn $PLATFORM_ARN
else
    echo "Platform application not found"
fi
Output: Deleting platform application
Django Context: # Safe deletion with existence check # Legacy mobile app removed from infrastructure # Prevents errors from deleting non-existent apps
Example 4: Delete multiple platforms

Remove all deprecated platform apps.

#!/bin/bash
PLATFORMS=(
    "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/old-app-1"
    "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/old-app-2"
)

for platform in "${PLATFORMS[@]}"; do
    echo "Deleting: $platform"
    aws sns delete-platform-application --platform-application-arn "$platform"
done
Output: Deleting: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/old-app-1 Deleting: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/old-app-2
Django Context: # Batch cleanup of deprecated mobile apps # All old versions removed from notification system # Clean infrastructure for current app versions only
Example 5: Delete with endpoint warning

Delete platform and warn about endpoints.

#!/bin/bash
PLATFORM_ARN="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios-old"
ENDPOINT_COUNT=$(aws sns list-endpoints-by-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn $PLATFORM_ARN \
    --query 'length(Endpoints)' --output text)

echo "Warning: $ENDPOINT_COUNT endpoints will be deleted"
aws sns delete-platform-application --platform-application-arn $PLATFORM_ARN
echo "Platform application deleted"
Output: Warning: 523 endpoints will be deleted Platform application deleted
Django Context: # 523 User device endpoints deleted with platform # All iOS users need to re-register with new app # Coordinate with User.objects bulk notification before deletion

delete-endpoint

Deletes the endpoint for a device from Amazon SNS platform application.

Syntax
aws sns delete-endpoint \
    --endpoint-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --endpoint-arn (string): ARN of the endpoint to delete
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Delete single device endpoint

Remove a user's device from push notifications.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns delete-endpoint \
    --endpoint-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-mobile/abc123
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # User.objects.get(id=123).push_token = None # Device unregistered from JobPost notifications # User must re-enable push notifications in app
Example 2: Delete endpoint for user logout

Clean up endpoint when user logs out.

#!/bin/bash
USER_ENDPOINT="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/APNS/anihpj-ios/user789"
aws sns delete-endpoint --endpoint-arn $USER_ENDPOINT
echo "User device unregistered from notifications"
Output: User device unregistered from notifications
Django Context: # User.objects.filter(id=789).update(device_token=None) # No more JobPost alerts to this device # Skills match notifications stopped
Example 3: Delete invalid endpoints

Clean up endpoints that have become invalid.

#!/bin/bash
# Get disabled endpoints and delete them
aws sns list-endpoints-by-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android \
    --query 'Endpoints[?Attributes.Enabled==`false`].EndpointArn' \
    --output text | while read endpoint; do
        aws sns delete-endpoint --endpoint-arn "$endpoint"
        echo "Deleted: $endpoint"
    done
Output: Deleted: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android/abc123 Deleted: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android/def456
Django Context: # Clean up disabled devices from notification system # User.objects.filter(device_status='disabled') correlation # Reduce notification costs by removing stale endpoints
Example 4: Delete endpoint with verification

Verify endpoint exists before deletion.

#!/bin/bash
ENDPOINT="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/APNS/anihpj-ios/xyz789"
if aws sns get-endpoint-attributes --endpoint-arn $ENDPOINT &> /dev/null; then
    aws sns delete-endpoint --endpoint-arn $ENDPOINT
    echo "Endpoint successfully deleted"
else
    echo "Endpoint not found"
fi
Output: Endpoint successfully deleted
Django Context: # Safe deletion with existence check # User device removed from Author JobPost alerts # Prevents errors from double-deletion
Example 5: Batch delete user endpoints

Delete all endpoints for a specific user.

#!/bin/bash
USER_ID="user123"
# Endpoints stored with user ID in custom data
ENDPOINTS=$(aws sns list-endpoints-by-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android \
    --query "Endpoints[?Attributes.CustomUserData=='$USER_ID'].EndpointArn" \
    --output text)

for endpoint in $ENDPOINTS; do
    aws sns delete-endpoint --endpoint-arn "$endpoint"
done
echo "All endpoints deleted for user: $USER_ID"
Output: All endpoints deleted for user: user123
Django Context: # User.objects.get(username='user123') devices cleared # All registered devices removed from notifications # Account deletion or privacy request handling

get-endpoint-attributes

Retrieves the attributes of a device endpoint.

Syntax
aws sns get-endpoint-attributes \
    --endpoint-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --endpoint-arn (string): ARN of the endpoint
Return Values
  • Attributes: Map of endpoint attributes including Enabled, Token, CustomUserData
Example 1: Get device endpoint status

Check if endpoint is enabled for notifications.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns get-endpoint-attributes \
    --endpoint-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android/abc123
Output: { "Attributes": { "Enabled": "true", "Token": "fcm_device_token_abc123xyz789...", "CustomUserData": "{\"user_id\": 123, \"skills\": [\"Python\", \"Django\"]}" } }
Django Context: # User.objects.get(id=123) device is active # Skills.objects.filter(name__in=['Python', 'Django']) # JobPost notifications will reach this device
Example 2: Check endpoint enabled status

Verify if endpoint can receive notifications.

#!/bin/bash
ENABLED=$(aws sns get-endpoint-attributes \
    --endpoint-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/APNS/anihpj-ios/xyz789 \
    --query 'Attributes.Enabled' --output text)

if [ "$ENABLED" == "true" ]; then
    echo "Endpoint is active and can receive notifications"
else
    echo "Endpoint is disabled - user won't receive JobPost alerts"
fi
Output: Endpoint is active and can receive notifications
Django Context: # User notification status verified before sending # JobPost.objects.filter(author__notifications=True) # Avoid sending to disabled endpoints
Example 3: Get custom user data

Retrieve user preferences stored with endpoint.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns get-endpoint-attributes \
    --endpoint-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android/user456 \
    --query 'Attributes.CustomUserData' --output text | jq .
Output: { "user_id": 456, "preferred_location": "New York", "skills": ["Python", "AWS", "Docker"], "notification_prefs": { "job_alerts": true, "salary_updates": true } }
Django Context: # User.objects.get(id=456) preferences # Location.objects.filter(city='New York') # Skills.objects.filter(name__in=['Python', 'AWS', 'Docker'])
Example 4: Validate device token

Check if the device token is still valid.

#!/bin/bash
TOKEN=$(aws sns get-endpoint-attributes \
    --endpoint-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/APNS/anihpj-ios/device789 \
    --query 'Attributes.Token' --output text)

if [ -n "$TOKEN" ] && [ "$TOKEN" != "None" ]; then
    echo "Valid device token: ${TOKEN:0:20}..."
else
    echo "Invalid or missing device token"
fi
Output: Valid device token: apns_device_token_ab...
Django Context: # Device token validation for User push notifications # JobPost alert delivery confirmation # Author update notifications can be sent
Example 5: Audit endpoint configuration

Get full endpoint details for troubleshooting.

#!/bin/bash
ENDPOINT="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android/abc123"
echo "=== Endpoint Audit Report ==="
ATTRS=$(aws sns get-endpoint-attributes --endpoint-arn $ENDPOINT)
echo "Enabled: $(echo $ATTRS | jq -r '.Attributes.Enabled')"
echo "Token Length: $(echo $ATTRS | jq -r '.Attributes.Token' | wc -c)"
echo "Custom Data: $(echo $ATTRS | jq -r '.Attributes.CustomUserData')"
Output: === Endpoint Audit Report === Enabled: true Token Length: 163 Custom Data: {"user_id": 123, "skills": ["Python"]}
Django Context: # Comprehensive endpoint audit for debugging # User.objects.get(id=123) device configuration # Notification troubleshooting for JobPost alerts

set-endpoint-attributes

Sets the attributes for an endpoint for a device on a platform application.

Syntax
aws sns set-endpoint-attributes \
    --endpoint-arn <value> \
    --attributes <value>
Parameters
  • --endpoint-arn (string): ARN of the endpoint
  • --attributes (map): Map of attributes to set (Enabled, Token, CustomUserData)
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Enable disabled endpoint

Re-enable an endpoint for notifications.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-endpoint-attributes \
    --endpoint-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android/abc123 \
    --attributes Enabled=true
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # User.objects.filter(id=123).update(notifications_enabled=True) # JobPost notifications re-enabled for this device # Skills match alerts will resume
Example 2: Update device token

Update the push notification token when it changes.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-endpoint-attributes \
    --endpoint-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/APNS/anihpj-ios/xyz789 \
    --attributes Token=new_apns_device_token_abc123def456...
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # User.objects.filter(id=789).update(device_token='new_token') # Token refresh after iOS app update # Continuous JobPost alert delivery
Example 3: Update custom user data

Store user preferences with the endpoint.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-endpoint-attributes \
    --endpoint-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android/user456 \
    --attributes 'CustomUserData={"user_id": 456, "skills": ["Python", "Django", "AWS"], "location": "San Francisco"}'
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # User profile sync with push endpoint # Skills.objects.filter(name__in=['Python', 'Django', 'AWS']) # Location.objects.get(city='San Francisco') preference stored
Example 4: Disable endpoint temporarily

Disable notifications during maintenance.

#!/bin/bash
ENDPOINT="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android/abc123"
# Disable
aws sns set-endpoint-attributes --endpoint-arn $ENDPOINT --attributes Enabled=false
echo "Endpoint disabled for maintenance"

# Do maintenance work...

# Re-enable
aws sns set-endpoint-attributes --endpoint-arn $ENDPOINT --attributes Enabled=true
echo "Endpoint re-enabled"
Output: Endpoint disabled for maintenance Endpoint re-enabled
Django Context: # Temporary notification pause for User # JobPost alerts queued during maintenance # Full notification restoration after work complete
Example 5: Bulk update user preferences

Update multiple attributes at once.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-endpoint-attributes \
    --endpoint-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/APNS/anihpj-ios/device123 \
    --attributes '{
        "Enabled": "true",
        "CustomUserData": "{\"user_id\": 123, \"job_alerts\": true, \"salary_min\": 100000}"
    }'
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # User.objects.filter(id=123).update(job_alerts=True) # JobPost.objects.filter(salary__gte=100000) matching # Personalized notification preferences stored

get-platform-application-attributes

Retrieves the attributes of the platform application object.

Syntax
aws sns get-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --platform-application-arn (string): ARN of platform application
Return Values
  • Attributes: Map including Enabled, EventEndpointCreated, EventEndpointDeleted, EventEndpointUpdated, EventDeliveryFailure
Example 1: Get Android app attributes

Retrieve configuration for GCM/FCM platform app.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns get-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android
Output: { "Attributes": { "Enabled": "true", "AppleCertificateExpirationDate": "", "EventEndpointCreated": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:topic/endpoint-events", "EventEndpointDeleted": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:topic/endpoint-events", "EventDeliveryFailure": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:topic/delivery-failures" } }
Django Context: # Android app configuration for JobPost notifications # Event topics for monitoring User device registrations # Delivery failure tracking for notification reliability
Example 2: Get iOS app certificate expiration

Check when APNS certificate expires.

#!/bin/bash
EXPIRY=$(aws sns get-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios \
    --query 'Attributes.AppleCertificateExpirationDate' --output text)

echo "APNS Certificate expires: $EXPIRY"
if [ -n "$EXPIRY" ]; then
    DAYS_LEFT=$(( ($(date -d "$EXPIRY" +%s) - $(date +%s)) / 86400 ))
    echo "Days until expiration: $DAYS_LEFT"
fi
Output: APNS Certificate expires: 2025-06-15T00:00:00Z Days until expiration: 180
Django Context: # iOS push certificate monitoring # User notifications depend on valid certificate # 180 days to renew before JobPost alerts stop
Example 3: Check event notification topics

Get topics for endpoint lifecycle events.

#!/bin/bash
ATTRS=$(aws sns get-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android)

echo "Event Topics:"
echo "  Created: $(echo $ATTRS | jq -r '.Attributes.EventEndpointCreated')"
echo "  Deleted: $(echo $ATTRS | jq -r '.Attributes.EventEndpointDeleted')"
echo "  Updated: $(echo $ATTRS | jq -r '.Attributes.EventEndpointUpdated')"
echo "  Failures: $(echo $ATTRS | jq -r '.Attributes.EventDeliveryFailure')"
Output: Event Topics: Created: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:topic/endpoint-created Deleted: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:topic/endpoint-deleted Updated: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:topic/endpoint-updated Failures: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:topic/delivery-failures
Django Context: # Event monitoring for User device lifecycle # Track new User registrations via endpoint-created # Monitor JobPost notification failures
Example 4: Verify platform is enabled

Check if platform app is active.

#!/bin/bash
PLATFORMS=("app/GCM/anihpj-android" "app/APNS/anihpj-ios")

for platform in "${PLATFORMS[@]}"; do
    ARN="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:$platform"
    ENABLED=$(aws sns get-platform-application-attributes \
        --platform-application-arn $ARN \
        --query 'Attributes.Enabled' --output text)
    echo "$platform: $ENABLED"
done
Output: app/GCM/anihpj-android: true app/APNS/anihpj-ios: true
Django Context: # Both mobile platforms active for notifications # All User devices can receive JobPost alerts # Full notification coverage across platforms
Example 5: Audit all platform configurations

Generate report of all platform apps.

#!/bin/bash
echo "=== Platform Application Audit ==="
aws sns list-platform-applications --query 'PlatformApplications[].PlatformApplicationArn' --output text | while read arn; do
    echo "Platform: $arn"
    aws sns get-platform-application-attributes --platform-application-arn $arn \
        --query 'Attributes.{Enabled:Enabled,FailureTopic:EventDeliveryFailure}' --output table
    echo "---"
done
Output: === Platform Application Audit === Platform: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android ---------------------------------- | Enabled | FailureTopic | | true | arn:aws:sns:...:topic/delivery-failures | ---
Django Context: # Comprehensive platform audit for notification system # All JobPost notification channels reviewed # Failure monitoring configured for all platforms

set-platform-application-attributes

Sets the attributes of the platform application object.

Syntax
aws sns set-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn <value> \
    --attributes <value>
Parameters
  • --platform-application-arn (string): ARN of platform application
  • --attributes (map): Attributes to set (Enabled, EventEndpointCreated, EventEndpointDeleted, EventEndpointUpdated, EventDeliveryFailure, SuccessFeedbackRoleArn, FailureFeedbackRoleArn, SuccessFeedbackSampleRate)
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Configure event notification topics

Set up topics for endpoint lifecycle events.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android \
    --attributes '{
        "EventEndpointCreated": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:topic/endpoint-created",
        "EventEndpointDeleted": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:topic/endpoint-deleted",
        "EventDeliveryFailure": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:topic/delivery-failures"
    }'
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Event monitoring configured for User device lifecycle # Track new registrations and unregistrations # Monitor JobPost notification delivery failures
Example 2: Update FCM credentials

Update Firebase Cloud Messaging API key.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android \
    --attributes PlatformCredential=new_fcm_server_key_abc123...
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # FCM credentials rotated for security # All Android User notifications use new key # JobPost alerts continue without interruption
Example 3: Configure delivery feedback

Set up CloudWatch logging for deliveries.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios \
    --attributes '{
        "SuccessFeedbackRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/SNSFeedbackRole",
        "FailureFeedbackRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/SNSFeedbackRole",
        "SuccessFeedbackSampleRate": "100"
    }'
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Full delivery logging enabled for iOS notifications # 100% of successful deliveries logged to CloudWatch # Track all JobPost notification deliveries to User devices
Example 4: Disable platform temporarily

Disable platform for maintenance.

#!/bin/bash
PLATFORM_ARN="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android"

# Disable for maintenance
aws sns set-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn $PLATFORM_ARN \
    --attributes Enabled=false
echo "Platform disabled for maintenance"

# Perform maintenance...

# Re-enable
aws sns set-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn $PLATFORM_ARN \
    --attributes Enabled=true
echo "Platform re-enabled"
Output: Platform disabled for maintenance Platform re-enabled
Django Context: # Android notifications paused during maintenance # JobPost alerts queued for later delivery # User.objects notifications resume after maintenance
Example 5: Update APNS certificate

Rotate iOS push certificate before expiration.

#!/bin/bash
# Read new certificate and key from files
CERT=$(cat apns_cert.pem | base64)
KEY=$(cat apns_key.pem | base64)

aws sns set-platform-application-attributes \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios \
    --attributes "PlatformCredential=$KEY,PlatformPrincipal=$CERT"

echo "APNS certificate updated successfully"
Output: APNS certificate updated successfully
Django Context: # iOS push certificate renewed before expiration # Uninterrupted JobPost notifications to iPhone users # User.objects iOS devices continue receiving alerts

list-platform-applications

Lists the platform application objects for the supported push notification services.

Syntax
aws sns list-platform-applications \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters
  • --next-token (string): Token for pagination
Return Values
  • PlatformApplications: List of platform application objects with ARN and attributes
  • NextToken: Token for next page of results
Example 1: List all platform applications

Get all configured push notification platforms.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-platform-applications
Output: { "PlatformApplications": [ { "PlatformApplicationArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android", "Attributes": { "Enabled": "true", "AppleCertificateExpirationDate": "" } }, { "PlatformApplicationArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios", "Attributes": { "Enabled": "true", "AppleCertificateExpirationDate": "2025-06-15T00:00:00Z" } } ] }
Django Context: # All mobile platforms for JobPost notifications # Android (GCM/FCM) and iOS (APNS) configured # User.objects can receive alerts on any device
Example 2: List platform ARNs only

Get just the ARNs for scripting.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-platform-applications \
    --query 'PlatformApplications[].PlatformApplicationArn' \
    --output text
Output: arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS_SANDBOX/anihpj-ios-dev
Django Context: # Three platforms: Android prod, iOS prod, iOS sandbox # Development and production notification channels # JobPost alerts configured for all environments
Example 3: Count platforms by type

Group platforms by service type.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Platform Application Summary:"
aws sns list-platform-applications \
    --query 'PlatformApplications[].PlatformApplicationArn' \
    --output text | while read arn; do
    if [[ $arn == *"/GCM/"* ]]; then
        echo "  Android (FCM): $(echo $arn | cut -d'/' -f4)"
    elif [[ $arn == *"/APNS/"* ]]; then
        echo "  iOS (APNS): $(echo $arn | cut -d'/' -f4)"
    elif [[ $arn == *"/APNS_SANDBOX/"* ]]; then
        echo "  iOS Sandbox: $(echo $arn | cut -d'/' -f4)"
    fi
done
Output: Platform Application Summary: Android (FCM): anihpj-android iOS (APNS): anihpj-ios iOS Sandbox: anihpj-ios-dev
Django Context: # Platform inventory for notification infrastructure # User devices mapped to appropriate platforms # JobPost.objects notifications route to correct service
Example 4: Check enabled platforms

List only active platforms.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-platform-applications \
    --query 'PlatformApplications[?Attributes.Enabled==`true`].PlatformApplicationArn' \
    --output table
Output: --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListPlatformApplications | +--------------------------------------------------------------------+ | arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android | | arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios | +--------------------------------------------------------------------+
Django Context: # Active notification platforms for User devices # Production Android and iOS enabled # JobPost alerts can reach all active platforms
Example 5: Export platform inventory

Generate platform application report.

#!/bin/bash
echo "=== SNS Platform Applications Report ==="
echo "Generated: $(date)"
echo ""

aws sns list-platform-applications | jq -r '
    .PlatformApplications[] | 
    "Platform: \(.PlatformApplicationArn | split("/") | .[3])
Type: \(.PlatformApplicationArn | split("/") | .[2])
Enabled: \(.Attributes.Enabled)
---"'
Output: === SNS Platform Applications Report === Generated: Wed Jan 15 10:30:00 UTC 2025 Platform: anihpj-android Type: GCM Enabled: true --- Platform: anihpj-ios Type: APNS Enabled: true ---
Django Context: # Notification infrastructure documentation # All JobPost delivery channels documented # User notification capabilities mapped

list-endpoints-by-platform-application

Lists the endpoints and their attributes for devices in a supported push notification service.

Syntax
aws sns list-endpoints-by-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn <value> \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters
  • --platform-application-arn (string): ARN of platform application
  • --next-token (string): Token for pagination
Return Values
  • Endpoints: List of endpoint objects with ARN and attributes
  • NextToken: Token for next page of results
Example 1: List all Android endpoints

Get all registered Android devices.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-endpoints-by-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android
Output: { "Endpoints": [ { "EndpointArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android/abc123", "Attributes": { "Enabled": "true", "Token": "fcm_token_abc123...", "CustomUserData": "{\"user_id\": 123}" } }, { "EndpointArn": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:endpoint/GCM/anihpj-android/def456", "Attributes": { "Enabled": "true", "Token": "fcm_token_def456...", "CustomUserData": "{\"user_id\": 456}" } } ] }
Django Context: # All User Android devices registered for notifications # User.objects.filter(id__in=[123, 456]) have active devices # JobPost alerts can reach these endpoints
Example 2: Count registered devices

Get total number of registered endpoints.

#!/bin/bash
TOTAL=$(aws sns list-endpoints-by-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android \
    --query 'length(Endpoints)' --output text)

echo "Total Android devices registered: $TOTAL"
Output: Total Android devices registered: 1523
Django Context: # 1523 User devices can receive JobPost notifications # Matches User.objects.filter(has_android_device=True).count() # Notification reach for Android platform
Example 3: List enabled endpoints only

Get only active devices.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-endpoints-by-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/APNS/anihpj-ios \
    --query 'Endpoints[?Attributes.Enabled==`true`].EndpointArn' \
    --output text | wc -l
Output: 892
Django Context: # 892 active iOS devices for notifications # User.objects.filter(ios_notifications=True) correlation # Effective JobPost notification reach
Example 4: Find disabled endpoints

Identify endpoints that need cleanup.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Disabled endpoints to clean up:"
aws sns list-endpoints-by-platform-application \
    --platform-application-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android \
    --query 'Endpoints[?Attributes.Enabled==`false`].[EndpointArn,Attributes.CustomUserData]' \
    --output table
Output: Disabled endpoints to clean up: ---------------------------------- | EndpointArn | CustomUserData | | arn:...endpoint/abc123 | {"user_id": 789} | | arn:...endpoint/xyz999 | {"user_id": 321} | ----------------------------------
Django Context: # Disabled devices for User IDs 789 and 321 # User.objects.filter(id__in=[789, 321]) need device re-registration # Cleanup candidates to reduce notification costs
Example 5: Paginate through all endpoints

Handle large numbers of registered devices.

#!/bin/bash
PLATFORM_ARN="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:app/GCM/anihpj-android"
NEXT_TOKEN=""
TOTAL=0

while true; do
    if [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        RESULT=$(aws sns list-endpoints-by-platform-application \
            --platform-application-arn $PLATFORM_ARN)
    else
        RESULT=$(aws sns list-endpoints-by-platform-application \
            --platform-application-arn $PLATFORM_ARN \
            --next-token "$NEXT_TOKEN")
    fi
    
    COUNT=$(echo $RESULT | jq '.Endpoints | length')
    TOTAL=$((TOTAL + COUNT))
    
    NEXT_TOKEN=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.NextToken // empty')
    [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ] && break
done

echo "Total endpoints across all pages: $TOTAL"
Output: Total endpoints across all pages: 15234
Django Context: # 15234 total User devices registered # Pagination required for large User bases # Complete JobPost notification audience count

get-sms-attributes

Returns the settings for sending SMS messages from your account.

Syntax
aws sns get-sms-attributes \
    [--attributes <value>]
Parameters
  • --attributes (list): List of attribute names to retrieve (DefaultSenderID, MonthlySpendLimit, DeliveryStatusIAMRole, DeliveryStatusSuccessSamplingRate, DefaultSMSType, UsageReportS3Bucket)
Return Values
  • attributes: Map of SMS attribute names and values
Example 1: Get all SMS attributes

Retrieve complete SMS configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns get-sms-attributes
Output: { "attributes": { "DefaultSenderID": "ANIHPJ", "DefaultSMSType": "Transactional", "MonthlySpendLimit": "100", "DeliveryStatusIAMRole": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/SNSSMSRole", "DeliveryStatusSuccessSamplingRate": "100", "UsageReportS3Bucket": "anihpj-sms-reports" } }
Django Context: # SMS settings for User phone notifications # Transactional messages for JobPost alerts # $100/month spending limit for SMS
Example 2: Check monthly spend limit

Get current SMS spending limit.

#!/bin/bash
LIMIT=$(aws sns get-sms-attributes \
    --attributes MonthlySpendLimit \
    --query 'attributes.MonthlySpendLimit' --output text)

echo "Monthly SMS spend limit: \$$LIMIT"
Output: Monthly SMS spend limit: $100
Django Context: # SMS budget for User notifications # JobPost SMS alerts limited by monthly cap # Monitor spending to avoid notification interruption
Example 3: Get default SMS type

Check if using Promotional or Transactional.

#!/bin/bash
SMS_TYPE=$(aws sns get-sms-attributes \
    --attributes DefaultSMSType \
    --query 'attributes.DefaultSMSType' --output text)

echo "Default SMS Type: $SMS_TYPE"
if [ "$SMS_TYPE" == "Transactional" ]; then
    echo "Using higher delivery rate for time-sensitive messages"
else
    echo "Using cost-effective rate for marketing messages"
fi
Output: Default SMS Type: Transactional Using higher delivery rate for time-sensitive messages
Django Context: # Transactional for urgent JobPost notifications # Higher priority delivery for User alerts # Author notifications delivered promptly
Example 4: Check delivery logging

Verify SMS delivery logging configuration.

#!/bin/bash
echo "=== SMS Delivery Logging Configuration ==="
ATTRS=$(aws sns get-sms-attributes \
    --attributes DeliveryStatusIAMRole DeliveryStatusSuccessSamplingRate)

ROLE=$(echo $ATTRS | jq -r '.attributes.DeliveryStatusIAMRole // "Not configured"')
RATE=$(echo $ATTRS | jq -r '.attributes.DeliveryStatusSuccessSamplingRate // "0"')

echo "IAM Role: $ROLE"
echo "Success Sampling Rate: $RATE%"
Output: === SMS Delivery Logging Configuration === IAM Role: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/SNSSMSRole Success Sampling Rate: 100%
Django Context: # Full SMS delivery tracking enabled # 100% of User SMS notifications logged # JobPost alert delivery confirmation available
Example 5: Get sender ID

Check the default sender ID for SMS.

#!/bin/bash
SENDER=$(aws sns get-sms-attributes \
    --attributes DefaultSenderID \
    --query 'attributes.DefaultSenderID' --output text)

if [ "$SENDER" != "None" ] && [ -n "$SENDER" ]; then
    echo "SMS messages sent from: $SENDER"
else
    echo "No custom sender ID configured - using default"
fi
Output: SMS messages sent from: ANIHPJ
Django Context: # ANIHPJ branding on all SMS notifications # User sees "ANIHPJ" as message sender # Professional JobPost alert branding

set-sms-attributes

Sets the default settings for sending SMS messages and receiving daily SMS usage reports.

Syntax
aws sns set-sms-attributes \
    --attributes <value>
Parameters
  • --attributes (map): Map of attributes to set (DefaultSenderID, MonthlySpendLimit, DeliveryStatusIAMRole, DeliveryStatusSuccessSamplingRate, DefaultSMSType, UsageReportS3Bucket)
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Set default sender ID

Configure custom sender ID for SMS branding.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-sms-attributes \
    --attributes DefaultSenderID=ANIHPJ
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # ANIHPJ branding for all User SMS notifications # JobPost alerts show professional sender name # Better message recognition and trust
Example 2: Set monthly spending limit

Configure SMS budget limit.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-sms-attributes \
    --attributes MonthlySpendLimit=500

echo "SMS monthly spend limit set to \$500"
Output: SMS monthly spend limit set to $500
Django Context: # $500/month SMS budget for User notifications # Increased capacity for JobPost alerts # Scale for growing User.objects.count()
Example 3: Configure delivery logging

Enable SMS delivery status logging.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-sms-attributes \
    --attributes '{
        "DeliveryStatusIAMRole": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/SNSSMSRole",
        "DeliveryStatusSuccessSamplingRate": "100"
    }'

echo "SMS delivery logging enabled at 100% sampling"
Output: SMS delivery logging enabled at 100% sampling
Django Context: # Full delivery tracking for User SMS notifications # 100% of JobPost alerts logged # Audit trail for all SMS communications
Example 4: Set transactional SMS type

Configure high-priority delivery for alerts.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-sms-attributes \
    --attributes DefaultSMSType=Transactional

echo "SMS type set to Transactional for high-priority delivery"
Output: SMS type set to Transactional for high-priority delivery
Django Context: # Transactional priority for urgent notifications # JobPost application deadline alerts # Author.objects urgent communications
Example 5: Configure complete SMS settings

Set up all SMS attributes at once.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns set-sms-attributes \
    --attributes '{
        "DefaultSenderID": "ANIHPJ",
        "DefaultSMSType": "Transactional",
        "MonthlySpendLimit": "200",
        "DeliveryStatusIAMRole": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/SNSSMSRole",
        "DeliveryStatusSuccessSamplingRate": "100",
        "UsageReportS3Bucket": "anihpj-sms-reports"
    }'

echo "Complete SMS configuration applied"
Output: Complete SMS configuration applied
Django Context: # Full SMS configuration for User notifications # ANIHPJ branding, $200 budget, full logging # Usage reports stored for JobPost SMS analytics

check-if-phone-number-is-opted-out

Accepts a phone number and indicates whether the phone holder has opted out of receiving SMS messages from your account.

Syntax
aws sns check-if-phone-number-is-opted-out \
    --phone-number <value>
Parameters
  • --phone-number (string): Phone number in E.164 format (e.g., +14155551234)
Return Values
  • isOptedOut: Boolean indicating if number has opted out
Example 1: Check single phone number

Verify if a user has opted out of SMS.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns check-if-phone-number-is-opted-out \
    --phone-number "+14155551234"
Output: { "isOptedOut": false }
Django Context: # User phone +14155551234 can receive SMS # JobPost notifications allowed for this User # SMS alerts will be delivered
Example 2: Check opted out number

Identify number that has unsubscribed.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws sns check-if-phone-number-is-opted-out \
    --phone-number "+14155559999" \
    --query 'isOptedOut' --output text)

if [ "$RESULT" == "True" ]; then
    echo "Number has opted out - cannot send SMS"
else
    echo "Number can receive SMS notifications"
fi
Output: Number has opted out - cannot send SMS
Django Context: # User.objects.get(phone='+14155559999').sms_opted_out = True # No JobPost SMS alerts will be sent # Respect user's communication preferences
Example 3: Validate before sending

Check opt-out status before sending notification.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"
OPTED_OUT=$(aws sns check-if-phone-number-is-opted-out \
    --phone-number "$PHONE" \
    --query 'isOptedOut' --output text)

if [ "$OPTED_OUT" == "False" ]; then
    aws sns publish \
        --phone-number "$PHONE" \
        --message "New Python job posted: Senior Django Developer at TechCorp"
    echo "SMS sent successfully"
else
    echo "Skipping - user opted out of SMS"
fi
Output: SMS sent successfully
Django Context: # Pre-send validation for User SMS # JobPost.objects.filter(skills__name='Python') notification # Compliant SMS delivery workflow
Example 4: Batch check multiple numbers

Check opt-out status for multiple users.

#!/bin/bash
PHONES=("+14155551234" "+14155555678" "+14155559012")

echo "Opt-out Status Check:"
for phone in "${PHONES[@]}"; do
    STATUS=$(aws sns check-if-phone-number-is-opted-out \
        --phone-number "$phone" \
        --query 'isOptedOut' --output text)
    echo "  $phone: $([ "$STATUS" == "True" ] && echo "Opted Out" || echo "Active")"
done
Output: Opt-out Status Check: +14155551234: Active +14155555678: Opted Out +14155559012: Active
Django Context: # Bulk User SMS status verification # 2 users active, 1 opted out # Filter JobPost SMS recipients accordingly
Example 5: Sync with Django user preferences

Update database based on opt-out status.

#!/bin/bash
# Export script for syncing opt-out status
while read phone user_id; do
    OPTED_OUT=$(aws sns check-if-phone-number-is-opted-out \
        --phone-number "$phone" \
        --query 'isOptedOut' --output text 2>/dev/null)
    
    echo "UPDATE users SET sms_opted_out=$OPTED_OUT WHERE id=$user_id;"
done << EOF
+14155551234 123
+14155555678 456
+14155559012 789
EOF
Output: UPDATE users SET sms_opted_out=False WHERE id=123; UPDATE users SET sms_opted_out=True WHERE id=456; UPDATE users SET sms_opted_out=False WHERE id=789;
Django Context: # User.objects.filter(id=456).update(sms_opted_out=True) # Sync AWS opt-out status with Django database # Maintain consistent User notification preferences

list-phone-numbers-opted-out

Returns a list of phone numbers that are opted out, meaning they can't receive SMS messages from your account.

Syntax
aws sns list-phone-numbers-opted-out \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters
  • --next-token (string): Token for pagination
Return Values
  • phoneNumbers: List of phone numbers that have opted out
  • nextToken: Token for next page of results
Example 1: List all opted-out numbers

Get complete list of numbers that unsubscribed.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-phone-numbers-opted-out
Output: { "phoneNumbers": [ "+14155551111", "+14155552222", "+14155553333" ] }
Django Context: # 3 Users have opted out of JobPost SMS alerts # User.objects.filter(phone__in=[...]).update(sms_opted_out=True) # Exclude from SMS notification campaigns
Example 2: Count opted-out numbers

Get total count of unsubscribed numbers.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws sns list-phone-numbers-opted-out \
    --query 'length(phoneNumbers)' --output text)

echo "Total opted-out phone numbers: $COUNT"
Output: Total opted-out phone numbers: 47
Django Context: # 47 Users have unsubscribed from SMS # User.objects.filter(sms_opted_out=True).count() == 47 # Measure SMS notification opt-out rate
Example 3: Export opted-out list

Export numbers for database synchronization.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-phone-numbers-opted-out \
    --query 'phoneNumbers[]' --output text | tr '\t' '\n' > opted_out_phones.txt

echo "Exported $(wc -l < opted_out_phones.txt) opted-out numbers"
Output: Exported 47 opted-out numbers
Django Context: # Export for User.objects bulk update # Sync opt-out list with Django database # Maintain compliance with SMS preferences
Example 4: Sync with Django database

Update user records based on opt-out list.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Syncing opted-out numbers with Django..."

aws sns list-phone-numbers-opted-out \
    --query 'phoneNumbers[]' --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read phone; do
    echo "UPDATE users SET sms_opted_out=true WHERE phone='$phone';"
done
Output: Syncing opted-out numbers with Django... UPDATE users SET sms_opted_out=true WHERE phone='+14155551111'; UPDATE users SET sms_opted_out=true WHERE phone='+14155552222'; UPDATE users SET sms_opted_out=true WHERE phone='+14155553333';
Django Context: # Bulk sync User.objects.filter(phone__in=[...]) # Update sms_opted_out field for matching users # Keep Django and AWS in sync
Example 5: Paginate through large lists

Handle accounts with many opt-outs.

#!/bin/bash
NEXT_TOKEN=""
ALL_PHONES=()

while true; do
    if [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        RESULT=$(aws sns list-phone-numbers-opted-out)
    else
        RESULT=$(aws sns list-phone-numbers-opted-out --next-token "$NEXT_TOKEN")
    fi
    
    PHONES=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.phoneNumbers[]? // empty')
    ALL_PHONES+=($PHONES)
    
    NEXT_TOKEN=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.nextToken // empty')
    [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ] && break
done

echo "Total opted-out numbers: ${#ALL_PHONES[@]}"
Output: Total opted-out numbers: 523
Django Context: # 523 Users opted out across all pages # Large-scale User notification management # Complete opt-out inventory for JobPost SMS

opt-in-phone-number

Opts in a phone number that is currently opted out, allowing you to resume sending SMS messages to that number.

Syntax
aws sns opt-in-phone-number \
    --phone-number <value>
Parameters
  • --phone-number (string): Phone number in E.164 format to opt back in
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Opt in single number

Re-enable SMS for a phone number.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns opt-in-phone-number \
    --phone-number "+14155551234"

echo "Phone number opted back in for SMS"
Output: Phone number opted back in for SMS
Django Context: # User.objects.filter(phone='+14155551234').update(sms_opted_out=False) # JobPost SMS alerts re-enabled for user # User can receive notifications again
Example 2: Opt in with verification

Verify opt-out status before opting in.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"

# Check current status
OPTED_OUT=$(aws sns check-if-phone-number-is-opted-out \
    --phone-number "$PHONE" \
    --query 'isOptedOut' --output text)

if [ "$OPTED_OUT" == "True" ]; then
    aws sns opt-in-phone-number --phone-number "$PHONE"
    echo "Number successfully opted back in"
else
    echo "Number was not opted out"
fi
Output: Number successfully opted back in
Django Context: # Conditional opt-in with status verification # User requested SMS re-subscription # JobPost notifications restored
Example 3: Batch opt-in numbers

Opt in multiple phone numbers.

#!/bin/bash
PHONES=("+14155551234" "+14155555678" "+14155559012")

echo "Opting in phone numbers..."
for phone in "${PHONES[@]}"; do
    aws sns opt-in-phone-number --phone-number "$phone" && \
        echo "  Opted in: $phone"
done
Output: Opting in phone numbers... Opted in: +14155551234 Opted in: +14155555678 Opted in: +14155559012
Django Context: # Bulk User.objects SMS re-subscription # Multiple users re-enabled for JobPost alerts # Campaign re-enrollment processing
Example 4: Opt in from database

Re-subscribe users based on Django records.

#!/bin/bash
# Users who requested to re-subscribe
PHONES=$(cat << EOF
+14155551234
+14155555678
EOF
)

echo "$PHONES" | while read phone; do
    if [ -n "$phone" ]; then
        aws sns opt-in-phone-number --phone-number "$phone"
        echo "Opted in: $phone"
    fi
done
Output: Opted in: +14155551234 Opted in: +14155555678
Django Context: # Process User.objects.filter(wants_sms_resubscribe=True) # Re-enable JobPost notifications for requesting users # Sync preferences with AWS SNS
Example 5: Opt in with confirmation SMS

Opt in and send welcome message.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"

# Opt in the number
aws sns opt-in-phone-number --phone-number "$PHONE"

# Send confirmation message
aws sns publish \
    --phone-number "$PHONE" \
    --message "Welcome back to ANIHPJ! You'll now receive JobPost alerts for your preferred skills." \
    --message-attributes '{"AWS.SNS.SMS.SenderID":{"DataType":"String","StringValue":"ANIHPJ"}}'

echo "User opted in and confirmation sent"
Output: User opted in and confirmation sent
Django Context: # User re-subscribed with welcome message # JobPost alerts resume for Skills.objects matches # Professional re-engagement workflow

list-origination-numbers

Lists the calling AWS account's dedicated origination numbers and their metadata.

Syntax
aws sns list-origination-numbers \
    [--next-token <value>] \
    [--max-results <value>]
Parameters
  • --next-token (string): Token for pagination
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of results (1-100)
Return Values
  • PhoneNumbers: List of origination phone numbers with status and capabilities
  • NextToken: Token for next page of results
Example 1: List all origination numbers

Get dedicated numbers for SMS sending.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-origination-numbers
Output: { "PhoneNumbers": [ { "CreatedAt": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z", "PhoneNumber": "+18005551234", "Status": "ACTIVE", "Iso2CountryCode": "US", "RouteType": "Transactional", "NumberCapabilities": ["SMS", "MMS"] }, { "CreatedAt": "2024-02-01T14:00:00Z", "PhoneNumber": "+18005555678", "Status": "ACTIVE", "Iso2CountryCode": "US", "RouteType": "Promotional", "NumberCapabilities": ["SMS"] } ] }
Django Context: # Dedicated numbers for JobPost SMS notifications # +18005551234 for transactional User alerts # +18005555678 for promotional campaigns
Example 2: List active numbers only

Filter for usable origination numbers.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-origination-numbers \
    --query 'PhoneNumbers[?Status==`ACTIVE`].PhoneNumber' \
    --output text
Output: +18005551234 +18005555678
Django Context: # Active SMS origination numbers # Available for JobPost notifications # Use for User.objects SMS campaigns
Example 3: Check number capabilities

See what each number can do.

#!/bin/bash
echo "=== Origination Numbers Capabilities ==="
aws sns list-origination-numbers | jq -r '
    .PhoneNumbers[] | 
    "\(.PhoneNumber): \(.NumberCapabilities | join(", "))"'
Output: === Origination Numbers Capabilities === +18005551234: SMS, MMS +18005555678: SMS
Django Context: # +18005551234 supports MMS for rich notifications # Send JobPost images via MMS capable number # Text-only number for simple User alerts
Example 4: List transactional numbers

Find numbers for time-sensitive messages.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-origination-numbers \
    --query 'PhoneNumbers[?RouteType==`Transactional`].[PhoneNumber,Status]' \
    --output table
Output: ----------------------------------- | ListOriginationNumbers | +------------------+---------------+ | +18005551234 | ACTIVE | +------------------+---------------+
Django Context: # Transactional number for urgent JobPost alerts # High-priority User notification delivery # Time-sensitive application deadline reminders
Example 5: Audit origination numbers

Generate comprehensive number report.

#!/bin/bash
echo "=== SNS Origination Numbers Audit ==="
echo "Generated: $(date)"
echo ""

aws sns list-origination-numbers | jq -r '
    .PhoneNumbers[] | 
    "Number: \(.PhoneNumber)
  Status: \(.Status)
  Country: \(.Iso2CountryCode)
  Route: \(.RouteType)
  Capabilities: \(.NumberCapabilities | join(", "))
  Created: \(.CreatedAt)
---"'
Output: === SNS Origination Numbers Audit === Generated: Wed Jan 15 10:30:00 UTC 2025 Number: +18005551234 Status: ACTIVE Country: US Route: Transactional Capabilities: SMS, MMS Created: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z --- Number: +18005555678 Status: ACTIVE Country: US Route: Promotional Capabilities: SMS Created: 2024-02-01T14:00:00Z ---
Django Context: # Complete origination number inventory # JobPost notification infrastructure documentation # User SMS capability assessment

get-sms-sandbox-account-status

Retrieves the SMS sandbox status for the calling AWS account in the target region.

Syntax
aws sns get-sms-sandbox-account-status
Parameters
  • No parameters required
Return Values
  • IsInSandbox: Boolean indicating if account is in sandbox mode
Example 1: Check sandbox status

Determine if account is in SMS sandbox.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns get-sms-sandbox-account-status
Output: { "IsInSandbox": true }
Django Context: # Account in SMS sandbox mode # Can only send to verified phone numbers # Must request production access for User notifications
Example 2: Production status check

Verify production SMS access.

#!/bin/bash
IN_SANDBOX=$(aws sns get-sms-sandbox-account-status \
    --query 'IsInSandbox' --output text)

if [ "$IN_SANDBOX" == "False" ]; then
    echo "Production access granted - can send SMS to any number"
else
    echo "Sandbox mode - limited to verified numbers only"
fi
Output: Production access granted - can send SMS to any number
Django Context: # Production SMS access for User notifications # All JobPost alerts can be sent to any User # No phone verification required
Example 3: Conditional SMS sending

Adjust behavior based on sandbox status.

#!/bin/bash
IN_SANDBOX=$(aws sns get-sms-sandbox-account-status \
    --query 'IsInSandbox' --output text)

PHONE="+14155551234"
MESSAGE="New Python developer position posted!"

if [ "$IN_SANDBOX" == "True" ]; then
    echo "Sandbox mode: Verifying phone before sending..."
    # In sandbox, must verify number first
    VERIFIED=$(aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
        --query "PhoneNumbers[?PhoneNumber=='$PHONE'].Status" --output text)
    if [ "$VERIFIED" != "Verified" ]; then
        echo "Error: Phone not verified in sandbox"
        exit 1
    fi
fi

aws sns publish --phone-number "$PHONE" --message "$MESSAGE"
Output: Sandbox mode: Verifying phone before sending... { "MessageId": "abc123-def456" }
Django Context: # Sandbox-aware notification sending # JobPost alert with verification check # Production-ready code handling both modes
Example 4: Environment validation script

Check SMS readiness for deployment.

#!/bin/bash
echo "=== SMS Environment Validation ==="

# Check sandbox status
IN_SANDBOX=$(aws sns get-sms-sandbox-account-status \
    --query 'IsInSandbox' --output text)

echo "Sandbox Mode: $IN_SANDBOX"

if [ "$IN_SANDBOX" == "True" ]; then
    VERIFIED_COUNT=$(aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
        --query 'length(PhoneNumbers[?Status==`Verified`])' --output text)
    echo "Verified Numbers: $VERIFIED_COUNT"
    echo "WARNING: Production deployment requires sandbox exit"
else
    echo "STATUS: Ready for production SMS"
fi
Output: === SMS Environment Validation === Sandbox Mode: True Verified Numbers: 5 WARNING: Production deployment requires sandbox exit
Django Context: # Pre-deployment validation for User SMS # 5 verified test numbers available # Request production access before launch
Example 5: CI/CD pipeline check

Validate SMS access in deployment pipeline.

#!/bin/bash
# CI/CD check for SMS capabilities
ENVIRONMENT=${DEPLOY_ENV:-"development"}

IN_SANDBOX=$(aws sns get-sms-sandbox-account-status \
    --query 'IsInSandbox' --output text)

if [ "$ENVIRONMENT" == "production" ] && [ "$IN_SANDBOX" == "True" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Cannot deploy to production with sandbox SMS account"
    exit 1
elif [ "$ENVIRONMENT" == "development" ]; then
    echo "Development environment - sandbox mode acceptable"
fi

echo "SMS validation passed for $ENVIRONMENT"
Output: Development environment - sandbox mode acceptable SMS validation passed for development
Django Context: # CI/CD SMS access validation # Block production deploy without SMS access # JobPost notification readiness check

create-sms-sandbox-phone-number

Adds a destination phone number to an AWS account in the SMS sandbox and sends a verification code.

Syntax
aws sns create-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
    --phone-number <value> \
    [--language-code <value>]
Parameters
  • --phone-number (string): Phone number in E.164 format to verify
  • --language-code (string): Language for verification message (en-US, de-DE, es-ES, etc.)
Return Values
  • No output on success - verification code sent to phone
Example 1: Add phone number for verification

Start verification process for a test number.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
    --phone-number "+14155551234"

echo "Verification code sent to +14155551234"
Output: Verification code sent to +14155551234
Django Context: # Test User phone verification started # 6-digit code sent via SMS # Use verify-sms-sandbox-phone-number to complete
Example 2: Add with language preference

Send verification in specific language.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns create-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
    --phone-number "+34612345678" \
    --language-code "es-ES"

echo "Código de verificación enviado"
Output: Código de verificación enviado
Django Context: # Spanish User test number verification # Localized verification message sent # International User testing support
Example 3: Add team test numbers

Register multiple team phones for testing.

#!/bin/bash
TEAM_PHONES=(
    "+14155551111"
    "+14155552222"
    "+14155553333"
)

echo "Adding team test numbers to sandbox..."
for phone in "${TEAM_PHONES[@]}"; do
    aws sns create-sms-sandbox-phone-number --phone-number "$phone"
    echo "Verification sent to: $phone"
done
Output: Adding team test numbers to sandbox... Verification sent to: +14155551111 Verification sent to: +14155552222 Verification sent to: +14155553333
Django Context: # Development team phone verification # Multiple testers for JobPost notifications # QA team can receive test SMS alerts
Example 4: Add with error handling

Handle already registered numbers.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"

if aws sns create-sms-sandbox-phone-number --phone-number "$PHONE" 2>&1; then
    echo "Verification code sent"
else
    echo "Number may already be registered - check status"
    aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
        --query "PhoneNumbers[?PhoneNumber=='$PHONE']"
fi
Output: Verification code sent
Django Context: # Safe number registration with duplicate check # Test User phone added to sandbox # Ready for JobPost notification testing
Example 5: Complete verification workflow

Add number and provide verification instructions.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"

echo "Step 1: Sending verification code..."
aws sns create-sms-sandbox-phone-number --phone-number "$PHONE"

echo ""
echo "Step 2: Check your phone for the 6-digit verification code"
echo "Step 3: Run the following command with your code:"
echo ""
echo "aws sns verify-sms-sandbox-phone-number \\"
echo "    --phone-number \"$PHONE\" \\"
echo "    --one-time-password \"YOUR_6_DIGIT_CODE\""
Output: Step 1: Sending verification code... Step 2: Check your phone for the 6-digit verification code Step 3: Run the following command with your code: aws sns verify-sms-sandbox-phone-number \ --phone-number "+14155551234" \ --one-time-password "YOUR_6_DIGIT_CODE"
Django Context: # Complete verification workflow documented # Test User can follow steps to verify # Ready for JobPost SMS testing after verification

verify-sms-sandbox-phone-number

Verifies a destination phone number with a one-time password (OTP) for the calling AWS account.

Syntax
aws sns verify-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
    --phone-number <value> \
    --one-time-password <value>
Parameters
  • --phone-number (string): Phone number in E.164 format to verify
  • --one-time-password (string): 6-digit verification code received via SMS
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Verify phone number

Complete verification with OTP code.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns verify-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
    --phone-number "+14155551234" \
    --one-time-password "123456"

echo "Phone number verified successfully!"
Output: Phone number verified successfully!
Django Context: # Test User phone now verified # Can receive JobPost SMS notifications # Sandbox testing enabled for this number
Example 2: Verify with status check

Verify and confirm the status.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"
OTP="654321"

aws sns verify-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
    --phone-number "$PHONE" \
    --one-time-password "$OTP"

# Confirm verification
STATUS=$(aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
    --query "PhoneNumbers[?PhoneNumber=='$PHONE'].Status" --output text)

echo "Verification status: $STATUS"
Output: Verification status: Verified
Django Context: # User phone verification confirmed # Status now "Verified" in sandbox # JobPost alerts will reach this number
Example 3: Interactive verification

Prompt user for OTP code.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"

echo "Enter the 6-digit verification code sent to $PHONE:"
read -r OTP

if [ ${#OTP} -eq 6 ]; then
    aws sns verify-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
        --phone-number "$PHONE" \
        --one-time-password "$OTP"
    echo "Verification successful!"
else
    echo "Error: Code must be 6 digits"
fi
Output: Enter the 6-digit verification code sent to +14155551234: 123456 Verification successful!
Django Context: # Interactive test User verification # Developer-friendly OTP entry # Ready for JobPost notification testing
Example 4: Verify with retry on failure

Handle incorrect OTP with retry.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"
MAX_ATTEMPTS=3

for i in $(seq 1 $MAX_ATTEMPTS); do
    echo "Attempt $i: Enter verification code:"
    read -r OTP
    
    if aws sns verify-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
        --phone-number "$PHONE" \
        --one-time-password "$OTP" 2>&1; then
        echo "Verification successful!"
        exit 0
    else
        echo "Invalid code. $((MAX_ATTEMPTS - i)) attempts remaining."
    fi
done

echo "Max attempts reached. Request new verification code."
Output: Attempt 1: Enter verification code: 123456 Verification successful!
Django Context: # Retry logic for User phone verification # Handles typos in verification code # User-friendly error recovery
Example 5: Verify and send test message

Complete verification and test SMS delivery.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"
OTP="123456"

# Verify the number
aws sns verify-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
    --phone-number "$PHONE" \
    --one-time-password "$OTP"

# Send test message
aws sns publish \
    --phone-number "$PHONE" \
    --message "Welcome to ANIHPJ! Your phone is now verified for JobPost alerts."

echo "Verification complete and test message sent!"
Output: { "MessageId": "abc123-def456" } Verification complete and test message sent!
Django Context: # Full verification workflow with test # User receives welcome message # JobPost notification testing ready

list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers

Lists the calling AWS account's current verified and pending destination phone numbers in the SMS sandbox.

Syntax
aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
    [--next-token <value>] \
    [--max-results <value>]
Parameters
  • --next-token (string): Token for pagination
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of results (1-100)
Return Values
  • PhoneNumbers: List of phone numbers with their verification status
  • NextToken: Token for next page of results
Example 1: List all sandbox numbers

Get all verified and pending numbers.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers
Output: { "PhoneNumbers": [ { "PhoneNumber": "+14155551234", "Status": "Verified" }, { "PhoneNumber": "+14155555678", "Status": "Verified" }, { "PhoneNumber": "+14155559999", "Status": "Pending" } ] }
Django Context: # Test User phones in SMS sandbox # 2 verified, 1 pending verification # JobPost SMS can reach verified numbers
Example 2: List verified numbers only

Get numbers that can receive SMS.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
    --query 'PhoneNumbers[?Status==`Verified`].PhoneNumber' \
    --output text
Output: +14155551234 +14155555678
Django Context: # Verified test User phones # Can receive JobPost SMS notifications # Safe for sandbox SMS testing
Example 3: Count by status

Get counts of verified and pending numbers.

#!/bin/bash
echo "=== SMS Sandbox Phone Status ==="
VERIFIED=$(aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
    --query 'length(PhoneNumbers[?Status==`Verified`])' --output text)
PENDING=$(aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
    --query 'length(PhoneNumbers[?Status==`Pending`])' --output text)

echo "Verified: $VERIFIED"
echo "Pending: $PENDING"
echo "Total: $((VERIFIED + PENDING))"
Output: === SMS Sandbox Phone Status === Verified: 5 Pending: 2 Total: 7
Django Context: # 5 test Users can receive JobPost SMS # 2 Users still need verification # Sandbox capacity for testing
Example 4: Check specific number status

Verify if a number is in the sandbox.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"

STATUS=$(aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
    --query "PhoneNumbers[?PhoneNumber=='$PHONE'].Status" --output text)

if [ -n "$STATUS" ]; then
    echo "$PHONE status: $STATUS"
else
    echo "$PHONE is not in the sandbox"
fi
Output: +14155551234 status: Verified
Django Context: # Test User phone status verification # Number confirmed in sandbox # Ready for JobPost notification testing
Example 5: Export sandbox inventory

Generate report of all sandbox numbers.

#!/bin/bash
echo "=== SMS Sandbox Phone Numbers Report ==="
echo "Generated: $(date)"
echo ""
echo "Phone Number        | Status"
echo "--------------------|----------"

aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers | jq -r '
    .PhoneNumbers[] | "\(.PhoneNumber) | \(.Status)"'
Output: === SMS Sandbox Phone Numbers Report === Generated: Wed Jan 15 10:30:00 UTC 2025 Phone Number | Status --------------------|---------- +14155551234 | Verified +14155555678 | Verified +14155559999 | Pending
Django Context: # Complete sandbox inventory report # All test User phones documented # QA team reference for JobPost testing

delete-sms-sandbox-phone-number

Deletes an AWS account's verified or pending phone number from the SMS sandbox.

Syntax
aws sns delete-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
    --phone-number <value>
Parameters
  • --phone-number (string): Phone number in E.164 format to remove
Return Values
  • No output on success
Example 1: Delete sandbox number

Remove a number from the sandbox.

#!/bin/bash
aws sns delete-sms-sandbox-phone-number \
    --phone-number "+14155551234"

echo "Phone number removed from sandbox"
Output: Phone number removed from sandbox
Django Context: # Test User phone removed from sandbox # No longer receives SMS in sandbox mode # Clean up after testing complete
Example 2: Delete pending number

Remove number waiting for verification.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155559999"

# Check if pending
STATUS=$(aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
    --query "PhoneNumbers[?PhoneNumber=='$PHONE'].Status" --output text)

if [ "$STATUS" == "Pending" ]; then
    aws sns delete-sms-sandbox-phone-number --phone-number "$PHONE"
    echo "Pending number removed"
else
    echo "Number status is: $STATUS"
fi
Output: Pending number removed
Django Context: # Unverified test User phone cleaned up # Abandoned verification removed # Sandbox slot freed for new number
Example 3: Clean up old test numbers

Remove multiple numbers after testing.

#!/bin/bash
PHONES_TO_REMOVE=("+14155551111" "+14155552222" "+14155553333")

echo "Cleaning up sandbox test numbers..."
for phone in "${PHONES_TO_REMOVE[@]}"; do
    aws sns delete-sms-sandbox-phone-number --phone-number "$phone" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "Removed: $phone" || \
        echo "Not found: $phone"
done
Output: Cleaning up sandbox test numbers... Removed: +14155551111 Removed: +14155552222 Not found: +14155553333
Django Context: # Test User phones cleaned up # Post-sprint sandbox maintenance # Ready for new test numbers
Example 4: Delete with confirmation

Verify number exists before deletion.

#!/bin/bash
PHONE="+14155551234"

EXISTS=$(aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
    --query "PhoneNumbers[?PhoneNumber=='$PHONE'].PhoneNumber" --output text)

if [ -n "$EXISTS" ]; then
    echo "Found $PHONE in sandbox - removing..."
    aws sns delete-sms-sandbox-phone-number --phone-number "$PHONE"
    echo "Removed successfully"
else
    echo "Number not found in sandbox"
fi
Output: Found +14155551234 in sandbox - removing... Removed successfully
Django Context: # Safe deletion with existence check # Test User phone confirmed and removed # Clean sandbox state
Example 5: Remove all sandbox numbers

Clear all numbers from sandbox.

#!/bin/bash
echo "=== Removing All Sandbox Numbers ==="

aws sns list-sms-sandbox-phone-numbers \
    --query 'PhoneNumbers[].PhoneNumber' --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read phone; do
    if [ -n "$phone" ]; then
        aws sns delete-sms-sandbox-phone-number --phone-number "$phone"
        echo "Removed: $phone"
    fi
done

echo "Sandbox cleared"
Output: === Removing All Sandbox Numbers === Removed: +14155551234 Removed: +14155555678 Removed: +14155559999 Sandbox cleared
Django Context: # Complete sandbox reset # All test User phones removed # Fresh start for new testing cycle

get-data-protection-policy
