Output: { "items": [ {"version": "2.0", "description": "v2.0 release"}, {"version": "1.1", "description": "v1.1 patch"}, {"version": "1.0", "description": "Initial release"} ] }
Django Context: # All doc versions # JobPost.objects documentation history # Version management
Example 2: Get latest version

Find most recent documentation.

#!/bin/bash
LATEST=$(aws apigateway get-documentation-versions \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | sort_by(@, &createdDate) | [-1].version' \
    --output text)

echo "Latest documentation: $LATEST"
Output: Latest documentation: 2.0
Django Context: # Find latest docs # User.objects current version # Portal default
Example 3: Count versions

Get total version count.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-documentation-versions \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Documentation versions: $COUNT"
Output: Documentation versions: 5
Django Context: # Version count # Skills.objects doc history # Lifecycle tracking
Example 4: Version history table

Display versions in table.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-versions \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{Version: version, Description: description, Created: createdDate}' \
    --output table
Output: --------------------------------------------------- | GetDocumentationVersions | +---------+------------------+--------------------+ | Version | Description | Created | +---------+------------------+--------------------+ | 2.0 | v2.0 release | 2024-01-15T10:00Z | | 1.1 | v1.1 patch | 2024-01-05T10:00Z | +---------+------------------+--------------------+
Django Context: # Version history table # Author.objects changelog # Documentation timeline
Example 5: List version identifiers

Export version list.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-versions \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].version' --output text
Output: 2.0 1.1 1.0
Django Context: # Export versions # Location.objects scripting # Automation support

get-domain-name

Gets a custom domain name configuration.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name <value>
Parameters
  • --domain-name (string): Custom domain name
Return Values
  • domainName: Domain name
  • certificateArn: ACM certificate ARN
  • distributionDomainName: CloudFront distribution
Example 1: Get domain configuration

Retrieve domain details.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "certificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123", "distributionDomainName": "d1234567890.cloudfront.net", "endpointConfiguration": {"types": ["EDGE"]} }
Django Context: # Get domain config # JobPost.objects custom domain # DNS configuration
Example 2: Get CloudFront distribution

Find CloudFront domain for DNS.

#!/bin/bash
CF_DOMAIN=$(aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'distributionDomainName' --output text)

echo "CNAME target: $CF_DOMAIN"
Output: CNAME target: d1234567890.cloudfront.net
Django Context: # CloudFront distribution # User.objects DNS setup # CNAME record target
Example 3: Get certificate ARN

Find associated ACM certificate.

#!/bin/bash
CERT=$(aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'certificateArn' --output text)

echo "Certificate: $CERT"
Output: Certificate: arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123
Django Context: # Get SSL certificate # Skills.objects HTTPS config # Certificate management
Example 4: Check endpoint type

Verify if edge or regional.

#!/bin/bash
TYPE=$(aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'endpointConfiguration.types[0]' --output text)

echo "Endpoint type: $TYPE"
Output: Endpoint type: EDGE
Django Context: # Check endpoint type # Author.objects edge/regional # Performance configuration
Example 5: Full domain summary

Display complete domain info.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Custom Domain Configuration"
aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --output yaml
Output: Custom Domain Configuration domainName: api.anihpj.com certificateArn: arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123 distributionDomainName: d1234567890.cloudfront.net endpointConfiguration: types: - EDGE
Django Context: # Full domain config # Location.objects DNS setup # Infrastructure documentation

get-domain-name-access-associations

Gets access associations for a private custom domain.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations \
    [--domain-name <value>]
Parameters
  • --domain-name (string): Custom domain name filter
Return Values
  • items: List of access associations
Example 1: List access associations

Get all VPC access associations.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations \
    --domain-name internal.anihpj.com
Output: { "items": [ {"domainNameAccessAssociationArn": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc001"}, {"domainNameAccessAssociationArn": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc002"} ] }
Django Context: # List VPC associations # JobPost.objects private access # Internal API configuration
Example 2: Count associations

Get number of VPC accesses.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations \
    --domain-name internal.anihpj.com \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "VPC access associations: $COUNT"
Output: VPC access associations: 2
Django Context: # Count VPC accesses # User.objects private domains # Network inventory
Example 3: List all associations

Get associations across all domains.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations
Output: { "items": [ {"domainNameAccessAssociationArn": "...assoc001"}, {"domainNameAccessAssociationArn": "...assoc002"}, {"domainNameAccessAssociationArn": "...assoc003"} ] }
Django Context: # All VPC associations # Skills.objects network overview # Private API audit
Example 4: Extract ARNs

Get association ARNs list.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations \
    --domain-name internal.anihpj.com \
    --query 'items[].domainNameAccessAssociationArn' --output text
Output: arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc001 arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc002
Django Context: # Export association ARNs # Author.objects scripting # Automation support
Example 5: Association summary

Display associations in table.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Private Domain Access Associations"
aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations \
    --domain-name internal.anihpj.com \
    --output yaml
Output: Private Domain Access Associations items: - domainNameAccessAssociationArn: arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc001 - domainNameAccessAssociationArn: arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc002
Django Context: # Association overview # Location.objects VPC access # Network documentation

get-domain-names

Gets all custom domain names in the account.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-domain-names
Parameters
  • No required parameters
Return Values
  • items: List of custom domain names
Example 1: List all domains

Get all custom domains.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-names
Output: { "items": [ {"domainName": "api.anihpj.com"}, {"domainName": "staging-api.anihpj.com"}, {"domainName": "internal.anihpj.com"} ] }
Django Context: # List all domains # JobPost.objects custom domains # DNS inventory
Example 2: Count custom domains

Get total domain count.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-domain-names \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Custom domains: $COUNT"
Output: Custom domains: 3
Django Context: # Count domains # User.objects domain inventory # Infrastructure audit
Example 3: List domain names only

Extract just domain names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-names \
    --query 'items[].domainName' --output text
Output: api.anihpj.com staging-api.anihpj.com internal.anihpj.com
Django Context: # Export domain names # Skills.objects DNS list # Configuration export
Example 4: Domain summary table

Display domains with details.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-names \
    --query 'items[].{Domain: domainName, Endpoint: endpointConfiguration.types[0]}' \
    --output table
Output: -------------------------------------- | GetDomainNames | +-------------------------+----------+ | Domain | Endpoint | +-------------------------+----------+ | api.anihpj.com | EDGE | | staging-api.anihpj.com | EDGE | | internal.anihpj.com | REGIONAL | +-------------------------+----------+
Django Context: # Domain overview # Author.objects API domains # Infrastructure documentation
Example 5: Find production domains

Filter for production domains.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-names \
    --query 'items[?!contains(domainName, `staging`) && !contains(domainName, `test`)].domainName' \
    --output text
Output: api.anihpj.com internal.anihpj.com
Django Context: # Production domains only # Location.objects live APIs # Production inventory

get-export

Exports a REST API in a specified format.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --stage-name <value> \
    --export-type <value> \
    [--parameters <value>] \
    [--accepts <value>] \
    <outfile>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --stage-name (string): Stage to export
  • --export-type (string): Export format (oas30, swagger)
  • --accepts (string): Content type (application/json, application/yaml)
  • outfile (string): Output file path
Return Values
  • API specification written to output file
Example 1: Export OpenAPI 3.0

Export API as OpenAPI 3.0 JSON.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    --accepts application/json \
    api-spec.json

echo "API exported to api-spec.json"
Output: API exported to api-spec.json
Django Context: # Export OpenAPI spec # JobPost.objects API documentation # Client SDK generation
Example 2: Export as YAML

Export API as OpenAPI YAML.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    --accepts application/yaml \
    api-spec.yaml
Output: (No output, file created)
Django Context: # Export as YAML # User.objects human-readable spec # Developer documentation
Example 3: Export Swagger 2.0

Export legacy Swagger format.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type swagger \
    swagger.json
Output: (No output, file created)
Django Context: # Swagger 2.0 export # Skills.objects legacy tooling # Backwards compatibility
Example 4: Export with extensions

Include API Gateway extensions.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    --parameters extensions='integrations' \
    api-with-integrations.json
Output: (No output, file created)
Django Context: # Export with integrations # Author.objects full API config # Infrastructure as code
Example 5: Export staging for comparison

Export staging API for diff.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name staging \
    --export-type oas30 \
    staging-spec.json

aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    prod-spec.json

echo "Compare: diff staging-spec.json prod-spec.json"
Output: Compare: diff staging-spec.json prod-spec.json
Django Context: # Export for comparison # Location.objects API diff # Release verification

get-gateway-response

Gets a gateway response configuration.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --response-type <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --response-type (string): Response type
Return Values
  • responseType: Response type
  • statusCode: HTTP status code
  • responseParameters: Response headers
  • responseTemplates: Response body templates
Example 1: Get 4XX response

Get default 4XX error config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_4XX
Output: { "responseType": "DEFAULT_4XX", "statusCode": "400", "responseParameters": { "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "'*'" }, "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"error\": \"$context.error.message\"}" } }
Django Context: # Get 4XX response config # JobPost.objects error handling # CORS headers
Example 2: Get unauthorized response

Check 401 response config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type UNAUTHORIZED
Output: { "responseType": "UNAUTHORIZED", "statusCode": "401", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"message\": \"Authentication required\"}" } }
Django Context: # Get 401 config # User.objects auth errors # Custom error messages
Example 3: Check 5XX response

Get server error configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_5XX
Output: { "responseType": "DEFAULT_5XX", "statusCode": "500", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"message\": \"Internal server error\"}" } }
Django Context: # Get 5XX config # Skills.objects server errors # Error handling
Example 4: Get response headers

Check CORS headers.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_4XX \
    --query 'responseParameters'
Output: { "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "'*'", "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "'*'" }
Django Context: # CORS headers config # Author.objects cross-origin # Browser security
Example 5: Get quota exceeded response

Check throttling error config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type QUOTA_EXCEEDED
Output: { "responseType": "QUOTA_EXCEEDED", "statusCode": "429", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"message\": \"Rate limit exceeded\"}" } }
Django Context: # Quota exceeded config # Location.objects rate limits # Throttling messages

get-gateway-responses

Gets all gateway responses for a REST API.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
Return Values
  • items: List of gateway responses
Example 1: List all gateway responses

Get all error response configs.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "items": [ {"responseType": "DEFAULT_4XX", "statusCode": "400"}, {"responseType": "DEFAULT_5XX", "statusCode": "500"}, {"responseType": "UNAUTHORIZED", "statusCode": "401"} ] }
Django Context: # All gateway responses # JobPost.objects error config # Error handling overview
Example 2: Count configured responses

Get number of custom responses.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Configured responses: $COUNT"
Output: Configured responses: 8
Django Context: # Count responses # User.objects error types # Configuration audit
Example 3: List response types

Get all response type names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].responseType' --output text
Output: DEFAULT_4XX DEFAULT_5XX UNAUTHORIZED ACCESS_DENIED QUOTA_EXCEEDED THROTTLED
Django Context: # List response types # Skills.objects error catalog # Documentation
Example 4: Response summary table

Display responses in table.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{Type: responseType, Status: statusCode}' \
    --output table
Output: -------------------------------------- | GetGatewayResponses | +------------------+-----------------+ | Type | Status | +------------------+-----------------+ | DEFAULT_4XX | 400 | | DEFAULT_5XX | 500 | | UNAUTHORIZED | 401 | +------------------+-----------------+
Django Context: # Response overview # Author.objects error config # API documentation
Example 5: Find custom responses

List non-default responses.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[?defaultResponse==`false`]'
Output: [ {"responseType": "UNAUTHORIZED", "defaultResponse": false} ]
Django Context: # Find custom responses # Location.objects customizations # Configuration review

get-integration

Gets an integration configuration for an API method.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
Return Values
  • type: Integration type
  • uri: Integration URI
  • httpMethod: HTTP method
Example 1: Get Lambda integration

Retrieve Lambda integration config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET
Output: { "type": "AWS_PROXY", "uri": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-handler/invocations", "httpMethod": "POST", "passthroughBehavior": "WHEN_NO_MATCH" }
Django Context: # Lambda integration # JobPost.objects handler # Serverless backend
Example 2: Get HTTP integration

Check HTTP proxy config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res456 \
    --http-method GET
Output: { "type": "HTTP_PROXY", "uri": "https://backend.anihpj.com/api/jobs", "httpMethod": "GET", "connectionType": "INTERNET" }
Django Context: # HTTP proxy integration # User.objects Django backend # Direct proxy to Django
Example 3: Check integration type

Get just the integration type.

#!/bin/bash
TYPE=$(aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --query 'type' --output text)

echo "Integration type: $TYPE"
Output: Integration type: AWS_PROXY
Django Context: # Check integration type # Skills.objects backend type # Architecture documentation
Example 4: Get VPC link integration

Check private integration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id resvpc \
    --http-method GET \
    --query '{type: type, connectionId: connectionId}'
Output: { "type": "HTTP_PROXY", "connectionId": "vpc123link" }
Django Context: # VPC link integration # Author.objects private backend # Network configuration
Example 5: Full integration details

Display complete integration config.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Integration Configuration"
aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --output yaml
Output: Integration Configuration type: AWS_PROXY uri: arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/... httpMethod: POST passthroughBehavior: WHEN_NO_MATCH contentHandling: CONVERT_TO_TEXT
Django Context: # Full integration config # Location.objects backend setup # Infrastructure documentation

get-integration-response

Gets an integration response configuration.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --status-code <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --status-code (string): Status code
Return Values
  • statusCode: HTTP status code
  • selectionPattern: Response selection regex
  • responseTemplates: Response body templates
Example 1: Get 200 response config

Retrieve success response config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "selectionPattern": "", "responseParameters": { "method.response.header.Content-Type": "'application/json'" }, "responseTemplates": {} }
Django Context: # 200 response config # JobPost.objects success response # Content type headers
Example 2: Get error response

Check 400 error response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res456 \
    --http-method POST \
    --status-code 400
Output: { "statusCode": "400", "selectionPattern": ".*[Bb]ad[Rr]equest.*", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"error\": \"$input.path('$.errorMessage')\"}" } }
Django Context: # 400 error response # User.objects validation errors # Error transformation
Example 3: Check selection pattern

Get regex for response selection.

#!/bin/bash
PATTERN=$(aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 500 \
    --query 'selectionPattern' --output text)

echo "Selection pattern: $PATTERN"
Output: Selection pattern: .*[Ee]rror.*
Django Context: # Response selection # Skills.objects error matching # Regex configuration
Example 4: Get response template

Check response body template.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --query 'responseTemplates'
Output: { "application/json": "#set($root=$input.path('$'))$root" }
Django Context: # Response template # Author.objects body mapping # VTL transformation
Example 5: Full response details

Display complete response config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --output yaml
Output: statusCode: '200' selectionPattern: '' responseParameters: method.response.header.Content-Type: "'application/json'" responseTemplates: {}
Django Context: # Full response config # Location.objects response setup # Documentation

get-method

Gets information about an API method.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
Return Values
  • httpMethod: HTTP method
  • authorizationType: Auth type
  • apiKeyRequired: Whether API key is required
  • methodResponses: Response definitions
Example 1: Get method configuration

Retrieve method details.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET
Output: { "httpMethod": "GET", "authorizationType": "COGNITO_USER_POOLS", "authorizerId": "auth123", "apiKeyRequired": true, "requestParameters": {}, "methodResponses": { "200": {"statusCode": "200"} } }
Django Context: # Method configuration # JobPost.objects GET endpoint # Auth and API key config
Example 2: Check authorization

Get auth configuration for method.

#!/bin/bash
AUTH=$(aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --query 'authorizationType' --output text)

echo "Authorization: $AUTH"
Output: Authorization: COGNITO_USER_POOLS
Django Context: # Check auth type # User.objects security config # Endpoint protection
Example 3: Check API key requirement

Verify if API key is required.

#!/bin/bash
KEY_REQUIRED=$(aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --query 'apiKeyRequired' --output text)

echo "API key required: $KEY_REQUIRED"
Output: API key required: True
Django Context: # Check API key # Skills.objects access control # Usage tracking
Example 4: Get request parameters

Check required parameters.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --query 'requestParameters'
Output: { "method.request.querystring.page": true, "method.request.querystring.limit": false }
Django Context: # Request parameters # Author.objects pagination # Query string validation
Example 5: Full method details

Display complete method config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --output yaml
Output: httpMethod: GET authorizationType: COGNITO_USER_POOLS authorizerId: auth123 apiKeyRequired: true methodResponses: '200': statusCode: '200'
Django Context: # Full method config # Location.objects endpoint setup # API documentation

get-method-response

Gets a method response configuration.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --status-code <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --status-code (string): Status code
Return Values
  • statusCode: HTTP status code
  • responseParameters: Response header definitions
  • responseModels: Response body models
Example 1: Get 200 method response

Retrieve success response definition.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseParameters": { "method.response.header.Content-Type": true }, "responseModels": { "application/json": "JobPostList" } }
Django Context: # 200 response definition # JobPost.objects success response # Response schema
Example 2: Get 404 response

Check not found response config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 404
Output: { "statusCode": "404", "responseModels": { "application/json": "Error" } }
Django Context: # 404 response definition # User.objects not found # Error model
Example 3: Get response model

Check which model is used.

#!/bin/bash
MODEL=$(aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --query 'responseModels."application/json"' --output text)

echo "Response model: $MODEL"
Output: Response model: JobPostList
Django Context: # Response model name # Skills.objects schema # API documentation
Example 4: Check response headers

Get header definitions.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --query 'responseParameters'
Output: { "method.response.header.Content-Type": true, "method.response.header.X-Request-Id": true }
Django Context: # Response headers # Author.objects header config # API contract
Example 5: Full response config

Display complete response definition.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --output yaml
Output: statusCode: '200' responseParameters: method.response.header.Content-Type: true responseModels: application/json: JobPostList
Django Context: # Full response definition # Location.objects API contract # OpenAPI generation

get-model

Gets information about a data model.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --model-name <value> \
    [--flatten]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --model-name (string): Model name
  • --flatten (boolean): Include resolved schema
Return Values
  • id: Model identifier
  • name: Model name
  • schema: JSON Schema
  • contentType: Content type
Example 1: Get model schema

Retrieve model definition.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost", "contentType": "application/json", "schema": "{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"title\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"company\":{\"type\":\"string\"}}}" }
Django Context: # Get JobPost model # JobPost.objects schema # API contract definition
Example 2: Get flattened schema

Resolve all schema references.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --flatten
Output: { "name": "JobPost", "schema": "{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{...expanded...}}" }
Django Context: # Flattened schema # User.objects full definition # No $ref references
Example 3: Extract schema only

Get just the JSON schema.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --query 'schema' --output text | jq .
Output: { "type": "object", "properties": { "title": {"type": "string"}, "company": {"type": "string"} } }
Django Context: # Extract schema # Skills.objects JSON Schema # Validation rules
Example 4: Get error model

Check error response model.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name Error
Output: { "name": "Error", "schema": "{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"message\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"code\":{\"type\":\"integer\"}}}" }
Django Context: # Error model schema # Author.objects error format # Consistent errors
Example 5: Model summary

Display model info.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Model: JobPost"
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --query '{Name: name, ContentType: contentType}' \
    --output yaml
Output: Model: JobPost Name: JobPost ContentType: application/json
Django Context: # Model overview # Location.objects schema info # Documentation

get-models

Gets all models for a REST API.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
Return Values
  • items: List of models
Example 1: List all models

Get all data models.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "m001", "name": "JobPost"}, {"id": "m002", "name": "User"}, {"id": "m003", "name": "Error"}, {"id": "m004", "name": "Empty"} ] }
Django Context: # List all models # JobPost.objects schema inventory # API contract models
Example 2: Count models

Get number of defined models.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Models defined: $COUNT"
Output: Models defined: 8
Django Context: # Count models # User.objects schema count # API complexity
Example 3: List model names

Get all model names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].name' --output text
Output: JobPost User Error Empty Skills Author Location
Django Context: # Model names # Skills.objects schema list # Documentation index
Example 4: Models table

Display models in table.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{ID: id, Name: name, ContentType: contentType}' \
    --output table
Output: ------------------------------------------------- | GetModels | +--------+------------+------------------------+ | ID | Name | ContentType | +--------+------------+------------------------+ | m001 | JobPost | application/json | | m002 | User | application/json | | m003 | Error | application/json | +--------+------------+------------------------+
Django Context: # Models overview # Author.objects schema inventory # API documentation
Example 5: Export model IDs

Get model IDs for scripting.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{name: name, id: id}' --output json
Output: [ {"name": "JobPost", "id": "m001"}, {"name": "User", "id": "m002"} ]
Django Context: # Export model IDs # Location.objects automation # Scripting support

get-model-template

Gets the model template for a specified model.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-model-template \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --model-name <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --model-name (string): Model name
Return Values
  • value: Template string
Example 1: Get model template

Retrieve mapping template.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model-template \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost
Output: { "value": "{\"title\": \"\", \"company\": \"\", \"location\": \"\", \"skills\": []}" }
Django Context: # Model template # JobPost.objects request template # Request body structure
Example 2: Extract template value

Get just the template string.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model-template \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --query 'value' --output text | jq .
Output: { "title": "", "company": "", "location": "", "skills": [] }
Django Context: # Template structure # User.objects request format # Client documentation
Example 3: Save template to file

Export template for client use.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model-template \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --query 'value' --output text > jobpost-template.json

echo "Template saved to jobpost-template.json"
Output: Template saved to jobpost-template.json
Django Context: # Export template # Skills.objects request sample # SDK generation
Example 4: Generate all templates

Export templates for all models.

#!/bin/bash
API_ID="abc123def4"

for MODEL in JobPost User Skills; do
    aws apigateway get-model-template \
        --rest-api-id $API_ID \
        --model-name $MODEL \
        --query 'value' --output text > "${MODEL,,}-template.json"
    echo "Generated: ${MODEL,,}-template.json"
done
Output: Generated: jobpost-template.json Generated: user-template.json Generated: skills-template.json
Django Context: # Generate all templates # Author.objects batch export # Client SDK support
Example 5: Verify template structure

Check template is valid JSON.

#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE=$(aws apigateway get-model-template \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --query 'value' --output text)

if echo "$TEMPLATE" | jq . > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Valid JSON template"
else
    echo "Invalid template format"
fi
Output: Valid JSON template
Django Context: # Validate template # Location.objects JSON check # Quality assurance

get-request-validator

Retrieves a specific request validator from an API Gateway REST API. Request validators validate incoming requests before they reach your backend integration.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --request-validator-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): The REST API identifier
  • --request-validator-id (string): The request validator identifier
Return Value
  • id (string): Validator identifier
  • name (string): Validator name
  • validateRequestBody (boolean): Whether body is validated
  • validateRequestParameters (boolean): Whether parameters are validated
Example 1: Get JobPost body validator

Retrieve validator for JobPost creation requests.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "name": "jobpost-body-validator", "validateRequestBody": true, "validateRequestParameters": false }
Django Context: # Validate JobPost.objects.create() body # Skills.objects validation # Required fields check
Example 2: Get parameter validator

Retrieve validator for query parameters.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv456def
Output: { "id": "rv456def", "name": "param-validator", "validateRequestBody": false, "validateRequestParameters": true }
Django Context: # Parameter validation # User.objects.filter() params # Location.objects query check
Example 3: Get full validator

Retrieve validator that checks both body and parameters.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv789full \
    --output table
Output: -------------------------------------------------- | GetRequestValidator | +---------------------------+--------------------+ | id | rv789full | | name | full-validator | | validateRequestBody | True | | validateRequestParameters| True | +---------------------------+--------------------+
Django Context: # Full validation # Author.objects complete check # Both body and params
Example 4: Check validator status

Query specific validator settings.

#!/bin/bash
BODY_VAL=$(aws apigateway get-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --query 'validateRequestBody' --output text)

echo "Body validation: $BODY_VAL"
Output: Body validation: True
Django Context: # Check validation status # Skills.objects body check # Boolean response
Example 5: Export validator config

Save validator configuration to file.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --output json > validator-config.json

echo "Validator config saved"
Output: Validator config saved
Django Context: # Export config # JobPost.objects validator # Backup configuration

get-request-validators

Retrieves all request validators from an API Gateway REST API. Lists all validators that can be applied to methods for input validation.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-request-validators \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    [--position <value>] \
    [--limit <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): The REST API identifier
  • --position (string): Pagination token
  • --limit (integer): Maximum results to return
Return Value
  • items (array): List of request validators
  • position (string): Pagination token for next page
Example 1: List all validators

Get all request validators for the API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-request-validators \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "rv123abc", "name": "jobpost-body-validator", "validateRequestBody": true, "validateRequestParameters": false }, { "id": "rv456def", "name": "param-validator", "validateRequestBody": false, "validateRequestParameters": true } ] }
Django Context: # List all validators # JobPost.objects validation rules # Skills.objects input checks
Example 2: List validators with limit

Get limited list of validators.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-request-validators \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --limit 5
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "rv123abc", "name": "jobpost-body-validator", "validateRequestBody": true, "validateRequestParameters": false } ] }
Django Context: # Limited validator list # User.objects validation # Pagination support
Example 3: Get validator names only

Query just validator names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-request-validators \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].name' --output text
Output: jobpost-body-validator param-validator full-validator
Django Context: # Validator names # Author.objects validators # Quick reference
Example 4: Count validators

Count total number of validators.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-request-validators \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total validators: $COUNT"
Output: Total validators: 3
Django Context: # Count validators # Location.objects check count # API coverage
Example 5: Find body validators

Filter validators that check request body.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-request-validators \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[?validateRequestBody==`true`]'
Output: [ { "id": "rv123abc", "name": "jobpost-body-validator", "validateRequestBody": true, "validateRequestParameters": false } ]
Django Context: # Body validators filter # Skills.objects body validation # POST request checks

get-resource

Retrieves information about a specific resource in an API Gateway REST API. Resources represent the paths in your API hierarchy.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-resource \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    [--embed <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): The REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): The resource identifier
  • --embed (list): Embed methods in response
Return Value
  • id (string): Resource identifier
  • parentId (string): Parent resource ID
  • pathPart (string): Path segment
  • path (string): Full resource path
  • resourceMethods (object): HTTP methods on resource
Example 1: Get jobposts resource

Retrieve the /jobposts resource details.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc
Output: { "id": "res123abc", "parentId": "rootres123", "pathPart": "jobposts", "path": "/jobposts", "resourceMethods": { "GET": {}, "POST": {} } }
Django Context: # /jobposts endpoint # JobPost.objects.all() - GET # JobPost.objects.create() - POST
Example 2: Get resource with methods

Retrieve resource with embedded method details.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --embed methods
Output: { "id": "res123abc", "parentId": "rootres123", "pathPart": "jobposts", "path": "/jobposts", "resourceMethods": { "GET": { "httpMethod": "GET", "authorizationType": "AWS_IAM" }, "POST": { "httpMethod": "POST", "authorizationType": "AWS_IAM" } } }
Django Context: # Embedded methods # User.objects auth required # IAM authorization
Example 3: Get skills resource

Retrieve the /skills nested resource.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res456skills \
    --output table
Output: ----------------------------------------- | GetResource | +---------------+-----------------------+ | id | res456skills | | parentId | res123abc | | pathPart | skills | | path | /jobposts/skills | +---------------+-----------------------+
Django Context: # /jobposts/skills endpoint # Skills.objects.filter() # Nested resource
Example 4: Get path parameter resource

Retrieve resource with path parameter.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789id
Output: { "id": "res789id", "parentId": "res123abc", "pathPart": "{id}", "path": "/jobposts/{id}", "resourceMethods": { "GET": {}, "PUT": {}, "DELETE": {} } }
Django Context: # /jobposts/{id} endpoint # JobPost.objects.get(pk=id) # Single item operations
Example 5: Query resource path

Get just the path for a resource.

#!/bin/bash
PATH_INFO=$(aws apigateway get-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --query 'path' --output text)

echo "Resource path: $PATH_INFO"
Output: Resource path: /jobposts
Django Context: # Query path # Location.objects endpoint # URL routing

get-resources

Retrieves all resources from an API Gateway REST API. Lists all API paths and their configurations.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-resources \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    [--position <value>] \
    [--limit <value>] \
    [--embed <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): The REST API identifier
  • --position (string): Pagination token
  • --limit (integer): Maximum results to return
  • --embed (list): Embed methods in response
Return Value
  • items (array): List of resources
  • position (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List all resources

Get all API resources.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-resources \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "rootres123", "path": "/" }, { "id": "res123abc", "parentId": "rootres123", "pathPart": "jobposts", "path": "/jobposts" }, { "id": "res456skills", "parentId": "res123abc", "pathPart": "skills", "path": "/jobposts/skills" } ] }
Django Context: # All API paths # JobPost.objects endpoints # Skills.objects routes
Example 2: List resources with methods

Get resources with embedded HTTP methods.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-resources \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --embed methods
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "res123abc", "path": "/jobposts", "resourceMethods": { "GET": {"httpMethod": "GET"}, "POST": {"httpMethod": "POST"} } } ] }
Django Context: # Resources with methods # User.objects CRUD # HTTP verbs included
Example 3: Get resource paths only

Query just the paths from all resources.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-resources \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].path' --output text
Output: / /jobposts /jobposts/{id} /jobposts/skills /users
Django Context: # All paths # Author.objects routes # URL listing
Example 4: Count total resources

Count all resources in API.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-resources \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total resources: $COUNT"
Output: Total resources: 8
Django Context: # Resource count # Location.objects endpoints # API coverage
Example 5: Find parameterized resources

Filter resources with path parameters.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-resources \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[?contains(path, `{`)]'
Output: [ { "id": "res789id", "parentId": "res123abc", "pathPart": "{id}", "path": "/jobposts/{id}" }, { "id": "res101userid", "parentId": "resusers", "pathPart": "{userId}", "path": "/users/{userId}" } ]
Django Context: # Parameterized routes # JobPost.objects.get(pk=id) # User.objects.get(pk=userId)

get-rest-api

Retrieves information about a specific REST API in API Gateway. Returns configuration details including name, description, and API keys settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): The REST API identifier
Return Value
  • id (string): API identifier
  • name (string): API name
  • description (string): API description
  • createdDate (timestamp): Creation date
  • apiKeySource (string): API key source
  • endpointConfiguration (object): Endpoint settings
Example 1: Get JobPost API details

Retrieve the main JobPost REST API configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api", "description": "JobPost management API for Django backend", "createdDate": "2025-01-10T10:00:00Z", "apiKeySource": "HEADER", "endpointConfiguration": { "types": ["REGIONAL"] }, "disableExecuteApiEndpoint": false }
Django Context: # JobPost API details # Skills.objects endpoint config # Regional deployment
Example 2: Get API name and description

Query specific API metadata.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query '[name, description]' --output text
Output: anihpj-jobpost-api JobPost management API for Django backend
Django Context: # API metadata # User.objects API info # Quick lookup
Example 3: Check endpoint type

Determine API endpoint configuration.

#!/bin/bash
ENDPOINT=$(aws apigateway get-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'endpointConfiguration.types[0]' --output text)

echo "Endpoint type: $ENDPOINT"
Output: Endpoint type: REGIONAL
Django Context: # Endpoint type check # Author.objects API type # Regional vs Edge
Example 4: Get API creation date

Check when API was created.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'createdDate' --output text
Output: 2025-01-10T10:00:00Z
Django Context: # Creation timestamp # Location.objects API age # Audit tracking
Example 5: Export API config

Save full API configuration to file.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --output json > api-config.json

echo "API configuration exported"
Output: API configuration exported
Django Context: # Export config # Skills.objects backup # Infrastructure as code

get-rest-apis

Retrieves all REST APIs in your AWS account. Lists all APIs with their configurations.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-rest-apis \
    [--position <value>] \
    [--limit <value>]
Parameters
  • --position (string): Pagination token
  • --limit (integer): Maximum results to return
Return Value
  • items (array): List of REST APIs
  • position (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List all REST APIs

Get all APIs in the account.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-rest-apis
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "abc123def4", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api", "description": "JobPost management API", "createdDate": "2025-01-10T10:00:00Z" }, { "id": "xyz789ghi0", "name": "anihpj-user-api", "description": "User management API", "createdDate": "2025-01-10T11:00:00Z" } ] }
Django Context: # All APIs # JobPost.objects API # User.objects API
Example 2: List APIs with limit

Get limited number of APIs.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-rest-apis \
    --limit 5
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "abc123def4", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api" } ], "position": "nexttoken123" }
Django Context: # Limited results # Skills.objects[:5] # Pagination
Example 3: Get API names only

Query just API names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-rest-apis \
    --query 'items[].name' --output text
Output: anihpj-jobpost-api anihpj-user-api anihpj-skills-api
Django Context: # API names # Author.objects API list # Quick reference
Example 4: Count total APIs

Count all REST APIs.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-rest-apis \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total APIs: $COUNT"
Output: Total APIs: 3
Django Context: # API count # Location.objects APIs # Resource inventory
Example 5: Find API by name

Filter APIs by name pattern.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-rest-apis \
    --query "items[?contains(name, 'jobpost')]"
Output: [ { "id": "abc123def4", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api", "description": "JobPost management API" } ]
Django Context: # Filter by name # JobPost.objects API # Name search

get-sdk

Generates a client SDK for a REST API and stage. Creates SDK packages for various platforms like JavaScript, Java, Android, iOS, etc.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-sdk \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --stage-name <value> \
    --sdk-type <value> \
    [--parameters <value>] \
    <output-file>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): The REST API identifier
  • --stage-name (string): Stage name (prod, dev, etc.)
  • --sdk-type (string): SDK type (javascript, java, android, objectivec, swift)
  • --parameters (map): SDK-specific parameters
Return Value
  • contentType (string): Content type of SDK file
  • contentDisposition (string): Filename header
  • body (blob): SDK zip file content
Example 1: Generate JavaScript SDK

Create JS client SDK for frontend.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --sdk-type javascript \
    jobpost-sdk.zip

echo "JavaScript SDK generated: jobpost-sdk.zip"
Output: JavaScript SDK generated: jobpost-sdk.zip
Django Context: # JavaScript SDK # JobPost.objects client # Frontend integration
Example 2: Generate Java SDK

Create Java client SDK for Android.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --sdk-type java \
    --parameters groupId=com.anihpj,artifactId=jobpost-client \
    jobpost-java-sdk.zip

echo "Java SDK generated"
Output: Java SDK generated
Django Context: # Java SDK # Skills.objects Android client # Mobile integration
Example 3: Generate iOS SDK

Create Swift SDK for iOS app.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --sdk-type swift \
    --parameters classPrefix=JP \
    jobpost-swift-sdk.zip

echo "Swift SDK generated for iOS"
Output: Swift SDK generated for iOS
Django Context: # Swift SDK # User.objects iOS client # Mobile app integration
Example 4: Generate dev environment SDK

Create SDK for development stage.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name dev \
    --sdk-type javascript \
    jobpost-dev-sdk.zip

echo "Dev SDK generated for testing"
Output: Dev SDK generated for testing
Django Context: # Dev SDK # Author.objects test client # Development testing
Example 5: Extract and use SDK

Download and extract SDK for use.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --sdk-type javascript \
    sdk.zip

unzip sdk.zip -d ./client-sdk/
echo "SDK extracted to ./client-sdk/"
ls -la ./client-sdk/
Output: SDK extracted to ./client-sdk/ total 24 drwxr-xr-x 3 user user 4096 Jan 10 10:00 . drwxr-xr-x 5 user user 4096 Jan 10 10:00 .. -rw-r--r-- 1 user user 8192 Jan 10 10:00 apigClient.js -rw-r--r-- 1 user user 2048 Jan 10 10:00 README.md
Django Context: # Extract SDK # Location.objects client # Ready for integration

get-sdk-type

Retrieves information about a specific SDK type available for API Gateway. Returns configuration parameters for generating SDKs.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-sdk-type \
    --id <value>
Parameters
  • --id (string): SDK type identifier (javascript, java, android, objectivec, swift)
Return Value
  • id (string): SDK type identifier
  • friendlyName (string): Display name
  • description (string): SDK type description
  • configurationProperties (array): Available parameters
Example 1: Get JavaScript SDK type info

Check JavaScript SDK configuration options.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk-type \
    --id javascript
Output: { "id": "javascript", "friendlyName": "JavaScript", "description": "JavaScript SDK for browser applications", "configurationProperties": [ { "name": "serviceName", "friendlyName": "Service Name", "description": "Service name for the generated SDK" } ] }
Django Context: # JavaScript SDK type # JobPost.objects JS client # Browser integration
Example 2: Get Java SDK type info

Check Java SDK parameters.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk-type \
    --id java
Output: { "id": "java", "friendlyName": "Java", "description": "Java SDK for Android and server applications", "configurationProperties": [ { "name": "groupId", "friendlyName": "Group ID", "description": "Maven group ID" }, { "name": "artifactId", "friendlyName": "Artifact ID", "description": "Maven artifact ID" } ] }
Django Context: # Java SDK type # Skills.objects Java client # Maven configuration
Example 3: Get Swift SDK type

Check iOS Swift SDK options.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk-type \
    --id swift
Output: { "id": "swift", "friendlyName": "Swift", "description": "Swift SDK for iOS applications", "configurationProperties": [ { "name": "classPrefix", "friendlyName": "Class Prefix", "description": "Prefix for generated classes" } ] }
Django Context: # Swift SDK type # User.objects iOS client # Class prefix option
Example 4: Get SDK parameters list

Query available parameters for SDK type.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk-type \
    --id java \
    --query 'configurationProperties[].name' --output text
Output: groupId artifactId invokerPackage modelPackage
Django Context: # SDK parameters # Author.objects config # Required settings
Example 5: Get SDK description

Check SDK type description.

#!/bin/bash
DESC=$(aws apigateway get-sdk-type \
    --id android \
    --query 'description' --output text)

echo "Android SDK: $DESC"
Output: Android SDK: Android SDK for mobile applications
Django Context: # SDK description # Location.objects Android # Platform info

get-sdk-types

Retrieves all available SDK types for API Gateway. Lists all platforms you can generate SDKs for.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-sdk-types \
    [--position <value>] \
    [--limit <value>]
Parameters
  • --position (string): Pagination token
  • --limit (integer): Maximum results to return
Return Value
  • items (array): List of SDK types
  • position (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List all SDK types

Get all available SDK platforms.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk-types
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "javascript", "friendlyName": "JavaScript"}, {"id": "android", "friendlyName": "Android"}, {"id": "objectivec", "friendlyName": "Objective-C"}, {"id": "swift", "friendlyName": "Swift"}, {"id": "java", "friendlyName": "Java"} ] }
Django Context: # All SDK types # JobPost.objects clients # Multi-platform support
Example 2: Get SDK type IDs

List just the SDK type identifiers.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk-types \
    --query 'items[].id' --output text
Output: javascript android objectivec swift java
Django Context: # SDK IDs # Skills.objects platforms # Quick reference
Example 3: Count SDK types

Count available SDK platforms.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-sdk-types \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Available SDK types: $COUNT"
Output: Available SDK types: 5
Django Context: # SDK count # User.objects platforms # Platform support
Example 4: Get mobile SDK types

Filter for mobile platforms.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk-types \
    --query "items[?id=='android' || id=='swift' || id=='objectivec']"
Output: [ {"id": "android", "friendlyName": "Android"}, {"id": "objectivec", "friendlyName": "Objective-C"}, {"id": "swift", "friendlyName": "Swift"} ]
Django Context: # Mobile SDKs # Author.objects mobile clients # iOS and Android
Example 5: Display SDK types table

Show SDK types in table format.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-sdk-types \
    --query 'items[].{ID:id,Name:friendlyName}' \
    --output table
Output: ---------------------------------- | GetSdkTypes | +-------------+------------------+ | ID | Name | +-------------+------------------+ | javascript | JavaScript | | android | Android | | objectivec | Objective-C | | swift | Swift | | java | Java | +-------------+------------------+
Django Context: # SDK types table # Location.objects platforms # Formatted output

get-stage

Retrieves information about a specific deployment stage of a REST API. Returns configuration including caching, throttling, and variables.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-stage \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --stage-name <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): The REST API identifier
  • --stage-name (string): Stage name (prod, dev, staging)
Return Value
  • stageName (string): Stage name
  • deploymentId (string): Current deployment
  • cacheClusterEnabled (boolean): Cache status
  • variables (object): Stage variables
  • methodSettings (object): Method configurations
Example 1: Get production stage

Retrieve production stage configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod
Output: { "stageName": "prod", "deploymentId": "dep123abc", "description": "Production environment", "cacheClusterEnabled": true, "cacheClusterSize": "0.5", "variables": { "dbHost": "prod-db.cluster.amazonaws.com", "environment": "production" }, "createdDate": "2025-01-10T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Production stage # JobPost.objects prod config # Cache enabled
Example 2: Get dev stage variables

Retrieve development stage variables.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name dev \
    --query 'variables'
Output: { "dbHost": "dev-db.cluster.amazonaws.com", "environment": "development", "debugMode": "true" }
Django Context: # Dev variables # Skills.objects dev config # Debug enabled
Example 3: Check cache status

Verify if caching is enabled on stage.

#!/bin/bash
CACHE=$(aws apigateway get-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --query 'cacheClusterEnabled' --output text)

echo "Cache enabled: $CACHE"
Output: Cache enabled: True
Django Context: # Cache check # User.objects caching # Performance setting
Example 4: Get deployment ID

Find current deployment for stage.

#!/bin/bash
DEP_ID=$(aws apigateway get-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --query 'deploymentId' --output text)

echo "Current deployment: $DEP_ID"
Output: Current deployment: dep123abc
Django Context: # Deployment ID # Author.objects version # Release tracking
Example 5: Export stage config

Save stage configuration to file.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --output json > stage-config.json

echo "Stage configuration saved"
Output: Stage configuration saved
Django Context: # Export config # Location.objects stage # Backup settings

get-stages

Retrieves all stages of a REST API. Lists all deployment environments like prod, dev, staging.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-stages \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    [--deployment-id <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): The REST API identifier
  • --deployment-id (string): Filter by deployment ID
Return Value
  • item (array): List of stages
Example 1: List all stages

Get all stages for the API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-stages \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "item": [ { "stageName": "prod", "deploymentId": "dep123abc", "cacheClusterEnabled": true }, { "stageName": "dev", "deploymentId": "dep456def", "cacheClusterEnabled": false }, { "stageName": "staging", "deploymentId": "dep789ghi", "cacheClusterEnabled": false } ] }
Django Context: # All stages # JobPost.objects environments # prod, dev, staging
Example 2: Get stage names

List just the stage names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-stages \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'item[].stageName' --output text
Output: prod dev staging
Django Context: # Stage names # Skills.objects envs # Environment list
Example 3: Filter by deployment

Get stages for specific deployment.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-stages \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep123abc
Output: { "item": [ { "stageName": "prod", "deploymentId": "dep123abc" } ] }
Django Context: # Deployment filter # User.objects release # Version tracking
Example 4: Count stages

Count total number of stages.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-stages \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'item | length(@)')

echo "Total stages: $COUNT"
Output: Total stages: 3
Django Context: # Stage count # Author.objects environments # Deployment targets
Example 5: Find cached stages

Filter stages with cache enabled.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-stages \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'item[?cacheClusterEnabled==`true`].stageName'
Output: ["prod"]
Django Context: # Cached stages # Location.objects cache # Performance optimization

get-tags

Retrieves tags associated with an API Gateway resource. Tags are key-value pairs for organizing and tracking resources.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-tags \
    --resource-arn <value> \
    [--position <value>] \
    [--limit <value>]
Parameters
  • --resource-arn (string): ARN of the API Gateway resource
  • --position (string): Pagination token
  • --limit (integer): Maximum results
Return Value
  • tags (object): Map of tag key-value pairs
Example 1: Get API tags

Retrieve tags for REST API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-tags \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4
Output: { "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Project": "anihpj", "Team": "backend", "CostCenter": "engineering" } }
Django Context: # API tags # JobPost.objects metadata # Resource organization
Example 2: Get stage tags

Retrieve tags for a specific stage.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-tags \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4/stages/prod
Output: { "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Version": "v2.1.0" } }
Django Context: # Stage tags # Skills.objects prod metadata # Version tracking
Example 3: Get specific tag value

Query a specific tag.

#!/bin/bash
ENV=$(aws apigateway get-tags \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --query 'tags.Environment' --output text)

echo "Environment: $ENV"
Output: Environment: production
Django Context: # Environment tag # User.objects env check # Single value query
Example 4: List tag keys

Get all tag keys for resource.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-tags \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --query 'tags | keys(@)' --output text
Output: Environment Project Team CostCenter
Django Context: # Tag keys # Author.objects labels # Metadata fields
Example 5: Export tags to JSON

Save tags configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-tags \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --query 'tags' --output json > api-tags.json

echo "Tags exported to api-tags.json"
Output: Tags exported to api-tags.json
Django Context: # Export tags # Location.objects metadata # Configuration backup

get-usage

Retrieves API usage data for a usage plan and time period. Shows request counts and quota consumption.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-usage \
    --usage-plan-id <value> \
    --start-date <value> \
    --end-date <value> \
    [--key-id <value>] \
    [--position <value>] \
    [--limit <value>]
Parameters
  • --usage-plan-id (string): Usage plan identifier
  • --start-date (string): Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)
  • --end-date (string): End date (YYYY-MM-DD)
  • --key-id (string): Filter by API key
  • --position (string): Pagination token
  • --limit (integer): Maximum results
Return Value
  • usagePlanId (string): Usage plan identifier
  • startDate (string): Period start
  • endDate (string): Period end
  • items (object): Usage data by API key
Example 1: Get weekly usage

Retrieve usage for the past week.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123abc \
    --start-date 2025-01-10 \
    --end-date 2025-01-17
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123abc", "startDate": "2025-01-10", "endDate": "2025-01-17", "items": { "key123abc": [ [1500, 5000], [1600, 5000], [1450, 5000] ] } }
Django Context: # Weekly usage # JobPost.objects API calls # Quota tracking
Example 2: Get usage for specific key

Filter usage by API key.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123abc \
    --start-date 2025-01-10 \
    --end-date 2025-01-17 \
    --key-id key123abc
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123abc", "items": { "key123abc": [ [1500, 5000] ] } }
Django Context: # Key-specific usage # Skills.objects API key # User consumption
Example 3: Get monthly usage

Retrieve full month usage data.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123abc \
    --start-date 2025-01-01 \
    --end-date 2025-01-31
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123abc", "startDate": "2025-01-01", "endDate": "2025-01-31", "items": { "key123abc": [[45000, 100000]], "key456def": [[32000, 100000]] } }
Django Context: # Monthly usage # User.objects billing # Cost analysis
Example 4: Check quota percentage

Calculate usage percentage of quota.

#!/bin/bash
USAGE=$(aws apigateway get-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123abc \
    --start-date 2025-01-10 \
    --end-date 2025-01-17 \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --query 'items.key123abc[0]' --output text)

echo "Usage: $USAGE"
Output: Usage: 1500 5000
Django Context: # Quota check # Author.objects usage # Limit monitoring
Example 5: Export usage report

Save usage data to file.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123abc \
    --start-date 2025-01-01 \
    --end-date 2025-01-31 \
    --output json > usage-report.json

echo "Usage report saved"
Output: Usage report saved
Django Context: # Export report # Location.objects analytics # Billing documentation

get-usage-plan

Retrieves a specific usage plan. Shows quota limits, throttling settings, and associated API stages.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id <value>
Parameters
  • --usage-plan-id (string): Usage plan identifier
Return Value
  • id (string): Usage plan identifier
  • name (string): Plan name
  • quota (object): Quota limits
  • throttle (object): Throttling settings
  • apiStages (array): Associated stages
Example 1: Get premium plan details

Retrieve premium usage plan configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "name": "anihpj-premium-plan", "description": "Premium tier for enterprise users", "quota": { "limit": 100000, "period": "MONTH", "offset": 0 }, "throttle": { "rateLimit": 1000, "burstLimit": 2000 }, "apiStages": [ {"apiId": "abc123def4", "stage": "prod"} ] }
Django Context: # Premium plan # JobPost.objects enterprise # High quota limits
Example 2: Get free tier plan

Retrieve free tier usage plan.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up456free
Output: { "id": "up456free", "name": "anihpj-free-plan", "quota": { "limit": 1000, "period": "MONTH" }, "throttle": { "rateLimit": 10, "burstLimit": 20 } }
Django Context: # Free tier plan # Skills.objects basic # Limited access
Example 3: Get quota settings

Query quota configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --query 'quota'
Output: { "limit": 100000, "period": "MONTH", "offset": 0 }
Django Context: # Quota settings # User.objects limits # Monthly allowance
Example 4: Get throttle limits

Check throttling configuration.

#!/bin/bash
RATE=$(aws apigateway get-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --query 'throttle.rateLimit' --output text)

echo "Rate limit: $RATE requests/second"
Output: Rate limit: 1000 requests/second
Django Context: # Throttle limits # Author.objects rate # Performance caps
Example 5: Get associated stages

List API stages linked to plan.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --query 'apiStages'
Output: [ {"apiId": "abc123def4", "stage": "prod"}, {"apiId": "xyz789ghi0", "stage": "prod"} ]
Django Context: # Associated stages # Location.objects APIs # Multi-API plan

get-usage-plan-key

Retrieves a specific API key associated with a usage plan. Shows the key details and plan association.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-usage-plan-key \
    --usage-plan-id <value> \
    --key-id <value>
Parameters
  • --usage-plan-id (string): Usage plan identifier
  • --key-id (string): API key identifier
Return Value
  • id (string): API key identifier
  • type (string): Key type
  • name (string): Key name
  • value (string): API key value (if enabled)
Example 1: Get key details

Retrieve API key from usage plan.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan-key \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "type": "API_KEY", "name": "anihpj-premium-key-1" }
Django Context: # Key details # JobPost.objects API key # Premium access
Example 2: Get key name

Query just the key name.

#!/bin/bash
NAME=$(aws apigateway get-usage-plan-key \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --query 'name' --output text)

echo "Key name: $NAME"
Output: Key name: anihpj-premium-key-1
Django Context: # Key name # Skills.objects identifier # Customer reference
Example 3: Verify key type

Check the key type.

#!/bin/bash
TYPE=$(aws apigateway get-usage-plan-key \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --query 'type' --output text)

echo "Key type: $TYPE"
Output: Key type: API_KEY
Django Context: # Key type # User.objects auth type # Authentication method
Example 4: Get key in table format

Display key info as table.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan-key \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --output table
Output: ----------------------------------------- | GetUsagePlanKey | +----------+----------------------------+ | id | key123abc | | name | anihpj-premium-key-1 | | type | API_KEY | +----------+----------------------------+
Django Context: # Table format # Author.objects key # Formatted output
Example 5: Export key config

Save key configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan-key \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --output json > key-config.json

echo "Key configuration saved"
Output: Key configuration saved
Django Context: # Export config # Location.objects backup # Key documentation

get-usage-plan-keys

Retrieves all API keys associated with a usage plan. Lists all customers/applications using the plan.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-usage-plan-keys \
    --usage-plan-id <value> \
    [--position <value>] \
    [--limit <value>] \
    [--name-query <value>]
Parameters
  • --usage-plan-id (string): Usage plan identifier
  • --position (string): Pagination token
  • --limit (integer): Maximum results
  • --name-query (string): Filter by name pattern
Return Value
  • items (array): List of API keys
  • position (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List all plan keys

Get all API keys for usage plan.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan-keys \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "key123abc", "type": "API_KEY", "name": "anihpj-premium-key-1" }, { "id": "key456def", "type": "API_KEY", "name": "anihpj-premium-key-2" } ] }
Django Context: # All plan keys # JobPost.objects customers # Premium subscribers
Example 2: Filter keys by name

Search keys by name pattern.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan-keys \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --name-query "enterprise"
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "key789ent", "name": "anihpj-enterprise-key" } ] }
Django Context: # Name filter # Skills.objects search # Enterprise customers
Example 3: Get key names only

List just key names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan-keys \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --query 'items[].name' --output text
Output: anihpj-premium-key-1 anihpj-premium-key-2 anihpj-enterprise-key
Django Context: # Key names # User.objects list # Customer identifiers
Example 4: Count plan keys

Count total keys in plan.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-usage-plan-keys \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total keys: $COUNT"
Output: Total keys: 3
Django Context: # Key count # Author.objects count # Subscriber metrics
Example 5: List with pagination

Get keys with limit.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plan-keys \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --limit 10
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "key123abc", "name": "anihpj-premium-key-1"} ], "position": "nexttoken123" }
Django Context: # Paginated keys # Location.objects[:10] # Batch retrieval

get-usage-plans

Retrieves all usage plans in your account. Lists all pricing tiers and their configurations.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-usage-plans \
    [--position <value>] \
    [--limit <value>] \
    [--key-id <value>]
Parameters
  • --position (string): Pagination token
  • --limit (integer): Maximum results
  • --key-id (string): Filter by API key
Return Value
  • items (array): List of usage plans
  • position (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List all usage plans

Get all pricing tiers.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plans
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "up123free", "name": "anihpj-free-plan", "quota": {"limit": 1000, "period": "MONTH"} }, { "id": "up456basic", "name": "anihpj-basic-plan", "quota": {"limit": 10000, "period": "MONTH"} }, { "id": "up789premium", "name": "anihpj-premium-plan", "quota": {"limit": 100000, "period": "MONTH"} } ] }
Django Context: # All usage plans # JobPost.objects tiers # Pricing structure
Example 2: Find plans for API key

Get plans associated with specific key.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plans \
    --key-id key123abc
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "up789premium", "name": "anihpj-premium-plan" } ] }
Django Context: # Key's plans # Skills.objects subscription # User tier lookup
Example 3: Get plan names

List just plan names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plans \
    --query 'items[].name' --output text
Output: anihpj-free-plan anihpj-basic-plan anihpj-premium-plan
Django Context: # Plan names # User.objects tiers # Pricing options
Example 4: Count total plans

Count available plans.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-usage-plans \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total plans: $COUNT"
Output: Total plans: 3
Django Context: # Plan count # Author.objects tiers # Pricing levels
Example 5: Export plans config

Save all plans to file.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-usage-plans \
    --output json > usage-plans.json

echo "Usage plans exported"
Output: Usage plans exported
Django Context: # Export plans # Location.objects pricing # Configuration backup

get-vpc-link

Retrieves a specific VPC link for private API integrations. VPC links allow API Gateway to access resources in your VPC.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id <value>
Parameters
  • --vpc-link-id (string): VPC link identifier
Return Value
  • id (string): VPC link identifier
  • name (string): VPC link name
  • status (string): Link status
  • targetArns (array): Target NLB ARNs
Example 1: Get VPC link details

Retrieve VPC link configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "name": "anihpj-private-link", "description": "VPC link to private Django backend", "status": "AVAILABLE", "targetArns": [ "arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789012:loadbalancer/net/anihpj-nlb/abc123" ] }
Django Context: # VPC link details # JobPost.objects private access # Backend connectivity
Example 2: Check VPC link status

Verify link is available.

#!/bin/bash
STATUS=$(aws apigateway get-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --query 'status' --output text)

echo "VPC link status: $STATUS"
Output: VPC link status: AVAILABLE
Django Context: # Link status # Skills.objects connectivity # Health check
Example 3: Get target ARNs

Retrieve NLB targets.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --query 'targetArns'
Output: [ "arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789012:loadbalancer/net/anihpj-nlb/abc123" ]
Django Context: # Target ARNs # User.objects NLB # Load balancer config
Example 4: Get link name

Query VPC link name.

#!/bin/bash
NAME=$(aws apigateway get-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --query 'name' --output text)

echo "VPC link: $NAME"
Output: VPC link: anihpj-private-link
Django Context: # Link name # Author.objects identifier # Resource reference
Example 5: Export VPC link config

Save link configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --output json > vpc-link-config.json

echo "VPC link config saved"
Output: VPC link config saved
Django Context: # Export config # Location.objects backup # Infrastructure docs

get-vpc-links

Retrieves all VPC links in your account. Lists all private connectivity options for API Gateway.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-vpc-links \
    [--position <value>] \
    [--limit <value>]
Parameters
  • --position (string): Pagination token
  • --limit (integer): Maximum results
Return Value
  • items (array): List of VPC links
  • position (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List all VPC links

Get all VPC links.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-vpc-links
Output: { "items": [ { "id": "vpcl123abc", "name": "anihpj-private-link", "status": "AVAILABLE" }, { "id": "vpcl456def", "name": "anihpj-staging-link", "status": "AVAILABLE" } ] }
Django Context: # All VPC links # JobPost.objects private endpoints # Multi-environment
Example 2: Get available links

Filter for active links.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-vpc-links \
    --query "items[?status=='AVAILABLE']"
Output: [ {"id": "vpcl123abc", "name": "anihpj-private-link", "status": "AVAILABLE"}, {"id": "vpcl456def", "name": "anihpj-staging-link", "status": "AVAILABLE"} ]
Django Context: # Available links # Skills.objects active # Ready to use
Example 3: Get link names

List VPC link names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-vpc-links \
    --query 'items[].name' --output text
Output: anihpj-private-link anihpj-staging-link
