Django Context: # Link names # User.objects endpoints # Quick reference
Example 4: Count VPC links

Count total links.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-vpc-links \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total VPC links: $COUNT"
Output: Total VPC links: 2
Django Context: # Link count # Author.objects connections # Infrastructure inventory
Example 5: Export all links

Save links configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-vpc-links \
    --output json > vpc-links.json

echo "VPC links exported"
Output: VPC links exported
Django Context: # Export all # Location.objects backup # Network documentation

import-api-keys

Imports API keys from an external source file. Useful for bulk provisioning of API keys.

Syntax
aws apigateway import-api-keys \
    --body <value> \
    --format csv|yaml \
    [--fail-on-warnings | --no-fail-on-warnings]
Parameters
  • --body (blob): File containing keys to import
  • --format (string): File format (csv or yaml)
  • --fail-on-warnings (boolean): Fail on import warnings
Return Value
  • ids (array): Imported key IDs
  • warnings (array): Import warnings
Example 1: Import keys from CSV

Bulk import API keys from CSV file.

#!/bin/bash
cat > api-keys.csv << 'EOF'
Name,Key,UsagePlanIds
anihpj-customer-1,abc123def456,up123premium
anihpj-customer-2,xyz789ghi012,up456basic
EOF

aws apigateway import-api-keys \
    --body fileb://api-keys.csv \
    --format csv
Output: { "ids": [ "key123new1", "key456new2" ], "warnings": [] }
Django Context: # Bulk import keys # JobPost.objects customers # Mass provisioning
Example 2: Import with warnings check

Import and fail on warnings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway import-api-keys \
    --body fileb://api-keys.csv \
    --format csv \
    --fail-on-warnings
Output: { "ids": ["key789new3"], "warnings": [] }
Django Context: # Strict import # Skills.objects validation # Error handling
Example 3: Import from YAML

Import keys from YAML file.

#!/bin/bash
cat > api-keys.yaml << 'EOF'
- name: anihpj-enterprise-1
  key: ent123abc456
  usagePlanIds:
    - up789premium
EOF

aws apigateway import-api-keys \
    --body fileb://api-keys.yaml \
    --format yaml
Output: { "ids": ["key101ent"], "warnings": [] }
Django Context: # YAML import # User.objects enterprise # Structured data
Example 4: Import with plan association

Import keys and associate with plans.

#!/bin/bash
cat > bulk-keys.csv << 'EOF'
Name,Key,UsagePlanIds
anihpj-user-100,user100key,up123free
anihpj-user-101,user101key,up123free
anihpj-user-102,user102key,up456basic
EOF

aws apigateway import-api-keys \
    --body fileb://bulk-keys.csv \
    --format csv

echo "Bulk import complete"
Output: { "ids": ["key201", "key202", "key203"] } Bulk import complete
Django Context: # Bulk with plans # Author.objects onboarding # User provisioning
Example 5: Verify import results

Import and count results.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws apigateway import-api-keys \
    --body fileb://api-keys.csv \
    --format csv)

COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.ids | length')
echo "Imported $COUNT keys"
Output: Imported 3 keys
Django Context: # Verify import # Location.objects count # Success tracking

import-documentation-parts

Imports API documentation parts from an external file. Bulk imports documentation for API resources, methods, and models.

Syntax
aws apigateway import-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --body <value> \
    --mode merge|overwrite \
    [--fail-on-warnings | --no-fail-on-warnings]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --body (blob): Documentation file content
  • --mode (string): Import mode (merge or overwrite)
  • --fail-on-warnings (boolean): Fail on warnings
Return Value
  • ids (array): Imported documentation part IDs
  • warnings (array): Import warnings
Example 1: Import docs in merge mode

Import documentation while preserving existing.

#!/bin/bash
cat > api-docs.json << 'EOF'
{
  "documentationParts": [
    {
      "location": {"type": "API"},
      "properties": "{\"description\": \"JobPost Management API\"}"
    },
    {
      "location": {"type": "RESOURCE", "path": "/jobposts"},
      "properties": "{\"description\": \"JobPost collection endpoint\"}"
    }
  ]
}
EOF

aws apigateway import-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --body fileb://api-docs.json \
    --mode merge
Output: { "ids": ["doc123abc", "doc456def"], "warnings": [] }
Django Context: # Merge docs # JobPost.objects documentation # Preserve existing
Example 2: Overwrite all docs

Replace all existing documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway import-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --body fileb://complete-docs.json \
    --mode overwrite
Output: { "ids": ["doc001", "doc002", "doc003"], "warnings": [] }
Django Context: # Overwrite mode # Skills.objects full replace # Clean slate
Example 3: Import with warnings

Import and fail on any warnings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway import-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --body fileb://api-docs.json \
    --mode merge \
    --fail-on-warnings
Output: { "ids": ["doc789"], "warnings": [] }
Django Context: # Strict import # User.objects validation # Quality control
Example 4: Import model documentation

Import documentation for models.

#!/bin/bash
cat > model-docs.json << 'EOF'
{
  "documentationParts": [
    {
      "location": {"type": "MODEL", "name": "JobPost"},
      "properties": "{\"description\": \"JobPost model for job listings\"}"
    }
  ]
}
EOF

aws apigateway import-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --body fileb://model-docs.json \
    --mode merge
Output: { "ids": ["docmodel1"] }
Django Context: # Model docs # Author.objects schema docs # Type definitions
Example 5: Count imported parts

Import and verify count.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws apigateway import-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --body fileb://api-docs.json \
    --mode merge)

COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.ids | length')
echo "Imported $COUNT documentation parts"
Output: Imported 5 documentation parts
Django Context: # Count imported # Location.objects verify # Success tracking

import-rest-api

Creates a new REST API by importing an OpenAPI/Swagger definition. Allows API creation from standard specifications.

Syntax
aws apigateway import-rest-api \
    --body <value> \
    [--fail-on-warnings | --no-fail-on-warnings] \
    [--parameters <value>] \
    [--endpoint-configuration-types <value>]
Parameters
  • --body (blob): OpenAPI/Swagger specification
  • --fail-on-warnings (boolean): Fail on import warnings
  • --parameters (map): Import parameters
  • --endpoint-configuration-types (list): Endpoint types
Return Value
  • id (string): Created API identifier
  • name (string): API name
  • warnings (array): Import warnings
Example 1: Import OpenAPI 3.0 spec

Create API from OpenAPI specification.

#!/bin/bash
cat > jobpost-api.yaml << 'EOF'
openapi: "3.0.1"
info:
  title: "anihpj-jobpost-api"
  version: "1.0.0"
paths:
  /jobposts:
    get:
      summary: "List all job posts"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: "Success"
    post:
      summary: "Create job post"
      responses:
        "201":
          description: "Created"
EOF

aws apigateway import-rest-api \
    --body fileb://jobpost-api.yaml
Output: { "id": "newapi123", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api", "createdDate": "2025-01-17T10:00:00Z", "apiKeySource": "HEADER" }
Django Context: # Import OpenAPI # JobPost.objects API # Spec-based creation
Example 2: Import with regional endpoint

Create regional API from spec.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway import-rest-api \
    --body fileb://jobpost-api.yaml \
    --endpoint-configuration-types REGIONAL
Output: { "id": "regapi456", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api", "endpointConfiguration": { "types": ["REGIONAL"] } }
Django Context: # Regional endpoint # Skills.objects deployment # Low-latency regional
Example 3: Import with basepath override

Import with custom base path.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway import-rest-api \
    --body fileb://jobpost-api.yaml \
    --parameters basePath=ignore
Output: { "id": "api789base", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api" }
Django Context: # Base path override # User.objects routing # Path configuration
Example 4: Import Swagger 2.0

Import from Swagger specification.

#!/bin/bash
cat > swagger-api.json << 'EOF'
{
  "swagger": "2.0",
  "info": {"title": "anihpj-api", "version": "1.0"},
  "paths": {
    "/jobposts": {
      "get": {"summary": "List jobs"}
    }
  }
}
EOF

aws apigateway import-rest-api \
    --body fileb://swagger-api.json
Output: { "id": "swagger123", "name": "anihpj-api" }
Django Context: # Swagger import # Author.objects legacy # v2.0 compatibility
Example 5: Import with strict validation

Import and fail on any issues.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway import-rest-api \
    --body fileb://jobpost-api.yaml \
    --fail-on-warnings

echo "API imported successfully"
Output: { "id": "strict456" } API imported successfully
Django Context: # Strict import # Location.objects validation # Quality assurance

put-gateway-response

Creates or updates a gateway response for a specific response type. Customizes API Gateway error responses.

Syntax
aws apigateway put-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --response-type <value> \
    [--status-code <value>] \
    [--response-parameters <value>] \
    [--response-templates <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --response-type (string): Response type (e.g., DEFAULT_4XX, UNAUTHORIZED)
  • --status-code (string): HTTP status code
  • --response-parameters (map): Response headers
  • --response-templates (map): Response body templates
Return Value
  • responseType (string): Response type
  • statusCode (string): Status code
  • responseParameters (object): Headers
  • responseTemplates (object): Body templates
Example 1: Custom 401 response

Create custom unauthorized response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type UNAUTHORIZED \
    --status-code 401 \
    --response-templates '{"application/json": "{\"error\": \"Authentication required for JobPost access\"}"}'
Output: { "responseType": "UNAUTHORIZED", "statusCode": "401", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"error\": \"Authentication required for JobPost access\"}" } }
Django Context: # Custom 401 # JobPost.objects auth error # User-friendly message
Example 2: Custom 403 response

Create access denied response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type ACCESS_DENIED \
    --status-code 403 \
    --response-templates '{"application/json": "{\"error\": \"Access denied to JobPost resource\"}"}'
Output: { "responseType": "ACCESS_DENIED", "statusCode": "403" }
Django Context: # Custom 403 # Skills.objects permission # Authorization error
Example 3: Add CORS headers

Add CORS headers to error responses.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_4XX \
    --response-parameters '{"gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "'\''*'\''", "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "'\''*'\''"}'
Output: { "responseType": "DEFAULT_4XX", "responseParameters": { "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "'*'", "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "'*'" } }
Django Context: # CORS headers # User.objects cross-origin # Frontend access
Example 4: Custom 5XX response

Customize server error responses.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_5XX \
    --response-templates '{"application/json": "{\"error\": \"Internal server error\", \"requestId\": \"$context.requestId\"}"}'
Output: { "responseType": "DEFAULT_5XX", "statusCode": "500" }
Django Context: # Custom 5XX # Author.objects server error # Debug info included
Example 5: Rate limit response

Customize throttling response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type QUOTA_EXCEEDED \
    --status-code 429 \
    --response-templates '{"application/json": "{\"error\": \"Rate limit exceeded\", \"retryAfter\": 60}"}'
Output: { "responseType": "QUOTA_EXCEEDED", "statusCode": "429" }
Django Context: # Rate limit response # Location.objects throttle # Retry guidance

put-integration

Creates or updates an integration for an API method. Connects API methods to backend services like Lambda, HTTP, or AWS services.

Syntax
aws apigateway put-integration \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --type <value> \
    [--integration-http-method <value>] \
    [--uri <value>] \
    [--request-parameters <value>] \
    [--request-templates <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --type (string): Integration type (AWS, AWS_PROXY, HTTP, HTTP_PROXY, MOCK)
  • --integration-http-method (string): Backend HTTP method
  • --uri (string): Backend endpoint URI
Return Value
  • type (string): Integration type
  • httpMethod (string): Backend method
  • uri (string): Backend URI
Example 1: Lambda proxy integration

Connect to Lambda with proxy integration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --type AWS_PROXY \
    --integration-http-method POST \
    --uri "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:get-jobposts/invocations"
Output: { "type": "AWS_PROXY", "httpMethod": "POST", "uri": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:get-jobposts/invocations", "passthroughBehavior": "WHEN_NO_MATCH" }
Django Context: # Lambda proxy # JobPost.objects.all() via Lambda # Serverless backend
Example 2: HTTP integration

Connect to HTTP backend.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --type HTTP_PROXY \
    --integration-http-method GET \
    --uri "https://api.anihpj.com/jobposts"
Output: { "type": "HTTP_PROXY", "httpMethod": "GET", "uri": "https://api.anihpj.com/jobposts" }
Django Context: # HTTP proxy # Skills.objects Django backend # Direct passthrough
Example 3: Mock integration

Create mock response for testing.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --type MOCK \
    --request-templates '{"application/json": "{\"statusCode\": 200}"}'
Output: { "type": "MOCK", "requestTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"statusCode\": 200}" } }
Django Context: # Mock integration # User.objects testing # Development mode
Example 4: VPC link integration

Connect to private VPC endpoint.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --type HTTP_PROXY \
    --integration-http-method GET \
    --uri "http://internal-nlb.vpc.local:8000/jobposts" \
    --connection-type VPC_LINK \
    --connection-id vpcl123abc
Output: { "type": "HTTP_PROXY", "connectionType": "VPC_LINK", "connectionId": "vpcl123abc" }
Django Context: # VPC integration # Author.objects private # Internal backend
Example 5: DynamoDB integration

Direct integration with DynamoDB.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --type AWS \
    --integration-http-method POST \
    --uri "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:dynamodb:action/Scan" \
    --request-templates '{"application/json": "{\"TableName\": \"JobPosts\"}"}'
Output: { "type": "AWS", "uri": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:dynamodb:action/Scan" }
Django Context: # DynamoDB direct # Location.objects alternative # NoSQL integration

put-integration-response

Creates or updates an integration response for a method. Maps backend responses to API responses with transformations.

Syntax
aws apigateway put-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --status-code <value> \
    [--selection-pattern <value>] \
    [--response-parameters <value>] \
    [--response-templates <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --status-code (string): Status code
  • --selection-pattern (string): Regex pattern for response
  • --response-parameters (map): Response header mappings
  • --response-templates (map): Response body templates
Return Value
  • statusCode (string): Status code
  • selectionPattern (string): Pattern
  • responseParameters (object): Headers
  • responseTemplates (object): Templates
Example 1: Map 200 success response

Create success response mapping.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --response-templates '{"application/json": ""}'
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "" } }
Django Context: # 200 response # JobPost.objects.all() success # Pass-through mapping
Example 2: Map error response

Map backend errors to 400 status.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --status-code 400 \
    --selection-pattern ".*ValidationError.*" \
    --response-templates '{"application/json": "{\"error\": \"Invalid JobPost data\"}"}'
Output: { "statusCode": "400", "selectionPattern": ".*ValidationError.*" }
Django Context: # 400 error mapping # Skills.objects validation # Error transformation
Example 3: Add CORS headers

Include CORS headers in response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --response-parameters '{"method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "'\''*'\''"}'
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseParameters": { "method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "'*'" } }
Django Context: # CORS headers # User.objects cross-origin # Frontend access
Example 4: Transform response body

Apply VTL template to response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --response-templates '{"application/json": "#set($jobs = $input.path('\''$.Items'\''))\n{\"jobposts\": $jobs}"}'
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "#set($jobs = $input.path('$.Items'))\n{\"jobposts\": $jobs}" } }
Django Context: # VTL transformation # Author.objects formatting # Custom response shape
Example 5: Map 404 not found

Handle not found responses.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789id \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 404 \
    --selection-pattern ".*NotFound.*" \
    --response-templates '{"application/json": "{\"error\": \"JobPost not found\"}"}'
Output: { "statusCode": "404", "selectionPattern": ".*NotFound.*" }
Django Context: # 404 mapping # Location.objects.get() fail # DoesNotExist handling

put-method

Creates or updates a method for an API resource. Defines HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, DELETE on API endpoints.

Syntax
aws apigateway put-method \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --authorization-type <value> \
    [--authorizer-id <value>] \
    [--api-key-required | --no-api-key-required] \
    [--request-parameters <value>] \
    [--request-models <value>] \
    [--request-validator-id <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --authorization-type (string): Auth type (NONE, AWS_IAM, CUSTOM, COGNITO_USER_POOLS)
  • --api-key-required (boolean): Require API key
  • --request-parameters (map): Required parameters
  • --request-models (map): Request body models
Return Value
  • httpMethod (string): HTTP method
  • authorizationType (string): Auth type
  • apiKeyRequired (boolean): API key flag
Example 1: Create GET method

Add GET method for listing jobposts.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --authorization-type NONE
Output: { "httpMethod": "GET", "authorizationType": "NONE", "apiKeyRequired": false }
Django Context: # GET method # JobPost.objects.all() # Public access
Example 2: Create POST with IAM auth

Add POST method with IAM authorization.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --authorization-type AWS_IAM \
    --api-key-required
Output: { "httpMethod": "POST", "authorizationType": "AWS_IAM", "apiKeyRequired": true }
Django Context: # POST with auth # JobPost.objects.create() # Secured endpoint
Example 3: Create with Cognito auth

Add method with Cognito authorization.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method PUT \
    --authorization-type COGNITO_USER_POOLS \
    --authorizer-id auth123cognito
Output: { "httpMethod": "PUT", "authorizationType": "COGNITO_USER_POOLS", "authorizerId": "auth123cognito" }
Django Context: # Cognito auth # Skills.objects.update() # User pool protection
Example 4: Create with request validation

Add method with request validator.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --authorization-type AWS_IAM \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --request-models '{"application/json": "JobPost"}'
Output: { "httpMethod": "POST", "requestValidatorId": "rv123abc", "requestModels": { "application/json": "JobPost" } }
Django Context: # Request validation # User.objects create # Body schema check
Example 5: Create with path parameter

Add method requiring path parameter.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789id \
    --http-method GET \
    --authorization-type NONE \
    --request-parameters '{"method.request.path.id": true}'
Output: { "httpMethod": "GET", "requestParameters": { "method.request.path.id": true } }
Django Context: # Path parameter # Author.objects.get(pk=id) # Required param

put-method-response

Creates or updates a method response for an API method. Defines expected response status codes and headers.

Syntax
aws apigateway put-method-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --status-code <value> \
    [--response-parameters <value>] \
    [--response-models <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --status-code (string): Response status code
  • --response-parameters (map): Response headers
  • --response-models (map): Response body models
Return Value
  • statusCode (string): Status code
  • responseParameters (object): Headers
  • responseModels (object): Models
Example 1: Define 200 response

Add successful response definition.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --response-models '{"application/json": "JobPostList"}'
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseModels": { "application/json": "JobPostList" } }
Django Context: # 200 response # JobPost.objects.all() # List response model
Example 2: Define 201 created response

Add created response for POST.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --status-code 201 \
    --response-models '{"application/json": "JobPost"}'
Output: { "statusCode": "201", "responseModels": { "application/json": "JobPost" } }
Django Context: # 201 created # JobPost.objects.create() # Single object model
Example 3: Add CORS header response

Include CORS header in response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --response-parameters '{"method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": false}'
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseParameters": { "method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": false } }
Django Context: # CORS response # Skills.objects headers # Cross-origin support
Example 4: Define 400 error response

Add bad request response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --status-code 400 \
    --response-models '{"application/json": "Error"}'
Output: { "statusCode": "400", "responseModels": { "application/json": "Error" } }
Django Context: # 400 error # User.objects validation fail # Error model
Example 5: Define 404 not found

Add not found response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789id \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 404 \
    --response-models '{"application/json": "Error"}'
Output: { "statusCode": "404", "responseModels": { "application/json": "Error" } }
Django Context: # 404 not found # Author.objects DoesNotExist # Error response

put-rest-api

Updates an existing REST API by importing an OpenAPI definition. Replaces or merges with existing API configuration.

Syntax
aws apigateway put-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --body <value> \
    --mode overwrite|merge \
    [--fail-on-warnings | --no-fail-on-warnings] \
    [--parameters <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier to update
  • --body (blob): OpenAPI/Swagger specification
  • --mode (string): Update mode (overwrite or merge)
  • --fail-on-warnings (boolean): Fail on warnings
  • --parameters (map): Import parameters
Return Value
  • id (string): API identifier
  • name (string): API name
  • warnings (array): Import warnings
Example 1: Overwrite API from spec

Replace entire API with new spec.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --body fileb://updated-api.yaml \
    --mode overwrite
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api", "warnings": [] }
Django Context: # Overwrite API # JobPost.objects full replace # Clean update
Example 2: Merge changes

Merge new paths with existing API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --body fileb://new-paths.yaml \
    --mode merge
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api" }
Django Context: # Merge mode # Skills.objects add paths # Incremental update
Example 3: Update with validation

Update and fail on any warnings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --body fileb://updated-api.yaml \
    --mode overwrite \
    --fail-on-warnings
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "warnings": [] }
Django Context: # Strict update # User.objects validation # Quality control
Example 4: Update with basepath

Update with custom basepath handling.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway put-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --body fileb://updated-api.yaml \
    --mode merge \
    --parameters basePath=prepend
Output: { "id": "abc123def4" }
Django Context: # Basepath update # Author.objects routing # Path configuration
Example 5: Version update

Update API to new version.

#!/bin/bash
cat > api-v2.yaml << 'EOF'
openapi: "3.0.1"
info:
  title: "anihpj-jobpost-api"
  version: "2.0.0"
paths:
  /v2/jobposts:
    get:
      summary: "List jobs v2"
EOF

aws apigateway put-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --body fileb://api-v2.yaml \
    --mode merge

echo "API updated to v2"
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api" } API updated to v2
Django Context: # Version update # Location.objects v2 # API evolution

reject-domain-name-access-association

Rejects a domain name access association request. Denies cross-account domain name sharing.

Syntax
aws apigateway reject-domain-name-access-association \
    --domain-name-access-association-arn <value> \
    --domain-name-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --domain-name-access-association-arn (string): ARN of the access association to reject
  • --domain-name-arn (string): ARN of the domain name
Return Value

No return value on success.

Example 1: Reject access association

Deny a cross-account domain name request.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway reject-domain-name-access-association \
    --domain-name-access-association-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:123456789012:/domainnames/api.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc123 \
    --domain-name-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:123456789012:/domainnames/api.anihpj.com

echo "Access association rejected"
Output: Access association rejected
Django Context: # Reject association # JobPost.objects deny # Security boundary
Example 2: Reject with confirmation

Reject and confirm denial.

#!/bin/bash
DOMAIN_ARN="arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:123456789012:/domainnames/api.anihpj.com"
ASSOC_ARN="${DOMAIN_ARN}/accessassociations/assoc456"

aws apigateway reject-domain-name-access-association \
    --domain-name-access-association-arn $ASSOC_ARN \
    --domain-name-arn $DOMAIN_ARN

echo "Rejected cross-account access to $DOMAIN_ARN"
Output: Rejected cross-account access to arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:123456789012:/domainnames/api.anihpj.com
Django Context: # Reject confirmed # Skills.objects blocked # Access denied
Example 3: Reject unknown requester

Deny association from unrecognized account.

#!/bin/bash
# Reject request from unknown account
aws apigateway reject-domain-name-access-association \
    --domain-name-access-association-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:987654321098:/domainnames/api.anihpj.com/accessassociations/unknown123 \
    --domain-name-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:123456789012:/domainnames/api.anihpj.com

echo "Unknown requester rejected"
Output: Unknown requester rejected
Django Context: # Unknown rejected # User.objects unauthorized # Security enforcement
Example 4: Batch reject associations

Reject multiple pending associations.

#!/bin/bash
DOMAIN_ARN="arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:123456789012:/domainnames/api.anihpj.com"

for ASSOC_ID in assoc001 assoc002 assoc003; do
    aws apigateway reject-domain-name-access-association \
        --domain-name-access-association-arn "${DOMAIN_ARN}/accessassociations/${ASSOC_ID}" \
        --domain-name-arn $DOMAIN_ARN
    echo "Rejected: $ASSOC_ID"
done
Output: Rejected: assoc001 Rejected: assoc002 Rejected: assoc003
Django Context: # Batch reject # Author.objects cleanup # Bulk denial
Example 5: Reject with audit logging

Reject and log for audit purposes.

#!/bin/bash
DOMAIN_ARN="arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:123456789012:/domainnames/api.anihpj.com"
ASSOC_ARN="${DOMAIN_ARN}/accessassociations/assoc789"
TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

aws apigateway reject-domain-name-access-association \
    --domain-name-access-association-arn $ASSOC_ARN \
    --domain-name-arn $DOMAIN_ARN

echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Rejected association: $ASSOC_ARN" >> /var/log/apigateway-audit.log
echo "Rejection logged"
Output: Rejection logged
Django Context: # Audit logging # Location.objects compliance # Security tracking

tag-resource

Adds tags to an API Gateway resource. Tags are key-value pairs for organizing and tracking resources.

Syntax
aws apigateway tag-resource \
    --resource-arn <value> \
    --tags <value>
Parameters
  • --resource-arn (string): ARN of the resource to tag
  • --tags (map): Key-value pairs for tags
Return Value

No return value on success.

Example 1: Tag REST API

Add environment and project tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --tags Environment=production,Project=anihpj

echo "API tagged successfully"
Output: API tagged successfully
Django Context: # Tag REST API # JobPost.objects metadata # Resource organization
Example 2: Tag for cost tracking

Add cost center and team tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --tags CostCenter=engineering,Team=backend

echo "Cost tags added"
Output: Cost tags added
Django Context: # Cost tracking # Skills.objects billing # Department allocation
Example 3: Tag stage

Add tags to a deployment stage.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4/stages/prod \
    --tags Version=v2.1.0,DeployDate=2025-01-17

echo "Stage tagged"
Output: Stage tagged
Django Context: # Stage tags # User.objects version # Deployment tracking
Example 4: Add compliance tags

Tag for compliance requirements.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --tags DataClassification=internal,Compliance=SOC2

echo "Compliance tags added"
Output: Compliance tags added
Django Context: # Compliance tags # Author.objects security # Audit requirements
Example 5: Tag VPC link

Add tags to VPC link resource.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/vpclinks/vpcl123abc \
    --tags Purpose=private-backend,Network=internal

echo "VPC link tagged"
Output: VPC link tagged
Django Context: # VPC link tags # Location.objects network # Infrastructure labeling

test-invoke-authorizer

Tests an authorizer by simulating an authorization request. Validates custom authorizers before deployment.

Syntax
aws apigateway test-invoke-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --authorizer-id <value> \
    [--headers <value>] \
    [--path-with-query-string <value>] \
    [--body <value>] \
    [--stage-variables <value>] \
    [--additional-context <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --authorizer-id (string): Authorizer identifier
  • --headers (map): Request headers
  • --path-with-query-string (string): Request path
  • --body (string): Request body
  • --stage-variables (map): Stage variables
Return Value
  • clientStatus (integer): HTTP status code
  • log (string): Execution log
  • latency (long): Execution latency
  • principalId (string): Authenticated principal
  • policy (string): IAM policy generated
  • authorization (object): Authorization response
Example 1: Test token authorizer

Test Lambda authorizer with token.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --headers Authorization="Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..."
Output: { "clientStatus": 200, "log": "Authorizer execution logs...", "latency": 150, "principalId": "user123", "policy": "{\"Version\":\"2012-10-17\",\"Statement\":[{\"Effect\":\"Allow\",\"Action\":\"execute-api:Invoke\",\"Resource\":\"*\"}]}" }
Django Context: # Test authorizer # JobPost.objects auth check # Token validation
Example 2: Test with invalid token

Verify authorizer rejects bad tokens.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --headers Authorization="Bearer invalid-token"
Output: { "clientStatus": 401, "log": "Token validation failed", "latency": 50 }
Django Context: # Invalid token test # Skills.objects unauthorized # Rejection verification
Example 3: Test request authorizer

Test authorizer with request parameters.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth456request \
    --headers 'x-api-key=key123abc' \
    --path-with-query-string "/jobposts?user_id=123"
Output: { "clientStatus": 200, "principalId": "user123", "latency": 120 }
Django Context: # Request authorizer # User.objects.get(pk=123) # Multi-source auth
Example 4: Test with stage variables

Test authorizer with env variables.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --headers Authorization="Bearer testtoken" \
    --stage-variables environment=dev,authEndpoint=dev-auth.anihpj.com
Output: { "clientStatus": 200, "principalId": "dev-user", "latency": 100 }
Django Context: # Stage variables test # Author.objects dev env # Environment-specific auth
Example 5: Check authorization policy

Verify generated IAM policy.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws apigateway test-invoke-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --headers Authorization="Bearer validtoken")

echo "$RESULT" | jq '.policy' | jq -r '.' | jq .
Output: { "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Allow", "Action": "execute-api:Invoke", "Resource": "arn:aws:execute-api:us-east-1:123456789012:abc123def4/*/GET/jobposts" } ] }
Django Context: # Policy verification # Location.objects permissions # IAM policy check

test-invoke-method

Tests an API method by simulating a request. Validates method configuration before deployment.

Syntax
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    [--path-with-query-string <value>] \
    [--body <value>] \
    [--headers <value>] \
    [--stage-variables <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method to test
  • --path-with-query-string (string): Path with query params
  • --body (string): Request body
  • --headers (map): Request headers
Return Value
  • status (integer): HTTP status code
  • body (string): Response body
  • headers (object): Response headers
  • latency (long): Execution latency
  • log (string): Execution log
Example 1: Test GET method

Test listing jobposts endpoint.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET
Output: { "status": 200, "body": "[{\"id\": 1, \"title\": \"Django Developer\"}, {\"id\": 2, \"title\": \"Python Engineer\"}]", "headers": { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, "latency": 250, "log": "Execution log..." }
Django Context: # Test GET # JobPost.objects.all() # List endpoint test
Example 2: Test POST method

Test creating a jobpost.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --body '{"title": "New Django Job", "skills": ["Python", "Django"]}' \
    --headers Content-Type=application/json
Output: { "status": 201, "body": "{\"id\": 3, \"title\": \"New Django Job\", \"created\": true}", "latency": 300 }
Django Context: # Test POST # JobPost.objects.create() # Create endpoint test
Example 3: Test with path parameter

Test getting single jobpost by ID.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789id \
    --http-method GET \
    --path-with-query-string "/jobposts/123"
Output: { "status": 200, "body": "{\"id\": 123, \"title\": \"Senior Python Developer\"}", "latency": 150 }
Django Context: # Test with ID # JobPost.objects.get(pk=123) # Detail endpoint test
Example 4: Test with query parameters

Test filtering with query params.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --path-with-query-string "/jobposts?skill=Django&location=NYC"
Output: { "status": 200, "body": "[{\"id\": 1, \"title\": \"Django Developer NYC\"}]", "latency": 200 }
Django Context: # Query params test # Skills.objects.filter() # Location filtering
Example 5: Test with stage variables

Test method with environment config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --stage-variables environment=staging,dbHost=staging-db.anihpj.com
Output: { "status": 200, "body": "[{\"source\": \"staging\"}]", "latency": 180 }
Django Context: # Stage variables test # Author.objects staging # Environment config

untag-resource

Removes tags from an API Gateway resource. Deletes specified tag keys from the resource.

Syntax
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn <value> \
    --tag-keys <value>
Parameters
  • --resource-arn (string): ARN of the resource
  • --tag-keys (list): List of tag keys to remove
Return Value

No return value on success.

Example 1: Remove single tag

Remove environment tag from API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --tag-keys Environment

echo "Environment tag removed"
Output: Environment tag removed
Django Context: # Remove tag # JobPost.objects cleanup # Tag management
Example 2: Remove multiple tags

Remove several tags at once.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --tag-keys Environment Project Team

echo "Multiple tags removed"
Output: Multiple tags removed
Django Context: # Remove multiple # Skills.objects batch # Bulk cleanup
Example 3: Remove stage tags

Remove tags from deployment stage.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4/stages/prod \
    --tag-keys Version DeployDate

echo "Stage tags removed"
Output: Stage tags removed
Django Context: # Stage untag # User.objects cleanup # Deployment metadata
Example 4: Remove deprecated tags

Clean up old or invalid tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --tag-keys OldTag DeprecatedTag LegacyProject

echo "Deprecated tags cleaned up"
Output: Deprecated tags cleaned up
Django Context: # Cleanup deprecated # Author.objects maintenance # Tag housekeeping
Example 5: Remove VPC link tags

Remove tags from VPC link.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/vpclinks/vpcl123abc \
    --tag-keys Purpose Network

echo "VPC link tags removed"
Output: VPC link tags removed
Django Context: # VPC link untag # Location.objects cleanup # Network metadata

update-account

Updates the API Gateway account settings. Configures CloudWatch logging role and other account-level settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --patch-operations (list): List of patch operations to apply
Return Value
  • cloudwatchRoleArn (string): CloudWatch IAM role ARN
  • throttleSettings (object): Account throttle settings
  • features (array): Enabled features
Example 1: Set CloudWatch role

Configure logging role for API Gateway.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/cloudwatchRoleArn,value=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/api-gateway-cloudwatch-role
Output: { "cloudwatchRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/api-gateway-cloudwatch-role", "throttleSettings": { "rateLimit": 10000.0, "burstLimit": 5000 } }
Django Context: # CloudWatch role # JobPost.objects logging # Account configuration
Example 2: Remove CloudWatch role

Disable CloudWatch logging.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/cloudwatchRoleArn,value=

echo "CloudWatch logging disabled"
Output: { "cloudwatchRoleArn": "" } CloudWatch logging disabled
Django Context: # Remove logging # Skills.objects disable # Cost optimization
Example 3: Check current settings

Update and verify account settings.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/cloudwatchRoleArn,value=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/api-logging)

echo "$RESULT" | jq '.cloudwatchRoleArn'
Output: "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/api-logging"
Django Context: # Verify settings # User.objects config # Account validation
Example 4: Update with new role

Replace existing role with new one.

#!/bin/bash
NEW_ROLE="arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/enhanced-api-logging"

aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations "op=replace,path=/cloudwatchRoleArn,value=$NEW_ROLE"

echo "Logging role updated"
Output: { "cloudwatchRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/enhanced-api-logging" } Logging role updated
Django Context: # Role replacement # Author.objects logging # Enhanced monitoring
Example 5: View throttle settings

Get account throttle configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/cloudwatchRoleArn,value=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/api-role \
    --query 'throttleSettings'
Output: { "rateLimit": 10000.0, "burstLimit": 5000 }
Django Context: # Throttle settings # Location.objects limits # Account quotas

update-api-key

Updates an existing API key. Modifies key properties like name, description, and enabled status.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --api-key (string): API key identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations to apply
Return Value
  • id (string): API key identifier
  • name (string): Updated name
  • enabled (boolean): Enabled status
  • description (string): Updated description
Example 1: Update key name

Rename an API key.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-premium-customer-key
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "name": "anihpj-premium-customer-key", "enabled": true }
Django Context: # Rename key # JobPost.objects customer # Key management
Example 2: Disable API key

Temporarily disable a key.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/enabled,value=false
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "enabled": false }
Django Context: # Disable key # Skills.objects revoke # Access control
Example 3: Enable API key

Re-enable a disabled key.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/enabled,value=true

echo "API key re-enabled"
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "enabled": true } API key re-enabled
Django Context: # Enable key # User.objects activate # Restore access
Example 4: Update description

Change key description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Enterprise customer key for JobPost API"
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "description": "Enterprise customer key for JobPost API" }
Django Context: # Update description # Author.objects metadata # Documentation
Example 5: Multiple updates

Update name and description together.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key key123abc \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/name,value=enterprise-key \
        op=replace,path=/description,value="Updated enterprise key"
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "name": "enterprise-key", "description": "Updated enterprise key" }
Django Context: # Multiple updates # Location.objects batch # Combined operations

update-authorizer

Updates an existing authorizer. Modifies Lambda authorizer or Cognito settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --authorizer-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --authorizer-id (string): Authorizer identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Authorizer identifier
  • name (string): Updated name
  • authorizerResultTtlInSeconds (integer): Cache TTL
Example 1: Update cache TTL

Increase authorization cache time.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/authorizerResultTtlInSeconds,value=600
Output: { "id": "auth123lambda", "name": "anihpj-jwt-authorizer", "authorizerResultTtlInSeconds": 600 }
Django Context: # Update cache TTL # JobPost.objects auth cache # Performance optimization
Example 2: Update Lambda function

Change authorizer Lambda ARN.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/authorizerUri,value=arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:new-authorizer/invocations
Output: { "id": "auth123lambda", "authorizerUri": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/..." }
Django Context: # Update Lambda # Skills.objects new auth # Function migration
Example 3: Rename authorizer

Change authorizer name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-enhanced-authorizer
Output: { "id": "auth123lambda", "name": "anihpj-enhanced-authorizer" }
Django Context: # Rename authorizer # User.objects auth name # Documentation update
Example 4: Update identity source

Change token extraction header.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/identitySource,value=method.request.header.X-Auth-Token
Output: { "id": "auth123lambda", "identitySource": "method.request.header.X-Auth-Token" }
Django Context: # Identity source # Author.objects header # Token location
Example 5: Disable caching

Set TTL to 0 to disable caching.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/authorizerResultTtlInSeconds,value=0

echo "Authorizer caching disabled"
Output: { "id": "auth123lambda", "authorizerResultTtlInSeconds": 0 } Authorizer caching disabled
Django Context: # Disable cache # Location.objects real-time # Always authenticate

update-base-path-mapping

Updates a base path mapping for a custom domain. Changes the API and stage association.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name <value> \
    --base-path <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --domain-name (string): Custom domain name
  • --base-path (string): Base path (use "(none)" for empty)
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • basePath (string): Base path
  • restApiId (string): REST API ID
  • stage (string): Stage name
Example 1: Change stage

Update mapping to new stage.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path v1 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/stage,value=prod
Output: { "basePath": "v1", "restApiId": "abc123def4", "stage": "prod" }
Django Context: # Change stage # JobPost.objects production # Deployment update
Example 2: Change API

Point mapping to different API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path v1 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/restApiId,value=newapi456
Output: { "basePath": "v1", "restApiId": "newapi456", "stage": "prod" }
Django Context: # Change API # Skills.objects new backend # API migration
Example 3: Update root mapping

Update mapping without base path.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path "(none)" \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/stage,value=v2-prod
Output: { "basePath": "(none)", "restApiId": "abc123def4", "stage": "v2-prod" }
Django Context: # Root mapping # User.objects root path # Domain root
Example 4: Update both API and stage

Change API and stage together.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path v2 \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/restApiId,value=apiV2123 \
        op=replace,path=/stage,value=prod
Output: { "basePath": "v2", "restApiId": "apiV2123", "stage": "prod" }
Django Context: # Update both # Author.objects v2 migration # Complete update
Example 5: Staging to production

Promote staging to production.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path jobposts \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/stage,value=prod

echo "Promoted to production"
Output: { "basePath": "jobposts", "stage": "prod" } Promoted to production
Django Context: # Stage promotion # Location.objects live # Production deployment

update-client-certificate

Updates an existing client certificate. Modifies certificate description and metadata.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --client-certificate-id (string): Certificate identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • clientCertificateId (string): Certificate ID
  • description (string): Updated description
  • pemEncodedCertificate (string): Certificate content
Example 1: Update description

Change certificate description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Production backend certificate for JobPost API"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123abc", "description": "Production backend certificate for JobPost API", "expirationDate": "2026-01-17T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Update description # JobPost.objects cert # Certificate metadata
Example 2: Add environment info

Update description with environment.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="[PROD] Django backend mTLS cert"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123abc", "description": "[PROD] Django backend mTLS cert" }
Django Context: # Environment tag # Skills.objects production # Environment labeling
Example 3: Clear description

Remove certificate description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value=""

echo "Description cleared"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123abc", "description": "" } Description cleared
Django Context: # Clear description # User.objects cleanup # Metadata removal
Example 4: Update with timestamp

Add last updated info to description.

#!/bin/bash
TIMESTAMP=$(date -I)
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123abc \
    --patch-operations "op=replace,path=/description,value=Updated: $TIMESTAMP"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123abc", "description": "Updated: 2025-01-17" }
Django Context: # Timestamp update # Author.objects date # Audit trail
Example 5: Add contact info

Include owner contact in description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Owner: backend-team@anihpj.com | Purpose: mTLS"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123abc", "description": "Owner: backend-team@anihpj.com | Purpose: mTLS" }
Django Context: # Contact info # Location.objects owner # Documentation

update-deployment

Updates an existing deployment. Modifies deployment description metadata.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --deployment-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --deployment-id (string): Deployment identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Deployment ID
  • description (string): Updated description
  • createdDate (timestamp): Creation date
Example 1: Update deployment description

Add release notes to deployment.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Release v2.1.0 - Added Skills filtering"
Output: { "id": "dep123abc", "description": "Release v2.1.0 - Added Skills filtering", "createdDate": "2025-01-17T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Update description # JobPost.objects release # Release notes
Example 2: Add version tag

Tag deployment with version.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="v2.1.0"
Output: { "id": "dep123abc", "description": "v2.1.0" }
Django Context: # Version tag # Skills.objects version # Deployment labeling
Example 3: Add timestamp

Include deployment timestamp.

#!/bin/bash
TIMESTAMP=$(date -Iseconds)
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep123abc \
    --patch-operations "op=replace,path=/description,value=Deployed: $TIMESTAMP"
Output: { "id": "dep123abc", "description": "Deployed: 2025-01-17T10:30:00+00:00" }
Django Context: # Timestamp # User.objects deploy time # Audit trail
Example 4: Add rollback marker

Mark deployment as rollback target.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep789stable \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="[STABLE] Last known good - safe for rollback"
Output: { "id": "dep789stable", "description": "[STABLE] Last known good - safe for rollback" }
Django Context: # Rollback marker # Author.objects stable # Recovery point
Example 5: Clear description

Remove deployment description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value=""

echo "Deployment description cleared"
Output: { "id": "dep123abc", "description": "" } Deployment description cleared
Django Context: # Clear description # Location.objects cleanup # Metadata removal

update-documentation-part

Updates an existing documentation part. Modifies API documentation content and properties.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --documentation-part-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --documentation-part-id (string): Documentation part ID
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Documentation part ID
  • location (object): Location specifier
  • properties (string): Updated documentation content
Example 1: Update API description

Change API-level documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc123api \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/properties,value="{\"description\": \"JobPost Management API v2.0\"}"'
Output: { "id": "doc123api", "location": {"type": "API"}, "properties": "{\"description\": \"JobPost Management API v2.0\"}" }
Django Context: # Update API docs # JobPost.objects description # API-level documentation
Example 2: Update resource docs

Change resource documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc456res \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/properties,value="{\"description\": \"JobPost collection with Skills filtering\"}"'
Output: { "id": "doc456res", "location": {"type": "RESOURCE", "path": "/jobposts"}, "properties": "{\"description\": \"JobPost collection with Skills filtering\"}" }
Django Context: # Resource docs # Skills.objects endpoint # Path documentation
Example 3: Update method docs

Change method documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc789method \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/properties,value="{\"summary\": \"Create new JobPost\", \"description\": \"Creates a JobPost with required Skills\"}"'
Output: { "id": "doc789method", "location": {"type": "METHOD", "path": "/jobposts", "method": "POST"}, "properties": "{\"summary\": \"Create new JobPost\"}" }
Django Context: # Method docs # User.objects POST # Operation documentation
Example 4: Update parameter docs

Change query parameter documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc101param \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/properties,value="{\"description\": \"Filter by skill name (e.g., Python, Django)\"}"'
Output: { "id": "doc101param", "location": {"type": "QUERY_PARAMETER", "name": "skill"}, "properties": "{\"description\": \"Filter by skill name\"}" }
Django Context: # Parameter docs # Author.objects query # Query parameter help
Example 5: Update model docs

Change model documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc202model \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/properties,value="{\"description\": \"JobPost model representing a job listing\"}"'
Output: { "id": "doc202model", "location": {"type": "MODEL", "name": "JobPost"} }
Django Context: # Model docs # Location.objects schema # Data model documentation

update-documentation-version

Updates a documentation version. Modifies version description metadata.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --documentation-version <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --documentation-version (string): Version identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • version (string): Version identifier
  • description (string): Updated description
  • createdDate (timestamp): Creation date
Example 1: Update version description

Add release notes to version.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-version 2.1.0 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Added Skills filtering documentation"
Output: { "version": "2.1.0", "description": "Added Skills filtering documentation", "createdDate": "2025-01-17T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Version description # JobPost.objects docs # Release documentation
Example 2: Mark as current

Tag version as current release.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-version 2.1.0 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="[CURRENT] Production documentation"
Output: { "version": "2.1.0", "description": "[CURRENT] Production documentation" }
Django Context: # Current marker # Skills.objects active # Version tagging
Example 3: Add change summary

Include changes in description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-version 2.0.0 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Major: New User endpoints, Updated JobPost schema"
Output: { "version": "2.0.0", "description": "Major: New User endpoints, Updated JobPost schema" }
Django Context: # Change summary # User.objects changes # Changelog
Example 4: Mark deprecated

Tag version as deprecated.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-version 1.0.0 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="[DEPRECATED] Use v2.x - EOL: 2025-06-01"
Output: { "version": "1.0.0", "description": "[DEPRECATED] Use v2.x - EOL: 2025-06-01" }
Django Context: # Deprecation marker # Author.objects EOL # Version lifecycle
Example 5: Clear description

Remove version description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-version 2.1.0 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value=""

echo "Description cleared"
Output: { "version": "2.1.0", "description": "" } Description cleared
Django Context: # Clear description # Location.objects cleanup # Metadata removal

update-domain-name

Updates a custom domain name configuration. Modifies certificate, security policy, and other settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --domain-name (string): Custom domain name
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • domainName (string): Domain name
  • certificateArn (string): ACM certificate ARN
  • securityPolicy (string): TLS version policy
Example 1: Update certificate

Change ACM certificate for domain.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/certificateArn,value=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/new-cert-id
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "certificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/new-cert-id", "distributionDomainName": "d123abc.cloudfront.net" }
Django Context: # Update certificate # JobPost.objects SSL # Certificate rotation
Example 2: Update security policy

Upgrade TLS version policy.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/securityPolicy,value=TLS_1_2
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "securityPolicy": "TLS_1_2" }
Django Context: # Security policy # Skills.objects TLS # Security upgrade
Example 3: Update regional certificate

Change regional endpoint certificate.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/regionalCertificateArn,value=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/regional-cert
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "regionalCertificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/regional-cert" }
Django Context: # Regional cert # User.objects endpoint # Regional SSL
Example 4: Update mutual TLS

Configure mTLS settings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/mutualTlsAuthentication/truststoreUri,value=s3://anihpj-certs/truststore.pem
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "mutualTlsAuthentication": { "truststoreUri": "s3://anihpj-certs/truststore.pem" } }
Django Context: # mTLS update # Author.objects auth # Client certificates
Example 5: Enable ownership verification

Update domain ownership settings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/ownershipVerificationCertificateArn,value=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/ownership-cert
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "ownershipVerificationCertificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/ownership-cert" }
Django Context: # Ownership verification # Location.objects domain # Domain validation

update-gateway-response

Updates a gateway response configuration. Modifies error response templates and headers.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --response-type <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --response-type (string): Response type to update
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • responseType (string): Response type
  • statusCode (string): Status code
  • responseTemplates (object): Updated templates
Example 1: Update 401 message

Change unauthorized error message.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type UNAUTHORIZED \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/responseTemplates/application~1json,value="{\"error\": \"Authentication required for JobPost API\"}"'
Output: { "responseType": "UNAUTHORIZED", "statusCode": "401", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"error\": \"Authentication required for JobPost API\"}" } }
Django Context: # Update 401 # JobPost.objects auth error # Error message update
Example 2: Add CORS header

Add CORS header to error response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_4XX \
    --patch-operations "op=add,path=/responseParameters/gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin,value='*'"
Output: { "responseType": "DEFAULT_4XX", "responseParameters": { "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "'*'" } }
Django Context: # Add CORS # Skills.objects cross-origin # Error CORS headers
Example 3: Update rate limit message

Change quota exceeded message.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type QUOTA_EXCEEDED \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/responseTemplates/application~1json,value="{\"error\": \"Rate limit exceeded\", \"retryAfter\": 60}"'
Output: { "responseType": "QUOTA_EXCEEDED", "statusCode": "429" }
Django Context: # Rate limit message # User.objects throttle # Retry guidance
Example 4: Update status code

Change response status code.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type BAD_REQUEST_BODY \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/statusCode,value=422
Output: { "responseType": "BAD_REQUEST_BODY", "statusCode": "422" }
Django Context: # Status code update # Author.objects validation # HTTP code change
Example 5: Remove header

Remove response header.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_5XX \
    --patch-operations op=remove,path=/responseParameters/gatewayresponse.header.X-Debug
Output: { "responseType": "DEFAULT_5XX", "responseParameters": {} }
Django Context: # Remove header # Location.objects cleanup # Header removal

update-integration

Updates an integration configuration. Modifies backend connection settings and request mappings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • type (string): Integration type
  • uri (string): Backend URI
  • httpMethod (string): Backend method
Example 1: Update Lambda function

Change Lambda backend.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/uri,value="arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:new-get-jobposts/invocations"
Output: { "type": "AWS_PROXY", "uri": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/..." }
Django Context: # Update Lambda # JobPost.objects new function # Backend migration
Example 2: Update timeout

Change integration timeout.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/timeoutInMillis,value=15000
Output: { "type": "AWS_PROXY", "timeoutInMillis": 15000 }
Django Context: # Update timeout # Skills.objects longer wait # Performance tuning
Example 3: Update HTTP endpoint

Change HTTP backend URL.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/uri,value="https://api-v2.anihpj.com/jobposts"
Output: { "type": "HTTP_PROXY", "uri": "https://api-v2.anihpj.com/jobposts" }
Django Context: # HTTP endpoint # User.objects new backend # URL update
Example 4: Add request parameter

Add parameter mapping.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations "op=add,path=/requestParameters/integration.request.querystring.api_version,value='method.request.header.X-API-Version'"
Output: { "requestParameters": { "integration.request.querystring.api_version": "method.request.header.X-API-Version" } }
Django Context: # Parameter mapping # Author.objects headers # Request transformation
Example 5: Update VPC link

Change VPC link connection.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/connectionId,value=vpcl456new
Output: { "connectionType": "VPC_LINK", "connectionId": "vpcl456new" }
Django Context: # VPC link update # Location.objects connection # Network change

update-integration-response

Updates an integration response. Modifies response mappings and transformations.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --status-code <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --status-code (string): Status code
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • statusCode (string): Status code
  • responseTemplates (object): Updated templates
  • responseParameters (object): Updated headers
Example 1: Update response template

Change response body transformation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/responseTemplates/application~1json,value="{\"jobs\": $input.json('\''$.Items'\'')}"'
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"jobs\": $input.json('$.Items')}" } }
Django Context: # Response template # JobPost.objects transform # Body mapping
Example 2: Add header mapping

Add response header.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations "op=add,path=/responseParameters/method.response.header.X-Total-Count,value='integration.response.header.X-Total-Count'"
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseParameters": { "method.response.header.X-Total-Count": "integration.response.header.X-Total-Count" } }
Django Context: # Header mapping # Skills.objects count # Pagination header
Example 3: Update selection pattern

Change error pattern matching.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 404 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/selectionPattern,value=".*NotFound.*|.*DoesNotExist.*"
Output: { "statusCode": "404", "selectionPattern": ".*NotFound.*|.*DoesNotExist.*" }
Django Context: # Selection pattern # User.objects error match # Django exception handling
Example 4: Remove header

Remove mapped header.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations op=remove,path=/responseParameters/method.response.header.X-Debug
Output: { "statusCode": "200" }
Django Context: # Remove header # Author.objects cleanup # Header removal
Example 5: Clear template

Set empty pass-through template.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/responseTemplates/application~1json,value=""'
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "" } }
Django Context: # Pass-through # Location.objects direct # No transformation

update-method

Updates an API method configuration. Modifies authorization, parameters, and request settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-method \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • httpMethod (string): HTTP method
  • authorizationType (string): Updated auth type
  • apiKeyRequired (boolean): API key setting
Example 1: Enable API key requirement

Require API key for method.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/apiKeyRequired,value=true
Output: { "httpMethod": "GET", "authorizationType": "NONE", "apiKeyRequired": true }
Django Context: # Require API key # JobPost.objects auth # Access control
Example 2: Change authorization type

Switch to IAM authorization.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/authorizationType,value=AWS_IAM
Output: { "httpMethod": "POST", "authorizationType": "AWS_IAM" }
Django Context: # IAM authorization # Skills.objects secure # Auth upgrade
Example 3: Add request validator

Attach request validator to method.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/requestValidatorId,value=rv123abc
Output: { "httpMethod": "POST", "requestValidatorId": "rv123abc" }
Django Context: # Request validator # User.objects validation # Input checking
Example 4: Update authorizer

Change custom authorizer.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/authorizationType,value=CUSTOM \
        op=replace,path=/authorizerId,value=auth456new
Output: { "httpMethod": "GET", "authorizationType": "CUSTOM", "authorizerId": "auth456new" }
Django Context: # Custom authorizer # Author.objects Lambda auth # Authorization update
Example 5: Add required parameter

Make query parameter required.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/requestParameters/method.request.querystring.skill,value=true
Output: { "httpMethod": "GET", "requestParameters": { "method.request.querystring.skill": true } }
Django Context: # Required parameter # Location.objects filter # Query validation

update-method-response

Updates a method response configuration. Modifies response models and headers.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-method-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --status-code <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
