  • --status-code (string): Status code
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • statusCode (string): Status code
  • responseModels (object): Updated models
  • responseParameters (object): Updated headers
Example 1: Update response model

Change response body model.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/responseModels/application~1json,value=JobPostListV2'
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseModels": { "application/json": "JobPostListV2" } }
Django Context: # Update model # JobPost.objects schema # Response format
Example 2: Add header definition

Define new response header.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/responseParameters/method.response.header.X-Total-Count,value=false
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseParameters": { "method.response.header.X-Total-Count": false } }
Django Context: # Add header # Skills.objects pagination # Count header

Define CORS response header.

    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/responseParameters/method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin,value=false

Example 4: Remove header

Remove response header definition.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations op=remove,path=/responseParameters/method.response.header.X-Debug
Output: { "statusCode": "200" }

Example 5: Clear model

Remove response model.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations op=remove,path=/responseModels/application~1json
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseModels": {} }
Django Context: # Clear model # Location.objects flexible # Schema removal

update-model

Updates a model definition. Modifies schema and description.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --model-name <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --model-name (string): Model name
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Model identifier
  • name (string): Model name
  • schema (string): Updated schema
  • description (string): Updated description
Example 1: Update model schema

Change model JSON schema.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/schema,value="{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"title\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"skills\":{\"type\":\"array\"}}}"'
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost", "schema": "{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"title\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"skills\":{\"type\":\"array\"}}}" }
Django Context: # Update schema # JobPost.objects model # Schema evolution
Example 2: Update description

Change model description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="JobPost model with Skills array support"
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost", "description": "JobPost model with Skills array support" }
Django Context: # Update description # Skills.objects docs # Documentation update
Example 3: Add required field

Update schema with required fields.

#!/bin/bash
SCHEMA='{"type":"object","required":["title","skills"],"properties":{"title":{"type":"string"},"skills":{"type":"array"}}}'

aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --patch-operations "op=replace,path=/schema,value='$SCHEMA'"
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost" }
Django Context: # Required fields # User.objects validation # Schema constraints
Example 4: Update content type

Change model content type.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/contentType,value=application/json
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost", "contentType": "application/json" }
Django Context: # Content type # Author.objects format # Media type
Example 5: Clear description

Remove model description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value=""

echo "Model description cleared"
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost", "description": "" } Model description cleared
Django Context: # Clear description # Location.objects cleanup # Metadata removal

update-request-validator

Updates a request validator configuration. Modifies validation settings for request body and parameters.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --request-validator-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --request-validator-id (string): Validator identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Validator identifier
  • name (string): Validator name
  • validateRequestBody (boolean): Body validation
  • validateRequestParameters (boolean): Parameter validation
Example 1: Enable body validation

Turn on request body validation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/validateRequestBody,value=true
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "name": "jobpost-validator", "validateRequestBody": true, "validateRequestParameters": false }
Django Context: # Enable body validation # JobPost.objects schema check # Request validation
Example 2: Enable parameter validation

Turn on parameter validation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/validateRequestParameters,value=true
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "validateRequestParameters": true }
Django Context: # Parameter validation # Skills.objects query # Input checking
Example 3: Enable full validation

Enable both body and parameter validation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/validateRequestBody,value=true \
        op=replace,path=/validateRequestParameters,value=true
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "validateRequestBody": true, "validateRequestParameters": true }
Django Context: # Full validation # User.objects complete # Comprehensive check
Example 4: Rename validator

Change validator name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=jobpost-full-validator
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "name": "jobpost-full-validator" }
Django Context: # Rename validator # Author.objects naming # Documentation
Example 5: Disable validation

Turn off all validation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/validateRequestBody,value=false \
        op=replace,path=/validateRequestParameters,value=false

echo "Validation disabled"
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "validateRequestBody": false, "validateRequestParameters": false } Validation disabled
Django Context: # Disable validation # Location.objects pass-through # Development mode

update-resource

Updates a resource configuration. Modifies resource path and parent relationships.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Resource identifier
  • parentId (string): Parent resource ID
  • pathPart (string): Path segment
  • path (string): Full path
Example 1: Update path part

Change resource path segment.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/pathPart,value=jobs
Output: { "id": "res123abc", "parentId": "rootres", "pathPart": "jobs", "path": "/jobs" }
Django Context: # Update path # JobPost.objects endpoint # URL change
Example 2: Rename to v2 path

Update resource for API versioning.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/pathPart,value=v2-jobposts
Output: { "id": "res123abc", "pathPart": "v2-jobposts", "path": "/v2-jobposts" }
Django Context: # Version path # Skills.objects v2 # API versioning
Example 3: Update parameter path

Change path parameter name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789id \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/pathPart,value='{jobId}'
Output: { "id": "res789id", "pathPart": "{jobId}", "path": "/jobposts/{jobId}" }
Django Context: # Parameter rename # User.objects.get(pk=jobId) # Path variable
Example 4: Move resource

Change parent resource.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res456skills \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/parentId,value=resV2
Output: { "id": "res456skills", "parentId": "resV2", "path": "/v2/skills" }
Django Context: # Move resource # Author.objects reorganize # API restructure
Example 5: Verify update

Update and verify new path.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/pathPart,value=listings)

echo "New path: $(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.path')"
Output: New path: /listings
Django Context: # Verify path # Location.objects endpoint # URL confirmation

update-rest-api

Updates a REST API configuration. Modifies name, description, and API settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier

Return Value
  • id (string): API identifier
  • name (string): Updated name
  • description (string): Updated description

Rename the REST API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-jobpost-api-v2
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api-v2" }
Django Context: # Rename API # JobPost.objects new name # API evolution
Example 2: Update description

Change API description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="JobPost Management API v2.0 with Skills filtering"
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "description": "JobPost Management API v2.0 with Skills filtering" }
Django Context: # Update description # Skills.objects docs # API documentation

Add binary media type support.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/binaryMediaTypes/-,value="image/png"
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "binaryMediaTypes": ["image/png"] }
Django Context: # Binary media # User.objects images # File upload support
Example 4: Update API key source

Change where API key is read from.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/apiKeySource,value=AUTHORIZER
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "apiKeySource": "AUTHORIZER" }
Django Context: # API key source # Author.objects auth # Key configuration
Example 5: Disable execute-api endpoint

Disable default API endpoint.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/disableExecuteApiEndpoint,value=true
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "disableExecuteApiEndpoint": true }
Django Context: # Disable default # Location.objects custom domain # Security hardening

update-stage

Updates a deployment stage configuration. Modifies caching, throttling, and stage variables.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --stage-name <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --stage-name (string): Stage name
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • stageName (string): Stage name
  • variables (object): Stage variables
  • cacheClusterEnabled (boolean): Cache status
Example 1: Enable caching

Turn on API caching.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/cacheClusterEnabled,value=true
Output: { "stageName": "prod", "cacheClusterEnabled": true }
Django Context: # Enable cache # JobPost.objects cache # Performance boost
Example 2: Update stage variable

Change environment variable.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/variables/dbHost,value=prod-db.anihpj.com
Output: { "stageName": "prod", "variables": { "dbHost": "prod-db.anihpj.com" } }
Django Context: # Stage variable # Skills.objects database # Environment config
Example 3: Update description

Change stage description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Production environment v2.1.0"
Output: { "stageName": "prod", "description": "Production environment v2.1.0" }
Django Context: # Stage description # User.objects version # Environment labeling
Example 4: Update throttling

Change method throttling settings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations \
        'op=replace,path=/*/GET/throttling/rateLimit,value=1000' \
        'op=replace,path=/*/GET/throttling/burstLimit,value=500'
Output: { "stageName": "prod", "methodSettings": { "*/*": { "throttlingRateLimit": 1000, "throttlingBurstLimit": 500 } } }
Django Context: # Throttling update # Author.objects rate limit # Performance tuning
Example 5: Enable logging

Turn on CloudWatch logging.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path/*/*/logging/loglevel,value=INFO
Output: { "stageName": "prod" }
Django Context: # Enable logging # Location.objects monitoring # Observability

update-usage

Updates API usage data for a key. Grants additional quota to API consumers.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id <value> \
    --key-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --usage-plan-id (string): Usage plan identifier
  • --key-id (string): API key identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • usagePlanId (string): Usage plan ID
  • items (object): Updated usage data
Example 1: Grant extra quota

Add extra requests to customer quota.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/remaining,value=5000
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123premium", "items": { "key123abc": [[6500, 10000]] } }
Django Context: # Grant quota # JobPost.objects extra # Customer bonus
Example 2: Reduce remaining quota

Decrease available requests.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/remaining,value=-1000
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123premium", "items": { "key123abc": [[5500, 10000]] } }
Django Context: # Reduce quota # Skills.objects penalty # Usage adjustment
Example 3: Reset to full quota

Restore full quota allowance.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/remaining,value=10000
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123premium", "items": { "key123abc": [[10000, 10000]] } }
Django Context: # Reset quota # User.objects restore # Full allowance
Example 4: Emergency quota boost

Double quota for special event.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/remaining,value=10000

echo "Emergency quota boost applied"
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123premium", "items": { "key123abc": [[20000, 10000]] } } Emergency quota boost applied
Django Context: # Emergency boost # Author.objects event # Special allocation
Example 5: Verify quota update

Update and confirm new quota.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/remaining,value=1000)

echo "Updated quota: $(echo $RESULT | jq '.items')"
Output: Updated quota: {"key123abc": [[7500, 10000]]}
Django Context: # Verify update # Location.objects confirm # Quota validation

update-usage-plan

Updates a usage plan configuration. Modifies quota limits, throttling, and API associations.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --usage-plan-id (string): Usage plan identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Usage plan ID
  • name (string): Plan name
  • quota (object): Quota settings
  • throttle (object): Throttle settings
Example 1: Update quota limit

Increase monthly quota.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/quota/limit,value=200000
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "quota": { "limit": 200000, "period": "MONTH" } }
Django Context: # Update quota # JobPost.objects limit # Plan upgrade
Example 2: Update throttle rate

Increase rate limit.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/throttle/rateLimit,value=2000
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "throttle": { "rateLimit": 2000, "burstLimit": 4000 } }
Django Context: # Update rate # Skills.objects throttle # Performance upgrade
Example 3: Rename plan

Change plan name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-enterprise-plan
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "name": "anihpj-enterprise-plan" }
Django Context: # Rename plan # User.objects tier # Plan branding
Example 4: Add API stage

Associate new API stage with plan.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --patch-operations 'op=add,path=/apiStages,value={"apiId":"xyz789","stage":"prod"}'
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "apiStages": [ {"apiId": "abc123", "stage": "prod"}, {"apiId": "xyz789", "stage": "prod"} ] }
Django Context: # Add API stage # Author.objects multi-API # Plan expansion
Example 5: Update description

Change plan description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Enterprise tier with 200K requests/month"
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "description": "Enterprise tier with 200K requests/month" }
Django Context: # Update description # Location.objects docs # Plan documentation

update-vpc-link

Updates a VPC link configuration. Modifies name, description, and target settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --vpc-link-id (string): VPC link identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): VPC link identifier
  • name (string): Updated name
  • description (string): Updated description
  • status (string): Link status
Example 1: Rename VPC link

Change VPC link name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-production-link
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "name": "anihpj-production-link", "status": "AVAILABLE" }
Django Context: # Rename link # JobPost.objects connection # Network naming
Example 2: Update description

Change VPC link description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Production VPC link to Django backend"
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "description": "Production VPC link to Django backend" }
Django Context: # Update description # Skills.objects docs # Documentation
Example 3: Add environment tag

Add environment to name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value="[PROD] anihpj-backend-link"
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "name": "[PROD] anihpj-backend-link" }
Django Context: # Environment tag # User.objects production # Environment labeling
Example 4: Clear description

Remove VPC link description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value=""

echo "Description cleared"
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "description": "" } Description cleared
Django Context: # Clear description # Author.objects cleanup # Metadata removal
Example 5: Full update

Update name and description together.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-v2-backend \
        op=replace,path=/description,value="V2 Django backend connection"
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "name": "anihpj-v2-backend", "description": "V2 Django backend connection" }
Django Context: # Full update # Location.objects v2 # Complete metadata

AWS Step Functions

Orchestrate distributed applications and microservices with visual workflows

📋 Step Functions Overview

AWS Step Functions is a serverless orchestration service that lets you combine AWS Lambda functions and other AWS services to build business-critical applications. Use it to create complex jobpost workflows like multi-step job approval, skill matching pipelines, and batch processing.

Core Step Functions Operations

create-state-machine - Create workflow
list-state-machines - List workflows
start-execution - Start workflow run
describe-execution - Get execution status
stop-execution - Stop running workflow
list-executions - List workflow runs

create-activity

Creates an activity that can be used as a task in a state machine workflow.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name <value> \
    [--tags <value>]
Parameters
  • --name (string): Name of the activity (required)
  • --tags (list): Tags to attach to the activity
Example 1: Create JobPost Review Activity

Create an activity for manual job post review tasks.

#!/bin/bash
# Create activity for human review of job posts
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name "anihpj-jobpost-manual-review"
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-jobpost-manual-review", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z" }
Django Context: # Activity for JobPost.objects.filter(status='pending_review') # Manual review step before publishing # Author.objects verification required
Example 2: Create Skills Verification Activity

Create activity for verifying job skills requirements.

#!/bin/bash
# Activity for skills verification process
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name "anihpj-skills-verification" \
    --tags key=Environment,value=production key=App,value=anihpj
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T10:35:00Z" }
Django Context: # Activity validates Skills.objects linked to JobPost # Checks skill_name, proficiency_level fields # Related to JobPost.skills ManyToMany relationship
Example 3: Create Author Approval Activity

Create activity for author account approval workflow.

#!/bin/bash
# Activity for new author approvals
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name "anihpj-author-approval" \
    --tags key=Team,value=hr key=Process,value=onboarding
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-author-approval", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T10:40:00Z" }
Django Context: # Approval activity for Author.objects.create() # Validates author credentials and company info # Links to User.objects for authentication
Example 4: Create Location Validation Activity

Create activity for validating job locations.

#!/bin/bash
# Activity for location data validation
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name "anihpj-location-validation" \
    --tags key=DataQuality,value=high key=Service,value=geocoding
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-location-validation", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T10:45:00Z" }
Django Context: # Validates Location.objects address data # Checks city, state, country fields # Geocoding verification for JobPost.location FK
Example 5: Create Batch Processing Activity

Create activity for batch job post processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Activity for batch processing expired job posts
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name "anihpj-batch-expiry-check" \
    --tags key=Type,value=batch key=Schedule,value=daily
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-batch-expiry-check", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T10:50:00Z" }
Django Context: # Batch check: JobPost.objects.filter(expiry_date__lt=now()) # Updates status field to 'expired' # Notifies Author.objects about expired listings

create-state-machine

Creates a new state machine (workflow) definition.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine \
    --name <value> \
    --definition <value> \
    --role-arn <value> \
    [--type STANDARD|EXPRESS]
Parameters
  • --name (string): Name of the state machine
  • --definition (string): ASL definition in JSON
  • --role-arn (string): IAM role for execution
  • --type (string): STANDARD or EXPRESS workflow
Example 1: Create JobPost Approval Workflow

Create multi-step workflow for job post approval process.

#!/bin/bash
cat > jobpost-approval.json << 'EOF'
{
  "Comment": "JobPost approval workflow for anihpj Django app",
  "StartAt": "ValidateJobPost",
  "States": {
    "ValidateJobPost": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:validate-jobpost",
      "Next": "CheckSkillsMatch"
    },
    "CheckSkillsMatch": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:check-skills",
      "Next": "ApprovalDecision"
    },
    "ApprovalDecision": {
      "Type": "Choice",
      "Choices": [
        {
          "Variable": "$.approved",
          "BooleanEquals": true,
          "Next": "PublishJobPost"
        }
      ],
      "Default": "SendRejectionNotification"
    },
    "PublishJobPost": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:publish-jobpost",
      "End": true
    },
    "SendRejectionNotification": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:send-rejection",
      "End": true
    }
  }
}
EOF

aws stepfunctions create-state-machine \
    --name "anihpj-jobpost-approval" \
    --definition file://jobpost-approval.json \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/stepfunctions-role \
    --type STANDARD
Output: { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval", "creationDate": "2025-01-11T17:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Workflow for JobPost.objects.create() approval: # 1. ValidateJobPost: Check required fields # 2. CheckSkillsMatch: Verify Skills.objects exist # 3. ApprovalDecision: Auto or manual approval # 4. PublishJobPost: JobPost.status = 'published' # 5. Or SendRejection: Notify Author.user
Example 2: Create Skill Matching Pipeline

Process skill matching for job recommendations.

#!/bin/bash
cat > skill-matching.json << 'EOF'
{
  "StartAt": "FetchUserSkills",
  "States": {
    "FetchUserSkills": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:fetch-user-skills",
      "Next": "FetchJobRequirements"
    },
    "FetchJobRequirements": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:fetch-job-requirements",
      "Next": "CalculateMatch"
    },
    "CalculateMatch": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:calculate-match",
      "End": true
    }
  }
}
EOF

aws stepfunctions create-state-machine \
    --name "anihpj-skill-matching" \
    --definition file://skill-matching.json \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/stepfunctions-role
Output: { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skill-matching" }
Django Context: # Skill matching workflow: # 1. User.skills.all() → fetch user's Skills # 2. JobPost.required_skills.all() → get job requirements # 3. Calculate percentage match between user and job
Example 3: Create Express workflow for real-time

High-volume, short-duration workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine \
    --name "anihpj-quick-validate" \
    --definition '{"StartAt":"Validate","States":{"Validate":{"Type":"Task","Resource":"arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:quick-validate","End":true}}}' \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/stepfunctions-role \
    --type EXPRESS
Output: { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-quick-validate", "type": "EXPRESS" }
Django Context: # Express workflow for fast JobPost validation # Sub-second response for Skills.objects.exists() # Real-time Location.objects.get() checks

create-state-machine-alias

Creates an alias for a state machine that points to one or two versions.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name <value> \
    --routing-configuration <value> \
    [--description <value>]
Parameters
  • --name (string): Name for the alias (required)
  • --routing-configuration (list): Version routing weights (required)
  • --description (string): Description of the alias
Example 1: Create Production Alias

Create alias pointing to stable production version.

#!/bin/bash
# Create production alias for jobpost workflow
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow#prod" \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:1","weight":100}]' \
    --description "Production alias for JobPost approval workflow"
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:prod", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T11:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Production alias handles all JobPost.objects workflows # Stable version for Author.objects approval process # Used by production Django app settings
Example 2: Create Canary Deployment Alias

Create alias with traffic split between versions.

#!/bin/bash
# Canary deployment: 90% old, 10% new version
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow#canary" \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:1","weight":90},{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:2","weight":10}]'
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:canary", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T11:05:00Z" }
Django Context: # Canary testing new JobPost validation logic # 10% of Skills.objects checks use new version # Gradual rollout for Location.objects updates
Example 3: Create Staging Alias

Create alias for staging environment testing.

#!/bin/bash
# Staging alias for pre-production testing
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher#staging" \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:3","weight":100}]' \
    --description "Staging environment for skills matching workflow"
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:staging", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T11:10:00Z" }
Django Context: # Staging tests Skills.objects matching algorithms # Pre-production JobPost.objects.filter() validation # QA testing before User.objects impacted
Example 4: Create Blue-Green Alias

Create alias for blue-green deployment switching.

#!/bin/bash
# Blue alias - can quickly switch to green
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard#blue" \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:5","weight":100}]' \
    --description "Blue deployment for author onboarding"
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:blue", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T11:15:00Z" }
Django Context: # Blue deployment for Author.objects.create() flow # Instant rollback capability for User.objects # Zero-downtime deployment strategy
Example 5: Create Regional Alias

Create alias for region-specific workflow version.

#!/bin/bash
# Regional alias with compliance-specific version
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name "arn:aws:states:eu-west-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow#eu-gdpr" \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:eu-west-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:2","weight":100}]' \
    --description "EU GDPR compliant workflow version"
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:eu-west-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:eu-gdpr", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T11:20:00Z" }
Django Context: # GDPR compliant JobPost.objects handling # EU Location.objects with data residency # Author.objects privacy compliance workflow

start-execution

Starts a new execution of a state machine.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions start-execution \
    --state-machine-arn <value> \
    [--name <value>] \
    [--input <value>]
Example 1: Start JobPost approval workflow

Execute approval for new job post.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions start-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --name "jobpost-12345-approval" \
    --input '{"jobpost_id": 12345, "author_id": 67, "title": "Senior Python Developer"}'
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:jobpost-12345-approval", "startDate": "2025-01-11T17:15:00Z" }
Django Context: # Workflow started for: # JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) # Author.objects.get(pk=67) # Processing title: "Senior Python Developer"
Example 2: Batch process multiple job posts

Start execution for batch processing.

#!/bin/bash
for jobpost_id in 101 102 103 104 105; do
    aws stepfunctions start-execution \
        --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
        --name "batch-jobpost-$jobpost_id" \
        --input "{\"jobpost_id\": $jobpost_id}"
done
Output: { "executionArn": "...execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:batch-jobpost-101" } { "executionArn": "...execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:batch-jobpost-102" } ...
Django Context: # Batch processing: JobPost.objects.filter(pk__in=[101,102,103,104,105]) # Each job post goes through approval workflow # Similar to Django bulk_update pattern
Example 3: Start with complex input payload

Execute workflow with detailed job post data.

#!/bin/bash
# Complex input with nested data
aws stepfunctions start-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --name "jobpost-full-789" \
    --input '{
        "jobpost_id": 789,
        "author": {"id": 45, "name": "Tech Corp"},
        "skills": ["Python", "Django", "AWS"],
        "location": {"city": "New York", "remote": true},
        "salary_range": {"min": 120000, "max": 180000}
    }'
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:jobpost-full-789", "startDate": "2025-01-15T12:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Full JobPost.objects data submitted # Skills.objects.filter(name__in=['Python','Django','AWS']) # Location.objects with remote=True option
Example 4: Start author onboarding workflow

Execute new author registration process.

#!/bin/bash
# Start author onboarding
aws stepfunctions start-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --name "author-onboard-$(date +%s)" \
    --input '{"user_id": 1001, "email": "newauthor@company.com", "company": "StartupXYZ"}'
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-author-onboard:author-onboard-1705312800", "startDate": "2025-01-15T12:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Author.objects.create() workflow started # User.objects.get(pk=1001) verification # Company profile setup for StartupXYZ
Example 5: Start skills verification workflow

Execute skill matching and verification process.

#!/bin/bash
# Skills verification for job matching
aws stepfunctions start-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher \
    --name "skills-match-jobpost-555" \
    --input '{"jobpost_id": 555, "required_skills": ["AWS", "Docker", "Kubernetes"], "min_match_percent": 80}'
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-matcher:skills-match-jobpost-555", "startDate": "2025-01-15T12:05:00Z" }
Django Context: # Skills matching for JobPost.objects.get(pk=555) # Skills.objects.filter(name__in=['AWS','Docker','Kubernetes']) # Minimum 80% skill match required

describe-execution

Gets the status and details of a workflow execution.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn <value>
Example 1: Check execution status

Get status of running workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:jobpost-12345-approval
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:...:jobpost-12345-approval", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "startDate": "2025-01-11T17:15:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-11T17:15:45Z", "input": "{\"jobpost_id\": 12345}", "output": "{\"approved\": true, \"published\": true}" }
Django Context: # Workflow completed successfully # JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345).status = 'published' # Duration: 45 seconds for full approval process
Example 2: Check failed execution

Get details of failed workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:failed-exec
Output: { "status": "FAILED", "error": "ValidationError", "cause": "JobPost missing required skills" }
Django Context: # Workflow failed: JobPost validation error # Missing required_skills for JobPost # Author notified of rejection
Example 3: Check running execution progress

Monitor in-progress workflow execution.

#!/bin/bash
# Check ongoing execution
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-author-onboard:author-101
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-author-onboard:author-101", "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard", "name": "author-101", "status": "RUNNING", "startDate": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "input": "{\"user_id\": 101, \"email\": \"author@example.com\"}" }
Django Context: # Author onboarding in progress # User.objects.get(pk=101) being processed # Waiting for verification steps to complete
Example 4: Get execution output data

Retrieve workflow output for processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Get output from completed execution
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-matcher:match-500 \
    --query '{Status:status,Output:output}'
Output: { "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Output": "{\"matched_candidates\": 15, \"top_match_score\": 95, \"job_post_id\": 500}" }
Django Context: # Skills matching completed for JobPost 500 # 15 candidates matched required Skills.objects # Top candidate scored 95% match
Example 5: Check aborted execution details

Review details of manually stopped workflow.

#!/bin/bash
# Check aborted execution
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:cancelled-post-999
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:cancelled-post-999", "status": "ABORTED", "startDate": "2025-01-15T08:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-15T08:05:30Z", "input": "{\"jobpost_id\": 999}", "error": "UserAborted", "cause": "Author requested cancellation" }
Django Context: # JobPost.objects.get(pk=999) workflow cancelled # Author.objects requested stop via admin # Status reset to 'draft' for re-submission

list-executions

Lists all executions of a state machine.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn <value> \
    [--status-filter RUNNING|SUCCEEDED|FAILED|TIMED_OUT|ABORTED]
Example 1: List running executions

Get all currently running workflows.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --status-filter RUNNING
Output: { "executions": [ { "executionArn": "...batch-jobpost-103", "name": "batch-jobpost-103", "status": "RUNNING", "startDate": "2025-01-11T17:20:00Z" }, { "executionArn": "...batch-jobpost-104", "name": "batch-jobpost-104", "status": "RUNNING" } ] }
Django Context: # 2 JobPost approvals currently in progress # JobPost.objects.filter(pk__in=[103, 104]) # Still processing through approval states
Example 2: List failed executions

Review failed workflows for debugging.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --status-filter FAILED \
    --max-results 10
Output: { "executions": [ { "executionArn": "...failed-jobpost-999", "status": "FAILED", "stopDate": "2025-01-11T16:45:00Z" } ] }
Django Context: # Failed JobPost.objects approval to investigate # Review validation errors # Check Skills.objects requirements
Example 3: List succeeded executions with pagination

Paginate through successful workflow executions.

#!/bin/bash
# Get first page of succeeded executions
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --status-filter SUCCEEDED \
    --max-results 25
Output: { "executions": [ { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:jobpost-500", "name": "jobpost-500", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "startDate": "2025-01-15T09:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-15T09:00:15Z" } ], "nextToken": "eyJuZXh0VG9rZW4iOiBudWxsfQ==" }
Django Context: # Successfully approved JobPost.objects entries # JobPost.objects.filter(status='approved') # Author.objects notified of approval
Example 4: List all executions for audit

Get all executions regardless of status for audit purposes.

#!/bin/bash
# List all executions for compliance audit
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --max-results 100 \
    --query 'executions[*].{Name:name,Status:status,Start:startDate}'
Output: [ {"Name": "author-onboard-101", "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Start": "2025-01-15T08:00:00Z"}, {"Name": "author-onboard-102", "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Start": "2025-01-15T08:30:00Z"}, {"Name": "author-onboard-103", "Status": "FAILED", "Start": "2025-01-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"Name": "author-onboard-104", "Status": "RUNNING", "Start": "2025-01-15T09:30:00Z"} ]
Django Context: # Audit trail for Author.objects.all() onboarding # Tracks User.objects account creation status # Compliance reporting for HR processes
Example 5: List timed out executions

Find executions that exceeded timeout limits.

#!/bin/bash
# Find workflows that timed out
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-verification \
    --status-filter TIMED_OUT \
    --max-results 50
Output: { "executions": [ { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-verification:skill-check-789", "name": "skill-check-789", "status": "TIMED_OUT", "startDate": "2025-01-14T10:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-14T10:05:00Z" } ] }
Django Context: # Skills.objects verification took too long # External API timeout for skill validation # Needs retry with JobPost.objects.get(pk=789)

delete-activity

Deletes an activity from Step Functions.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions delete-activity \
    --activity-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --activity-arn (string): ARN of the activity to delete (required)
Example 1: Delete Unused Review Activity

Remove deprecated job post review activity.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete old review activity no longer in use
aws stepfunctions delete-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-old-review
Output: {}
Django Context: # Removed deprecated JobPost review activity # No longer used in current workflows # Clean up from migration to automated approval
Example 2: Delete Test Activity

Clean up activity created for testing.

#!/bin/bash
# Remove test activity after QA
aws stepfunctions delete-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-test-skills-check
Output: {}
Django Context: # Test activity for Skills.objects validation removed # QA testing complete # Production uses automated Lambda instead
Example 3: Delete Activity with Confirmation

Delete activity after verifying it's not in use.

#!/bin/bash
# First check if activity exists
ACTIVITY_ARN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-manual-approval"
if aws stepfunctions describe-activity --activity-arn "$ACTIVITY_ARN" 2>/dev/null; then
    aws stepfunctions delete-activity --activity-arn "$ACTIVITY_ARN"
    echo "Activity deleted successfully"
fi
Output: Activity deleted successfully
Django Context: # Manual Author.objects approval activity removed # Confirmed no pending JobPost.objects tasks # Workflow updated to use automated process
Example 4: Batch Delete Old Activities

Clean up multiple deprecated activities.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete multiple old activities
for activity in "legacy-review" "old-validation" "deprecated-check"; do
    aws stepfunctions delete-activity \
        --activity-arn "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-$activity" \
        2>/dev/null && echo "Deleted: $activity"
done
Output: Deleted: legacy-review Deleted: old-validation Deleted: deprecated-check
Django Context: # Cleaned up legacy JobPost.objects activities # Removed deprecated Skills.objects checks # Streamlined workflow for Author.objects
Example 5: Delete Regional Activity

Remove activity from specific region.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete activity in EU region
aws stepfunctions delete-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:eu-west-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-eu-location-verify \
    --region eu-west-1
Output: {}
Django Context: # EU Location.objects verification activity removed # Migrated to centralized workflow # GDPR compliance handled differently now

delete-state-machine

Deletes a state machine and all associated versions and aliases.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --state-machine-arn (string): ARN of the state machine to delete (required)
Example 1: Delete Deprecated Workflow

Remove old job post workflow no longer in use.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete deprecated workflow
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-old-approval-v1
Output: {}
Django Context: # Old JobPost.objects approval workflow removed # Replaced by v2 with Skills.objects matching # All executions completed before deletion
Example 2: Delete Test Workflow

Clean up workflow created for testing.

#!/bin/bash
# Remove test workflow after QA
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-test-workflow
Output: {}
Django Context: # Test workflow for Author.objects flow removed # QA testing completed successfully # Production workflow validated and deployed
Example 3: Delete with Running Check

Delete workflow after ensuring no running executions.

#!/bin/bash
STATE_MACHINE="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-legacy-skills"
# Check for running executions
RUNNING=$(aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn "$STATE_MACHINE" \
    --status-filter RUNNING \
    --query 'length(executions)')
if [ "$RUNNING" -eq 0 ]; then
    aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine --state-machine-arn "$STATE_MACHINE"
    echo "State machine deleted"
else
    echo "Cannot delete: $RUNNING executions still running"
fi
Output: State machine deleted
Django Context: # Safe deletion of Skills.objects workflow # Verified no JobPost.objects processing # Clean shutdown with zero data loss
Example 4: Delete Development Workflow

Remove development environment workflow.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete dev workflow
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-dev-jobpost-flow
Output: {}
Django Context: # Development JobPost.objects workflow cleaned # dev environment tear down # Resources freed for new sprint testing
Example 5: Delete with Tag-Based Selection

Delete workflows matching specific criteria.

#!/bin/bash
# Find and delete deprecated workflows by listing and filtering
aws stepfunctions list-state-machines \
    --query "stateMachines[?contains(name, 'deprecated')].[stateMachineArn]" \
    --output text | while read arn; do
    aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine --state-machine-arn "$arn"
    echo "Deleted: $arn"
done
Output: Deleted: arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:deprecated-author-flow Deleted: arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:deprecated-skills-check
Django Context: # Batch cleanup of deprecated workflows # Author.objects and Skills.objects old flows # Infrastructure cleanup for cost savings

delete-state-machine-alias

Deletes a state machine alias.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --state-machine-alias-arn (string): ARN of the alias to delete (required)
Example 1: Delete Canary Alias

Remove canary deployment alias after promotion.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete canary alias - promoted to production
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:canary
Output: {}
Django Context: # Canary testing complete for JobPost.objects # New version promoted to production alias # Skills.objects matching validated
Example 2: Delete Staging Alias

Remove staging alias after production deployment.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete staging alias
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:staging
Output: {}
Django Context: # Staging Author.objects workflow alias removed # Production deployment successful # User.objects onboarding live
Example 3: Delete Old Blue-Green Alias

Clean up blue alias after green is active.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete blue alias - green is now active
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:blue
Output: {}
Django Context: # Blue-green deployment complete # Skills.objects matcher on green version # Blue alias no longer needed
Example 4: Delete Test Alias

Remove testing alias from development.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete test alias
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify:test
Output: {}
Django Context: # Test Location.objects verification alias removed # QA cycle complete # Production alias remains active
Example 5: Delete Regional Alias

Remove alias from specific region.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete EU regional alias
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:eu-west-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:eu-staging \
    --region eu-west-1
Output: {}
Django Context: # EU staging alias removed # JobPost.objects EU workflow consolidated # Single production alias for GDPR region

delete-state-machine-version

Deletes a specific version of a state machine.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-version-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --state-machine-version-arn (string): ARN of the version to delete (required)
Example 1: Delete Old Version

Remove deprecated workflow version.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete old version after migration
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-version-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:1
Output: {}
Django Context: # Version 1 of JobPost.objects workflow deleted # All traffic migrated to version 3 # No active executions on old version
Example 2: Delete Failed Version

Remove version that had issues in production.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete problematic version 2
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-version-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:2
Output: {}
Django Context: # Buggy Skills.objects version removed # Caused false negative matches # Rolled back to stable version 1
Example 3: Delete Multiple Old Versions

Clean up multiple old versions.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete versions 1-3, keep 4+
for version in 1 2 3; do
    aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version \
        --state-machine-version-arn "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:$version" \
        2>/dev/null && echo "Deleted version $version"
done
Output: Deleted version 1 Deleted version 2 Deleted version 3
Django Context: # Old Author.objects onboarding versions cleaned # Kept versions 4+ for rollback capability # User.objects flow modernized
Example 4: Delete After Verification

Delete version after confirming no aliases point to it.

#!/bin/bash
VERSION_ARN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify:5"
# Check if any alias references this version
ALIASES=$(aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-aliases \
    --state-machine-arn "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify" \
    --query "stateMachineAliases[?contains(routingConfiguration[].stateMachineVersionArn, '$VERSION_ARN')]")
if [ "$ALIASES" == "[]" ]; then
    aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version --state-machine-version-arn "$VERSION_ARN"
fi
Output: {}
Django Context: # Safe deletion of Location.objects version # No aliases reference this version # Version 6 is current production
Example 5: Delete Development Version

Remove version used only in development.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete dev-only version
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-version-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:99
Output: {}
Django Context: # Dev version 99 of batch JobPost.objects removed # Experimental features not promoted # Clean development environment

describe-activity

Gets information about an activity, including its creation date and ARN.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-activity \
    --activity-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --activity-arn (string): ARN of the activity to describe (required)
Example 1: Describe Review Activity

Get details about manual review activity.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-jobpost-manual-review
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-jobpost-manual-review", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-manual-review", "creationDate": "2025-01-10T09:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Activity for JobPost.objects manual review # Created for content moderation workflow # Used by Author.objects approval process
Example 2: Describe Skills Verification Activity

Get details of skills verification activity.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification", "name": "anihpj-skills-verification", "creationDate": "2025-01-12T14:30:00Z" }
Django Context: # Skills.objects verification activity # Validates required skills for JobPost # Human-in-the-loop for complex validations
Example 3: Check Activity Age

Get creation date for lifecycle management.

#!/bin/bash
# Check when activity was created
aws stepfunctions describe-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-author-approval \
    --query '{Name:name,Created:creationDate}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-author-approval", "Created": "2024-06-15T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Author.objects approval activity # Created 7 months ago # Still active in production workflow
Example 4: Describe Location Activity

Get details of location validation activity.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-location-validation
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-location-validation", "name": "anihpj-location-validation", "creationDate": "2025-01-08T16:45:00Z" }
Django Context: # Location.objects validation activity # Geocoding and address verification # JobPost.location ForeignKey validation
Example 5: Verify Activity Exists

Check if activity exists before using in workflow.

#!/bin/bash
# Verify activity exists
ACTIVITY_ARN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-batch-processor"
if aws stepfunctions describe-activity --activity-arn "$ACTIVITY_ARN" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Activity exists and is ready"
else
    echo "Activity not found - creating..."
fi
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-batch-processor", "name": "anihpj-batch-processor", "creationDate": "2025-01-05T08:00:00Z" } Activity exists and is ready
Django Context: # Batch processor for JobPost.objects.all() # Activity ready for workflow integration # Handles bulk Skills.objects updates

describe-map-run

Gets details about a Map Run, including item counts and execution statistics.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-map-run \
    --map-run-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --map-run-arn (string): ARN of the Map Run to describe (required)
Example 1: Check Batch Processing Status

Get status of parallel job post processing.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-batch-workflow/batch-run-001:abc123
Output: { "mapRunArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-batch-workflow/batch-run-001:abc123", "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-batch-workflow:batch-run-001", "status": "RUNNING", "startDate": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "maxConcurrency": 10, "itemCounts": { "pending": 45, "running": 10, "succeeded": 145, "failed": 0, "total": 200 } }
Django Context: # Processing 200 JobPost.objects in parallel # 145 completed, 55 remaining # Max 10 concurrent Skills.objects validations
Example 2: Check Completed Map Run

Review completed batch processing results.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-skills-batch/skills-update:def456
Output: { "mapRunArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-skills-batch/skills-update:def456", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "startDate": "2025-01-15T08:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-15T08:15:00Z", "itemCounts": { "pending": 0, "running": 0, "succeeded": 500, "failed": 0, "total": 500 } }
Django Context: # All 500 Skills.objects updated successfully # 15 minute total processing time # Zero failures in batch update
Example 3: Check Map Run with Failures

Analyze Map Run with some failed items.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-author-notify/notify-batch:ghi789 \
    --query '{Status:status,Succeeded:itemCounts.succeeded,Failed:itemCounts.failed}'
Output: { "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Succeeded": 95, "Failed": 5 }
Django Context: # Author.objects notifications: 95 sent, 5 failed # Failed likely due to invalid email addresses # User.objects email validation needed
Example 4: Monitor Long-Running Map

Check progress of large batch operation.

#!/bin/bash
# Monitor progress every 30 seconds
MAP_RUN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-migrate/full-migration:jkl012"
while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws stepfunctions describe-map-run --map-run-arn "$MAP_RUN" \
        --query '{Status:status,Done:itemCounts.succeeded,Total:itemCounts.total}')
    echo "$(date): $STATUS"
    [[ $(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.Status') == "SUCCEEDED" ]] && break
    sleep 30
done
Output: Mon Jan 15 10:00:00 2025: {"Status": "RUNNING", "Done": 1000, "Total": 5000} Mon Jan 15 10:00:30 2025: {"Status": "RUNNING", "Done": 1500, "Total": 5000} Mon Jan 15 10:01:00 2025: {"Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Done": 5000, "Total": 5000}
Django Context: # Full JobPost.objects migration: 5000 records # Location.objects geocoding updates # Skills.objects standardization complete
Example 5: Get Detailed Execution Stats

Get comprehensive Map Run statistics.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-daily-process/daily-2025-01-15:mno345
Output: { "mapRunArn": "arn:aws:states:...:daily-2025-01-15:mno345", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "startDate": "2025-01-15T02:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-15T02:45:00Z", "maxConcurrency": 20, "toleratedFailurePercentage": 5.0, "toleratedFailureCount": 10, "itemCounts": { "pending": 0, "running": 0, "succeeded": 1850, "failed": 2, "timedOut": 0, "total": 1852 }, "executionCounts": { "pending": 0, "running": 0, "succeeded": 1850, "failed": 2, "total": 1852 } }
Django Context: # Daily JobPost.objects processing complete # 1850 succeeded, 2 failed (within tolerance) # 45 min runtime with 20 parallel workers

describe-state-machine

Gets information about a state machine, including its definition and configuration.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --state-machine-arn (string): ARN of the state machine to describe (required)
Example 1: Get Workflow Details

Describe job post approval workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval
Output: { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-approval", "status": "ACTIVE", "definition": "{\"StartAt\":\"ValidateJobPost\",...}", "roleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/StepFunctionsRole", "type": "STANDARD", "creationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # JobPost.objects approval workflow details # STANDARD type for long-running approvals # Active and processing submissions
Example 2: Get Express Workflow Details

Describe Express workflow configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-quick-validate \
    --query '{Name:name,Type:type,Status:status}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-quick-validate", "Type": "EXPRESS", "Status": "ACTIVE" }
Django Context: # Express workflow for fast validation # Sub-second JobPost.objects checks # Skills.objects.exists() validation
Example 3: Extract Workflow Definition

Get workflow ASL definition for review.

#!/bin/bash
# Extract and format workflow definition
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher \
    --query 'definition' --output text | jq .
Output: { "StartAt": "FetchJobPost", "States": { "FetchJobPost": { "Type": "Task", "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:fetch-jobpost", "Next": "MatchSkills" }, "MatchSkills": { "Type": "Task", "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:match-skills", "End": true } } }
Django Context: # Skills.objects matching workflow definition # FetchJobPost -> MatchSkills states # Lambda functions for JobPost processing
Example 4: Check Workflow IAM Role

Verify IAM role assigned to workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --query '{Name:name,Role:roleArn}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-author-onboard", "Role": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-stepfunctions-author-role" }
Django Context: # Author.objects onboarding workflow # IAM role with User.objects permissions # Limited to author-related resources
Example 5: Get Workflow Logging Config

Check logging configuration for workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify \
    --query '{Name:name,Logging:loggingConfiguration,Tracing:tracingConfiguration}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-location-verify", "Logging": { "level": "ALL", "includeExecutionData": true, "destinations": [ {"cloudWatchLogsLogGroup": {"logGroupArn": "arn:aws:logs:...:anihpj-stepfunctions"}} ] }, "Tracing": {"enabled": true} }
Django Context: # Location.objects verification with full logging # X-Ray tracing enabled for debugging # All execution data captured in CloudWatch

describe-state-machine-alias

Gets information about a state machine alias, including routing configuration.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --state-machine-alias-arn (string): ARN of the alias to describe (required)
Example 1: Describe Production Alias

Get details of production routing configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:prod
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:prod", "name": "prod", "description": "Production alias for job post workflow", "routingConfiguration": [ { "stateMachineVersionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:3", "weight": 100 } ], "creationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "updateDate": "2025-01-10T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Production JobPost.objects workflow alias # 100% traffic to version 3 # All Skills.objects processing stable
Example 2: Check Canary Deployment

View canary traffic split configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:canary
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:...:anihpj-skills-matcher:canary", "name": "canary", "routingConfiguration": [ {"stateMachineVersionArn": "...:anihpj-skills-matcher:5", "weight": 90}, {"stateMachineVersionArn": "...:anihpj-skills-matcher:6", "weight": 10} ] }
Django Context: # Canary deployment for Skills.objects matcher # 90% on stable v5, 10% testing v6 # Gradual rollout of new matching algorithm
Example 3: Verify Alias Routing

Check which version alias points to.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:live \
    --query 'routingConfiguration[0].stateMachineVersionArn'
Output: "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:4"
Django Context: # Live alias points to Author.objects v4 # User.objects onboarding version confirmed # Ready for production traffic
Example 4: Check Alias Update Time

See when alias was last modified.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify:stable \
    --query '{Name:name,Created:creationDate,Updated:updateDate}'
Output: { "Name": "stable", "Created": "2024-12-01T00:00:00Z", "Updated": "2025-01-14T12:30:00Z" }
Django Context: # Stable Location.objects alias # Created Dec 2024, updated yesterday # Recent version update deployed
Example 5: Compare Alias Configurations

Compare prod and staging alias configurations.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Production:"
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:prod \
    --query 'routingConfiguration'
echo "Staging:"
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:staging \
    --query 'routingConfiguration'
Output: Production: [{"stateMachineVersionArn": "...:anihpj-batch-process:2", "weight": 100}] Staging: [{"stateMachineVersionArn": "...:anihpj-batch-process:3", "weight": 100}]
Django Context: # Prod on v2, Staging testing v3 # JobPost.objects batch processing versions # v3 ready for promotion after validation

describe-state-machine-for-execution

Gets the state machine definition used for a specific execution.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --execution-arn (string): ARN of the execution (required)
Example 1: Get Definition for Running Execution

Get workflow definition used by current execution.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:job-12345
Output: { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-approval", "definition": "{\"StartAt\":\"ValidateJobPost\",\"States\":{...}}", "roleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/StepFunctionsRole", "updateDate": "2025-01-10T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Definition used for JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) # Shows exact workflow version in use # Useful for debugging Skills.objects validation
Example 2: Debug Failed Execution

Get definition to debug failed workflow.

#!/bin/bash
# Get definition for failed execution to review
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-matcher:failed-match-999 \
    --query 'definition' --output text | jq .
Output: { "StartAt": "FetchSkills", "States": { "FetchSkills": { "Type": "Task", "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:fetch-skills", "Next": "MatchCandidates", "Catch": [{"ErrorEquals": ["States.ALL"], "Next": "HandleError"}] } } }
Django Context: # Skills.objects fetch definition for debugging # Shows error handling configuration # Catch block for all States errors
Example 3: Compare Historical Execution

Get definition from past execution for comparison.

#!/bin/bash
# Get definition from old execution
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-author-onboard:author-old-100 \
    --query '{Name:name,Updated:updateDate}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-author-onboard", "Updated": "2024-12-15T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Old Author.objects workflow version # Updated Dec 15, 2024 # Compare with current for User.objects changes
Example 4: Verify Express Workflow

Check Express workflow type for execution.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:express:anihpj-quick-validate:fast-check-001:abc \
    --query '{Name:name,Type:type}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-quick-validate", "Type": "EXPRESS" }
Django Context: # Express workflow for JobPost.objects validation # Sub-second execution type # Location.objects quick checks
Example 5: Extract Role for Troubleshooting

Get IAM role used by execution for permission debugging.

#!/bin/bash
# Get role ARN for permission troubleshooting
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-location-verify:loc-check-500 \
    --query 'roleArn'
Output: "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-stepfunctions-location-role"
Django Context: # IAM role for Location.objects verification # Check permissions for geocoding API calls # Troubleshoot JobPost.location access

get-activity-task

Gets a task from an activity for processing by a worker.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
    --activity-arn <value> \
    [--worker-name <value>]
Parameters
  • --activity-arn (string): ARN of the activity (required)
  • --worker-name (string): Name identifying the worker
Example 1: Poll for Review Task

Worker polls for manual review task.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-jobpost-manual-review \
    --worker-name "review-worker-01"
Output: { "taskToken": "AAAAKgAAAAIAAAAAAAAAAX9Z...", "input": "{\"jobpost_id\": 12345, \"title\": \"Senior Developer\", \"author_id\": 67}" }
Django Context: # Task received for JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) # Manual review required for Author.objects(pk=67) # Worker processes then sends success/failure
Example 2: Skills Verification Worker

Worker polls for skills verification task.

#!/bin/bash
# Worker continuously polls for tasks
aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification \
    --worker-name "skills-verifier-$(hostname)"
Output: { "taskToken": "AAAAKgAAAAIBBBBBBBBBBBBB...", "input": "{\"skills\": [\"Python\", \"Django\", \"AWS\"], \"jobpost_id\": 789}" }
Django Context: # Verify Skills.objects requirements # Check against JobPost.objects(pk=789) # Return verification result with taskToken
Example 3: Long Polling Worker Loop

Worker loop for continuous task processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Worker loop with long polling
ACTIVITY="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-author-approval"
while true; do
    TASK=$(aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
        --activity-arn "$ACTIVITY" \
        --worker-name "approval-worker-01")
    if [ -n "$TASK" ] && [ "$TASK" != "{}" ]; then
        echo "Processing task: $TASK"
        # Process task here
    fi
done
Output: Processing task: {"taskToken": "AAAA...", "input": "{\"author_id\": 101}"}
Django Context: # Continuous Author.objects approval processing # Long polling waits for tasks (up to 60s) # User.objects verification workflow
Example 4: Location Validation Worker

Worker for location data validation tasks.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-location-validation \
    --worker-name "geocoder-worker-east"
Output: { "taskToken": "AAAAKgAAAAICCCCCCCCCCCCC...", "input": "{\"location_id\": 456, \"address\": \"123 Main St\", \"city\": \"New York\"}" }
Django Context: # Location.objects.get(pk=456) validation # Geocode and verify address data # Update JobPost.location coordinates
Example 5: Batch Processing Worker

Worker for batch job post processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Batch worker with timeout handling
TASK=$(timeout 65 aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-batch-processor \
    --worker-name "batch-worker-$(date +%s)")
if [ -n "$TASK" ] && [ "$TASK" != "{}" ]; then
    echo "Batch task received"
    TOKEN=$(echo "$TASK" | jq -r '.taskToken')
    INPUT=$(echo "$TASK" | jq -r '.input')
    echo "Processing: $INPUT"
fi
Output: Batch task received Processing: {"batch_id": "batch-2025-01-15", "jobpost_ids": [100, 101, 102]}
Django Context: # Batch JobPost.objects.filter(pk__in=[100,101,102]) # Process multiple records in single task # Skills.objects bulk validation

get-execution-history

Gets the execution history of a workflow with all state transitions.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions get-execution-history \
    --execution-arn <value> \
    [--max-results <value>] \
    [--reverse-order] \
    [--include-execution-data | --no-include-execution-data]
Parameters
  • --execution-arn (string): ARN of the execution (required)
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum events to return
  • --reverse-order (boolean): Return newest events first
  • --include-execution-data (boolean): Include input/output data
Example 1: Get Full Execution History

Get complete history of workflow execution.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions get-execution-history \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:job-12345 \
    --include-execution-data
Output: { "events": [ { "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "type": "ExecutionStarted", "id": 1, "executionStartedEventDetails": { "input": "{\"jobpost_id\": 12345}" } }, { "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:00:01Z", "type": "TaskStateEntered", "id": 2, "stateEnteredEventDetails": { "name": "ValidateJobPost" } } ] }
Django Context: # Full history for JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) # Shows each state transition # Execution started -> ValidateJobPost entered
Example 2: Get Recent Events

Get most recent events in reverse order.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions get-execution-history \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-matcher:match-500 \
    --reverse-order \
    --max-results 10
Output: { "events": [ {"type": "ExecutionSucceeded", "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:05:00Z"}, {"type": "TaskStateExited", "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:04:59Z"}, {"type": "LambdaFunctionSucceeded", "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:04:58Z"} ] }
