Output: ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Log Group Metric Filter Inventory - Django Monitoring ║ ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ ✅ /aws/django/anihpj-production Metric Filters: 10 | Storage: 250 MB ✅ /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Metric Filters: 5 | Storage: 125 MB ✅ /aws/django/anihpj/users Metric Filters: 3 | Storage: 50 MB ⚠️ /aws/django/anihpj/skills Metric Filters: 0 (UNMONITORED) | Storage: 25 MB ⚠️ /aws/django/anihpj/authors Metric Filters: 0 (UNMONITORED) | Storage: 15 MB ⚠️ /aws/django/anihpj/locations Metric Filters: 0 (UNMONITORED) | Storage: 5 MB ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Detailed Metric Filter Information: 📊 /aws/django/anihpj-production: ----------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeMetricFilters | +----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+ | Name | Pattern | Metric | +----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+ | error-filter | [ERROR] | DjangoErrors | | exception-filter | Exception | DjangoExceptions | | 5xx-filter | HTTP 5[0-9][0-9] | Django5xxErrors | | latency-filter | latency > 1000 | HighLatencyCount | | db-error-filter | DatabaseError | DBErrors | +----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+ 📊 /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost: ----------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeMetricFilters | +----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+ | Name | Pattern | Metric | +----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+ | jobpost-create | JobPost.create | JobPostCreated | | jobpost-error | JobPost.*ERROR | JobPostErrors | | skill-mismatch | skill not found | SkillMismatch | +----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+ ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Recommendation: Add metric filters to unmonitored groups for alerting
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import logging # Metric filter monitoring context: # Production group (10 filters) monitors all model errors: logger = logging.getLogger('django.production') # These operations trigger metric filters: try: # This would trigger 'error-filter' if it fails job = JobPost.objects.get(id=99999) except JobPost.DoesNotExist: logger.error("[ERROR] JobPost not found: 99999") # Increments DjangoErrors metric # JobPost operations (5 filters): # Creating jobs triggers 'jobpost-create' metric new_job = JobPost.objects.create( title="Backend Developer", company="TechStartup" ) logger.info(f"JobPost.create: {new_job.id}") # Increments JobPostCreated metric # Skills mismatch logging: missing_skill = "Nonexistent Framework" if not Skills.objects.filter(name=missing_skill).exists(): logger.warning(f"skill not found: {missing_skill}") # Increments SkillMismatch metric # Unmonitored groups (Skills, Authors, Locations): # Should add filters for: print("Recommended filters for unmonitored groups:") print(" Skills: skill-create, skill-delete, skill-error") print(" Authors: author-create, author-update, author-error") print(" Locations: location-create, location-error") # Model counts for monitoring baseline: print(f"\nCurrent model counts:") print(f" JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f" Users: {User.objects.count()}") print(f" Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Output: # JobPosts: 250 # Users: 120 # Skills: 45

describe-log-streams

Lists and describes the log streams within a specified log group in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Returns comprehensive information about each stream including creation time, last event timestamp, stored bytes, and upload sequence token. Essential for monitoring Django application log organization and identifying active streams for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs describe-log-streams \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    [--log-stream-name-prefix <value>] \
    [--order-by LogStreamName|LastEventTime] \
    [--descending | --no-descending] \
    [--limit <value>] \
    [--next-token <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --log-group-name (string, required): The name of the log group containing the streams. Example: "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
  • --log-stream-name-prefix (string): Filter streams by name prefix. Example: "web-server-01/" returns all streams for that server
  • --order-by (string): Sort order for results. Values: LogStreamName | LastEventTime. Use LastEventTime to find recently active streams
  • --descending: Return results in descending order. Use with LastEventTime to get most recent streams first
  • --no-descending: Return results in ascending order (default)
  • --limit (integer): Maximum number of streams to return (1-50). Default: 50
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from previous response for next page of results
Return Value
  • logStreams (Array): List of log stream objects
  • logStreamName (string): Name of the log stream
  • creationTime (long): When the stream was created (milliseconds since epoch)
  • firstEventTimestamp (long): Timestamp of first log event
  • lastEventTimestamp (long): Timestamp of most recent log event
  • lastIngestionTime (long): When CloudWatch last received events
  • uploadSequenceToken (string): Token for put-log-events ordering
  • arn (string): Amazon Resource Name for the stream
  • storedBytes (long): Total bytes of log data in stream
  • nextToken (string): Pagination token if more results exist
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: List Most Recently Active Log Streams for Django Application

Retrieves the most recently active log streams sorted by last event time in descending order. Helps identify which Django instances are currently logging JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location model operations.

#!/bin/bash
# List most recently active log streams
# Useful for monitoring active Django instances

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Most Recently Active Log Streams - anihpj JobPost            ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo ""

# Get streams ordered by last event time (most recent first)
aws logs describe-log-streams \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --order-by LastEventTime \
    --descending \
    --limit 10 \
    --output json | jq -r '
    .logStreams[] | 
    "Stream: \(.logStreamName)
     Last Event: \((.lastEventTimestamp/1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")))
     Created: \((.creationTime/1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d")))
     Storage: \((.storedBytes/1024 | floor)) KB
     ─────────────────────────────────────────"'

# Summary statistics
echo ""
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
TOTAL_STREAMS=$(aws logs describe-log-streams \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query 'length(logStreams)' \
    --output text)
echo "Total streams in log group: $TOTAL_STREAMS"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Most Recently Active Log Streams - anihpj JobPost ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Stream: web-server-01/2025/01/18 Last Event: 2025-01-18 14:55:23 Created: 2025-01-18 Storage: 1250 KB ───────────────────────────────────────── Stream: web-server-02/2025/01/18 Last Event: 2025-01-18 14:54:45 Created: 2025-01-18 Storage: 980 KB ───────────────────────────────────────── Stream: i-0abc123def/2025/01/18/14 Last Event: 2025-01-18 14:52:10 Created: 2025-01-18 Storage: 450 KB ───────────────────────────────────────── Stream: ecs-task-xyz/jobpost-container Last Event: 2025-01-18 14:50:30 Created: 2025-01-15 Storage: 3200 KB ───────────────────────────────────────── Stream: web-server-01/2025/01/17 Last Event: 2025-01-17 23:59:45 Created: 2025-01-17 Storage: 2100 KB ───────────────────────────────────────── ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Total streams in log group: 45
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime import logging # Active stream context - each stream represents a Django instance # web-server-01 (most recent activity) # Processing JobPost operations right now: recent_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now().replace(minute=0) ).order_by('-updated_at')[:5] print(f"Recent JobPost activity (web-server-01):") for job in recent_jobs: print(f" - {job.title} at {job.updated_at.strftime('%H:%M:%S')}") # Output: # - Senior Python Dev at 14:55:20 # - Django Engineer at 14:52:15 # - Backend Developer at 14:48:30 # web-server-02 (also active) # Processing Skills associations: recent_skills = Skills.objects.filter( jobpost__updated_at__gte=datetime.now().replace(minute=0) ).distinct() print(f"\nSkills used in recent jobs: {list(recent_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))}") # Output: Skills used in recent jobs: ['Python', 'Django', 'AWS', 'Docker'] # Stream storage correlates with model operation volume: # web-server-01: 1.2 MB → High JobPost activity # ecs-task-xyz: 3.2 MB → Batch processing job
Example 2: Find Streams by Instance ID Prefix

Filters log streams by EC2 instance ID prefix to find all streams from a specific server. Useful for investigating issues on a particular Django instance handling User, Author, and JobPost operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Find all streams for a specific EC2 instance
# Useful for instance-level log analysis

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
INSTANCE_ID="i-0abc123def456789"

echo "Searching for log streams from instance: $INSTANCE_ID"
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo ""

# Get all streams for this instance
aws logs describe-log-streams \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name-prefix "$INSTANCE_ID" \
    --order-by LastEventTime \
    --descending \
    --output json | jq '
    {
        instance: "'$INSTANCE_ID'",
        total_streams: (.logStreams | length),
        total_storage_kb: ([.logStreams[].storedBytes] | add / 1024 | floor),
        streams: [.logStreams[] | {
            name: .logStreamName,
            last_event: (.lastEventTimestamp/1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")),
            storage_kb: (.storedBytes/1024 | floor)
        }]
    }'

echo ""
echo "Stream date coverage:"
aws logs describe-log-streams \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name-prefix "$INSTANCE_ID" \
    --query 'logStreams[*].logStreamName' \
    --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort
Output: Searching for log streams from instance: i-0abc123def456789 Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-production { "instance": "i-0abc123def456789", "total_streams": 7, "total_storage_kb": 8500, "streams": [ { "name": "i-0abc123def456789/2025/01/18", "last_event": "2025-01-18 14:55", "storage_kb": 1250 }, { "name": "i-0abc123def456789/2025/01/17", "last_event": "2025-01-17 23:59", "storage_kb": 2100 }, { "name": "i-0abc123def456789/2025/01/16", "last_event": "2025-01-16 23:58", "storage_kb": 1800 }, { "name": "i-0abc123def456789/2025/01/15", "last_event": "2025-01-15 23:55", "storage_kb": 1650 }, { "name": "i-0abc123def456789/2025/01/14", "last_event": "2025-01-14 23:50", "storage_kb": 1700 } ] } Stream date coverage: i-0abc123def456789/2025/01/14 i-0abc123def456789/2025/01/15 i-0abc123def456789/2025/01/16 i-0abc123def456789/2025/01/17 i-0abc123def456789/2025/01/18
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta import logging # Instance i-0abc123def456789 handles these operations: # Daily JobPost creation on this instance: instance_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( metadata__contains={'instance': 'i-0abc123def456789'} ) # Jobs per day for this instance from django.db.models.functions import TruncDate from django.db.models import Count daily_stats = instance_jobs.annotate( date=TruncDate('created_at') ).values('date').annotate( count=Count('id') ).order_by('-date')[:5] print("Jobs created per day on i-0abc123def456789:") for stat in daily_stats: print(f" {stat['date']}: {stat['count']} jobs") # Output: # 2025-01-18: 23 jobs # 2025-01-17: 45 jobs # 2025-01-16: 38 jobs # 2025-01-15: 35 jobs # 2025-01-14: 42 jobs # User registrations on this instance: users_on_instance = User.objects.filter( metadata__contains={'instance': 'i-0abc123def456789'} ).count() print(f"\nUsers registered on this instance: {users_on_instance}") # Output: Users registered on this instance: 35 # Storage correlates with operation volume print(f"\nTotal logs: 8.5 MB across 7 daily streams")
Example 3: Monitor Stream Health and Identify Stale Streams

Analyzes log streams to identify stale or inactive streams that haven't received events recently. Helps detect Django instances that may have stopped logging due to issues with Skills, User, Author, Location, or JobPost model operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Monitor stream health - identify stale streams
# Helps detect instances that stopped logging

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
STALE_THRESHOLD_HOURS=2

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Log Stream Health Monitor - Stale Stream Detection           ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Stale Threshold: $STALE_THRESHOLD_HOURS hours"
echo ""

# Calculate threshold timestamp
THRESHOLD_MS=$(( ($(date +%s) - ($STALE_THRESHOLD_HOURS * 3600)) * 1000 ))

# Get all streams
STREAMS=$(aws logs describe-log-streams \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --order-by LastEventTime \
    --descending \
    --output json)

# Analyze each stream
echo "Stream Health Status:"
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""

echo "🟢 ACTIVE STREAMS (events within $STALE_THRESHOLD_HOURS hours):"
echo "$STREAMS" | jq -r --arg threshold "$THRESHOLD_MS" '
    .logStreams[] | select(.lastEventTimestamp > ($threshold | tonumber)) |
    "   ✅ \(.logStreamName)
      Last event: \((.lastEventTimestamp/1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")))
      Storage: \((.storedBytes/1024 | floor)) KB"'

echo ""
echo "🔴 STALE STREAMS (no events for $STALE_THRESHOLD_HOURS+ hours):"
echo "$STREAMS" | jq -r --arg threshold "$THRESHOLD_MS" '
    .logStreams[] | select(.lastEventTimestamp <= ($threshold | tonumber)) |
    "   ⚠️  \(.logStreamName)
      Last event: \((.lastEventTimestamp/1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")))
      Hours since event: \(((now - (.lastEventTimestamp/1000)) / 3600 | floor))"'

echo ""
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"

# Summary counts
ACTIVE=$(echo "$STREAMS" | jq --arg threshold "$THRESHOLD_MS" '[.logStreams[] | select(.lastEventTimestamp > ($threshold | tonumber))] | length')
STALE=$(echo "$STREAMS" | jq --arg threshold "$THRESHOLD_MS" '[.logStreams[] | select(.lastEventTimestamp <= ($threshold | tonumber))] | length')

echo "Summary: $ACTIVE active, $STALE stale streams"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Log Stream Health Monitor - Stale Stream Detection ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-production Stale Threshold: 2 hours Stream Health Status: ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 🟢 ACTIVE STREAMS (events within 2 hours): ✅ web-server-01/2025/01/18 Last event: 2025-01-18 14:55:23 Storage: 1250 KB ✅ web-server-02/2025/01/18 Last event: 2025-01-18 14:54:45 Storage: 980 KB ✅ i-0abc123def/2025/01/18/14 Last event: 2025-01-18 14:52:10 Storage: 450 KB ✅ ecs-task-xyz/jobpost-container Last event: 2025-01-18 13:30:15 Storage: 3200 KB 🔴 STALE STREAMS (no events for 2+ hours): ⚠️ web-server-03/2025/01/18 Last event: 2025-01-18 10:15:30 Hours since event: 4 ⚠️ i-0dead999/2025/01/18 Last event: 2025-01-18 09:00:00 Hours since event: 6 ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Summary: 4 active, 2 stale streams
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta import logging # Stream health correlates with Django instance status # Active instances (web-server-01, web-server-02): # Currently processing model operations active_ops = JobPost.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=2) ).count() print(f"Active JobPost operations (2 hours): {active_ops}") # Output: Active JobPost operations (2 hours): 45 # Stale instances may indicate problems: # web-server-03: Stopped 4 hours ago - investigate! # i-0dead999: Stopped 6 hours ago - likely terminated # Check for incomplete operations on stale instances: stale_cutoff = datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=2) pending_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( status='processing', updated_at__lt=stale_cutoff ) print(f"\nPending jobs on stale instances: {pending_jobs.count()}") # Output: Pending jobs on stale instances: 3 for job in pending_jobs: print(f" - {job.title}: stuck since {job.updated_at}") # Output: # - Backend Dev: stuck since 2025-01-18 10:15:00 # - API Engineer: stuck since 2025-01-18 09:30:00 # Recommended action: Restart or replace stale instances # Re-process stuck JobPost records
Example 4: Generate Stream Inventory Report by Date Range

Creates a comprehensive inventory of log streams organized by date, showing storage usage and event activity patterns for Django application logs tracking all five models.

#!/bin/bash
# Generate stream inventory report by date
# Shows daily logging patterns

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"

echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Log Stream Inventory Report - Daily Pattern Analysis           ║"
echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Report Date: $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')"
echo ""

# Get all streams
ALL_STREAMS=$(aws logs describe-log-streams \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --order-by LogStreamName \
    --output json)

# Group by date pattern (assumes YYYY/MM/DD in stream names)
echo "Daily Stream Summary:"
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
printf "%-15s %10s %15s %15s\n" "Date" "Streams" "Total Storage" "Avg/Stream"
printf "%-15s %10s %15s %15s\n" "────" "───────" "─────────────" "──────────"

# Extract and group by date
echo "$ALL_STREAMS" | jq -r '
    .logStreams | 
    group_by(.logStreamName | capture("(?\\d{4}/\\d{2}/\\d{2})") | .date // "other") |
    .[] |
    {
        date: (.[0].logStreamName | capture("(?\\d{4}/\\d{2}/\\d{2})") | .date // "other"),
        count: length,
        total_bytes: ([.[].storedBytes] | add),
        avg_bytes: ([.[].storedBytes] | add / length)
    } |
    "\(.date)\t\(.count)\t\((.total_bytes/1048576*100|floor)/100) MB\t\((.avg_bytes/1024|floor)) KB"
' | sort -r | head -10 | while IFS=$'\t' read -r date count total avg; do
    printf "%-15s %10s %15s %15s\n" "$date" "$count" "$total" "$avg"
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"

# Server distribution
echo ""
echo "Stream Distribution by Server:"
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo "$ALL_STREAMS" | jq -r '
    .logStreams | 
    group_by(.logStreamName | split("/")[0]) |
    .[] |
    "\(.[0].logStreamName | split("/")[0]): \(length) streams, \(([.[].storedBytes] | add / 1048576 * 100 | floor) / 100) MB total"
' | head -10
Output: ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Log Stream Inventory Report - Daily Pattern Analysis ║ ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Report Date: 2025-01-18 15:00:00 Daily Stream Summary: ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Date Streams Total Storage Avg/Stream ──── ─────── ───────────── ────────── 2025/01/18 5 4.25 MB 870 KB 2025/01/17 5 8.50 MB 1740 KB 2025/01/16 5 7.80 MB 1596 KB 2025/01/15 5 6.90 MB 1413 KB 2025/01/14 5 7.20 MB 1475 KB 2025/01/13 4 5.50 MB 1408 KB 2025/01/12 4 4.80 MB 1229 KB ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Stream Distribution by Server: ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ web-server-01: 15 streams, 22.5 MB total web-server-02: 14 streams, 18.3 MB total i-0abc123def: 10 streams, 12.8 MB total ecs-task-xyz: 5 streams, 8.5 MB total i-0def456abc: 3 streams, 3.2 MB total
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models.functions import TruncDate from django.db.models import Count, Sum from datetime import datetime, timedelta # Stream inventory correlates with model activity patterns # Daily JobPost creation pattern: daily_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=7) ).annotate( date=TruncDate('created_at') ).values('date').annotate( count=Count('id') ).order_by('-date') print("JobPost creation by day:") for day in daily_jobs: print(f" {day['date']}: {day['count']} posts") # Output: # 2025-01-18: 45 posts # 2025-01-17: 78 posts # 2025-01-16: 65 posts # 2025-01-15: 52 posts # 2025-01-14: 60 posts # Server distribution matches stream distribution: # web-server-01: Primary JobPost handler # web-server-02: Secondary handler + Skills processing # ecs-task-xyz: Batch operations # Model operations per server: print("\nModel operation distribution:") print(f" JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f" Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") print(f" Users: {User.objects.count()}") print(f" Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") print(f" Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Output: # JobPosts: 250 # Skills: 45 # Users: 120 # Authors: 85 # Locations: 30
Example 5: Paginate Through All Streams for Large Log Groups

Demonstrates pagination to retrieve all log streams from large log groups that exceed the 50-stream limit per API call. Essential for comprehensive inventory of Django application logs across all instances.

#!/bin/bash
# Paginate through all streams in a large log group
# Handles groups with 100+ streams

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
OUTPUT_FILE="/tmp/all_streams.json"

echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Full Stream Inventory with Pagination                          ║"
echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo ""

# Initialize
NEXT_TOKEN=""
PAGE=1
TOTAL_STREAMS=0
echo "[]" > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Fetching all streams..."

while true; do
    echo -n "  Page $PAGE: "
    
    if [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        RESPONSE=$(aws logs describe-log-streams \
            --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
            --limit 50 \
            --output json)
    else
        RESPONSE=$(aws logs describe-log-streams \
            --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
            --limit 50 \
            --next-token "$NEXT_TOKEN" \
            --output json)
    fi
    
    # Count streams in this page
    PAGE_COUNT=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq '.logStreams | length')
    TOTAL_STREAMS=$((TOTAL_STREAMS + PAGE_COUNT))
    echo "$PAGE_COUNT streams"
    
    # Append to output file
    jq -s '.[0] + .[1].logStreams' "$OUTPUT_FILE" <(echo "$RESPONSE") > "${OUTPUT_FILE}.tmp"
    mv "${OUTPUT_FILE}.tmp" "$OUTPUT_FILE"
    
    # Check for next page
    NEXT_TOKEN=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.nextToken // empty')
    
    if [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        break
    fi
    
    ((PAGE++))
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo "Complete Inventory:"
echo "  Total pages: $PAGE"
echo "  Total streams: $TOTAL_STREAMS"
echo "  Output file: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""

# Generate summary statistics
echo "Stream Statistics:"
cat "$OUTPUT_FILE" | jq '
{
    total_streams: length,
    total_storage_mb: ([.[].storedBytes] | add / 1048576 | floor),
    oldest_stream: (sort_by(.creationTime) | .[0].logStreamName),
    newest_stream: (sort_by(.creationTime) | .[-1].logStreamName),
    most_active: (sort_by(.lastEventTimestamp) | .[-1].logStreamName),
    largest_stream: (sort_by(.storedBytes) | .[-1] | {name: .logStreamName, size_kb: (.storedBytes/1024 | floor)})
}'

echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
Output: ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Full Stream Inventory with Pagination ║ ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-production Fetching all streams... Page 1: 50 streams Page 2: 50 streams Page 3: 35 streams ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Complete Inventory: Total pages: 3 Total streams: 135 Output file: /tmp/all_streams.json Stream Statistics: { "total_streams": 135, "total_storage_mb": 485, "oldest_stream": "web-server-01/2024/12/01", "newest_stream": "i-0new789xyz/2025/01/18", "most_active": "web-server-01/2025/01/18", "largest_stream": { "name": "batch-processor/weekly-report/2025-01-15", "size_kb": 15360 } } ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime import json # Full stream inventory context for Django models # 135 streams = comprehensive logging coverage # Correlates with model operation history: print("Django Model Database Summary:") print("=" * 50) # JobPost model (most logging activity) total_jobs = JobPost.objects.count() active_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"JobPost: {total_jobs} total, {active_jobs} active") # Output: JobPost: 250 total, 180 active # User model total_users = User.objects.count() active_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"User: {total_users} total, {active_users} active") # Output: User: 120 total, 110 active # Author model (linked to users) total_authors = Author.objects.count() print(f"Author: {total_authors} total") # Output: Author: 85 total # Skills model total_skills = Skills.objects.count() used_skills = Skills.objects.filter(jobpost__isnull=False).distinct().count() print(f"Skills: {total_skills} total, {used_skills} used in jobs") # Output: Skills: 45 total, 35 used in jobs # Location model total_locations = Location.objects.count() print(f"Location: {total_locations} total") # Output: Location: 30 total # Stream-to-model correlation: # - 485 MB total logs across 135 streams # - Average 3.6 MB per stream # - Largest stream (15 MB): weekly batch reports # - Most active: current day's JobPost operations print("\nLog coverage period: 2024-12-01 to 2025-01-18 (49 days)") print(f"Average streams per day: {135 // 49}")

put-log-events

Uploads a batch of log events to a specified log stream in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. This is the primary method for sending Django application logs to CloudWatch, enabling centralized monitoring of Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations. Events must be in chronological order within each batch and contain timestamps within 14 days of the current time.

Syntax
aws logs put-log-events \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    --log-stream-name <value> \
    --log-events <value> \
    [--sequence-token <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>] \
    [--generate-cli-skeleton <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --log-group-name (string, required): The name of the log group where events will be stored. Example: "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
  • --log-stream-name (string, required): The name of the log stream within the group. Must exist before sending events
  • --log-events (list, required): Array of log event objects. Each event has: timestamp (milliseconds since epoch), message (string up to 256KB). Events must be chronologically ordered
  • --sequence-token (string): Token for ordering events in the stream. No longer required for newer API calls but still supported for backwards compatibility
  • --cli-input-json (string): Reads arguments from JSON file. Useful for large batches of log events
Return Value
  • nextSequenceToken (string): Token for the next put-log-events call (deprecated but still returned)
  • rejectedLogEventsInfo (object): Information about rejected events if any failed validation
  • tooNewLogEventStartIndex (integer): Index of first event with timestamp too far in the future
  • tooOldLogEventEndIndex (integer): Index of last event with timestamp too far in the past
  • expiredLogEventEndIndex (integer): Index of last event older than retention period
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Log Django JobPost Creation Events

Sends structured log events for JobPost model creation operations including all related data such as associated Skills, Author, and Location. Demonstrates proper timestamp formatting and JSON message structure for Django ORM operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Log Django JobPost creation events
# Captures full model operation details

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
LOG_STREAM="web-server-01/2025/01/18"

# Get current timestamp in milliseconds
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%s)000

echo "Logging JobPost creation events to CloudWatch"
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Stream: $LOG_STREAM"
echo ""

# Send JobPost creation log events
aws logs put-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    --log-events "[
        {
            \"timestamp\": $TIMESTAMP,
            \"message\": \"{\\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"JobPost.create\\\", \\\"id\\\": 156, \\\"title\\\": \\\"Senior Python Developer\\\", \\\"company\\\": \\\"TechCorp Inc\\\", \\\"location_id\\\": 12, \\\"author_id\\\": 45, \\\"salary_min\\\": 120000, \\\"salary_max\\\": 180000, \\\"remote\\\": true}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 100)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"Skills.attach\\\", \\\"jobpost_id\\\": 156, \\\"skills\\\": [\\\"Python\\\", \\\"Django\\\", \\\"AWS\\\", \\\"Docker\\\", \\\"PostgreSQL\\\"], \\\"count\\\": 5}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 200)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"JobPost.publish\\\", \\\"id\\\": 156, \\\"status\\\": \\\"active\\\", \\\"visibility\\\": \\\"public\\\", \\\"expires_at\\\": \\\"2025-02-18T00:00:00Z\\\"}\"
        }
    ]"

echo "Events logged successfully"
Output: Logging JobPost creation events to CloudWatch Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Stream: web-server-01/2025/01/18 { "nextSequenceToken": "49641234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789012345678" } Events logged successfully
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import logging import json # Django code that generates these log events: logger = logging.getLogger('django.jobpost') # Create location location = Location.objects.get(id=12) print(f"Location: {location.city}, {location.state}") # Output: Location: San Francisco, California # Get author author = Author.objects.get(id=45) print(f"Author: {author.user.username}") # Output: Author: john_developer # Create JobPost job = JobPost.objects.create( title="Senior Python Developer", company="TechCorp Inc", location=location, author=author, salary_min=120000, salary_max=180000, is_remote=True ) logger.info(json.dumps({ "level": "INFO", "event": "JobPost.create", "id": job.id, "title": job.title, "company": job.company })) print(f"Created JobPost: {job}") # Output: Created JobPost: Senior Python Developer at TechCorp Inc # Attach skills skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__in=["Python", "Django", "AWS", "Docker", "PostgreSQL"]) job.required_skills.set(skills) logger.info(json.dumps({ "event": "Skills.attach", "jobpost_id": job.id, "skills": list(skills.values_list('name', flat=True)) })) print(f"Skills attached: {list(job.required_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))}") # Output: Skills attached: ['Python', 'Django', 'AWS', 'Docker', 'PostgreSQL']
Example 2: Log User Registration and Author Profile Creation

Sends log events tracking the complete user registration flow including User model creation, Author profile setup, and Skills association. Captures all related model operations in a single transaction context.

#!/bin/bash
# Log user registration flow events
# Tracks User, Author, and Skills creation

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj/users"
LOG_STREAM="auth-service/2025/01/18"

TIMESTAMP=$(date +%s)000
REQUEST_ID="req-$(uuidgen | cut -c1-8)"

echo "Logging user registration events"
echo "Request ID: $REQUEST_ID"
echo ""

aws logs put-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    --log-events "[
        {
            \"timestamp\": $TIMESTAMP,
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"User.create_start\\\", \\\"username\\\": \\\"jane_developer\\\", \\\"email\\\": \\\"jane@techcorp.com\\\", \\\"ip\\\": \\\"192.168.1.100\\\"}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 50)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"User.create_success\\\", \\\"user_id\\\": 121, \\\"username\\\": \\\"jane_developer\\\", \\\"duration_ms\\\": 45}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 100)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"Author.create\\\", \\\"author_id\\\": 86, \\\"user_id\\\": 121, \\\"bio\\\": \\\"Full-stack developer with 8 years experience\\\"}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 150)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"Skills.associate\\\", \\\"author_id\\\": 86, \\\"skills\\\": [\\\"JavaScript\\\", \\\"React\\\", \\\"Node.js\\\", \\\"Python\\\"], \\\"count\\\": 4}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 200)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"Location.set\\\", \\\"author_id\\\": 86, \\\"location\\\": \\\"New York, NY\\\", \\\"remote_preferred\\\": true}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 250)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"registration_complete\\\", \\\"user_id\\\": 121, \\\"author_id\\\": 86, \\\"total_duration_ms\\\": 245}\"
        }
    ]"

echo ""
echo "Registration flow logged successfully"
Output: Logging user registration events Request ID: req-a1b2c3d4 { "nextSequenceToken": "49641234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789012345679" } Registration flow logged successfully
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db import transaction import logging import uuid logger = logging.getLogger('django.users') request_id = f"req-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}" # Complete registration flow with logging with transaction.atomic(): # Create User user = User.objects.create_user( username="jane_developer", email="jane@techcorp.com", password="secure_password_123" ) logger.info(f"User.create_success user_id={user.id}") print(f"User created: {user.username} (ID: {user.id})") # Output: User created: jane_developer (ID: 121) # Create Author profile author = Author.objects.create( user=user, bio="Full-stack developer with 8 years experience", website="https://jane-dev.com" ) logger.info(f"Author.create author_id={author.id}") print(f"Author profile: {author.bio[:30]}... (ID: {author.id})") # Output: Author profile: Full-stack developer with 8 y... (ID: 86) # Associate skills skill_names = ["JavaScript", "React", "Node.js", "Python"] skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__in=skill_names) author.skills.set(skills) print(f"Skills associated: {list(skills.values_list('name', flat=True))}") # Output: Skills associated: ['JavaScript', 'React', 'Node.js', 'Python'] # Set location preference location = Location.objects.get(city="New York", state="NY") author.preferred_location = location author.remote_preferred = True author.save() print(f"Location set: {location.city}, {location.state}") # Output: Location set: New York, NY print(f"\nRegistration complete for {user.username}") # Output: Registration complete for jane_developer
Example 3: Log Model Validation Errors with Stack Traces

Sends error-level log events capturing validation failures, exceptions, and stack traces from Django model operations. Essential for debugging issues with Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost data validation.

#!/bin/bash
# Log model validation errors with full context
# Captures exceptions and stack traces

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
LOG_STREAM="validation-errors/2025/01/18"

TIMESTAMP=$(date +%s)000

echo "Logging model validation errors"
echo ""

# Create log stream if it doesn't exist
aws logs create-log-stream \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    2>/dev/null

# Send validation error events
aws logs put-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    --log-events "[
        {
            \"timestamp\": $TIMESTAMP,
            \"message\": \"{\\\"level\\\": \\\"ERROR\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"ValidationError\\\", \\\"model\\\": \\\"JobPost\\\", \\\"field\\\": \\\"salary_min\\\", \\\"value\\\": -50000, \\\"error\\\": \\\"Salary cannot be negative\\\", \\\"traceback\\\": \\\"django.core.exceptions.ValidationError at jobpost/models.py:125\\\"}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 500)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"level\\\": \\\"ERROR\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"DoesNotExist\\\", \\\"model\\\": \\\"Author\\\", \\\"query\\\": \\\"Author.objects.get(user_id=999)\\\", \\\"error\\\": \\\"Author matching query does not exist\\\", \\\"request_path\\\": \\\"/api/jobpost/create/\\\"}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 1000)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"level\\\": \\\"ERROR\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"IntegrityError\\\", \\\"model\\\": \\\"Skills\\\", \\\"field\\\": \\\"name\\\", \\\"value\\\": \\\"Python\\\", \\\"error\\\": \\\"UNIQUE constraint failed: jobpost_skills.name\\\", \\\"sql\\\": \\\"INSERT INTO jobpost_skills (name) VALUES ('Python')\\\"}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 1500)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"level\\\": \\\"ERROR\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"ForeignKeyViolation\\\", \\\"model\\\": \\\"JobPost\\\", \\\"field\\\": \\\"location_id\\\", \\\"value\\\": 9999, \\\"error\\\": \\\"Location with id 9999 does not exist\\\", \\\"suggested_fix\\\": \\\"Verify location_id exists before creating JobPost\\\"}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((TIMESTAMP + 2000)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"level\\\": \\\"WARNING\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"DeprecationWarning\\\", \\\"model\\\": \\\"User\\\", \\\"field\\\": \\\"username\\\", \\\"message\\\": \\\"max_length will be reduced from 150 to 100 in next version\\\", \\\"affected_records\\\": 3}\"
        }
    ]"

echo "Validation errors logged"
echo ""

# Verify events were stored
echo "Verifying logged events:"
aws logs get-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    --limit 5 \
    --query 'events[*].message' \
    --output text | head -3
Output: Logging model validation errors { "nextSequenceToken": "49641234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789012345680" } Validation errors logged Verifying logged events: {"level": "ERROR", "event": "ValidationError", "model": "JobPost", ...} {"level": "ERROR", "event": "DoesNotExist", "model": "Author", ...} {"level": "ERROR", "event": "IntegrityError", "model": "Skills", ...}
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError from django.db import IntegrityError import logging logger = logging.getLogger('django.validation') # Error examples that generate these log events: # 1. ValidationError - negative salary try: job = JobPost( title="Developer", company="Test", salary_min=-50000 # Invalid! ) job.full_clean() except ValidationError as e: logger.error(f"ValidationError: {e.message_dict}") print(f"Validation failed: {e}") # Output: Validation failed: {'salary_min': ['Salary cannot be negative']} # 2. DoesNotExist - invalid user reference try: author = Author.objects.get(user_id=999) except Author.DoesNotExist as e: logger.error(f"Author.DoesNotExist: user_id=999") print(f"Author not found: {e}") # Output: Author not found: Author matching query does not exist. # 3. IntegrityError - duplicate skill try: Skills.objects.create(name="Python") # Already exists! except IntegrityError as e: logger.error(f"IntegrityError: {e}") print(f"Duplicate skill: {e}") # Output: Duplicate skill: UNIQUE constraint failed # 4. ForeignKey violation try: JobPost.objects.create( title="Test Job", location_id=9999 # Invalid location! ) except Exception as e: logger.error(f"ForeignKey violation: {e}") print(f"Invalid reference: {e}") # Output: Invalid reference: Location with id 9999 does not exist # Check for long usernames (deprecation warning) long_usernames = User.objects.filter(username__regex=r'^.{101,}$') print(f"Users with long usernames: {long_usernames.count()}") # Output: Users with long usernames: 3
Example 4: Batch Log Events from Django Management Command

Sends a large batch of log events from a Django management command execution. Captures the full lifecycle of a data migration or batch processing job affecting multiple models.

#!/bin/bash
# Batch log events from management command
# Logs full job lifecycle with metrics

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
LOG_STREAM="mgmt-commands/2025/01/18"

JOB_ID="batch-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
START_TIME=$(date +%s)000

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Logging Batch Job: $JOB_ID                                   ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Create events file for large batch
cat > /tmp/batch_events.json << EOF
[
    {"timestamp": $START_TIME, "message": "{\"job_id\": \"$JOB_ID\", \"level\": \"INFO\", \"event\": \"batch_start\", \"command\": \"update_job_statuses\", \"total_records\": 500}"},
    {"timestamp": $((START_TIME + 1000)), "message": "{\"job_id\": \"$JOB_ID\", \"level\": \"INFO\", \"event\": \"batch_progress\", \"processed\": 100, \"percent\": 20, \"model\": \"JobPost\"}"},
    {"timestamp": $((START_TIME + 2000)), "message": "{\"job_id\": \"$JOB_ID\", \"level\": \"INFO\", \"event\": \"batch_progress\", \"processed\": 200, \"percent\": 40, \"model\": \"JobPost\", \"updated\": 45}"},
    {"timestamp": $((START_TIME + 3000)), "message": "{\"job_id\": \"$JOB_ID\", \"level\": \"INFO\", \"event\": \"skills_sync\", \"model\": \"Skills\", \"new_skills\": 5, \"removed_skills\": 2}"},
    {"timestamp": $((START_TIME + 4000)), "message": "{\"job_id\": \"$JOB_ID\", \"level\": \"INFO\", \"event\": \"batch_progress\", \"processed\": 300, \"percent\": 60, \"model\": \"JobPost\", \"updated\": 85}"},
    {"timestamp": $((START_TIME + 5000)), "message": "{\"job_id\": \"$JOB_ID\", \"level\": \"WARNING\", \"event\": \"stale_authors\", \"model\": \"Author\", \"count\": 12, \"message\": \"Authors without recent activity\"}"},
    {"timestamp": $((START_TIME + 6000)), "message": "{\"job_id\": \"$JOB_ID\", \"level\": \"INFO\", \"event\": \"location_update\", \"model\": \"Location\", \"updated\": 8, \"message\": \"Updated remote_allowed flags\"}"},
    {"timestamp": $((START_TIME + 7000)), "message": "{\"job_id\": \"$JOB_ID\", \"level\": \"INFO\", \"event\": \"batch_progress\", \"processed\": 400, \"percent\": 80, \"model\": \"JobPost\", \"updated\": 120}"},
    {"timestamp": $((START_TIME + 8000)), "message": "{\"job_id\": \"$JOB_ID\", \"level\": \"INFO\", \"event\": \"user_notifications\", \"model\": \"User\", \"notified\": 95, \"message\": \"Job expiry notifications sent\"}"},
    {"timestamp": $((START_TIME + 9000)), "message": "{\"job_id\": \"$JOB_ID\", \"level\": \"INFO\", \"event\": \"batch_complete\", \"processed\": 500, \"updated\": 156, \"errors\": 3, \"duration_sec\": 9, \"status\": \"success\"}"}
]
EOF

# Send batch using file input
aws logs put-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    --log-events file:///tmp/batch_events.json

echo "Batch job logged: $JOB_ID"
echo "Events sent: 10"
echo ""

# Cleanup
rm /tmp/batch_events.json
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Logging Batch Job: batch-20250118150000 ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ { "nextSequenceToken": "49641234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789012345681" } Batch job logged: batch-20250118150000 Events sent: 10
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from datetime import datetime, timedelta import logging logger = logging.getLogger('django.management') # Management command that generates these logs: class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Update job statuses and sync related models' def handle(self, *args, **options): job_id = f"batch-{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}" # Get stale jobs stale_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( expires_at__lt=datetime.now(), status='active' ) total = stale_jobs.count() logger.info(f"batch_start total_records={total}") # Process in batches updated = 0 for i, job in enumerate(stale_jobs, 1): job.status = 'expired' job.save() updated += 1 if i % 100 == 0: logger.info(f"batch_progress processed={i} percent={i*100//total}") logger.info(f"batch_complete updated={updated}") # Simulated execution results: print("Batch Job Results:") print("=" * 50) # JobPost updates expired_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(status='expired') print(f"Expired JobPosts: {expired_jobs.count()}") # Output: Expired JobPosts: 156 # Skills sync new_skills = Skills.objects.filter(created_at__date=datetime.today().date()) print(f"New Skills added: {new_skills.count()}") # Output: New Skills added: 5 # Stale authors stale_authors = Author.objects.filter( user__last_login__lt=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=90) ) print(f"Stale Authors: {stale_authors.count()}") # Output: Stale Authors: 12 # Location updates remote_locations = Location.objects.filter(remote_allowed=True) print(f"Remote-friendly Locations: {remote_locations.count()}") # Output: Remote-friendly Locations: 18 # User notifications notified_users = User.objects.filter( author__jobpost__expires_at__lt=datetime.now() + timedelta(days=7) ).distinct() print(f"Users notified: {notified_users.count()}") # Output: Users notified: 95
Example 5: Real-time API Request Logging with Response Times

Logs API request/response events in real-time with detailed timing metrics, query counts, and model access patterns. Enables performance monitoring of Django REST endpoints serving JobPost, Skills, and User data.

#!/bin/bash
# Real-time API request logging with metrics
# Captures request lifecycle and performance data

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-api"
LOG_STREAM="api-gateway/2025/01/18"

REQUEST_ID="api-$(uuidgen | cut -c1-8)"
START_TIME=$(date +%s%3N)  # Milliseconds

echo "Logging API request: $REQUEST_ID"

# Simulate API request lifecycle
sleep 0.1  # Simulate processing

END_TIME=$(date +%s%3N)
DURATION=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))

aws logs put-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    --log-events "[
        {
            \"timestamp\": ${START_TIME},
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"request_start\\\", \\\"method\\\": \\\"GET\\\", \\\"path\\\": \\\"/api/v1/jobposts/\\\", \\\"query_params\\\": {\\\"skills\\\": \\\"Python,Django\\\", \\\"location\\\": \\\"remote\\\", \\\"limit\\\": 20}, \\\"user_id\\\": 45, \\\"ip\\\": \\\"192.168.1.100\\\"}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((START_TIME + 5)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"DEBUG\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"db_query\\\", \\\"model\\\": \\\"Skills\\\", \\\"query\\\": \\\"Skills.objects.filter(name__in=[...])\\\", \\\"rows\\\": 2, \\\"time_ms\\\": 3}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((START_TIME + 15)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"DEBUG\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"db_query\\\", \\\"model\\\": \\\"Location\\\", \\\"query\\\": \\\"Location.objects.filter(remote_allowed=True)\\\", \\\"rows\\\": 18, \\\"time_ms\\\": 8}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((START_TIME + 35)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"DEBUG\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"db_query\\\", \\\"model\\\": \\\"JobPost\\\", \\\"query\\\": \\\"JobPost.objects.filter(...).select_related().prefetch_related()\\\", \\\"rows\\\": 20, \\\"time_ms\\\": 25}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((START_TIME + 45)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"DEBUG\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"serialization\\\", \\\"serializer\\\": \\\"JobPostSerializer\\\", \\\"records\\\": 20, \\\"time_ms\\\": 8}\"
        },
        {
            \"timestamp\": $((START_TIME + DURATION)),
            \"message\": \"{\\\"request_id\\\": \\\"$REQUEST_ID\\\", \\\"level\\\": \\\"INFO\\\", \\\"event\\\": \\\"request_complete\\\", \\\"status_code\\\": 200, \\\"response_size\\\": 15240, \\\"total_time_ms\\\": $DURATION, \\\"db_queries\\\": 3, \\\"db_time_ms\\\": 36, \\\"cache_hits\\\": 2, \\\"cache_misses\\\": 1}\"
        }
    ]"

echo ""
echo "Request logged:"
echo "  Request ID: $REQUEST_ID"
echo "  Duration: ${DURATION}ms"
echo "  Status: 200 OK"
Output: Logging API request: api-f4e5d6c7 { "nextSequenceToken": "49641234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789012345682" } Request logged: Request ID: api-f4e5d6c7 Duration: 105ms Status: 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from rest_framework import serializers, viewsets from django.db import connection import time import logging logger = logging.getLogger('django.api') # API View that generates these log events: class JobPostViewSet(viewsets.ModelViewSet): def list(self, request): request_id = f"api-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}" start_time = time.time() # Parse query params skill_names = request.query_params.get('skills', '').split(',') location_filter = request.query_params.get('location') limit = int(request.query_params.get('limit', 20)) # Query 1: Get skills skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__in=skill_names) logger.debug(f"db_query model=Skills rows={skills.count()}") print(f"Skills found: {list(skills.values_list('name', flat=True))}") # Output: Skills found: ['Python', 'Django'] # Query 2: Get locations if location_filter == 'remote': locations = Location.objects.filter(remote_allowed=True) logger.debug(f"db_query model=Location rows={locations.count()}") print(f"Remote locations: {locations.count()}") # Output: Remote locations: 18 # Query 3: Get job posts with relations jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( required_skills__in=skills, location__in=locations, is_active=True ).select_related( 'location', 'author__user' ).prefetch_related( 'required_skills' ).distinct()[:limit] logger.debug(f"db_query model=JobPost rows={len(jobs)}") print(f"Jobs returned: {len(jobs)}") # Output: Jobs returned: 20 # Serialize serializer = JobPostSerializer(jobs, many=True) # Log completion duration_ms = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000 logger.info(f"request_complete status=200 time_ms={duration_ms:.0f}") # Query analysis print(f"\nQuery Summary:") print(f" Total queries: {len(connection.queries)}") print(f" Total DB time: {sum(float(q['time']) for q in connection.queries)*1000:.0f}ms") # Output: # Total queries: 3 # Total DB time: 36ms return Response(serializer.data)

get-log-events

Retrieves log events from a specific log stream in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Returns events in chronological order with support for time range filtering and pagination. Essential for debugging Django application issues by retrieving detailed logs for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations during specific time windows.

Syntax
aws logs get-log-events \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    --log-stream-name <value> \
    [--start-time <value>] \
    [--end-time <value>] \
    [--next-token <value>] \
    [--limit <value>] \
    [--start-from-head | --no-start-from-head] \
    [--unmask] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --log-group-name (string, required): The name of the log group containing the stream. Example: "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
  • --log-stream-name (string, required): The name of the log stream to read from. Example: "web-server-01/2025/01/18"
  • --start-time (long): Start of time range in milliseconds since epoch. Events with earlier timestamps are excluded
  • --end-time (long): End of time range in milliseconds since epoch. Events with later timestamps are excluded
  • --next-token (string): Token from previous response for pagination. Use to get next page of results
  • --limit (integer): Maximum number of events to return (1-10000). Default: 10000
  • --start-from-head: Read events from oldest to newest (chronological)
  • --no-start-from-head: Read events from newest to oldest (reverse chronological)
  • --unmask: Display log content unmasked if data protection is enabled
Return Value
  • events (Array): List of log event objects
  • timestamp (long): Event timestamp in milliseconds since epoch
  • message (string): The log message content
  • ingestionTime (long): When CloudWatch received the event
  • nextForwardToken (string): Token for getting next (newer) events
  • nextBackwardToken (string): Token for getting previous (older) events
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Retrieve Recent Django Application Logs with Timestamps

Retrieves the most recent log events from a Django web server stream, showing JobPost and Skills model operations with human-readable timestamps. Useful for real-time monitoring and quick debugging.

#!/bin/bash
# Retrieve recent Django application logs
# Shows model operations with formatted timestamps

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
LOG_STREAM="web-server-01/2025/01/18"
LIMIT=20

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Recent Django Application Logs                               ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Stream: $LOG_STREAM"
echo "Retrieving last $LIMIT events..."
echo ""

# Get recent events (newest first by default)
aws logs get-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    --limit $LIMIT \
    --output json | jq -r '
    .events | reverse | .[] |
    "\((.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))) | \(.message)"
' | while read -r line; do
    # Color code by log level
    if [[ "$line" == *"ERROR"* ]]; then
        echo -e "\033[31m$line\033[0m"  # Red
    elif [[ "$line" == *"WARNING"* ]]; then
        echo -e "\033[33m$line\033[0m"  # Yellow
    elif [[ "$line" == *"INFO"* ]]; then
        echo -e "\033[32m$line\033[0m"  # Green
    else
        echo "$line"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Recent Django Application Logs ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Stream: web-server-01/2025/01/18 Retrieving last 20 events... 2025-01-18 15:05:23 | {"level": "INFO", "event": "JobPost.create", "id": 251, "title": "DevOps Engineer"} 2025-01-18 15:05:24 | {"level": "INFO", "event": "Skills.attach", "jobpost_id": 251, "count": 4} 2025-01-18 15:04:15 | {"level": "INFO", "event": "JobPost.update", "id": 245, "field": "status", "value": "active"} 2025-01-18 15:03:45 | {"level": "WARNING", "event": "slow_query", "model": "JobPost", "time_ms": 1250} 2025-01-18 15:02:30 | {"level": "INFO", "event": "User.login", "user_id": 45, "ip": "192.168.1.100"} 2025-01-18 15:01:12 | {"level": "ERROR", "event": "ValidationError", "model": "JobPost", "field": "salary_min"} 2025-01-18 15:00:55 | {"level": "INFO", "event": "Author.update", "id": 32, "field": "bio"} 2025-01-18 15:00:30 | {"level": "INFO", "event": "Location.query", "filter": "remote_allowed=True", "count": 18} ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime import json # Log events correspond to these Django operations: # JobPost.create event (15:05:23) job = JobPost.objects.get(id=251) print(f"Recent JobPost: {job.title}") # Output: Recent JobPost: DevOps Engineer # Skills.attach event (15:05:24) attached_skills = job.required_skills.all() print(f"Skills attached: {list(attached_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))}") # Output: Skills attached: ['Python', 'Docker', 'Kubernetes', 'AWS'] # JobPost.update event (15:04:15) updated_job = JobPost.objects.get(id=245) print(f"Updated job status: {updated_job.status}") # Output: Updated job status: active # Slow query warning (15:03:45) # Complex query that triggered the warning: complex_query = JobPost.objects.select_related( 'location', 'author__user' ).prefetch_related( 'required_skills' ).filter( is_active=True, salary_min__gte=100000 ).order_by('-created_at') print(f"Complex query results: {complex_query.count()}") # Output: Complex query results: 85 # User.login event (15:02:30) user = User.objects.get(id=45) print(f"Logged in user: {user.username}") # Output: Logged in user: john_developer # Location.query event (15:00:30) remote_locations = Location.objects.filter(remote_allowed=True) print(f"Remote locations: {remote_locations.count()}") # Output: Remote locations: 18
Example 2: Retrieve Logs for Specific Time Window (Incident Investigation)

Fetches log events from a specific time window during an incident. Useful for investigating issues that occurred at known times affecting JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location model operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Retrieve logs for incident time window
# Investigate issues during specific period

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
LOG_STREAM="web-server-01/2025/01/18"

# Define incident window (14:30 - 14:45)
START_TIME=$(date -d "2025-01-18 14:30:00" +%s)000
END_TIME=$(date -d "2025-01-18 14:45:00" +%s)000

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Incident Investigation - Time Window Analysis                ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Incident Window: 14:30:00 - 14:45:00"
echo "Log Stream: $LOG_STREAM"
echo ""

# Get events in time window
EVENTS=$(aws logs get-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    --start-time $START_TIME \
    --end-time $END_TIME \
    --start-from-head \
    --output json)

# Count events by level
TOTAL=$(echo "$EVENTS" | jq '.events | length')
ERRORS=$(echo "$EVENTS" | jq '[.events[].message | select(contains("ERROR"))] | length')
WARNINGS=$(echo "$EVENTS" | jq '[.events[].message | select(contains("WARNING"))] | length')

echo "Event Summary:"
echo "  Total events: $TOTAL"
echo "  Errors: $ERRORS"
echo "  Warnings: $WARNINGS"
echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""

# Show errors first
echo "🔴 ERRORS during incident window:"
echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '
    .events[] | 
    select(.message | contains("ERROR")) |
    "  \((.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%H:%M:%S"))) - \(.message)"
'

echo ""
echo "🟡 WARNINGS during incident window:"
echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '
    .events[] | 
    select(.message | contains("WARNING")) |
    "  \((.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%H:%M:%S"))) - \(.message)"
' | head -10

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Incident Investigation - Time Window Analysis ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Incident Window: 14:30:00 - 14:45:00 Log Stream: web-server-01/2025/01/18 Event Summary: Total events: 156 Errors: 12 Warnings: 23 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ 🔴 ERRORS during incident window: 14:31:15 - {"level": "ERROR", "event": "DatabaseError", "message": "connection timeout"} 14:32:02 - {"level": "ERROR", "event": "JobPost.save", "id": 248, "error": "DB write failed"} 14:32:45 - {"level": "ERROR", "event": "Skills.query", "error": "QuerySet timeout"} 14:33:10 - {"level": "ERROR", "event": "User.authenticate", "error": "DB connection lost"} 14:34:22 - {"level": "ERROR", "event": "Author.get", "id": 45, "error": "Connection refused"} 14:35:00 - {"level": "ERROR", "event": "Location.filter", "error": "Database unavailable"} 🟡 WARNINGS during incident window: 14:30:45 - {"level": "WARNING", "event": "slow_query", "model": "JobPost", "time_ms": 2500} 14:31:00 - {"level": "WARNING", "event": "connection_pool", "available": 2, "max": 20} 14:31:30 - {"level": "WARNING", "event": "retry_attempt", "attempt": 1, "operation": "JobPost.create"} ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime import logging # Incident analysis context: # Database connection issues between 14:30 - 14:45 # Affected operations during incident: # Failed JobPost saves failed_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( updated_at__range=[ datetime(2025, 1, 18, 14, 30), datetime(2025, 1, 18, 14, 45) ], status='error' ) print(f"Jobs affected by incident: {failed_jobs.count()}") # Output: Jobs affected by incident: 8 # Retry queue after recovery from django.core.cache import cache retry_queue = cache.get('jobpost_retry_queue', []) print(f"Jobs pending retry: {len(retry_queue)}") # Output: Jobs pending retry: 8 # User sessions impacted affected_users = User.objects.filter( last_login__range=[ datetime(2025, 1, 18, 14, 30), datetime(2025, 1, 18, 14, 45) ] ) print(f"Users affected: {affected_users.count()}") # Output: Users affected: 15 # Recovery verification post_incident = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gt=datetime(2025, 1, 18, 14, 45) ).count() print(f"Jobs created after recovery: {post_incident}") # Output: Jobs created after recovery: 25 # Root cause: Database connection pool exhausted # Resolution: Increased pool size, added connection timeout handling
Example 3: Paginate Through Large Log Stream

Demonstrates pagination to retrieve all events from a large log stream that exceeds the single request limit. Essential for comprehensive log analysis of Django model operations over extended periods.

#!/bin/bash
# Paginate through large log stream
# Retrieves all events using pagination tokens

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
LOG_STREAM="batch-processor/2025/01/18"
OUTPUT_FILE="/tmp/all_events.json"
PAGE_SIZE=1000

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Full Log Stream Extraction with Pagination                   ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Initialize
NEXT_TOKEN=""
PAGE=1
TOTAL_EVENTS=0
echo "[]" > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Starting pagination from head (oldest first)..."

while true; do
    echo -n "  Page $PAGE: "
    
    if [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        RESPONSE=$(aws logs get-log-events \
            --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
            --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
            --start-from-head \
            --limit $PAGE_SIZE \
            --output json)
    else
        RESPONSE=$(aws logs get-log-events \
            --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
            --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
            --start-from-head \
            --limit $PAGE_SIZE \
            --next-token "$NEXT_TOKEN" \
            --output json)
    fi
    
    # Count events in this page
    PAGE_EVENTS=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq '.events | length')
    TOTAL_EVENTS=$((TOTAL_EVENTS + PAGE_EVENTS))
    echo "$PAGE_EVENTS events"
    
    # Append to output file
    jq -s '.[0] + .[1].events' "$OUTPUT_FILE" <(echo "$RESPONSE") > "${OUTPUT_FILE}.tmp"
    mv "${OUTPUT_FILE}.tmp" "$OUTPUT_FILE"
    
    # Check for end of stream (empty events or same token)
    NEW_TOKEN=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | jq -r '.nextForwardToken')
    
    if [ "$PAGE_EVENTS" -eq 0 ] || [ "$NEW_TOKEN" == "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        break
    fi
    
    NEXT_TOKEN="$NEW_TOKEN"
    ((PAGE++))
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo "Extraction Complete:"
echo "  Total pages: $PAGE"
echo "  Total events: $TOTAL_EVENTS"
echo "  Output file: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""

# Analyze extracted events
echo "Event Analysis:"
cat "$OUTPUT_FILE" | jq '
{
    total_events: length,
    time_range: {
        first: (sort_by(.timestamp) | .[0].timestamp/1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")),
        last: (sort_by(.timestamp) | .[-1].timestamp/1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
    },
    by_level: (group_by(.message | fromjson? | .level // "UNKNOWN") | map({key: .[0].message | fromjson? | .level // "UNKNOWN", value: length}) | from_entries)
}'
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Full Log Stream Extraction with Pagination ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Starting pagination from head (oldest first)... Page 1: 1000 events Page 2: 1000 events Page 3: 1000 events Page 4: 1000 events Page 5: 850 events Page 6: 0 events ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Extraction Complete: Total pages: 6 Total events: 4850 Output file: /tmp/all_events.json Event Analysis: { "total_events": 4850, "time_range": { "first": "2025-01-18 00:00:15", "last": "2025-01-18 23:59:45" }, "by_level": { "INFO": 4200, "WARNING": 520, "ERROR": 85, "DEBUG": 45 } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count from datetime import datetime, timedelta import json # Full day log analysis context: # 4850 events = comprehensive model operation coverage # Event distribution by model: # Parse log file to count by model model_counts = { 'JobPost': 2800, 'Skills': 850, 'User': 620, 'Author': 380, 'Location': 200 } print("Model Operation Distribution:") for model, count in model_counts.items(): pct = count * 100 // 4850 print(f" {model}: {count} events ({pct}%)") # Output: # JobPost: 2800 events (57%) # Skills: 850 events (17%) # User: 620 events (12%) # Author: 380 events (7%) # Location: 200 events (4%) # Verify against database operations today = datetime.now().date() jobs_today = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__date=today ).count() print(f"\nJobPosts created today: {jobs_today}") # Output: JobPosts created today: 156 users_today = User.objects.filter( date_joined__date=today ).count() print(f"Users registered today: {users_today}") # Output: Users registered today: 45 # Error rate calculation error_rate = 85 / 4850 * 100 print(f"\nError rate: {error_rate:.2f}%") # Output: Error rate: 1.75%
Example 4: Retrieve Events in Reverse Order (Newest First)

Fetches log events starting from the most recent, working backwards. Ideal for quickly finding the latest errors or checking current Django application state for model operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Retrieve events newest first
# Quick check of most recent activity

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
LOG_STREAM="web-server-01/2025/01/18"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Latest Log Events (Newest First)                             ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Get latest events (no start-from-head = newest first)
EVENTS=$(aws logs get-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    --no-start-from-head \
    --limit 25 \
    --output json)

# Display formatted events
echo "Last 25 events (newest to oldest):"
echo "──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo ""

echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '
    .events[] |
    "\((.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%H:%M:%S"))) [\(.message | fromjson? | .level // "LOG")] \(.message | fromjson? | .event // .message)"
'

echo ""
echo "──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"

# Quick stats
LATEST_TIME=$(echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '.events[0].timestamp/1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")')
OLDEST_TIME=$(echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '.events[-1].timestamp/1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")')

echo ""
echo "Time range: $OLDEST_TIME to $LATEST_TIME"
echo "Stream is actively receiving events: $([ $(echo "$EVENTS" | jq '.events | length') -gt 0 ] && echo "Yes" || echo "No")"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Latest Log Events (Newest First) ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Last 25 events (newest to oldest): ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 15:10:45 [INFO] JobPost.list 15:10:42 [INFO] Skills.filter 15:10:38 [INFO] User.authenticate 15:10:35 [INFO] Location.get 15:10:30 [INFO] JobPost.create 15:10:28 [INFO] Skills.attach 15:10:25 [INFO] Author.update 15:10:20 [INFO] JobPost.update 15:10:15 [WARNING] slow_query 15:10:10 [INFO] User.login 15:10:05 [INFO] JobPost.delete 15:10:00 [INFO] Skills.create 15:09:55 [ERROR] ValidationError 15:09:50 [INFO] Location.filter 15:09:45 [INFO] Author.get ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Time range: 2025-01-18 15:09:45 to 2025-01-18 15:10:45 Stream is actively receiving events: Yes
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta import logging # Latest events analysis - shows current application activity # Most recent operations (within last minute): # JobPost.list (15:10:45) recent_list = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True)[:20] print(f"JobPost list query: {recent_list.count()} results") # Output: JobPost list query: 20 results # JobPost.create (15:10:30) latest_job = JobPost.objects.latest('created_at') print(f"Latest job: {latest_job.title} (ID: {latest_job.id})") # Output: Latest job: Full Stack Developer (ID: 252) # Skills.attach (15:10:28) print(f"Skills on latest job: {list(latest_job.required_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))}") # Output: Skills on latest job: ['Python', 'JavaScript', 'React'] # User.login (15:10:10) recent_login = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(minutes=1) ).first() print(f"Recent login: {recent_login.username if recent_login else 'None'}") # Output: Recent login: sarah_dev # ValidationError (15:09:55) - investigate print("\nRecent validation error - check for bad data") # Likely cause: salary_min > salary_max or missing required field # Current stream health print(f"\nStream active: events received in last minute")
Example 5: Extract Model-Specific Events with JSON Parsing

Retrieves and filters log events for a specific Django model, parsing JSON-structured logs to extract detailed operation information for Skills, User, Author, Location, or JobPost analysis.

#!/bin/bash
# Extract model-specific events with JSON parsing
# Analyze operations for a specific Django model

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
LOG_STREAM="web-server-01/2025/01/18"
TARGET_MODEL="JobPost"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Model-Specific Log Analysis: $TARGET_MODEL                    ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Get events from last 2 hours
START_TIME=$(date -d "2 hours ago" +%s)000

EVENTS=$(aws logs get-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name "$LOG_STREAM" \
    --start-time $START_TIME \
    --start-from-head \
    --limit 5000 \
    --output json)

# Filter for target model and analyze
echo "Analyzing $TARGET_MODEL operations from last 2 hours..."
echo ""

# Extract model-specific events
MODEL_EVENTS=$(echo "$EVENTS" | jq --arg model "$TARGET_MODEL" '
    [.events[] | 
        select(.message | fromjson? | .event // "" | startswith($model))]
')

# Count by operation type
echo "Operation Breakdown:"
echo "───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$MODEL_EVENTS" | jq -r '
    group_by(.message | fromjson | .event) |
    map({operation: .[0].message | fromjson | .event, count: length}) |
    sort_by(-.count) | .[] |
    "  \(.operation): \(.count)"
'

echo ""
echo "Recent $TARGET_MODEL Operations (last 10):"
echo "───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$MODEL_EVENTS" | jq -r '
    .[-10:] | .[] |
    "\((.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%H:%M:%S"))) | \(.message | fromjson | {event, id, title} | to_entries | map("\(.key)=\(.value)") | join(", "))"
'

echo ""
echo "Error Analysis for $TARGET_MODEL:"
echo "───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
ERRORS=$(echo "$MODEL_EVENTS" | jq '[.[] | select(.message | fromjson | .level == "ERROR")]')
ERROR_COUNT=$(echo "$ERRORS" | jq 'length')
echo "  Total errors: $ERROR_COUNT"

if [ "$ERROR_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "  Error types:"
    echo "$ERRORS" | jq -r '
        group_by(.message | fromjson | .error // "Unknown") |
        .[] | "    - \(.[0].message | fromjson | .error // "Unknown"): \(length) occurrences"
    '
fi

echo ""
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Model-Specific Log Analysis: JobPost ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Analyzing JobPost operations from last 2 hours... Operation Breakdown: ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── JobPost.list: 245 JobPost.get: 180 JobPost.create: 45 JobPost.update: 38 JobPost.delete: 12 JobPost.search: 85 JobPost.filter: 120 Recent JobPost Operations (last 10): ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 15:08:30 | event=JobPost.create, id=250, title=Backend Engineer 15:08:45 | event=JobPost.update, id=248, title=Senior Developer 15:09:00 | event=JobPost.get, id=245, title=Python Developer 15:09:15 | event=JobPost.list, id=null, title=null 15:09:30 | event=JobPost.search, id=null, title=null 15:09:45 | event=JobPost.filter, id=null, title=null 15:10:00 | event=JobPost.create, id=251, title=DevOps Engineer 15:10:15 | event=JobPost.delete, id=230, title=Old Position 15:10:30 | event=JobPost.create, id=252, title=Full Stack Dev 15:10:45 | event=JobPost.list, id=null, title=null Error Analysis for JobPost: ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Total errors: 5 Error types: - ValidationError: 3 occurrences - DoesNotExist: 2 occurrences ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count from datetime import datetime, timedelta # JobPost model analysis based on log events: # Operation counts match database activity: two_hours_ago = datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=2) # JobPost.create (45 operations) created_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=two_hours_ago ).count() print(f"JobPosts created (2h): {created_jobs}") # Output: JobPosts created (2h): 45 # JobPost.update (38 operations) updated_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=two_hours_ago, created_at__lt=two_hours_ago ).count() print(f"JobPosts updated (2h): {updated_jobs}") # Output: JobPosts updated (2h): 38 # JobPost.delete (12 operations) # Check soft deletes deleted_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( deleted_at__gte=two_hours_ago ).count() print(f"JobPosts deleted (2h): {deleted_jobs}") # Output: JobPosts deleted (2h): 12 # Error investigation: # ValidationError (3) - check for invalid data invalid_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( status='validation_error', updated_at__gte=two_hours_ago ) print(f"\nValidation errors: {invalid_jobs.count()}") for job in invalid_jobs: print(f" - Job {job.id}: {job.validation_message}") # Output: # - Job 249: salary_min cannot exceed salary_max # - Job 250: title is required # - Job 251: location_id does not exist # DoesNotExist (2) - check for missing references print("\nDoesNotExist errors: Invalid job IDs requested") # Likely API calls with non-existent IDs

filter-log-events

Searches and filters log events across one or more log streams within a log group in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Uses powerful filter patterns to find specific events, errors, or patterns in Django application logs. Essential for troubleshooting issues across multiple instances tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    [--log-stream-names <value>] \
    [--log-stream-name-prefix <value>] \
    [--filter-pattern <value>] \
    [--start-time <value>] \
    [--end-time <value>] \
    [--next-token <value>] \
    [--limit <value>] \
    [--interleaved | --no-interleaved] \
    [--unmask]
Parameters Description
  • --log-group-name (string, required): The log group to search. Example: "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
  • --log-stream-names (list): Specific streams to search. Max 100 streams. Example: "web-server-01/2025/01/18" "web-server-02/2025/01/18"
  • --log-stream-name-prefix (string): Search all streams matching prefix. Example: "web-server-" searches all web server streams
  • --filter-pattern (string): Filter pattern for searching. Supports: literal text, metric filter pattern syntax, JSON field extraction. Empty string matches all events
  • --start-time (long): Start of time range (milliseconds since epoch). Required if end-time is specified
  • --end-time (long): End of time range (milliseconds since epoch)
  • --limit (integer): Maximum events to return (1-10000). Default: 10000
  • --interleaved: Interleave events from multiple streams by timestamp (deprecated but functional)
  • --unmask: Show unmasked content if data protection is enabled
Return Value
  • events (Array): List of matching log events
  • logStreamName (string): Stream where event was found
  • timestamp (long): Event timestamp in milliseconds
  • message (string): Log message content
  • ingestionTime (long): When CloudWatch received the event
  • eventId (string): Unique identifier for the event
  • searchedLogStreams (Array): Streams that were searched
  • nextToken (string): Token for pagination if more results exist
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Search for ERROR Level Logs Across All Django Streams

Filters log events to find all ERROR level messages across all web server streams in the Django application. Essential for identifying issues affecting JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location model operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Search for ERROR logs across all streams
# Critical for incident detection and response

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
