START_TIME=$(date -d "1 hour ago" +%s)000
END_TIME=$(date +%s)000

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Error Log Analysis - Last Hour                               ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Time Range: $(date -d '1 hour ago' '+%H:%M') - $(date '+%H:%M')"
echo ""

# Search for ERROR pattern
RESULTS=$(aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-pattern "ERROR" \
    --start-time $START_TIME \
    --end-time $END_TIME \
    --output json)

# Count total errors
ERROR_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '.events | length')
echo "Total ERROR events found: $ERROR_COUNT"
echo ""

# Group by stream
echo "Errors by Stream:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '
    .events | group_by(.logStreamName) | 
    map({stream: .[0].logStreamName, count: length}) |
    sort_by(-.count) | .[] |
    "  \(.stream): \(.count) errors"
'

echo ""
echo "Error Details (last 10):"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '
    .events | sort_by(.timestamp) | .[-10:] | .[] |
    "\((.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%H:%M:%S"))) [\(.logStreamName | split("/")[0])] \(.message | .[0:100])"
'

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Error Log Analysis - Last Hour ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-production Time Range: 14:15 - 15:15 Total ERROR events found: 23 Errors by Stream: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── web-server-01/2025/01/18: 12 errors web-server-02/2025/01/18: 8 errors ecs-task-xyz/jobpost-container: 3 errors Error Details (last 10): ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 15:10:15 [web-server-01] {"level": "ERROR", "event": "ValidationError", "model": "JobPost", "field": "salary_min"... 15:08:45 [web-server-02] {"level": "ERROR", "event": "DoesNotExist", "model": "Author", "id": 999... 15:07:30 [web-server-01] {"level": "ERROR", "event": "IntegrityError", "model": "Skills", "message": "duplicate"... 15:05:22 [ecs-task-xyz] {"level": "ERROR", "event": "ConnectionError", "database": "postgresql", "timeout": 30... 15:03:10 [web-server-01] {"level": "ERROR", "event": "PermissionDenied", "user_id": 45, "action": "JobPost.delete"... ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError, PermissionDenied from django.db import IntegrityError import logging logger = logging.getLogger('django.production') # Error events correlate with these Django exceptions: # ValidationError (JobPost.salary_min) try: job = JobPost(title="Test", salary_min=-5000) job.full_clean() except ValidationError as e: logger.error(f"ValidationError: {e}") print(f"Validation errors: {e.message_dict}") # Output: Validation errors: {'salary_min': ['Must be positive']} # DoesNotExist (Author.id=999) try: author = Author.objects.get(id=999) except Author.DoesNotExist: logger.error("Author.DoesNotExist id=999") print("Author not found") # Output: Author not found # IntegrityError (Skills duplicate) try: Skills.objects.create(name="Python") # Already exists except IntegrityError as e: logger.error(f"IntegrityError: {e}") print(f"Duplicate skill error") # Output: Duplicate skill error # PermissionDenied (JobPost.delete) user = User.objects.get(id=45) job = JobPost.objects.get(id=230) if job.author.user != user and not user.is_staff: logger.error(f"PermissionDenied user_id={user.id}") print(f"User {user.username} cannot delete job {job.id}") # Output: User john_dev cannot delete job 230 # Error summary print(f"\nError types found in last hour:") print(f" ValidationError: 8") print(f" DoesNotExist: 6") print(f" IntegrityError: 5") print(f" PermissionDenied: 4")
Example 2: Filter by Specific Django Model Name

Searches for all log events mentioning a specific Django model (Author in this case). Tracks all operations including creates, updates, queries, and errors for comprehensive model activity analysis.

#!/bin/bash
# Filter logs by Django model name
# Track all Author model operations

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
MODEL_NAME="Author"
START_TIME=$(date -d "24 hours ago" +%s)000

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Model Activity Report: $MODEL_NAME                            ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Time Range: Last 24 hours"
echo ""

# Search for model name in logs
RESULTS=$(aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-pattern "$MODEL_NAME" \
    --start-time $START_TIME \
    --limit 10000 \
    --output json)

TOTAL=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '.events | length')
echo "Total $MODEL_NAME events: $TOTAL"
echo ""

# Categorize operations
echo "Operation Breakdown:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"

# Count creates
CREATES=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.events[].message | select(contains("create"))] | length')
echo "  Creates: $CREATES"

# Count updates
UPDATES=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.events[].message | select(contains("update"))] | length')
echo "  Updates: $UPDATES"

# Count queries (get, filter, all)
QUERIES=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.events[].message | select(contains("get") or contains("filter") or contains("query"))] | length')
echo "  Queries: $QUERIES"

# Count errors
ERRORS=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.events[].message | select(contains("ERROR"))] | length')
echo "  Errors: $ERRORS"

echo ""
echo "Recent $MODEL_NAME Activity (last 15):"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '
    .events | sort_by(.timestamp) | .[-15:] | .[] |
    "\((.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%m/%d %H:%M:%S"))) \(.message | fromjson? | .event // .message[0:60])"
'

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Model Activity Report: Author ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Time Range: Last 24 hours Total Author events: 380 Operation Breakdown: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Creates: 45 Updates: 85 Queries: 238 Errors: 12 Recent Author Activity (last 15): ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 01/18 15:10:30 Author.update 01/18 15:08:45 Author.get 01/18 15:05:20 Author.filter 01/18 15:02:15 Author.create 01/18 14:58:30 Author.update 01/18 14:55:10 Author.query 01/18 14:52:45 Author.get 01/18 14:48:20 Author.filter 01/18 14:45:00 ERROR: Author.DoesNotExist 01/18 14:42:30 Author.create 01/18 14:38:15 Author.update 01/18 14:35:00 Author.get 01/18 14:30:45 Author.filter 01/18 14:28:20 Author.create 01/18 14:25:00 Author.update ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta import logging logger = logging.getLogger('django.models.author') # Author model activity analysis: # Creates (45 in 24h) new_authors = Author.objects.filter( created_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24) ) print(f"New authors (24h): {new_authors.count()}") # Output: New authors (24h): 45 # Sample new author latest_author = new_authors.order_by('-created_at').first() if latest_author: print(f"Latest: {latest_author.user.username} - {latest_author.bio[:50]}...") # Output: Latest: jane_developer - Full-stack developer with 8 years experience... # Updates (85 in 24h) updated_authors = Author.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24), created_at__lt=datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=24) ) print(f"Updated authors (24h): {updated_authors.count()}") # Output: Updated authors (24h): 85 # Most queried authors (based on JobPost relations) popular_authors = Author.objects.annotate( job_count=Count('jobpost') ).filter(job_count__gt=0).order_by('-job_count')[:5] print(f"\nTop authors by job posts:") for author in popular_authors: print(f" {author.user.username}: {author.job_count} jobs") # Output: # john_senior: 25 jobs # sarah_tech: 18 jobs # mike_dev: 15 jobs # Error context (12 errors) print(f"\nAuthor errors to investigate: 12") print(f" - DoesNotExist: Invalid author ID references") print(f" - ValidationError: Bio length or field validation") print(f" - IntegrityError: User-Author relationship conflicts")
Example 3: Complex Filter Pattern with JSON Field Extraction

Uses CloudWatch Logs filter pattern syntax to extract and filter on specific JSON fields in structured Django logs. Finds JobPost operations with specific salary ranges or status values.

#!/bin/bash
# Complex filter with JSON field extraction
# Find high-salary JobPost operations

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
START_TIME=$(date -d "7 days ago" +%s)000

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  High-Salary JobPost Analysis (>$150k)                        ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Filter pattern for JSON field extraction
# { $.salary_min >= 150000 }
FILTER_PATTERN='{ ($.salary_min >= 150000) || ($.salary_max >= 150000) }'

# Alternative simpler pattern (text-based)
SIMPLE_PATTERN='"salary_min": 1[5-9][0-9]000'

echo "Searching with pattern: salary >= 150000"
echo ""

# Search for high-salary jobs
RESULTS=$(aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-pattern "JobPost" \
    --start-time $START_TIME \
    --limit 5000 \
    --output json)

# Post-filter for salary in jq (more flexible)
HIGH_SALARY=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '
    [.events[] | 
        select(.message | fromjson? | 
            (.salary_min >= 150000) or (.salary_max >= 150000)
        )
    ]
')

COUNT=$(echo "$HIGH_SALARY" | jq 'length')
echo "High-salary JobPost events: $COUNT"
echo ""

# Analyze by operation type
echo "Operations on High-Salary Jobs:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$HIGH_SALARY" | jq -r '
    group_by(.message | fromjson | .event) |
    map({op: .[0].message | fromjson | .event, count: length}) |
    sort_by(-.count) | .[] |
    "  \(.op): \(.count)"
'

echo ""
echo "Recent High-Salary Job Activity:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$HIGH_SALARY" | jq -r '
    sort_by(.timestamp) | .[-10:] | .[] |
    {
        time: (.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%m/%d %H:%M")),
        event: (.message | fromjson | .event),
        title: (.message | fromjson | .title // "N/A"),
        salary: (.message | fromjson | "\(.salary_min // 0)/\(.salary_max // 0)")
    } |
    "  \(.time) | \(.event) | \(.title) | $\(.salary)"
'

echo ""
echo "Salary Distribution:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$HIGH_SALARY" | jq '
    [.[] | .message | fromjson | .salary_max // .salary_min // 0] |
    {
        avg: (add / length | floor),
        min: min,
        max: max,
        count: length
    } |
    "  Average: $\(.avg) | Range: $\(.min) - $\(.max)"
' -r

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ High-Salary JobPost Analysis (>$150k) ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Searching with pattern: salary >= 150000 High-salary JobPost events: 156 Operations on High-Salary Jobs: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── JobPost.create: 45 JobPost.update: 38 JobPost.list: 52 JobPost.get: 21 Recent High-Salary Job Activity: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 01/18 15:05 | JobPost.create | VP of Engineering | $180000/250000 01/18 14:45 | JobPost.update | CTO | $200000/300000 01/18 14:30 | JobPost.create | Senior Architect | $160000/220000 01/18 14:15 | JobPost.get | Director of Engineering | $175000/240000 01/18 13:50 | JobPost.create | Staff Engineer | $170000/230000 01/18 13:30 | JobPost.list | N/A | $0/0 01/18 13:00 | JobPost.update | Principal Developer | $155000/210000 Salary Distribution: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Average: $185000 | Range: $150000 - $300000 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Avg, Min, Max, Count from datetime import datetime, timedelta # High-salary JobPost analysis: # Filter high-salary jobs high_salary_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( salary_min__gte=150000 ) | JobPost.objects.filter( salary_max__gte=150000 ) print(f"High-salary jobs (>=$150k): {high_salary_jobs.distinct().count()}") # Output: High-salary jobs (>=$150k): 85 # Salary statistics salary_stats = high_salary_jobs.aggregate( avg_min=Avg('salary_min'), avg_max=Avg('salary_max'), max_salary=Max('salary_max'), min_salary=Min('salary_min') ) print(f"\nSalary Statistics:") print(f" Avg Min: ${salary_stats['avg_min']:,.0f}") print(f" Avg Max: ${salary_stats['avg_max']:,.0f}") print(f" Range: ${salary_stats['min_salary']:,} - ${salary_stats['max_salary']:,}") # Output: # Avg Min: $165,000 # Avg Max: $225,000 # Range: $150,000 - $300,000 # Top high-salary job titles top_titles = high_salary_jobs.values('title').annotate( count=Count('id'), avg_salary=Avg('salary_max') ).order_by('-avg_salary')[:5] print(f"\nTop high-salary positions:") for t in top_titles: print(f" {t['title']}: ${t['avg_salary']:,.0f} avg (n={t['count']})") # Output: # VP of Engineering: $275,000 avg (n=8) # CTO: $250,000 avg (n=5) # Director of Engineering: $220,000 avg (n=12) # Skills required for high-salary jobs high_salary_skills = Skills.objects.filter( jobpost__in=high_salary_jobs ).annotate( job_count=Count('jobpost') ).order_by('-job_count')[:5] print(f"\nTop skills for high-salary jobs:") for skill in high_salary_skills: print(f" {skill.name}: {skill.job_count} jobs") # Output: # Python: 65 jobs # AWS: 58 jobs # Kubernetes: 45 jobs
Example 4: Search Multiple Specific Streams for User Activity

Filters log events from specific named log streams to track User model activity across targeted Django instances. Useful for investigating user-specific issues or auditing access patterns.

#!/bin/bash
# Search multiple specific streams for User activity
# Track authentication and profile operations

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
START_TIME=$(date -d "6 hours ago" +%s)000

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  User Activity Audit - Multiple Streams                       ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Search specific web servers
RESULTS=$(aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-names \
        "web-server-01/2025/01/18" \
        "web-server-02/2025/01/18" \
        "auth-service/2025/01/18" \
    --filter-pattern "User" \
    --start-time $START_TIME \
    --output json)

TOTAL=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '.events | length')
echo "User events found: $TOTAL"
echo ""

# Authentication events
echo "🔐 Authentication Activity:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
AUTH_EVENTS=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '
    [.events[] | select(.message | contains("login") or contains("logout") or contains("authenticate"))]
')
echo "$AUTH_EVENTS" | jq -r '
    group_by(.message | fromjson? | .event // "unknown") |
    map({event: .[0].message | fromjson? | .event // "unknown", count: length}) |
    .[] | "  \(.event): \(.count)"
'

# Failed logins
echo ""
echo "⚠️  Failed Login Attempts:"
FAILED=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.events[] | select(.message | contains("failed") or contains("invalid"))]')
FAILED_COUNT=$(echo "$FAILED" | jq 'length')
echo "  Total failed attempts: $FAILED_COUNT"
echo "$FAILED" | jq -r '
    .[:5] | .[] |
    "  \((.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%H:%M:%S"))) - \(.message | fromjson? | "IP: \(.ip // "unknown"), User: \(.username // "unknown")")"
'

# User operations by stream
echo ""
echo "📊 Activity by Stream:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '
    .events | group_by(.logStreamName) |
    map({stream: .[0].logStreamName, count: length}) |
    sort_by(-.count) | .[] |
    "  \(.stream): \(.count) events"
'

# Recent user registrations
echo ""
echo "👤 Recent User Registrations:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '
    [.events[] | select(.message | contains("create") or contains("register"))] |
    sort_by(.timestamp) | .[-5:] | .[] |
    "\((.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%H:%M:%S"))) - \(.message | fromjson? | "User: \(.username // "N/A"), Email: \(.email // "N/A")")"
'

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ User Activity Audit - Multiple Streams ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ User events found: 620 🔐 Authentication Activity: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── User.login: 285 User.logout: 145 User.authenticate: 125 User.session_refresh: 65 ⚠️ Failed Login Attempts: Total failed attempts: 12 14:45:30 - IP: 192.168.1.50, User: admin 14:30:15 - IP: 10.0.1.100, User: test_user 14:15:45 - IP: 192.168.1.50, User: admin 13:58:20 - IP: 203.0.113.50, User: unknown 13:42:10 - IP: 192.168.1.75, User: john_dev 📊 Activity by Stream: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── auth-service/2025/01/18: 380 events web-server-01/2025/01/18: 150 events web-server-02/2025/01/18: 90 events 👤 Recent User Registrations: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 15:02:30 - User: sarah_backend, Email: sarah@company.com 14:48:15 - User: mike_frontend, Email: mike@startup.io 14:35:00 - User: lisa_devops, Email: lisa@tech.co 14:22:45 - User: tom_data, Email: tom@analytics.com 14:10:20 - User: anna_design, Email: anna@creative.io ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.contrib.auth import authenticate, login, logout from datetime import datetime, timedelta import logging logger = logging.getLogger('django.auth') # User activity analysis: # Login activity (285 logins in 6 hours) recent_logins = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=6) ) print(f"Users logged in (6h): {recent_logins.count()}") # Output: Users logged in (6h): 285 # Active sessions from django.contrib.sessions.models import Session active_sessions = Session.objects.filter( expire_date__gt=datetime.now() ).count() print(f"Active sessions: {active_sessions}") # Output: Active sessions: 145 # Failed login analysis failed_attempts = [ {'ip': '192.168.1.50', 'user': 'admin', 'count': 3}, {'ip': '203.0.113.50', 'user': 'unknown', 'count': 2}, ] print(f"\nFailed login patterns:") for attempt in failed_attempts: print(f" {attempt['ip']}: {attempt['count']} attempts for '{attempt['user']}'") # Potential brute force from 192.168.1.50 # Recent registrations new_users = User.objects.filter( date_joined__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=6) ).select_related('author') print(f"\nNew users (6h): {new_users.count()}") for user in new_users[:5]: has_author = hasattr(user, 'author') print(f" {user.username} ({user.email}) - Author: {'Yes' if has_author else 'No'}") # Output: # sarah_backend (sarah@company.com) - Author: Yes # mike_frontend (mike@startup.io) - Author: Yes # lisa_devops (lisa@tech.co) - Author: Yes # Cross-reference with Author creation new_authors = Author.objects.filter( user__in=new_users ) print(f"\nNew authors created: {new_authors.count()}")
Example 5: Search with Stream Name Prefix for Specific Environment

Filters logs from all streams matching a prefix pattern to analyze a specific environment (production vs staging). Tracks all model operations for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost in that environment.

#!/bin/bash
# Search with stream name prefix for environment analysis
# Compare production activity patterns

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
STREAM_PREFIX="web-server-"
START_TIME=$(date -d "4 hours ago" +%s)000

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Production Environment Analysis - Web Servers                ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Stream Prefix: $STREAM_PREFIX"
echo "Time Range: Last 4 hours"
echo ""

# Search all web server streams
RESULTS=$(aws logs filter-log-events \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --log-stream-name-prefix "$STREAM_PREFIX" \
    --start-time $START_TIME \
    --output json)

TOTAL=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '.events | length')
STREAMS=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.events[].logStreamName] | unique | length')
echo "Total events: $TOTAL"
echo "Active streams: $STREAMS"
echo ""

# Model distribution
echo "📊 Events by Django Model:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
for model in "JobPost" "Skills" "User" "Author" "Location"; do
    COUNT=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq "[.events[].message | select(contains(\"$model\"))] | length")
    PCT=$((COUNT * 100 / TOTAL))
    BAR=$(printf '█%.0s' $(seq 1 $((PCT / 5))))
    printf "  %-10s %6d (%2d%%) %s\n" "$model" "$COUNT" "$PCT" "$BAR"
done

# Event level distribution
echo ""
echo "📈 Events by Level:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '
    .events | group_by(.message | fromjson? | .level // "INFO") |
    map({level: .[0].message | fromjson? | .level // "INFO", count: length}) |
    sort_by(-.count) | .[] |
    "  \(.level): \(.count)"
'

# Hourly distribution
echo ""
echo "⏰ Events by Hour:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '
    .events | group_by(.timestamp/1000 | strftime("%H")) |
    sort_by(.[0].timestamp) | .[] |
    {hour: (.[0].timestamp/1000 | strftime("%H:00")), count: length} |
    "  \(.hour): \(.count) events"
'

# Server load comparison
echo ""
echo "🖥️  Server Load Distribution:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '
    .events | group_by(.logStreamName | split("/")[0]) |
    map({server: .[0].logStreamName | split("/")[0], events: length}) |
    sort_by(-.events) | .[] |
    "  \(.server): \(.events) events"
'

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Production Environment Analysis - Web Servers ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Stream Prefix: web-server- Time Range: Last 4 hours Total events: 4250 Active streams: 3 📊 Events by Django Model: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── JobPost 2420 (56%) ███████████ Skills 680 (16%) ███ User 510 (12%) ██ Author 425 (10%) ██ Location 215 ( 5%) █ 📈 Events by Level: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── INFO: 3850 WARNING: 285 ERROR: 85 DEBUG: 30 ⏰ Events by Hour: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 11:00: 850 events 12:00: 1250 events 13:00: 1100 events 14:00: 950 events 15:00: 100 events 🖥️ Server Load Distribution: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── web-server-01: 1850 events web-server-02: 1550 events web-server-03: 850 events ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count from django.db.models.functions import TruncHour from datetime import datetime, timedelta # Production environment analysis: # Model activity distribution matches log analysis: four_hours_ago = datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=4) # JobPost (56% of activity) jobpost_ops = JobPost.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=four_hours_ago ).count() print(f"JobPost operations (4h): {jobpost_ops}") # Output: JobPost operations (4h): 245 # Skills (16% of activity) skills_ops = Skills.objects.filter( jobpost__updated_at__gte=four_hours_ago ).distinct().count() print(f"Skills accessed (4h): {skills_ops}") # Output: Skills accessed (4h): 38 # User (12% of activity) user_ops = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=four_hours_ago ).count() print(f"User logins (4h): {user_ops}") # Output: User logins (4h): 125 # Author (10% of activity) author_ops = Author.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=four_hours_ago ).count() print(f"Author updates (4h): {author_ops}") # Output: Author updates (4h): 45 # Location (5% of activity) location_ops = Location.objects.filter( jobpost__updated_at__gte=four_hours_ago ).distinct().count() print(f"Locations used (4h): {location_ops}") # Output: Locations used (4h): 22 # Hourly breakdown hourly_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=four_hours_ago ).annotate( hour=TruncHour('created_at') ).values('hour').annotate( count=Count('id') ).order_by('hour') print(f"\nJobPosts created by hour:") for h in hourly_jobs: print(f" {h['hour'].strftime('%H:00')}: {h['count']} jobs") # Output: # 11:00: 35 jobs # 12:00: 52 jobs # 13:00: 45 jobs # 14:00: 38 jobs # Error rate: 85/4250 = 2% print(f"\nError rate: 2% (within acceptable threshold)")

put-retention-policy

Sets the retention policy for a log group in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Logs older than the specified retention period are automatically and permanently deleted. This is essential for managing storage costs and compliance requirements for Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs put-retention-policy \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    --retention-in-days <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --log-group-name (string, required): The name of the log group to set retention for. Example: "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
  • --retention-in-days (integer, required): Number of days to retain logs. Valid values: 1, 3, 5, 7, 14, 30, 60, 90, 120, 150, 180, 365, 400, 545, 731, 1096, 1827, 2192, 2557, 2922, 3288, 3653 (10 years). Choose based on compliance needs and cost optimization
Return Value
  • None: This command does not return any output on success
  • ResourceNotFoundException: Returned if the log group does not exist
  • InvalidParameterException: Returned if retention value is not in the allowed list
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Set 30-Day Retention for Production Django Logs

Configures 30-day retention for production Django application logs. This balances cost management with sufficient history for debugging issues affecting JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location model operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Set 30-day retention for production logs
# Standard retention for active Django application

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
RETENTION_DAYS=30

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Setting Log Retention Policy                                 ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Retention: $RETENTION_DAYS days"
echo ""

# Check current retention
CURRENT=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query 'logGroups[0].retentionInDays' \
    --output text)

echo "Current retention: ${CURRENT:-Never expires}"

# Set new retention policy
aws logs put-retention-policy \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --retention-in-days $RETENTION_DAYS

echo ""
echo "✅ Retention policy updated successfully"
echo ""

# Verify the change
NEW_RETENTION=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query 'logGroups[0].retentionInDays' \
    --output text)

echo "New retention: $NEW_RETENTION days"
echo ""

# Calculate storage impact
STORED_BYTES=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query 'logGroups[0].storedBytes' \
    --output text)
STORED_MB=$((STORED_BYTES / 1048576))

echo "Current storage: ${STORED_MB} MB"
echo "Logs older than $RETENTION_DAYS days will be automatically deleted"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Setting Log Retention Policy ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-production Retention: 30 days Current retention: 90 ✅ Retention policy updated successfully New retention: 30 days Current storage: 485 MB Logs older than 30 days will be automatically deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta import logging # 30-day retention impact on Django model logs: # JobPost logs within retention (will be kept) recent_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=30) ) print(f"JobPosts within 30-day retention: {recent_jobs.count()}") # Output: JobPosts within 30-day retention: 450 # JobPost logs outside retention (will be deleted) old_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__lt=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=30) ) print(f"JobPosts with logs to be deleted: {old_jobs.count()}") # Output: JobPosts with logs to be deleted: 1250 # Note: Database records remain, only CloudWatch logs are deleted # User activity within retention active_users = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=30) ) print(f"Active users (logs kept): {active_users.count()}") # Output: Active users (logs kept): 285 # Model operation log summary within retention: print(f"\nLogs retained for debugging:") print(f" - All JobPost CRUD operations (30 days)") print(f" - Skills associations and updates") print(f" - User authentication events") print(f" - Author profile changes") print(f" - Location lookups and filters")
Example 2: Configure Different Retention by Environment

Sets appropriate retention policies for different environments - longer for production (90 days), shorter for development (7 days) and staging (14 days). Optimizes costs while maintaining necessary log history for each Django environment.

#!/bin/bash
# Configure retention by environment
# Cost-optimized retention strategy

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Environment-Based Retention Configuration                    ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Define retention by environment
declare -A ENVIRONMENTS=(
    ["/aws/django/anihpj-production"]=90
    ["/aws/django/anihpj-staging"]=14
    ["/aws/django/anihpj-development"]=7
    ["/aws/django/anihpj-test"]=3
)

for LOG_GROUP in "${!ENVIRONMENTS[@]}"; do
    RETENTION=${ENVIRONMENTS[$LOG_GROUP]}
    
    echo "Setting retention for: $LOG_GROUP"
    echo "  Retention: $RETENTION days"
    
    # Check if log group exists
    EXISTS=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
        --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
        --query "logGroups[?logGroupName=='$LOG_GROUP'].logGroupName" \
        --output text)
    
    if [ -n "$EXISTS" ]; then
        aws logs put-retention-policy \
            --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
            --retention-in-days $RETENTION
        echo "  ✅ Retention set successfully"
    else
        echo "  ⚠️  Log group does not exist, skipping"
    fi
    echo ""
done

echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Retention Summary:"
echo "  Production: 90 days (debugging + compliance)"
echo "  Staging:    14 days (integration testing)"
echo "  Development: 7 days (active development)"
echo "  Test:        3 days (CI/CD pipeline)"
echo ""

# Calculate monthly cost savings
echo "Estimated Monthly Savings:"
echo "  Reduced from indefinite to tiered retention"
echo "  ~60% reduction in CloudWatch Logs storage costs"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Environment-Based Retention Configuration ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Setting retention for: /aws/django/anihpj-production Retention: 90 days ✅ Retention set successfully Setting retention for: /aws/django/anihpj-staging Retention: 14 days ✅ Retention set successfully Setting retention for: /aws/django/anihpj-development Retention: 7 days ✅ Retention set successfully Setting retention for: /aws/django/anihpj-test Retention: 3 days ✅ Retention set successfully ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Retention Summary: Production: 90 days (debugging + compliance) Staging: 14 days (integration testing) Development: 7 days (active development) Test: 3 days (CI/CD pipeline) Estimated Monthly Savings: Reduced from indefinite to tiered retention ~60% reduction in CloudWatch Logs storage costs
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta import logging # Environment-based log retention context: # PRODUCTION (90 days) - Full debugging capability # All model operations retained for 3 months prod_jobs = JobPost.objects.using('production').filter( created_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=90) ) print(f"Production JobPosts (90 days): {prod_jobs.count()}") # Output: Production JobPosts (90 days): 2500 # STAGING (14 days) - Integration testing # Short-term logs for QA testing staging_users = User.objects.using('staging').filter( date_joined__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=14) ) print(f"Staging test users (14 days): {staging_users.count()}") # Output: Staging test users (14 days): 50 # DEVELOPMENT (7 days) - Active development # Quick iteration, minimal log retention dev_skills = Skills.objects.using('development').all() print(f"Development Skills: {dev_skills.count()}") # Output: Development Skills: 15 # TEST (3 days) - CI/CD pipeline # Automated tests, rapid cleanup test_authors = Author.objects.using('test').all() print(f"Test Authors: {test_authors.count()}") # Output: Test Authors: 10 # Cost optimization summary print(f"\nStorage optimization:") print(f" Production: 485 MB (90 days)") print(f" Staging: 45 MB (14 days)") print(f" Development: 15 MB (7 days)") print(f" Test: 5 MB (3 days)") print(f" Total: 550 MB (vs 2GB if all indefinite)")
Example 3: Set Retention for Model-Specific Log Groups

Configures different retention policies for each Django model's dedicated log group based on importance and compliance requirements. Allows fine-grained log management for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Set retention for model-specific log groups
# Different retention based on model importance

LOG_PREFIX="/aws/django/anihpj/models"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Model-Specific Log Retention Configuration                   ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Define retention by model importance
declare -A MODEL_RETENTION=(
    ["jobpost"]=60     # 60 days - primary business object
    ["user"]=90        # 90 days - compliance/audit requirement
    ["author"]=30      # 30 days - profile changes
    ["skills"]=14      # 14 days - reference data
    ["location"]=14    # 14 days - reference data
)

echo "Configuring retention for Django model log groups:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo ""

for MODEL in "${!MODEL_RETENTION[@]}"; do
    LOG_GROUP="$LOG_PREFIX/$MODEL"
    RETENTION=${MODEL_RETENTION[$MODEL]}
    
    printf "%-15s %-40s %3d days  " "$MODEL" "$LOG_GROUP" "$RETENTION"
    
    # Set retention
    aws logs put-retention-policy \
        --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
        --retention-in-days $RETENTION \
        2>/dev/null
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "✅"
    else
        echo "⚠️  (group may not exist)"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Retention Strategy Rationale:"
echo "  JobPost (60 days): Primary business data, customer support needs"
echo "  User (90 days): Authentication audit trail, compliance"
echo "  Author (30 days): Profile management, moderate importance"
echo "  Skills (14 days): Reference data, low change frequency"
echo "  Location (14 days): Reference data, low change frequency"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Model-Specific Log Retention Configuration ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Configuring retention for Django model log groups: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── jobpost /aws/django/anihpj/models/jobpost 60 days ✅ user /aws/django/anihpj/models/user 90 days ✅ author /aws/django/anihpj/models/author 30 days ✅ skills /aws/django/anihpj/models/skills 14 days ✅ location /aws/django/anihpj/models/location 14 days ✅ ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Retention Strategy Rationale: JobPost (60 days): Primary business data, customer support needs User (90 days): Authentication audit trail, compliance Author (30 days): Profile management, moderate importance Skills (14 days): Reference data, low change frequency Location (14 days): Reference data, low change frequency
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count from datetime import datetime, timedelta # Model-specific retention rationale: # JobPost (60 days) - Most critical business object # Supports customer inquiries about job postings job_activity = JobPost.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=60) ).aggregate( total=Count('id'), active=Count('id', filter=models.Q(is_active=True)), applications=Count('application') ) print(f"JobPost (60-day window):") print(f" Records: {job_activity['total']}") print(f" Active: {job_activity['active']}") print(f" Applications: {job_activity['applications']}") # Output: Records: 850, Active: 650, Applications: 3200 # User (90 days) - Compliance requirement # Authentication logs, access audit trail user_auth = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=90) ) print(f"\nUser (90-day window): {user_auth.count()} active users") # Output: User (90-day window): 450 active users # Author (30 days) - Profile management # Track bio updates, skill changes author_updates = Author.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=30) ) print(f"Author (30-day window): {author_updates.count()} updated") # Output: Author (30-day window): 125 updated # Skills (14 days) - Reference data, low volume skills_changes = Skills.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=14) ) print(f"Skills (14-day window): {skills_changes.count()} changed") # Output: Skills (14-day window): 8 changed # Location (14 days) - Reference data locations_used = Location.objects.filter( jobpost__created_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=14) ).distinct() print(f"Location (14-day window): {locations_used.count()} used") # Output: Location (14-day window): 22 used
Example 4: Set Compliance-Required Long-Term Retention

Configures extended retention (365 days or more) for audit and compliance log groups that must be retained for regulatory requirements. Tracks sensitive Django model operations requiring long-term history.

#!/bin/bash
# Set compliance-required long-term retention
# Audit logs must be retained for 1+ years

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Compliance Log Retention Configuration                       ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Setting retention for compliance-required log groups"
echo ""

# Compliance log groups requiring long retention
declare -A COMPLIANCE_GROUPS=(
    ["/aws/django/anihpj-audit"]=365          # 1 year - general audit
    ["/aws/django/anihpj-security"]=731       # 2 years - security events
    ["/aws/django/anihpj-user-pii"]=1096      # 3 years - PII access
    ["/aws/django/anihpj-financial"]=2557     # 7 years - financial records
)

echo "Log Group Retention Policies:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
printf "%-40s %10s %s\n" "Log Group" "Days" "Years"
printf "%-40s %10s %s\n" "─────────" "────" "─────"

for LOG_GROUP in "${!COMPLIANCE_GROUPS[@]}"; do
    DAYS=${COMPLIANCE_GROUPS[$LOG_GROUP]}
    YEARS=$((DAYS / 365))
    
    # Create log group if not exists
    aws logs create-log-group \
        --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
        2>/dev/null
    
    # Set retention
    aws logs put-retention-policy \
        --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
        --retention-in-days $DAYS
    
    printf "%-40s %10d %d year(s)\n" "$LOG_GROUP" "$DAYS" "$YEARS"
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Compliance Requirements Addressed:"
echo "  - SOC 2: Security event logs (2 years)"
echo "  - GDPR: PII access logs (3 years recommended)"
echo "  - Financial: Transaction records (7 years SOX)"
echo "  - General Audit: All model changes (1 year)"
echo ""
echo "⚠️  Note: These logs should also be exported to S3 Glacier"
echo "   for cost-effective long-term archival"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Compliance Log Retention Configuration ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Setting retention for compliance-required log groups Log Group Retention Policies: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Log Group Days Years ───────── ──── ───── /aws/django/anihpj-audit 365 1 year(s) /aws/django/anihpj-security 731 2 year(s) /aws/django/anihpj-user-pii 1096 3 year(s) /aws/django/anihpj-financial 2557 7 year(s) ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Compliance Requirements Addressed: - SOC 2: Security event logs (2 years) - GDPR: PII access logs (3 years recommended) - Financial: Transaction records (7 years SOX) - General Audit: All model changes (1 year) ⚠️ Note: These logs should also be exported to S3 Glacier for cost-effective long-term archival
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import logging from datetime import datetime # Compliance logging context: # Audit logger (1 year retention) audit_logger = logging.getLogger('django.audit') # Log all model changes for audit trail def log_model_change(model, instance, action, user): audit_logger.info({ 'model': model.__name__, 'instance_id': instance.pk, 'action': action, # create, update, delete 'user_id': user.id if user else None, 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(), 'changes': instance.get_changes() if hasattr(instance, 'get_changes') else {} }) # Security logger (2 years retention) security_logger = logging.getLogger('django.security') # Log authentication and authorization events def log_security_event(event_type, user, details): security_logger.warning({ 'event': event_type, # login, logout, failed_login, permission_denied 'user_id': user.id if user else None, 'ip_address': details.get('ip'), 'user_agent': details.get('user_agent'), 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat() }) # PII access logger (3 years retention) pii_logger = logging.getLogger('django.pii') # Log access to User personal information def log_pii_access(user, accessed_user, fields_accessed): pii_logger.info({ 'accessor_id': user.id, 'subject_id': accessed_user.id, 'fields': fields_accessed, # ['email', 'phone', 'address'] 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat() }) # Example: Track all User PII access users_with_pii = User.objects.filter( email__isnull=False ).count() print(f"Users with PII to track: {users_with_pii}") # Output: Users with PII to track: 520 # JobPost financial data (7 years for salary info) jobs_with_salary = JobPost.objects.filter( salary_min__isnull=False ).count() print(f"JobPosts with salary data: {jobs_with_salary}") # Output: JobPosts with salary data: 780
Example 5: Bulk Update Retention Across All Django Log Groups

Updates retention policies for all Django application log groups matching a pattern. Includes verification and reporting of changes for comprehensive log management.

#!/bin/bash
# Bulk update retention for all Django log groups
# Standardize retention with verification

PREFIX="/aws/django/anihpj"
DEFAULT_RETENTION=30

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Bulk Retention Policy Update                                 ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Prefix: $PREFIX"
echo "Default Retention: $DEFAULT_RETENTION days"
echo ""

# Get all matching log groups
LOG_GROUPS=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$PREFIX" \
    --query 'logGroups[*].[logGroupName,retentionInDays,storedBytes]' \
    --output text)

# Count groups
GROUP_COUNT=$(echo "$LOG_GROUPS" | wc -l)
echo "Found $GROUP_COUNT log groups to update"
echo ""

echo "Before Update:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
printf "%-45s %12s %10s\n" "Log Group" "Current" "Size (MB)"
echo "$LOG_GROUPS" | while IFS=$'\t' read -r name retention bytes; do
    size_mb=$((${bytes:-0} / 1048576))
    printf "%-45s %12s %10d\n" "$name" "${retention:-Never}" "$size_mb"
done

echo ""
echo "Updating retention policies..."
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"

UPDATED=0
SKIPPED=0

echo "$LOG_GROUPS" | while IFS=$'\t' read -r name retention bytes; do
    # Skip audit/compliance groups (should have special retention)
    if [[ "$name" == *"audit"* ]] || [[ "$name" == *"compliance"* ]]; then
        echo "  ⊘ Skipping (compliance): $name"
        continue
    fi
    
    # Skip if already has correct retention
    if [ "$retention" == "$DEFAULT_RETENTION" ]; then
        echo "  ○ Already set: $name"
        continue
    fi
    
    # Update retention
    aws logs put-retention-policy \
        --log-group-name "$name" \
        --retention-in-days $DEFAULT_RETENTION
    
    echo "  ✅ Updated: $name ($retention → $DEFAULT_RETENTION days)"
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "After Update:"
aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$PREFIX" \
    --query 'logGroups[*].[logGroupName,retentionInDays]' \
    --output table
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Bulk Retention Policy Update ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Prefix: /aws/django/anihpj Default Retention: 30 days Found 12 log groups to update Before Update: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Log Group Current Size (MB) /aws/django/anihpj-production 90 485 /aws/django/anihpj-staging 14 45 /aws/django/anihpj-development 7 15 /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Never 125 /aws/django/anihpj/models/jobpost Never 85 /aws/django/anihpj/models/user Never 50 /aws/django/anihpj/models/author Never 25 /aws/django/anihpj/models/skills Never 15 /aws/django/anihpj-audit 365 95 Updating retention policies... ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ○ Already set: /aws/django/anihpj-staging ○ Already set: /aws/django/anihpj-development ✅ Updated: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost (Never → 30 days) ✅ Updated: /aws/django/anihpj/models/jobpost (Never → 30 days) ✅ Updated: /aws/django/anihpj/models/user (Never → 30 days) ✅ Updated: /aws/django/anihpj/models/author (Never → 30 days) ✅ Updated: /aws/django/anihpj/models/skills (Never → 30 days) ⊘ Skipping (compliance): /aws/django/anihpj-audit ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ After Update: ---------------------------------------------------- | DescribeLogGroups | +----------------------------------+---------------+ | /aws/django/anihpj-production | 90 | | /aws/django/anihpj-staging | 14 | | /aws/django/anihpj-development | 7 | | /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost | 30 | | /aws/django/anihpj/models/jobpost| 30 | | /aws/django/anihpj/models/user | 30 | | /aws/django/anihpj-audit | 365 | +----------------------------------+---------------+
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta # Bulk retention update impact analysis: # Groups with 30-day retention (standard) # These logs will be truncated to last 30 days: # JobPost logs jobpost_30d = JobPost.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=30) ) print(f"JobPost operations (30 days): {jobpost_30d.count()}") # Output: JobPost operations (30 days): 450 # User logs user_30d = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=30) ) print(f"User logins (30 days): {user_30d.count()}") # Output: User logins (30 days): 285 # Author logs author_30d = Author.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=30) ) print(f"Author updates (30 days): {author_30d.count()}") # Output: Author updates (30 days): 125 # Skills logs skills_30d = Skills.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=30) ) print(f"Skills changes (30 days): {skills_30d.count()}") # Output: Skills changes (30 days): 12 # Audit logs (365 days) - preserved print(f"\nAudit logs preserved: 365 days") print(f"Compliance groups excluded from bulk update") # Storage savings estimate print(f"\nEstimated storage after retention applied:") print(f" Before: 940 MB") print(f" After: 450 MB (52% reduction)")

delete-retention-policy

Removes the retention policy from a log group in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. After removal, logs in the group are retained indefinitely and never automatically deleted. This is typically used for audit, compliance, or critical Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations that must be preserved permanently.

Syntax
aws logs delete-retention-policy \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --log-group-name (string, required): The name of the log group to remove the retention policy from. Once removed, logs are retained indefinitely. Example: "/aws/django/anihpj-audit"
Return Value
  • None: This command does not return any output on success
  • ResourceNotFoundException: Returned if the log group does not exist
  • InvalidParameterException: Returned if the log group name is invalid
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Remove Retention for Compliance Audit Logs

Removes the retention policy from audit log groups that must be preserved indefinitely for compliance requirements. Ensures all Django model operation logs for JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location changes are never automatically deleted.

#!/bin/bash
# Remove retention for compliance audit logs
# Logs will be kept indefinitely

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-audit"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Remove Retention Policy - Indefinite Storage                 ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo ""

# Check current retention
CURRENT=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query 'logGroups[0].retentionInDays' \
    --output text)

echo "Current retention: ${CURRENT:-Not set (indefinite)}"
echo ""

# Remove retention policy
echo "Removing retention policy..."
aws logs delete-retention-policy \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP"

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "✅ Retention policy removed successfully"
    echo ""
    echo "Logs will now be retained INDEFINITELY"
else
    echo "❌ Failed to remove retention policy"
fi

# Verify the change
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
NEW_RETENTION=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query 'logGroups[0].retentionInDays' \
    --output text)

echo "New retention: ${NEW_RETENTION:-Never expires (indefinite)}"

# Storage information
STORED=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query 'logGroups[0].storedBytes' \
    --output text)
STORED_MB=$((${STORED:-0} / 1048576))

echo ""
echo "⚠️  Warning: Current storage is ${STORED_MB} MB"
echo "   Without retention, storage will grow indefinitely"
echo "   Consider S3 export for cost-effective archival"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Remove Retention Policy - Indefinite Storage ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-audit Current retention: 365 Removing retention policy... ✅ Retention policy removed successfully Logs will now be retained INDEFINITELY Verification: New retention: Never expires (indefinite) ⚠️ Warning: Current storage is 95 MB Without retention, storage will grow indefinitely Consider S3 export for cost-effective archival
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime import logging # Audit logs with indefinite retention: # Track ALL model changes forever audit_logger = logging.getLogger('django.audit') # Example audit entries that will never be deleted: # JobPost audit (business-critical) all_jobposts = JobPost.objects.all() print(f"JobPost audit records: {all_jobposts.count()}") # Output: JobPost audit records: 3500 # User audit (compliance requirement) all_users = User.objects.all() print(f"User audit records: {all_users.count()}") # Output: User audit records: 520 # Author audit (profile changes) all_authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Author audit records: {all_authors.count()}") # Output: Author audit records: 450 # Skills audit (reference data changes) all_skills = Skills.objects.all() print(f"Skills audit records: {all_skills.count()}") # Output: Skills audit records: 85 # Location audit all_locations = Location.objects.all() print(f"Location audit records: {all_locations.count()}") # Output: Location audit records: 120 # With indefinite retention: print(f"\n⚠️ All {3500 + 520 + 450 + 85 + 120} audit records") print(f" will be retained FOREVER") print(f" Estimated storage growth: 5MB/month")
Example 2: Remove Retention for Security Event Logs

Removes retention policy from security log groups that track authentication, authorization, and access events for Django applications. Ensures security audit trail is preserved indefinitely for forensic analysis.

#!/bin/bash
# Remove retention for security logs
# Critical for security investigations

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Security Log Retention Removal                               ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

SECURITY_LOGS=(
    "/aws/django/anihpj-security"
    "/aws/django/anihpj-authentication"
    "/aws/django/anihpj-authorization"
)

echo "Removing retention from security-critical log groups:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo ""

for LOG_GROUP in "${SECURITY_LOGS[@]}"; do
    echo "Processing: $LOG_GROUP"
    
    # Check if log group exists
    EXISTS=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
        --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
        --query "logGroups[?logGroupName=='$LOG_GROUP'].logGroupName" \
        --output text)
    
    if [ -n "$EXISTS" ]; then
        # Get current retention
        CURRENT=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
            --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
            --query 'logGroups[0].retentionInDays' \
            --output text)
        
        echo "  Current: ${CURRENT:-indefinite}"
        
        # Remove retention policy
        aws logs delete-retention-policy \
            --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP"
        
        echo "  ✅ Retention removed - now indefinite"
    else
        echo "  ⚠️  Log group does not exist"
    fi
    echo ""
done

echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Security logs will now be retained indefinitely:"
echo "  • Login/logout events"
echo "  • Failed authentication attempts"
echo "  • Permission denied events"
echo "  • Sensitive data access"
echo ""
echo "This supports forensic analysis and incident investigation"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Security Log Retention Removal ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Removing retention from security-critical log groups: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Processing: /aws/django/anihpj-security Current: 731 ✅ Retention removed - now indefinite Processing: /aws/django/anihpj-authentication Current: 365 ✅ Retention removed - now indefinite Processing: /aws/django/anihpj-authorization Current: 365 ✅ Retention removed - now indefinite ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Security logs will now be retained indefinitely: • Login/logout events • Failed authentication attempts • Permission denied events • Sensitive data access This supports forensic analysis and incident investigation
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.contrib.auth.models import Permission from datetime import datetime import logging # Security events logged indefinitely: security_logger = logging.getLogger('django.security') # Authentication events (User model) # All login attempts tracked forever users_with_logins = User.objects.filter( last_login__isnull=False ).count() print(f"Users with login history: {users_with_logins}") # Output: Users with login history: 485 # Authorization events # Permission checks on JobPost operations def log_permission_check(user, permission, granted): security_logger.info({ 'event': 'permission_check', 'user_id': user.id, 'permission': permission, 'granted': granted, 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat() }) # Example: JobPost edit permission job_owners = JobPost.objects.values('author__user_id').distinct() print(f"Users with JobPost ownership: {job_owners.count()}") # Output: Users with JobPost ownership: 320 # Access to sensitive User/Author data # PII access logged forever for compliance sensitive_access = Author.objects.filter( user__email__isnull=False ).count() print(f"Authors with PII: {sensitive_access}") # Output: Authors with PII: 450 print(f"\nSecurity events retained indefinitely:") print(f" - Login attempts: ~5000/month") print(f" - Failed logins: ~200/month") print(f" - Permission checks: ~15000/month") print(f" - PII access: ~3000/month")
Example 3: Remove Retention for Critical Model Operation Logs

Removes retention policy from log groups tracking critical Django model operations that require permanent audit trail. Useful for tracking all CRUD operations on JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location models.

#!/bin/bash
# Remove retention for model operation logs
# Keep full history of all model changes

LOG_PREFIX="/aws/django/anihpj/models"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Model Operation Logs - Indefinite Retention                  ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Get all model log groups
MODEL_GROUPS=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_PREFIX" \
    --query 'logGroups[*].logGroupName' \
    --output text)

echo "Model log groups found:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo ""

for LOG_GROUP in $MODEL_GROUPS; do
    # Extract model name from path
    MODEL_NAME=$(basename "$LOG_GROUP")
    
    # Get current settings
    INFO=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
        --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
        --query 'logGroups[0].[retentionInDays,storedBytes]' \
        --output text)
    
    RETENTION=$(echo "$INFO" | cut -f1)
    BYTES=$(echo "$INFO" | cut -f2)
    SIZE_MB=$((${BYTES:-0} / 1048576))
    
    printf "%-20s Current: %s days, Size: %d MB\n" "$MODEL_NAME" "${RETENTION:-∞}" "$SIZE_MB"
    
    # Remove retention if it exists
    if [ "$RETENTION" != "None" ] && [ -n "$RETENTION" ]; then
        aws logs delete-retention-policy \
            --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP"
        echo "                    ✅ Retention removed"
    else
        echo "                    ○ Already indefinite"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "All model operation logs now retained indefinitely:"
echo "  • JobPost: create, update, delete, status changes"
echo "  • User: registration, profile updates, deletions"
echo "  • Author: profile changes, skill associations"
echo "  • Skills: additions, removals, updates"
echo "  • Location: new locations, updates"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Model Operation Logs - Indefinite Retention ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Model log groups found: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── jobpost Current: 30 days, Size: 85 MB ✅ Retention removed user Current: 30 days, Size: 50 MB ✅ Retention removed author Current: 30 days, Size: 25 MB ✅ Retention removed skills Current: 30 days, Size: 15 MB ✅ Retention removed location Current: 30 days, Size: 10 MB ✅ Retention removed ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ All model operation logs now retained indefinitely: • JobPost: create, update, delete, status changes • User: registration, profile updates, deletions • Author: profile changes, skill associations • Skills: additions, removals, updates • Location: new locations, updates
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models.signals import post_save, post_delete import logging # Model operations logged indefinitely: model_logger = logging.getLogger('django.models') # Track all CRUD operations: # JobPost operations jobposts = JobPost.objects.all() print(f"JobPost total records: {jobposts.count()}") # Output: JobPost total records: 3500 # All create/update/delete events logged forever print(f"JobPost estimated log entries: ~{jobposts.count() * 3}") # Output: JobPost estimated log entries: ~10500 # User operations users = User.objects.all() print(f"User total records: {users.count()}") # Output: User total records: 520 # Author operations authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Author total records: {authors.count()}") # Output: Author total records: 450 # Skills operations skills = Skills.objects.all() print(f"Skills total records: {skills.count()}") # Output: Skills total records: 85 # Location operations locations = Location.objects.all() print(f"Location total records: {locations.count()}") # Output: Location total records: 120 # Signal-based logging example @receiver(post_save) def log_model_save(sender, instance, created, **kwargs): if sender in [JobPost, User, Author, Skills, Location]: model_logger.info({ 'model': sender.__name__, 'id': instance.pk, 'action': 'created' if created else 'updated', 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat() })
Example 4: Remove Retention with Cost Impact Analysis

Removes retention policy while providing detailed cost impact analysis. Shows the trade-off between compliance requirements and CloudWatch Logs storage costs for Django application logs.

#!/bin/bash
# Remove retention with cost analysis
# Understand the financial impact

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Retention Removal - Cost Impact Analysis                     ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Get current log group info
INFO=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query 'logGroups[0]' \
    --output json)

CURRENT_RETENTION=$(echo "$INFO" | jq -r '.retentionInDays // "None"')
STORED_BYTES=$(echo "$INFO" | jq -r '.storedBytes // 0')
STORED_GB=$(echo "scale=2; $STORED_BYTES / 1073741824" | bc)

echo "Current State:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Retention: ${CURRENT_RETENTION} days"
echo "Current Storage: ${STORED_GB} GB"
echo ""

# Calculate cost impact
# CloudWatch Logs: $0.03 per GB storage/month
CURRENT_COST=$(echo "scale=2; $STORED_GB * 0.03" | bc)
echo "Current Monthly Cost: \$${CURRENT_COST}"
echo ""

# Estimate growth rate (from metrics or logs)
DAILY_INGESTION_MB=50  # Example: 50MB/day
MONTHLY_GROWTH_GB=$(echo "scale=2; $DAILY_INGESTION_MB * 30 / 1024" | bc)

echo "Projected Impact (without retention):"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "Daily ingestion: ~${DAILY_INGESTION_MB} MB"
echo "Monthly growth: ~${MONTHLY_GROWTH_GB} GB"
echo ""

# Project 12-month cost
echo "12-Month Storage Projection:"
for MONTH in 1 3 6 12; do
    PROJECTED_GB=$(echo "scale=2; $STORED_GB + ($MONTHLY_GROWTH_GB * $MONTH)" | bc)
    PROJECTED_COST=$(echo "scale=2; $PROJECTED_GB * 0.03" | bc)
    printf "  Month %2d: %6.2f GB = \$%.2f/month\n" "$MONTH" "$PROJECTED_GB" "$PROJECTED_COST"
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
read -p "Proceed with removing retention? (y/n): " CONFIRM

if [ "$CONFIRM" == "y" ]; then
    aws logs delete-retention-policy \
        --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP"
    echo ""
    echo "✅ Retention policy removed"
    echo "   Logs will be retained indefinitely"
    echo ""
    echo "💡 Tip: Set up S3 export for cost-effective archival"
else
    echo "Operation cancelled"
fi
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Retention Removal - Cost Impact Analysis ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Current State: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-production Retention: 90 days Current Storage: 2.85 GB Current Monthly Cost: $0.09 Projected Impact (without retention): ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Daily ingestion: ~50 MB Monthly growth: ~1.46 GB 12-Month Storage Projection: Month 1: 4.31 GB = $0.13/month Month 3: 7.23 GB = $0.22/month Month 6: 11.61 GB = $0.35/month Month 12: 20.37 GB = $0.61/month ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Proceed with removing retention? (y/n): y ✅ Retention policy removed Logs will be retained indefinitely 💡 Tip: Set up S3 export for cost-effective archival
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta from django.db.models import Count from django.db.models.functions import TruncDate # Cost impact analysis context: # Daily log volume estimation by model: # JobPost activity (highest volume) daily_jobpost = JobPost.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=1) ).count() print(f"JobPost updates/day: {daily_jobpost}") # Output: JobPost updates/day: 250 # User activity daily_user = User.objects.filter( last_login__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=1) ).count() print(f"User logins/day: {daily_user}") # Output: User logins/day: 150 # Author activity daily_author = Author.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=1) ).count() print(f"Author updates/day: {daily_author}") # Output: Author updates/day: 45 # Skills changes (low volume) daily_skills = Skills.objects.filter( updated_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=1) ).count() print(f"Skills changes/day: {daily_skills}") # Output: Skills changes/day: 3 # Location lookups daily_location = Location.objects.filter( jobpost__created_at__gte=datetime.now() - timedelta(days=1) ).distinct().count() print(f"Locations accessed/day: {daily_location}") # Output: Locations accessed/day: 35 # Cost calculation estimated_mb_per_day = ( daily_jobpost * 0.1 + # ~100 bytes per JobPost log daily_user * 0.05 + # ~50 bytes per User log daily_author * 0.08 + # ~80 bytes per Author log daily_skills * 0.05 + # ~50 bytes per Skills log daily_location * 0.02 # ~20 bytes per Location log ) / 1024 # Convert KB to MB print(f"\nEstimated daily log volume: {estimated_mb_per_day:.1f} MB") print(f"Monthly growth: {estimated_mb_per_day * 30:.1f} MB") print(f"Yearly growth: {estimated_mb_per_day * 365 / 1024:.2f} GB")
Example 5: Conditional Retention Removal for Critical Log Groups

Removes retention policies only for log groups that meet specific criteria (size threshold, age, or criticality). Provides intelligent selection to avoid removing retention from non-essential logs.

#!/bin/bash
# Conditional retention removal
# Only for critical log groups meeting criteria

PREFIX="/aws/django/anihpj"
MIN_SIZE_MB=50  # Only process groups > 50MB (indicates important logs)

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Conditional Retention Removal                                ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Criteria: Log groups > ${MIN_SIZE_MB}MB with 'audit', 'security',"
echo "          'compliance', or 'critical' in name"
echo ""

# Get all log groups with details
aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$PREFIX" \
    --query 'logGroups[*].[logGroupName,retentionInDays,storedBytes]' \
    --output text | while IFS=$'\t' read -r name retention bytes; do
    
    # Calculate size in MB
    SIZE_MB=$((${bytes:-0} / 1048576))
    
    # Check if meets criteria
    IS_CRITICAL=false
    
    # Check name patterns
    if [[ "$name" == *"audit"* ]] || \
       [[ "$name" == *"security"* ]] || \
       [[ "$name" == *"compliance"* ]] || \
       [[ "$name" == *"critical"* ]]; then
        IS_CRITICAL=true
    fi
    
    # Check size threshold
    MEETS_SIZE=false
    if [ $SIZE_MB -ge $MIN_SIZE_MB ]; then
        MEETS_SIZE=true
    fi
    
    # Display status
    printf "%-50s %5dMB " "$name" "$SIZE_MB"
    
    # Process based on criteria
    if [ "$IS_CRITICAL" == "true" ]; then
        if [ "$retention" != "None" ] && [ -n "$retention" ]; then
            aws logs delete-retention-policy \
                --log-group-name "$name" \
                2>/dev/null
            echo "✅ CRITICAL - Retention removed"
        else
            echo "○ CRITICAL - Already indefinite"
        fi
    elif [ "$MEETS_SIZE" == "true" ]; then
        echo "○ Large group - retention kept (not critical)"
    else
        echo "○ Skipped - below threshold"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Summary:"
echo "  - Critical log groups: retention removed (indefinite)"
echo "  - Large non-critical groups: retention preserved"
echo "  - Small groups: unchanged"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Conditional Retention Removal ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Criteria: Log groups > 50MB with 'audit', 'security', 'compliance', or 'critical' in name /aws/django/anihpj-production 485MB ○ Large group - retention kept (not critical) /aws/django/anihpj-staging 45MB ○ Skipped - below threshold /aws/django/anihpj-audit 95MB ✅ CRITICAL - Retention removed /aws/django/anihpj-security 78MB ✅ CRITICAL - Retention removed /aws/django/anihpj-compliance 62MB ✅ CRITICAL - Retention removed /aws/django/anihpj/models/jobpost 85MB ○ Large group - retention kept (not critical) /aws/django/anihpj/models/user 50MB ○ Large group - retention kept (not critical) /aws/django/anihpj-development 15MB ○ Skipped - below threshold /aws/django/anihpj-critical-errors 55MB ✅ CRITICAL - Retention removed ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Summary: - Critical log groups: retention removed (indefinite) - Large non-critical groups: retention preserved - Small groups: unchanged
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime import logging # Conditional retention based on log criticality: # CRITICAL LOGS (retention removed): # 1. Audit logs - All model changes audit_events = { 'JobPost': JobPost.objects.count() * 3, # create, update, delete 'User': User.objects.count() * 3, 'Author': Author.objects.count() * 3, 'Skills': Skills.objects.count() * 3, 'Location': Location.objects.count() * 3, } print(f"Audit log entries (indefinite):") for model, count in audit_events.items(): print(f" {model}: ~{count} events") # Output: # JobPost: ~10500 events # User: ~1560 events # Author: ~1350 events # Skills: ~255 events # Location: ~360 events # 2. Security logs - Auth events security_events = User.objects.filter( last_login__isnull=False ).count() * 10 # Avg 10 security events per user print(f"\nSecurity log entries: ~{security_events}") # Output: Security log entries: ~4850 # 3. Compliance logs - Regulated data access compliance_events = JobPost.objects.filter( salary_min__isnull=False ).count() print(f"Compliance (salary data) entries: ~{compliance_events}") # Output: Compliance (salary data) entries: ~780 # NON-CRITICAL LOGS (retention preserved): # Production logs, model operation logs, etc. # These still have time-based retention print(f"\nCritical groups (indefinite): 4") print(f"Non-critical groups (30-90 day retention): 6") print(f"Small groups (7-14 day retention): 3")

put-metric-filter

Creates or updates a metric filter that extracts metric observations from CloudWatch Logs log events and transforms them into CloudWatch Metrics data points. This enables you to convert log data into actionable metrics for monitoring, alerting, and dashboards. Essential for tracking Django application performance, error rates, and model operation metrics for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models.

Syntax
aws logs put-metric-filter \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    --filter-name <value> \
    --filter-pattern <value> \
    --metric-transformations <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --log-group-name (string, required): The name of the log group to associate the metric filter with. Example: "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
  • --filter-name (string, required): A name for the metric filter. Must be unique within the log group. Example: "DjangoErrorCount"
  • --filter-pattern (string, required): A filter pattern for extracting metric data from log events. Can be a simple text match or a complex pattern with JSON field references. Example: "ERROR" or "{ $.level = \"ERROR\" }"
  • --metric-transformations (list, required): An array of metric transformations specifying how to transform extracted log data into metrics. Maximum 1 transformation per filter. Contains: metricName, metricNamespace, metricValue, defaultValue (optional), unit (optional), dimensions (optional)
Return Value
  • None: This command does not return any output on success
  • InvalidParameterException: Returned if the filter pattern or metric transformation is invalid
  • ResourceNotFoundException: Returned if the log group does not exist
  • LimitExceededException: Returned if you exceed the limit of metric filters per log group (100)
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Create Error Count Metric Filter for Django Application

Creates a metric filter that counts all ERROR level log events in the Django application logs. Tracks errors across all model operations including JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location to enable alerting and monitoring.

#!/bin/bash
# Create error count metric filter
# Tracks all Django ERROR level events

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
FILTER_NAME="DjangoErrorCount"
NAMESPACE="Anihpj/Django"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Creating Error Count Metric Filter                           ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Filter Name: $FILTER_NAME"
echo "Namespace: $NAMESPACE"
echo ""

# Create the metric filter
aws logs put-metric-filter \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name "$FILTER_NAME" \
    --filter-pattern "ERROR" \
    --metric-transformations '[{
        "metricName": "ErrorCount",
        "metricNamespace": "'"$NAMESPACE"'",
        "metricValue": "1",
        "defaultValue": 0,
        "unit": "Count"
    }]'

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "✅ Metric filter created successfully"
    echo ""
    echo "Metric Details:"
    echo "  Name: ErrorCount"
    echo "  Namespace: $NAMESPACE"
    echo "  Pattern: 'ERROR' (matches ERROR anywhere in log)"
    echo "  Value: 1 per match"
    echo ""
    
    # Verify the filter
    aws logs describe-metric-filters \
        --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
        --filter-name-prefix "$FILTER_NAME" \
        --query 'metricFilters[0]' \
        --output yaml
else
    echo "❌ Failed to create metric filter"
fi

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Next Steps:"
echo "  1. Create CloudWatch Alarm on ErrorCount > 10/minute"
echo "  2. Add to CloudWatch Dashboard"
echo "  3. Set up SNS notification for alerts"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Creating Error Count Metric Filter ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Filter Name: DjangoErrorCount Namespace: Anihpj/Django ✅ Metric filter created successfully Metric Details: Name: ErrorCount Namespace: Anihpj/Django Pattern: 'ERROR' (matches ERROR anywhere in log) Value: 1 per match creationTime: 1699123456789 filterName: DjangoErrorCount filterPattern: ERROR logGroupName: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost metricTransformations: - defaultValue: 0.0 metricName: ErrorCount metricNamespace: Anihpj/Django metricValue: '1' unit: Count ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Next Steps: 1. Create CloudWatch Alarm on ErrorCount > 10/minute 2. Add to CloudWatch Dashboard 3. Set up SNS notification for alerts
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import logging # Error count metric filter context: logger = logging.getLogger('django') # Errors tracked by this metric filter: # 1. JobPost validation errors try: job = JobPost.objects.create( title="", # Invalid - empty title author_id=999 # Invalid author ) except Exception as e: logger.error(f"JobPost creation failed: {e}") # This ERROR log increments the metric # 2. User authentication errors try: user = User.objects.get(email="nonexistent@test.com") except User.DoesNotExist as e: logger.error(f"User lookup failed: {e}") # This ERROR log increments the metric # 3. Author profile errors try: author = Author.objects.get(pk=999) except Author.DoesNotExist: logger.error("Author not found") # This ERROR log increments the metric # 4. Skills relationship errors try: skill = Skills.objects.get(name="NonexistentSkill") except Skills.DoesNotExist: logger.error("Skills lookup failed") # This ERROR log increments the metric # 5.
