User authentication errors try: user = User.objects.get(email="invalid@test.com") except User.DoesNotExist: logger.error("User authentication failed: not found") # → Streams to Lambda → Alert triggered # 3. Author profile errors try: author = Author.objects.get(pk=999) except Author.DoesNotExist: logger.error("Author not found for profile update") # → Streams to Lambda → Alert triggered # 4. Skills lookup errors try: skill = Skills.objects.get(name="NonexistentSkill") except Skills.DoesNotExist: logger.error("Skills lookup failed") # → Streams to Lambda → Alert triggered # Lambda function processes errors: print(f"Lambda Function Behavior:") print(f" 1. Receives: Base64/gzip CloudWatch Logs event") print(f" 2. Decodes: Extracts log events") print(f" 3. Parses: Identifies error type and model") print(f" 4. Alerts: Sends to Slack/PagerDuty/SNS") print(f"") print(f"Example alert:") print(f" 🚨 ERROR in anihpj-jobpost") print(f" Model: JobPost") print(f" Message: JobPost validation failed") print(f" Time: 2024-01-20 15:30:45")
Example 2: Stream All Logs to Kinesis for Analytics

Creates a subscription filter that streams all log events to Kinesis Data Stream for real-time analytics and dashboards. Enables comprehensive analysis of all Django model operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Stream all logs to Kinesis for analytics
# Real-time processing pipeline

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
FILTER_NAME="AllLogsToKinesis"
KINESIS_STREAM="arn:aws:kinesis:us-east-1:123456789012:stream/django-logs"
IAM_ROLE="arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CWLogsToKinesisRole"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Create Log Streaming to Kinesis                              ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Filter: $FILTER_NAME"
echo "Kinesis Stream: $KINESIS_STREAM"
echo "IAM Role: $IAM_ROLE"
echo ""

# Create subscription filter with role
echo "Creating subscription filter..."
aws logs put-subscription-filter \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name "$FILTER_NAME" \
    --filter-pattern "" \
    --destination-arn "$KINESIS_STREAM" \
    --role-arn "$IAM_ROLE" \
    --distribution "Random"

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "✅ Subscription filter created successfully"
    echo ""
    echo "Configuration:"
    echo "  Filter Pattern: '' (all events)"
    echo "  Destination: Kinesis Data Stream"
    echo "  Distribution: Random (load balanced across shards)"
    echo ""
    
    # Verify stream is receiving data
    echo "Kinesis Stream Status:"
    aws kinesis describe-stream \
        --stream-name "django-logs" \
        --query 'StreamDescription.{Status:StreamStatus,Shards:length(Shards)}' \
        --output yaml
else
    echo "❌ Failed to create subscription filter"
fi

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Analytics Pipeline:"
echo "  CloudWatch Logs → Kinesis → (Consumer Options):"
echo "    1. Kinesis Data Analytics (SQL/Flink)"
echo "    2. Lambda for custom processing"
echo "    3. Kinesis Firehose → S3/Redshift/OpenSearch"
echo "    4. EC2/ECS consumer applications"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Create Log Streaming to Kinesis ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Filter: AllLogsToKinesis Kinesis Stream: arn:aws:kinesis:us-east-1:123456789012:stream/django-logs IAM Role: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CWLogsToKinesisRole Creating subscription filter... ✅ Subscription filter created successfully Configuration: Filter Pattern: '' (all events) Destination: Kinesis Data Stream Distribution: Random (load balanced across shards) Kinesis Stream Status: Shards: 4 Status: ACTIVE ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Analytics Pipeline: CloudWatch Logs → Kinesis → (Consumer Options): 1. Kinesis Data Analytics (SQL/Flink) 2. Lambda for custom processing 3. Kinesis Firehose → S3/Redshift/OpenSearch 4. EC2/ECS consumer applications
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # ALL logs stream to Kinesis - complete visibility: # Every Django operation generates a log event: # 1. JobPost operations job = JobPost.objects.create( title="Python Developer", author=author, location=location ) # Log → Kinesis: {"model": "JobPost", "action": "created", ...} # 2. User operations user = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).first() # Log → Kinesis: {"model": "User", "query": "filter", ...} # 3. Author operations author = Author.objects.select_related('user').get(pk=1) # Log → Kinesis: {"model": "Author", "query": "select_related", ...} # 4. Skills operations skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains="python") # Log → Kinesis: {"model": "Skills", "query": "filter", ...} # 5. Location operations locations = Location.objects.filter(country="USA") # Log → Kinesis: {"model": "Location", "query": "filter", ...} # Kinesis consumer can build real-time dashboards: print(f"Real-Time Analytics Available:") print(f" - Operations per second by model") print(f" - Error rate trends") print(f" - Response time percentiles") print(f" - User activity patterns") print(f"") print(f"Log Volume Estimate:") print(f" JobPost: ~500 events/hour") print(f" User: ~300 events/hour") print(f" Author: ~150 events/hour") print(f" Skills: ~100 events/hour") print(f" Location: ~50 events/hour") print(f" Total: ~1100 events/hour → Kinesis")
Example 3: Stream Security Events to Firehose for S3 Archival

Creates a subscription filter that streams security-related log events to Kinesis Data Firehose for archival in S3. Enables long-term retention of authentication and authorization logs for compliance.

#!/bin/bash
# Stream security events to Firehose → S3
# Compliance archival for Django auth logs

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-security"
FILTER_NAME="SecurityToS3Archive"
FIREHOSE_ARN="arn:aws:firehose:us-east-1:123456789012:deliverystream/security-logs-archive"
IAM_ROLE="arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CWLogsToFirehoseRole"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Security Log Archival via Firehose                           ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Filter: $FILTER_NAME"
echo "Firehose: $FIREHOSE_ARN"
echo ""

# Create subscription filter for security events
aws logs put-subscription-filter \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name "$FILTER_NAME" \
    --filter-pattern "{ $.event_type = \"auth*\" }" \
    --destination-arn "$FIREHOSE_ARN" \
    --role-arn "$IAM_ROLE"

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "✅ Security archival subscription created"
    echo ""
    echo "Configuration:"
    echo "  Pattern: auth* events (login, logout, auth_failure)"
    echo "  Destination: Firehose → S3"
    echo ""
    
    # Show Firehose configuration
    echo "Firehose Delivery Stream:"
    aws firehose describe-delivery-stream \
        --delivery-stream-name "security-logs-archive" \
        --query 'DeliveryStreamDescription.Destinations[0].S3DestinationDescription.{Bucket:BucketARN,Prefix:Prefix,Compression:CompressionFormat}' \
        --output yaml
else
    echo "❌ Failed to create subscription"
fi

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "S3 Archive Structure:"
echo "  s3://security-logs-bucket/"
echo "    └── django-security/"
echo "        └── year=2024/"
echo "            └── month=01/"
echo "                └── day=20/"
echo "                    └── hour=15/"
echo "                        └── security-logs-1-2024-01-20-15-00.gz"
echo ""
echo "Compliance Features:"
echo "  - S3 Object Lock for immutability"
echo "  - S3 Glacier transition after 90 days"
echo "  - 7-year retention policy"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Security Log Archival via Firehose ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-security Filter: SecurityToS3Archive Firehose: arn:aws:firehose:us-east-1:123456789012:deliverystream/security-logs-archive ✅ Security archival subscription created Configuration: Pattern: auth* events (login, logout, auth_failure) Destination: Firehose → S3 Firehose Delivery Stream: Bucket: arn:aws:s3:::security-logs-bucket Compression: GZIP Prefix: django-security/year=!{timestamp:yyyy}/month=!{timestamp:MM}/day=!{timestamp:dd}/hour=!{timestamp:HH}/ ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ S3 Archive Structure: s3://security-logs-bucket/ └── django-security/ └── year=2024/ └── month=01/ └── day=20/ └── hour=15/ └── security-logs-1-2024-01-20-15-00.gz Compliance Features: - S3 Object Lock for immutability - S3 Glacier transition after 90 days - 7-year retention policy
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.contrib.auth import authenticate, login, logout import logging import json # Security events archived to S3: security_logger = logging.getLogger('django.security') # These events match the filter pattern and stream to S3: # 1. User login events def user_login(request, user): security_logger.info(json.dumps({ 'event_type': 'auth_login', 'user_id': user.id, 'email': user.email, 'ip_address': request.META.get('REMOTE_ADDR'), 'user_agent': request.META.get('HTTP_USER_AGENT'), 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat() })) # → Firehose → S3 archive # 2. User logout events def user_logout(request, user): security_logger.info(json.dumps({ 'event_type': 'auth_logout', 'user_id': user.id, 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat() })) # → Firehose → S3 archive # 3. Failed login attempts def failed_login(request, email): security_logger.warning(json.dumps({ 'event_type': 'auth_failure', 'attempted_email': email, 'ip_address': request.META.get('REMOTE_ADDR'), 'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat() })) # → Firehose → S3 archive # Compliance tracking: print(f"Security Events Archived:") print(f" auth_login: User login success") print(f" auth_logout: User session end") print(f" auth_failure: Failed login attempts") print(f"") print(f"Daily Security Volume:") users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f" Active Users: {users}") print(f" Est. logins/day: {users * 2}") print(f" Est. logouts/day: {users * 2}") print(f" Est. failures/day: ~50") print(f"") print(f"Archived in S3 for 7 years (compliance)")
Example 4: Stream Model-Specific Events with JSON Pattern

Creates a subscription filter using JSON pattern matching to stream only specific Django model operation events to a Lambda function for custom processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Stream JobPost events to Lambda for processing
# JSON pattern matching for specific model

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
FILTER_NAME="JobPostEventsToLambda"
LAMBDA_ARN="arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:process-jobpost-events"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  JobPost Event Streaming with JSON Pattern                    ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Create subscription with JSON pattern filter
# Match only JobPost model events
FILTER_PATTERN='{ $.model = "JobPost" }'

echo "Creating subscription filter..."
echo "  Pattern: $FILTER_PATTERN"
echo "  Matches: All logs with model='JobPost'"
echo ""

# Add Lambda permission
aws lambda add-permission \
    --function-name "process-jobpost-events" \
    --statement-id "CWLogsJobPostPermission" \
    --principal "logs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com" \
    --action "lambda:InvokeFunction" \
    --source-arn "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:${LOG_GROUP}:*" \
    --source-account "123456789012" \
    2>/dev/null

# Create subscription filter
aws logs put-subscription-filter \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name "$FILTER_NAME" \
    --filter-pattern "$FILTER_PATTERN" \
    --destination-arn "$LAMBDA_ARN"

echo "✅ Subscription filter created"
echo ""

# Create additional filters for other models
echo "Creating filters for other models..."

MODELS=("User" "Author" "Skills" "Location")
for MODEL in "${MODELS[@]}"; do
    FILTER_NAME="${MODEL}EventsToLambda"
    FILTER_PATTERN="{ \$.model = \"$MODEL\" }"
    LAMBDA_ARN="arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:process-${MODEL,,}-events"
    
    aws logs put-subscription-filter \
        --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
        --filter-name "$FILTER_NAME" \
        --filter-pattern "$FILTER_PATTERN" \
        --destination-arn "$LAMBDA_ARN" \
        2>/dev/null && echo "  ✅ $MODEL filter created" || echo "  ⚠️  $MODEL filter skipped (limit reached)"
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Note: Log groups support max 2 subscription filters"
echo "Consider using a single filter with broader pattern"
echo "and routing in the Lambda function"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ JobPost Event Streaming with JSON Pattern ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Creating subscription filter... Pattern: { $.model = "JobPost" } Matches: All logs with model='JobPost' ✅ Subscription filter created Creating filters for other models... ✅ User filter created ⚠️ Author filter skipped (limit reached) ⚠️ Skills filter skipped (limit reached) ⚠️ Location filter skipped (limit reached) ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Note: Log groups support max 2 subscription filters Consider using a single filter with broader pattern and routing in the Lambda function
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json import logging # JSON pattern matching for model-specific streaming: logger = logging.getLogger('django.models') # Structured logging that matches JSON patterns: # JobPost events - match { $.model = "JobPost" } def log_jobpost_event(action, instance): logger.info(json.dumps({ 'model': 'JobPost', # Matches filter pattern 'action': action, 'id': instance.pk, 'title': instance.title, 'author_id': instance.author_id, 'location_id': instance.location_id })) # → Lambda processes JobPost events # User events - match { $.model = "User" } def log_user_event(action, instance): logger.info(json.dumps({ 'model': 'User', # Matches filter pattern 'action': action, 'id': instance.pk, 'email': instance.email })) # → Lambda processes User events # Example events that stream to Lambda: print(f"Events Streamed to Lambda:") print(f"") print(f"JobPost Filter Matches:") print(f' {{"model": "JobPost", "action": "created", "id": 123}}') print(f' {{"model": "JobPost", "action": "updated", "title": "..."}}') print(f"") print(f"User Filter Matches:") print(f' {{"model": "User", "action": "registered", "id": 456}}') print(f' {{"model": "User", "action": "updated", "email": "..."}}') print(f"") print(f"Note: Author, Skills, Location events NOT streamed") print(f" (subscription filter limit of 2 per log group)")
Example 5: Create Multi-Destination Streaming Architecture

Sets up a comprehensive log streaming architecture with multiple destinations for different log types. Routes errors to Lambda, all logs to Kinesis, and creates the necessary IAM roles and permissions.

#!/bin/bash
# Multi-destination streaming architecture
# Comprehensive real-time log processing

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
ACCOUNT_ID="123456789012"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Multi-Destination Streaming Architecture                     ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Note: Log groups support max 2 subscription filters
# Strategy: Use broad filters and route in destinations

echo "Setting up streaming destinations..."
echo ""

# Destination 1: Errors to Lambda for alerting
echo "1. Error Alerting (Lambda)"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
LAMBDA_ARN="arn:aws:lambda:${REGION}:${ACCOUNT_ID}:function:error-alerting"

aws logs put-subscription-filter \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name "ErrorsToLambda" \
    --filter-pattern "?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception" \
    --destination-arn "$LAMBDA_ARN"

echo "  ✅ Errors/Criticals/Exceptions → Lambda"
echo "  Pattern: ?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception (OR matching)"
echo ""

# Destination 2: All logs to Firehose for S3/analytics
echo "2. Full Log Archive (Firehose → S3)"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
FIREHOSE_ARN="arn:aws:firehose:${REGION}:${ACCOUNT_ID}:deliverystream/django-logs-archive"
ROLE_ARN="arn:aws:iam::${ACCOUNT_ID}:role/CWLogsToFirehoseRole"

aws logs put-subscription-filter \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name "AllLogsToFirehose" \
    --filter-pattern "" \
    --destination-arn "$FIREHOSE_ARN" \
    --role-arn "$ROLE_ARN"

echo "  ✅ All logs → Firehose → S3 + OpenSearch"
echo "  Pattern: '' (matches everything)"
echo ""

echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Architecture Summary:"
echo ""
echo "  ┌─────────────────┐"
echo "  │  Django Logs    │"
echo "  │  (CloudWatch)   │"
echo "  └────────┬────────┘"
echo "           │"
echo "      ┌────┴────┐"
echo "      ▼         ▼"
echo "  ┌───────┐  ┌──────────┐"
echo "  │Lambda │  │ Firehose │"
echo "  │(Errors)│ │(All Logs)│"
echo "  └───┬───┘  └────┬─────┘"
echo "      │           │"
echo "      ▼           ├──────┐"
echo "   ┌──────┐       ▼      ▼"
echo "   │ SNS/ │    ┌────┐ ┌─────────┐"
echo "   │Slack │    │ S3 │ │OpenSearch│"
echo "   └──────┘    └────┘ └─────────┘"
echo ""
echo "Filter Utilization: 2/2 (maximum reached)"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Multi-Destination Streaming Architecture ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Setting up streaming destinations... 1. Error Alerting (Lambda) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ✅ Errors/Criticals/Exceptions → Lambda Pattern: ?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception (OR matching) 2. Full Log Archive (Firehose → S3) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ✅ All logs → Firehose → S3 + OpenSearch Pattern: '' (matches everything) ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Architecture Summary: ┌─────────────────┐ │ Django Logs │ │ (CloudWatch) │ └────────┬────────┘ │ ┌────┴────┐ ▼ ▼ ┌───────┐ ┌──────────┐ │Lambda │ │ Firehose │ │(Errors)│ │(All Logs)│ └───┬───┘ └────┬─────┘ │ │ ▼ ├──────┐ ┌──────┐ ▼ ▼ │ SNS/ │ ┌────┐ ┌─────────┐ │Slack │ │ S3 │ │OpenSearch│ └──────┘ └────┘ └─────────┘ Filter Utilization: 2/2 (maximum reached)
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import logging # Multi-destination streaming context: logger = logging.getLogger('django') # All logs go to Firehose (pattern: '') # Errors additionally go to Lambda (pattern: ?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception) # 1. Normal INFO logs → Firehose only logger.info(f"JobPost created: {job.title}") # → Firehose → S3/OpenSearch (for search/analytics) # 2. ERROR logs → Lambda AND Firehose logger.error(f"JobPost validation failed: {error}") # → Lambda (immediate alert to Slack) # → Firehose → S3/OpenSearch (for historical analysis) # 3. CRITICAL logs → Lambda AND Firehose logger.critical(f"Database connection lost!") # → Lambda (immediate PagerDuty alert) # → Firehose → S3/OpenSearch # Model operation flow: print(f"Streaming Destinations by Log Level:") print(f"") print(f" DEBUG → Firehose only (if enabled)") print(f" INFO → Firehose only") print(f" WARNING → Firehose only") print(f" ERROR → Lambda + Firehose") print(f" CRITICAL → Lambda + Firehose") print(f"") print(f"Model Coverage (all levels):") print(f" JobPost: {JobPost.objects.count()} records tracked") print(f" User: {User.objects.count()} records tracked") print(f" Author: {Author.objects.count()} records tracked") print(f" Skills: {Skills.objects.count()} records tracked") print(f" Location: {Location.objects.count()} records tracked") print(f"") print(f"Real-time Benefits:") print(f" - Errors: < 1 second to Slack alert") print(f" - Archive: < 60 seconds to S3") print(f" - Search: < 5 seconds to OpenSearch")

describe-subscription-filters

Lists and describes the subscription filters associated with a specific log group. Subscription filters stream matching log events to destinations like Lambda, Kinesis, or Firehose in real-time. This command helps you audit, monitor, and manage log streaming configurations for Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    [--filter-name-prefix <value>] \
    [--max-items <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --log-group-name (string, required): The name of the log group to list subscription filters for. Example: "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
  • --filter-name-prefix (string, optional): Filters the results to include only subscription filters with names starting with this prefix. Example: "Error"
  • --max-items (integer, optional): The maximum number of subscription filters to return. Default returns all
Return Value
  • subscriptionFilters (list): Array of subscription filter objects containing:
    • filterName: Name of the subscription filter
    • logGroupName: The associated log group name
    • filterPattern: The filter pattern used to match log events
    • destinationArn: ARN of the destination (Lambda, Kinesis, Firehose)
    • roleArn: IAM role ARN (for Kinesis destinations)
    • distribution: Distribution method (Random or ByLogStream)
    • creationTime: Timestamp when the filter was created
  • nextToken (string): Token for pagination if more results exist
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: List All Subscription Filters for Django Application

Retrieves all subscription filters configured for the Django application log group. Provides a complete inventory of real-time log streaming configurations.

#!/bin/bash
# List all subscription filters
# Complete streaming inventory

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Subscription Filters Inventory                               ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo ""

# Get all subscription filters
FILTERS=$(aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --output json)

# Count filters
COUNT=$(echo "$FILTERS" | jq '.subscriptionFilters | length')
echo "Total Subscription Filters: $COUNT / 2 (max allowed)"
echo ""

if [ "$COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
    echo ""
    
    # Display each filter
    echo "$FILTERS" | jq -r '.subscriptionFilters[] | 
        "Filter: \(.filterName)\n" +
        "  Pattern: \(.filterPattern | if . == "" then "(all events)" else . end)\n" +
        "  Destination: \(.destinationArn | split(":") | .[-1])\n" +
        "  Type: \(.destinationArn | split(":") | .[2])\n" +
        "  Created: \(.creationTime | . / 1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))\n" +
        "  Distribution: \(.distribution // "N/A")\n"'
else
    echo "No subscription filters configured"
fi

echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Streaming Destinations:"
echo "$FILTERS" | jq -r '.subscriptionFilters[].destinationArn | split(":") | .[2]' | sort | uniq -c | sed 's/^/  /'
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Subscription Filters Inventory ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Total Subscription Filters: 2 / 2 (max allowed) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Filter: ErrorsToLambda Pattern: ?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception Destination: process-django-errors Type: lambda Created: 2024-01-15 10:30:45 Distribution: ByLogStream Filter: AllLogsToFirehose Pattern: (all events) Destination: django-logs-archive Type: firehose Created: 2024-01-15 10:35:22 Distribution: Random ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Streaming Destinations: 1 firehose 1 lambda
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import logging # Subscription filter context: # Current streaming configuration: print(f"Log Streaming Configuration:") print(f"") print(f"1. ErrorsToLambda") print(f" Pattern: ?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception") print(f" Matches Django errors from all models:") # Errors that stream to Lambda: errors_logged = [ "ERROR: JobPost.DoesNotExist", "CRITICAL: Database connection lost", "Exception: Author profile validation failed" ] for error in errors_logged: print(f" • {error}") print(f"") print(f"2. AllLogsToFirehose") print(f" Pattern: '' (all events)") print(f" Archives ALL Django operations:") # All operations archived: print(f" • JobPost: {JobPost.objects.count()} records") print(f" • User: {User.objects.count()} records") print(f" • Author: {Author.objects.count()} records") print(f" • Skills: {Skills.objects.count()} records") print(f" • Location: {Location.objects.count()} records") # Filter utilization print(f"") print(f"Filter Utilization: 2/2 (at capacity)") print(f" ⚠️ Cannot add more subscription filters") print(f" 💡 Use Lambda routing for additional destinations")
Example 2: Audit Subscription Filters Across All Django Log Groups

Audits subscription filters across all Django application log groups to ensure consistent streaming coverage and identify gaps in real-time log processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Audit subscriptions across all Django log groups
# Comprehensive streaming coverage check

LOG_PREFIX="/aws/django/anihpj"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Cross-Log-Group Subscription Filter Audit                    ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Get all log groups
LOG_GROUPS=$(aws logs describe-log-groups \
    --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_PREFIX" \
    --query 'logGroups[*].logGroupName' \
    --output text)

echo "Auditing subscriptions for: $LOG_PREFIX*"
echo ""

# Track statistics
TOTAL_GROUPS=0
GROUPS_WITH_SUBS=0
TOTAL_SUBS=0

for LOG_GROUP in $LOG_GROUPS; do
    ((TOTAL_GROUPS++))
    
    echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
    echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
    
    # Get subscriptions
    SUBS=$(aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
        --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
        --output json 2>/dev/null)
    
    COUNT=$(echo "$SUBS" | jq '.subscriptionFilters | length')
    
    if [ "$COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
        ((GROUPS_WITH_SUBS++))
        TOTAL_SUBS=$((TOTAL_SUBS + COUNT))
        
        echo "  Subscriptions: $COUNT"
        echo "$SUBS" | jq -r '.subscriptionFilters[] | 
            "    • \(.filterName) → \(.destinationArn | split(":") | .[-1]) (\(.destinationArn | split(":") | .[2]))"'
    else
        echo "  ⚠️  No subscription filters"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Audit Summary:"
echo "  Total Log Groups: $TOTAL_GROUPS"
echo "  Groups with Subscriptions: $GROUPS_WITH_SUBS"
echo "  Groups without Subscriptions: $((TOTAL_GROUPS - GROUPS_WITH_SUBS))"
echo "  Total Subscription Filters: $TOTAL_SUBS"
echo ""
echo "Recommendations:"
if [ $((TOTAL_GROUPS - GROUPS_WITH_SUBS)) -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "  ⚠️  Add streaming to $((TOTAL_GROUPS - GROUPS_WITH_SUBS)) unmonitored groups"
fi
echo "  ✓ Consider Firehose for centralized archival"
echo "  ✓ Use Lambda for real-time alerting"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Cross-Log-Group Subscription Filter Audit ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Auditing subscriptions for: /aws/django/anihpj* ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-production Subscriptions: 2 • ErrorsToLambda → process-django-errors (lambda) • AllLogsToFirehose → django-logs-archive (firehose) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-staging Subscriptions: 1 • StagingErrorsToLambda → staging-error-processor (lambda) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-development ⚠️ No subscription filters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-security Subscriptions: 1 • SecurityToFirehose → security-logs-archive (firehose) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-audit ⚠️ No subscription filters ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Audit Summary: Total Log Groups: 5 Groups with Subscriptions: 3 Groups without Subscriptions: 2 Total Subscription Filters: 4 Recommendations: ⚠️ Add streaming to 2 unmonitored groups ✓ Consider Firehose for centralized archival ✓ Use Lambda for real-time alerting
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Cross-log-group audit context: # Production - full coverage print(f"Production Log Group:") print(f" ✅ ErrorsToLambda: Real-time alerting") print(f" ✅ AllLogsToFirehose: S3 archival") # Staging - partial coverage print(f"\nStaging Log Group:") print(f" ✅ StagingErrorsToLambda: Error alerting") print(f" ⚠️ No archival (acceptable for staging)") # Development - no streaming print(f"\nDevelopment Log Group:") print(f" ❌ No subscription filters") print(f" 💡 Consider: Add error alerting during dev") # Security - archival only print(f"\nSecurity Log Group:") print(f" ✅ SecurityToFirehose: Compliance archival") print(f" 💡 Consider: Add Lambda for breach alerts") # Audit - no streaming print(f"\nAudit Log Group:") print(f" ❌ No subscription filters") print(f" ⚠️ Should add Firehose for compliance") # Model coverage summary print(f"\nModel Coverage by Environment:") print(f" JobPost: Prod=✅ Stage=✅ Dev=❌") print(f" User: Prod=✅ Stage=✅ Dev=❌") print(f" Author: Prod=✅ Stage=✅ Dev=❌") print(f" Skills: Prod=✅ Stage=✅ Dev=❌") print(f" Location: Prod=✅ Stage=✅ Dev=❌")
Example 3: Monitor Subscription Filter Health and Delivery Status

Checks the health and delivery status of subscription filters by examining destination metrics. Ensures log streaming is working correctly for Django model operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Monitor subscription filter health
# Check delivery status to destinations

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Subscription Filter Health Check                             ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Get subscription filters
FILTERS=$(aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --output json)

echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo ""

echo "$FILTERS" | jq -r '.subscriptionFilters[] | @base64' | while read filter; do
    # Decode filter
    FILTER_DATA=$(echo "$filter" | base64 -d)
    
    NAME=$(echo "$FILTER_DATA" | jq -r '.filterName')
    DEST_ARN=$(echo "$FILTER_DATA" | jq -r '.destinationArn')
    DEST_TYPE=$(echo "$DEST_ARN" | cut -d: -f3)
    DEST_NAME=$(echo "$DEST_ARN" | rev | cut -d: -f1 | rev)
    
    echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
    echo "Filter: $NAME"
    echo "  Destination: $DEST_NAME ($DEST_TYPE)"
    
    # Check destination health based on type
    case $DEST_TYPE in
        "lambda")
            # Check Lambda invocation errors
            ERRORS=$(aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
                --namespace "AWS/Lambda" \
                --metric-name "Errors" \
                --dimensions "Name=FunctionName,Value=$DEST_NAME" \
                --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
                --end-time $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
                --period 3600 \
                --statistics Sum \
                --query 'Datapoints[0].Sum' \
                --output text 2>/dev/null)
            
            INVOCATIONS=$(aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
                --namespace "AWS/Lambda" \
                --metric-name "Invocations" \
                --dimensions "Name=FunctionName,Value=$DEST_NAME" \
                --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
                --end-time $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
                --period 3600 \
                --statistics Sum \
                --query 'Datapoints[0].Sum' \
                --output text 2>/dev/null)
            
            echo "  Last Hour: ${INVOCATIONS:-0} invocations, ${ERRORS:-0} errors"
            
            if [ "${ERRORS:-0}" == "0" ] || [ "$ERRORS" == "None" ]; then
                echo "  Status: ✅ Healthy"
            else
                echo "  Status: ⚠️  Errors detected"
            fi
            ;;
            
        "firehose")
            # Check Firehose delivery success
            echo "  Status: Checking Firehose metrics..."
            aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
                --namespace "AWS/Firehose" \
                --metric-name "DeliveryToS3.Success" \
                --dimensions "Name=DeliveryStreamName,Value=$DEST_NAME" \
                --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
                --end-time $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
                --period 3600 \
                --statistics Sum \
                --query 'Datapoints[0].Sum' \
                --output text 2>/dev/null | xargs -I{} echo "  Successful Deliveries: {}"
            ;;
    esac
    echo ""
done

echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Subscription Filter Health Check ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Filter: ErrorsToLambda Destination: process-django-errors (lambda) Last Hour: 45 invocations, 0 errors Status: ✅ Healthy ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Filter: AllLogsToFirehose Destination: django-logs-archive (firehose) Status: Checking Firehose metrics... Successful Deliveries: 1250 ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta # Subscription filter health context: # Lambda filter health: print(f"ErrorsToLambda Health:") print(f" Invocations (last hour): 45") print(f" Errors: 0") print(f" Success rate: 100%") print(f" Status: ✅ Healthy") # What triggered those 45 invocations? # Each error log event from Django triggers Lambda: errors_by_model = { 'JobPost': 25, # Validation errors, not found 'User': 10, # Auth failures 'Author': 5, # Profile errors 'Skills': 3, # Lookup failures 'Location': 2 # Query errors } print(f"\n Errors by Model:") for model, count in errors_by_model.items(): print(f" {model}: {count}") # Firehose filter health: print(f"\nAllLogsToFirehose Health:") print(f" Deliveries (last hour): 1250") print(f" Records per delivery: ~100 (batched)") print(f" Total logs archived: ~125,000") print(f" Status: ✅ Healthy") # Logs archived by model: logs_by_model = { 'JobPost': 50000, 'User': 30000, 'Author': 20000, 'Skills': 15000, 'Location': 10000 } print(f"\n Logs by Model:") for model, count in logs_by_model.items(): print(f" {model}: {count:,}")
Example 4: Generate Subscription Filter Documentation

Creates comprehensive documentation of all subscription filters including their configurations, destinations, and purposes. Useful for compliance auditing and team knowledge sharing.

#!/bin/bash
# Generate subscription filter documentation
# Compliance and team documentation

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
OUTPUT_FILE="/tmp/subscription-filters-doc.md"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Generating Subscription Filter Documentation                 ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Get filters
FILTERS=$(aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --output json)

# Generate markdown documentation
cat > "$OUTPUT_FILE" << EOF
# Subscription Filters Documentation

**Log Group:** \`$LOG_GROUP\`  
**Generated:** $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')  
**Environment:** Production

## Overview

This document describes the real-time log streaming configuration for the Django application.

### Filter Summary

| Filter Name | Pattern | Destination | Type |
|-------------|---------|-------------|------|
EOF

# Add filter rows
echo "$FILTERS" | jq -r '.subscriptionFilters[] | 
    "| \(.filterName) | `\(.filterPattern | if . == "" then "*" else . end)` | \(.destinationArn | split(":") | .[-1]) | \(.destinationArn | split(":") | .[2]) |"' >> "$OUTPUT_FILE"

# Add detailed sections
cat >> "$OUTPUT_FILE" << 'EOF'

## Filter Details

EOF

echo "$FILTERS" | jq -r '.subscriptionFilters[] | 
    "### \(.filterName)\n\n" +
    "- **Filter Pattern:** `\(.filterPattern | if . == "" then "(all events)" else . end)`\n" +
    "- **Destination ARN:** `\(.destinationArn)`\n" +
    "- **Distribution:** \(.distribution // "N/A")\n" +
    "- **Created:** \(.creationTime | . / 1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))\n\n" +
    "#### Purpose\n\n" +
    "Streams matching log events for real-time processing.\n\n"' >> "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Documentation generated: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
cat "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Generating Subscription Filter Documentation ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Documentation generated: /tmp/subscription-filters-doc.md # Subscription Filters Documentation **Log Group:** `/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost` **Generated:** 2024-01-20 15:30:45 **Environment:** Production ## Overview This document describes the real-time log streaming configuration for the Django application. ### Filter Summary | Filter Name | Pattern | Destination | Type | |-------------|---------|-------------|------| | ErrorsToLambda | `?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception` | process-django-errors | lambda | | AllLogsToFirehose | `*` | django-logs-archive | firehose | ## Filter Details ### ErrorsToLambda - **Filter Pattern:** `?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception` - **Destination ARN:** `arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:process-django-errors` - **Distribution:** ByLogStream - **Created:** 2024-01-15 10:30:45 #### Purpose Streams matching log events for real-time processing. ### AllLogsToFirehose - **Filter Pattern:** `(all events)` - **Destination ARN:** `arn:aws:firehose:us-east-1:123456789012:deliverystream/django-logs-archive` - **Distribution:** Random - **Created:** 2024-01-15 10:35:22 #### Purpose Streams matching log events for real-time processing.
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Documentation context: # Filter purposes in Django context: print(f"Subscription Filter Purposes:") print(f"") print(f"1. ErrorsToLambda") print(f" Matches: ?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception") print(f" Purpose: Real-time alerting for Django errors") print(f" Models: All (JobPost, User, Author, Skills, Location)") print(f" Actions:") print(f" - Send Slack notification") print(f" - Create PagerDuty incident (CRITICAL)") print(f" - Log to error tracking system") print(f"") print(f"2. AllLogsToFirehose") print(f" Matches: All events") print(f" Purpose: Long-term archival and analytics") print(f" Models: All (JobPost, User, Author, Skills, Location)") print(f" Destinations:") print(f" - S3: s3://logs-bucket/django-logs/") print(f" - OpenSearch: Full-text search") print(f" - Athena: SQL analytics") # Compliance documentation value: print(f"") print(f"Documentation Value:") print(f" - SOC 2 compliance evidence") print(f" - Audit trail for log handling") print(f" - Team onboarding reference") print(f" - Incident response playbook")
Example 5: Compare Subscription Filter Configurations Between Environments

Compares subscription filter configurations between production and staging environments to ensure consistency and identify configuration drift.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare subscriptions between environments
# Detect configuration drift

PROD_LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-production"
STAGING_LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-staging"

echo "╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Environment Subscription Filter Comparison                   ║"
echo "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Get filters for both environments
PROD_FILTERS=$(aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
    --log-group-name "$PROD_LOG_GROUP" \
    --output json 2>/dev/null)

STAGING_FILTERS=$(aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
    --log-group-name "$STAGING_LOG_GROUP" \
    --output json 2>/dev/null)

# Extract filter names
PROD_NAMES=$(echo "$PROD_FILTERS" | jq -r '.subscriptionFilters[].filterName' | sort)
STAGING_NAMES=$(echo "$STAGING_FILTERS" | jq -r '.subscriptionFilters[].filterName' | sort)

echo "Production Filters:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$PROD_FILTERS" | jq -r '.subscriptionFilters[] | 
    "  \(.filterName)\n    Pattern: \(.filterPattern | if . == "" then "*" else . end)\n    Dest: \(.destinationArn | split(":") | .[-1])"'
echo ""

echo "Staging Filters:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"
echo "$STAGING_FILTERS" | jq -r '.subscriptionFilters[] | 
    "  \(.filterName)\n    Pattern: \(.filterPattern | if . == "" then "*" else . end)\n    Dest: \(.destinationArn | split(":") | .[-1])"'
echo ""

echo "════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Comparison Results:"
echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"

# Find differences
PROD_COUNT=$(echo "$PROD_FILTERS" | jq '.subscriptionFilters | length')
STAGING_COUNT=$(echo "$STAGING_FILTERS" | jq '.subscriptionFilters | length')

echo "  Filter Count: Prod=$PROD_COUNT, Staging=$STAGING_COUNT"

# Check for missing in staging
echo ""
echo "  Production-only filters (missing in staging):"
comm -23 <(echo "$PROD_NAMES") <(echo "$STAGING_NAMES") | while read name; do
    echo "    ⚠️  $name"
done

# Check for staging-only
echo ""
echo "  Staging-only filters (not in production):"
comm -13 <(echo "$PROD_NAMES") <(echo "$STAGING_NAMES") | while read name; do
    echo "    ℹ️  $name"
done

echo ""
echo "Recommendations:"
if [ "$PROD_COUNT" -ne "$STAGING_COUNT" ]; then
    echo "  ⚠️  Configuration drift detected"
    echo "  ✓ Sync staging with production for consistency"
fi
echo "  ✓ Use infrastructure as code for filter management"
Output: ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Environment Subscription Filter Comparison ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ Production Filters: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ErrorsToLambda Pattern: ?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception Dest: process-django-errors AllLogsToFirehose Pattern: * Dest: django-logs-archive Staging Filters: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── StagingErrorsToLambda Pattern: ERROR Dest: staging-error-processor ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Comparison Results: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Filter Count: Prod=2, Staging=1 Production-only filters (missing in staging): ⚠️ AllLogsToFirehose ⚠️ ErrorsToLambda Staging-only filters (not in production): ℹ️ StagingErrorsToLambda Recommendations: ⚠️ Configuration drift detected ✓ Sync staging with production for consistency ✓ Use infrastructure as code for filter management
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Environment comparison context: # PRODUCTION (Full monitoring): print(f"Production Environment:") print(f" ErrorsToLambda:") print(f" Pattern: ?ERROR ?CRITICAL ?Exception") print(f" Catches: All error levels") print(f" Models: JobPost, User, Author, Skills, Location") print(f" AllLogsToFirehose:") print(f" Pattern: * (all)") print(f" Archival: Complete audit trail") # STAGING (Partial monitoring): print(f"\nStaging Environment:") print(f" StagingErrorsToLambda:") print(f" Pattern: ERROR only (missing CRITICAL, Exception)") print(f" ⚠️ Less comprehensive than production") print(f" ⚠️ Missing Firehose archival") # Configuration drift impact: print(f"\nDrift Impact:") print(f" 1. Staging may miss CRITICAL errors") print(f" 2. No log archival in staging (debugging harder)") print(f" 3. Staging doesn't reflect production behavior") # Model coverage comparison: print(f"\nModel Coverage:") models = ['JobPost', 'User', 'Author', 'Skills', 'Location'] for model in models: print(f" {model}:") print(f" Prod: ✅ Errors + Archive") print(f" Stage: ⚠️ Errors only (partial)") # Recommendation: print(f"\nRecommendation:") print(f" Use Terraform/CloudFormation to manage filters") print(f" Ensure staging mirrors production configuration")

delete-subscription-filter

Deletes a subscription filter from a CloudWatch Logs log group. Once deleted, log events will no longer be streamed to the destination (Lambda, Kinesis, or Firehose). Use this to stop real-time log streaming, reduce costs, or reconfigure streaming destinations for Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs delete-subscription-filter \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    --filter-name <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --log-group-name (string, required): The name of the log group containing the subscription filter to delete. Example: "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
  • --filter-name (string, required): The name of the subscription filter to delete. Must match exactly. Example: "ErrorsToLambda"
Return Value
  • None: This command does not return any output on success
  • ResourceNotFoundException: Returned if the subscription filter or log group does not exist
  • InvalidParameterException: Returned if the filter name is invalid
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Delete Lambda Subscription to Stop Real-Time Alerting

Removes a Lambda subscription filter to stop real-time error alerting. Useful when migrating to a different alerting system or decommissioning the Lambda function.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete Lambda subscription filter
# Stop real-time error streaming

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
FILTER_NAME="ErrorsToLambda"

echo "Deleting subscription filter: $FILTER_NAME"
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"

# Verify filter exists before deletion
EXISTS=$(aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name-prefix "$FILTER_NAME" \
    --query "subscriptionFilters[?filterName=='$FILTER_NAME'].filterName" \
    --output text)

if [ -n "$EXISTS" ]; then
    aws logs delete-subscription-filter \
        --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
        --filter-name "$FILTER_NAME"
    
    echo "Subscription filter deleted successfully"
    echo "Lambda will no longer receive Django error logs"
else
    echo "Filter not found"
fi
Output: Deleting subscription filter: ErrorsToLambda Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Subscription filter deleted successfully Lambda will no longer receive Django error logs
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import logging # After deleting ErrorsToLambda subscription: # Errors still logged but NOT streamed to Lambda logger = logging.getLogger('django') # These errors no longer trigger Lambda: try: job = JobPost.objects.get(pk=99999) except JobPost.DoesNotExist: logger.error("JobPost not found") # Previously: Lambda invoked, Slack alert sent # Now: Only logged to CloudWatch (no real-time alert) # Impact on model monitoring: print(f"Real-time alerting DISABLED for:") print(f" - JobPost errors") print(f" - User authentication failures") print(f" - Author profile errors") print(f" - Skills lookup failures") print(f" - Location query errors") print(f"") print(f"Errors still stored in CloudWatch Logs")
Example 2: Remove Firehose Subscription to Stop S3 Archival

Deletes a Firehose subscription filter to stop archiving logs to S3. Use when changing archival strategy or reducing storage costs.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete Firehose subscription filter
# Stop S3 log archival

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
FILTER_NAME="AllLogsToFirehose"

echo "Stopping log archival to S3..."
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "Filter: $FILTER_NAME"

# Get current filter details before deletion
DETAILS=$(aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query "subscriptionFilters[?filterName=='$FILTER_NAME']" \
    --output json)

echo ""
echo "Filter to delete:"
echo "$DETAILS" | jq -r '.[] | "  Destination: \(.destinationArn)"'

# Delete the filter
aws logs delete-subscription-filter \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name "$FILTER_NAME"

echo ""
echo "Subscription filter deleted"
echo "Logs will no longer be archived to S3"
Output: Stopping log archival to S3... Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Filter: AllLogsToFirehose Filter to delete: Destination: arn:aws:firehose:us-east-1:123456789012:deliverystream/django-logs-archive Subscription filter deleted Logs will no longer be archived to S3
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # After deleting Firehose subscription: # Logs no longer archived to S3 # Impact on data retention: print(f"S3 Archival STOPPED:") print(f" - New logs NOT sent to S3") print(f" - Existing S3 archives remain") print(f" - CloudWatch retention still applies") print(f"") # Models affected: print(f"Models no longer archived:") print(f" JobPost: {JobPost.objects.count()} records") print(f" User: {User.objects.count()} records") print(f" Author: {Author.objects.count()} records") print(f" Skills: {Skills.objects.count()} records") print(f" Location: {Location.objects.count()} records") print(f"") # Cost impact: print(f"Cost Impact:") print(f" - Firehose delivery: $0 (stopped)") print(f" - S3 storage growth: $0 (no new data)") print(f" - CloudWatch Logs: unchanged")
Example 3: Delete All Subscription Filters for Environment Cleanup

Removes all subscription filters from a log group being decommissioned. Ensures clean shutdown of all streaming configurations.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete all subscription filters for cleanup
# Decommission environment streaming

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-staging-old"

echo "Removing all subscription filters..."
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo ""

# Get all filters
FILTERS=$(aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query 'subscriptionFilters[].filterName' \
    --output text)

if [ -n "$FILTERS" ]; then
    for FILTER in $FILTERS; do
        echo "Deleting: $FILTER"
        aws logs delete-subscription-filter \
            --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
            --filter-name "$FILTER"
        echo "  Deleted"
    done
    echo ""
    echo "All subscription filters removed"
else
    echo "No subscription filters found"
fi
Output: Removing all subscription filters... Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-staging-old Deleting: StagingErrorsToLambda Deleted Deleting: StagingLogsToKinesis Deleted All subscription filters removed
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Environment cleanup context: print(f"Old Staging Environment Cleanup:") print(f" - StagingErrorsToLambda: DELETED") print(f" - StagingLogsToKinesis: DELETED") print(f"") print(f"Streaming stopped for all models:") print(f" JobPost, User, Author, Skills, Location") print(f"") print(f"Next steps:") print(f" 1. Delete the log group") print(f" 2. Remove Lambda permissions") print(f" 3. Clean up Kinesis stream")
Example 4: Replace Subscription Filter with New Configuration

Deletes an existing subscription filter to replace it with a new configuration. Subscription filters cannot be updated in-place, so deletion is required first.

#!/bin/bash
# Replace subscription filter
# Delete old, create new

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
OLD_FILTER="ErrorsToLambda"
NEW_FILTER="ErrorsToLambdaV2"
NEW_LAMBDA="arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:error-processor-v2"

echo "Replacing subscription filter..."
echo ""

# Delete old filter
echo "1. Deleting old filter: $OLD_FILTER"
aws logs delete-subscription-filter \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name "$OLD_FILTER"
echo "   Deleted"

# Create new filter with updated config
echo "2. Creating new filter: $NEW_FILTER"
aws logs put-subscription-filter \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name "$NEW_FILTER" \
    --filter-pattern "?ERROR ?CRITICAL" \
    --destination-arn "$NEW_LAMBDA"
echo "   Created"

echo ""
echo "Filter replacement complete"
Output: Replacing subscription filter... 1. Deleting old filter: ErrorsToLambda Deleted 2. Creating new filter: ErrorsToLambdaV2 Created Filter replacement complete
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Filter replacement context: print(f"Subscription Filter Upgraded:") print(f" OLD: ErrorsToLambda") print(f" Pattern: ERROR") print(f" Lambda: error-processor (v1)") print(f"") print(f" NEW: ErrorsToLambdaV2") print(f" Pattern: ?ERROR ?CRITICAL") print(f" Lambda: error-processor-v2") print(f"") print(f"Improvements:") print(f" - Now catches CRITICAL level too") print(f" - New Lambda has better error parsing") print(f" - Supports all Django models")
Example 5: Safely Delete Subscription with Backup

Creates a backup of the subscription filter configuration before deletion. Allows easy restoration if needed.

#!/bin/bash
# Safe deletion with backup
# Save config before removing

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
FILTER_NAME="AllLogsToFirehose"
BACKUP_FILE="/tmp/subscription-backup.json"

echo "Safe subscription filter deletion..."
echo ""

# Backup current config
echo "1. Backing up configuration..."
aws logs describe-subscription-filters \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --query "subscriptionFilters[?filterName=='$FILTER_NAME']" \
    --output json > "$BACKUP_FILE"

echo "   Saved to: $BACKUP_FILE"
cat "$BACKUP_FILE" | jq '.[0] | {filterName, filterPattern, destinationArn}'

# Delete filter
echo ""
echo "2. Deleting subscription filter..."
aws logs delete-subscription-filter \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --filter-name "$FILTER_NAME"
echo "   Deleted"

echo ""
echo "To restore, run:"
echo "  aws logs put-subscription-filter \\"
echo "    --log-group-name \"$LOG_GROUP\" \\"
echo "    --cli-input-json file://$BACKUP_FILE"
Output: Safe subscription filter deletion... 1. Backing up configuration... Saved to: /tmp/subscription-backup.json { "filterName": "AllLogsToFirehose", "filterPattern": "", "destinationArn": "arn:aws:firehose:us-east-1:123456789012:deliverystream/django-logs-archive" } 2. Deleting subscription filter... Deleted To restore, run: aws logs put-subscription-filter \ --log-group-name "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" \ --cli-input-json file:///tmp/subscription-backup.json
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Safe deletion with backup: print(f"Backup saved for restoration:") print(f" Filter: AllLogsToFirehose") print(f" Pattern: '' (all events)") print(f" Destination: Firehose to S3") print(f"") print(f"Models affected by deletion:") print(f" JobPost: Logs no longer archived") print(f" User: Logs no longer archived") print(f" Author: Logs no longer archived") print(f" Skills: Logs no longer archived") print(f" Location: Logs no longer archived") print(f"") print(f"Restoration available via backup file")

describe-queries

Lists all CloudWatch Logs Insights queries that are scheduled, currently running, or have recently completed. This command helps monitor query execution status, identify long-running queries, and manage concurrent query usage for analyzing Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs describe-queries \
    [--log-group-name <value>] \
    [--status Scheduled|Running|Complete|Failed|Cancelled|Timeout|Unknown] \
    [--max-results <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --log-group-name (string, optional): Limits results to queries that include this log group. Example: "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"
  • --status (string, optional): Filter by query status. Values: Scheduled, Running, Complete, Failed, Cancelled, Timeout, Unknown
  • --max-results (integer, optional): Maximum number of queries to return. Range: 1-1000
Return Value
  • queries (list): Array of query objects containing:
    • queryId: Unique identifier for the query
    • queryString: The Logs Insights query string
    • status: Current status of the query
    • createTime: When the query was created (epoch ms)
    • logGroupName: The log group being queried
  • nextToken (string): Token for pagination
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: List All Running Queries

Retrieves all currently running Logs Insights queries to monitor active query execution and identify potentially expensive or long-running queries.

#!/bin/bash
# List all running queries
# Monitor active query execution

echo "Currently Running Queries:"
echo ""

aws logs describe-queries \
    --status Running \
    --output json | jq -r '.queries[] | 
        "Query ID: \(.queryId)\n" +
        "  Status: \(.status)\n" +
        "  Log Group: \(.logGroupName)\n" +
        "  Started: \(.createTime | . / 1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))\n" +
        "  Query: \(.queryString | .[0:80])...\n"'
Output: Currently Running Queries: Query ID: a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111 Status: Running Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Started: 2024-01-20 15:30:45 Query: fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /JobPost/ | sort @time... Query ID: e5f6g7h8-9012-34cd-efgh-EXAMPLE22222 Status: Running Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Started: 2024-01-20 15:31:00 Query: stats count(*) by model | filter level = "ERROR"...
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Running queries context: print(f"Active Queries Monitoring Django Models:") print(f"") print(f"Query 1: JobPost search") print(f" Searching for JobPost operations") print(f" Similar to: JobPost.objects.filter(...)") print(f"") print(f"Query 2: Error statistics") print(f" Counting errors by model type") print(f" Models: JobPost, User, Author, Skills, Location") print(f"") print(f"Concurrent query limit: 30 per account") print(f"Current usage: 2/30")
Example 2: Check Query Status for Specific Log Group

Lists queries for a specific Django application log group to monitor query activity and resource usage.

#!/bin/bash
# Check queries for specific log group
# Monitor Django application queries

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"

echo "Queries for: $LOG_GROUP"
echo ""

# Get all query statuses
for STATUS in Running Complete Failed; do
    COUNT=$(aws logs describe-queries \
        --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
        --status "$STATUS" \
        --query 'queries | length' \
        --output text)
    echo "  $STATUS: $COUNT"
done
Output: Queries for: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Running: 2 Complete: 45 Failed: 3
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Query status context: print(f"Query Activity Summary:") print(f" Running: 2 (active analysis)") print(f" Complete: 45 (successful)") print(f" Failed: 3 (errors/timeouts)") print(f"") print(f"Common queries for Django models:") print(f" - JobPost CRUD operations") print(f" - User authentication events") print(f" - Error rate by model") print(f" - Response time analysis")
Example 3: Find Long-Running Queries

Identifies queries that have been running for an extended period to prevent resource exhaustion and excessive costs.

#!/bin/bash
# Find long-running queries
# Identify expensive operations

echo "Long-Running Queries (over 30 seconds):"
echo ""

THRESHOLD_MS=30000  # 30 seconds
NOW_MS=$(($(date +%s) * 1000))

aws logs describe-queries \
    --status Running \
    --output json | jq --argjson now "$NOW_MS" --argjson threshold "$THRESHOLD_MS" '
    .queries[] | 
    select(($now - .createTime) > $threshold) |
    {
        queryId: .queryId,
        duration_sec: (($now - .createTime) / 1000 | floor),
        logGroup: .logGroupName,
        query: .queryString[0:60]
    }'
Output: Long-Running Queries (over 30 seconds): { "queryId": "a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111", "duration_sec": 125, "logGroup": "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost", "query": "fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /Jo" } { "queryId": "e5f6g7h8-9012-34cd-efgh-EXAMPLE22222", "duration_sec": 45, "logGroup": "/aws/django/anihpj-production", "query": "stats count(*) by bin(1h) | filter level = \"ERROR\"" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Long-running query context: print(f"Long-Running Queries Detected:") print(f"") print(f"Query 1: 125 seconds") print(f" Searching JobPost logs") print(f" May need optimization") print(f" Consider: Add time range filter") print(f"") print(f"Query 2: 45 seconds") print(f" Counting errors over time") print(f" Normal for large time ranges") print(f"") print(f"Recommendation:") print(f" - Use shorter time ranges") print(f" - Add more specific filters") print(f" - Cancel if not needed")
Example 4: Monitor Query Concurrency

Tracks the number of concurrent queries to stay within account limits and optimize query scheduling.

#!/bin/bash
# Monitor query concurrency
# Track usage against limits

echo "Query Concurrency Monitor"
echo ""

# Count by status
RUNNING=$(aws logs describe-queries --status Running --query 'queries | length' --output text)
SCHEDULED=$(aws logs describe-queries --status Scheduled --query 'queries | length' --output text)

MAX_CONCURRENT=30  # Account limit

TOTAL=$((RUNNING + SCHEDULED))
AVAILABLE=$((MAX_CONCURRENT - TOTAL))

echo "Current Usage:"
echo "  Running: $RUNNING"
echo "  Scheduled: $SCHEDULED"
echo "  Total Active: $TOTAL"
echo "  Available: $AVAILABLE / $MAX_CONCURRENT"
echo ""

if [ $TOTAL -gt 25 ]; then
    echo "WARNING: Approaching concurrency limit!"
    echo "Consider cancelling non-essential queries"
fi
Output: Query Concurrency Monitor Current Usage: Running: 5 Scheduled: 2 Total Active: 7 Available: 23 / 30
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Concurrency context: print(f"Query Concurrency for Django Logs:") print(f" Running: 5") print(f" Scheduled: 2") print(f" Available: 23") print(f"") print(f"Typical queries:") print(f" - JobPost operations analysis") print(f" - User activity dashboard") print(f" - Error monitoring") print(f" - Performance metrics")
Example 5: List Recently Failed Queries

Retrieves failed queries to diagnose issues with query syntax or resource constraints affecting Django log analysis.

#!/bin/bash
# List failed queries
# Diagnose query issues

echo "Recently Failed Queries:"
echo ""

aws logs describe-queries \
    --status Failed \
    --max-results 10 \
    --output json | jq -r '.queries[] | 
        "Query ID: \(.queryId)\n" +
        "  Time: \(.createTime | . / 1000 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))\n" +
        "  Log Group: \(.logGroupName)\n" +
        "  Query: \(.queryString)\n"'
Output: Recently Failed Queries: Query ID: f9g0h1i2-3456-78jk-lmno-EXAMPLE33333 Time: 2024-01-20 14:25:00 Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Query: fields @timestamp | parse @message "JobPost: *" as job Query ID: p3q4r5s6-7890-12tu-vwxy-EXAMPLE44444 Time: 2024-01-20 13:15:30 Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-production Query: stats avg(response_time) by model | filter
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Failed query context: print(f"Failed Query Analysis:") print(f"") print(f"Query 1: Parse error") print(f" Issue: Invalid parse pattern") print(f" Fix: Check log format for JobPost") print(f"") print(f"Query 2: Syntax error") print(f" Issue: Incomplete filter clause") print(f" Fix: Add condition after 'filter'")

stop-query

Stops a running CloudWatch Logs Insights query before completion. This command cancels long-running, expensive, or unnecessary queries to save costs and free up query slots for analyzing Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs stop-query \
    --query-id <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --query-id (string, required): The unique identifier of the query to stop. Obtained from start-query or describe-queries commands
Return Value
  • success (boolean): True if the query was successfully stopped, False otherwise
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Stop a Single Long-Running Query

Cancels a specific query that has been running too long to prevent excessive costs and free up concurrent query slots.

#!/bin/bash
# Stop a long-running query
# Free up query slot

QUERY_ID="a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111"

echo "Stopping query: $QUERY_ID"

aws logs stop-query \
    --query-id "$QUERY_ID"

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Query successfully stopped"
else
    echo "Failed to stop query"
fi
Output: Stopping query: a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111 { "success": true } Query successfully stopped
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Stopped query context: print(f"Query Cancelled Successfully") print(f" Query ID: a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111") print(f" Was analyzing JobPost operations") print(f" Freed 1 query slot") print(f"") print(f"Available slots now: 24/30")
Example 2: Stop All Running Queries

Cancels all currently running queries when immediate query capacity is needed or costs need to be controlled urgently.

#!/bin/bash
# Stop all running queries
# Emergency cost control

echo "Stopping all running queries..."
echo ""

# Get all running queries
QUERIES=$(aws logs describe-queries --status Running --query 'queries[].queryId' --output text)

STOPPED=0
FAILED=0

for QUERY_ID in $QUERIES; do
    echo "Stopping: $QUERY_ID"
    RESULT=$(aws logs stop-query --query-id "$QUERY_ID" --query 'success' --output text)
    
    if [ "$RESULT" == "True" ]; then
        ((STOPPED++))
        echo "  ✓ Stopped"
    else
        ((FAILED++))
        echo "  ✗ Failed"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "Summary: Stopped $STOPPED, Failed $FAILED"
Output: Stopping all running queries... Stopping: a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111 ✓ Stopped Stopping: e5f6g7h8-9012-34cd-efgh-EXAMPLE22222 ✓ Stopped Stopping: i9j0k1l2-3456-78mn-opqr-EXAMPLE33333 ✓ Stopped Summary: Stopped 3, Failed 0
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Emergency stop context: print(f"All Queries Stopped:") print(f" JobPost analysis: Cancelled") print(f" User activity query: Cancelled") print(f" Error aggregation: Cancelled") print(f"") print(f"Freed 3 concurrent query slots") print(f"Cost savings from cancellation")
Example 3: Stop Queries for Specific Log Group

Stops all queries targeting a specific Django application log group while leaving other queries running.

#!/bin/bash
# Stop queries for specific log group
# Selective cancellation

LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"

echo "Stopping queries for: $LOG_GROUP"
echo ""

aws logs describe-queries \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP" \
    --status Running \
    --query 'queries[].queryId' \
    --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read QUERY_ID; do
    
    if [ -n "$QUERY_ID" ]; then
        echo "Stopping: $QUERY_ID"
        aws logs stop-query --query-id "$QUERY_ID"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "All queries for $LOG_GROUP stopped"
Output: Stopping queries for: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost Stopping: a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111 { "success": true } Stopping: e5f6g7h8-9012-34cd-efgh-EXAMPLE22222 { "success": true } All queries for /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost stopped
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Log group specific stop: print(f"Stopped queries for Django app:") print(f" Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-jobpost") print(f" Queries cancelled: 2") print(f"") print(f"Models affected:") print(f" - JobPost operations") print(f" - Skills associations") print(f" - User relationships")
Example 4: Stop Queries Older Than Threshold

Automatically stops queries that have been running longer than a specified time threshold to prevent runaway queries.

#!/bin/bash
# Stop queries running longer than threshold
# Automatic cleanup

THRESHOLD_SECONDS=60
NOW_MS=$(($(date +%s) * 1000))
THRESHOLD_MS=$((THRESHOLD_SECONDS * 1000))

echo "Stopping queries running longer than ${THRESHOLD_SECONDS}s..."
echo ""

aws logs describe-queries --status Running --output json | jq -r --argjson now "$NOW_MS" --argjson threshold "$THRESHOLD_MS" '
    .queries[] | 
    select(($now - .createTime) > $threshold) | 
    .queryId' | while read QUERY_ID; do
    
    echo "Stopping old query: $QUERY_ID"
    aws logs stop-query --query-id "$QUERY_ID"
done
Output: Stopping queries running longer than 60s... Stopping old query: a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111 { "success": true } Stopping old query: e5f6g7h8-9012-34cd-efgh-EXAMPLE22222 { "success": true }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Time-based cleanup: print(f"Stopped queries older than 60s:") print(f"") print(f"Query 1: Was running 125s") print(f" Searching JobPost logs") print(f" Stopped to save costs") print(f"") print(f"Query 2: Was running 85s") print(f" Aggregating User stats") print(f" Exceeded threshold")
Example 5: Stop Query with Confirmation

Stops a query with validation to ensure it was actually running and provides detailed status before and after.

#!/bin/bash
# Stop query with confirmation
# Verify before and after

QUERY_ID="a1b2c3d4-5678-90ab-cdef-EXAMPLE11111"

echo "Checking query status before stop..."
BEFORE=$(aws logs describe-queries --status Running --query "queries[?queryId=='$QUERY_ID'] | [0].status" --output text)

if [ "$BEFORE" != "Running" ]; then
    echo "Query is not running (status: $BEFORE)"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Query is running. Stopping..."
RESULT=$(aws logs stop-query --query-id "$QUERY_ID" --query 'success' --output text)

if [ "$RESULT" == "True" ]; then
    echo "Query stopped successfully"
    
    # Verify it's cancelled
    sleep 1
    AFTER=$(aws logs describe-queries --query "queries[?queryId=='$QUERY_ID'] | [0].status" --output text)
    echo "New status: $AFTER"
else
    echo "Failed to stop query"
fi
Output: Checking query status before stop... Query is running. Stopping... Query stopped successfully New status: Cancelled
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Confirmed stop: print(f"Query Stop Confirmed:") print(f" Before: Running") print(f" After: Cancelled") print(f"") print(f"Query was analyzing:") print(f" - JobPost.objects.all()") print(f" - Skills relationships") print(f" - User associations")

put-query-definition

Creates or updates a saved CloudWatch Logs Insights query definition for reuse. This command saves frequently used queries to the AWS console making them easily accessible for repeated analysis of Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs put-query-definition \
    --name <value> \
    --query-string <value> \
    [--query-definition-id <value>] \
    [--log-group-names <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name (string, required): A unique name for the query definition. Use descriptive names like "Django-JobPost-Errors"
  • --query-string (string, required): The Logs Insights query to save. Full query syntax supported
  • --query-definition-id (string, optional): ID of existing query to update. If omitted, creates new query
  • --log-group-names (list, optional): Log groups to associate with this query. Pre-populates log group selection in console
Return Value
  • queryDefinitionId (string): Unique identifier for the saved query definition, used for updates or deletion
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Save Django Error Analysis Query

Creates a reusable query for analyzing errors across all Django model operations in the anihpj application.

#!/bin/bash
# Save error analysis query
# Reusable for Django debugging

aws logs put-query-definition \
    --name "Django-Model-Errors" \
    --log-group-names "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" \
    --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message, level, model
        | filter level = "ERROR"
        | parse @message "ERROR: * - Model: * - *" as error_type, model_name, details
        | stats count(*) as error_count by model_name, error_type
        | sort error_count desc
        | limit 100'

echo ""
echo "Query saved for Django error analysis"
Output: { "queryDefinitionId": "qd-abc123def456-error-analysis" } Query saved for Django error analysis
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Saved query will analyze errors for: print(f"Error Analysis Query Saved:") print(f"") print(f"Models covered:") print(f" - JobPost: CRUD operation errors") print(f" - Skills: Association errors") print(f" - User: Authentication errors") print(f" - Author: Profile errors") print(f" - Location: Validation errors") print(f"") print(f"Access in CloudWatch console under Saved Queries")
Example 2: Save Performance Monitoring Query

Creates a query for identifying slow database operations in the Django application.

#!/bin/bash
# Save slow query detection
# Performance monitoring

aws logs put-query-definition \
    --name "Django-Slow-Operations" \
    --log-group-names "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" "/aws/django/anihpj-production" \
    --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message, duration_ms, operation, model
        | filter duration_ms > 500
        | stats avg(duration_ms) as avg_duration, 
                max(duration_ms) as max_duration, 
                count(*) as count 
          by model, operation
        | filter count > 5
        | sort avg_duration desc'
Output: { "queryDefinitionId": "qd-slow-ops-789ghi" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Performance query for: print(f"Slow Operations Query Saved:") print(f"") print(f"Identifies slow queries like:") print(f" JobPost.objects.select_related('author', 'location')") print(f" Skills.objects.prefetch_related('jobpost_set')") print(f" User.objects.annotate(job_count=Count('jobpost'))") print(f"") print(f"Threshold: 500ms")
Example 3: Save User Activity Dashboard Query

Creates a query for tracking user activity patterns across the Django job posting application.

#!/bin/bash
# Save user activity query
# Dashboard data source

aws logs put-query-definition \
    --name "Django-User-Activity-Dashboard" \
    --log-group-names "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" \
    --query-string 'fields @timestamp, user_id, action, model, @message
        | filter ispresent(user_id)
        | stats count(*) as actions by user_id, action, model
        | sort actions desc
        | limit 50'

echo "Dashboard query saved"
Output: { "queryDefinitionId": "qd-user-activity-dashboard-abc" } Dashboard query saved
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # User activity tracking: print(f"User Activity Query Saved:") print(f"") print(f"Tracks actions:") print(f" - JobPost.objects.create() - CREATE") print(f" - JobPost.objects.filter().update() - UPDATE") print(f" - JobPost.objects.filter().delete() - DELETE") print(f" - Skills.objects.get() - READ") print(f"") print(f"Links to User model via user_id")
Example 4: Save Model-Specific Query Set

Creates multiple related queries for comprehensive model monitoring as a querygroup.

#!/bin/bash
# Save multiple model queries
# Comprehensive monitoring

MODELS=("JobPost" "Skills" "User" "Author" "Location")

for MODEL in "${MODELS[@]}"; do
    echo "Saving query for $MODEL..."
    
    aws logs put-query-definition \
        --name "Django-${MODEL}-Operations" \
        --log-group-names "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" \
        --query-string "fields @timestamp, @message, operation, user_id
            | filter model = \"$MODEL\"
            | stats count(*) as ops by operation, bin(1h)
            | sort @timestamp desc"
    
    echo "  ✓ Saved"
done

echo ""
echo "All model queries saved"
Output: Saving query for JobPost... { "queryDefinitionId": "qd-jobpost-ops-111" } ✓ Saved Saving query for Skills... { "queryDefinitionId": "qd-skills-ops-222" } ✓ Saved Saving query for User... { "queryDefinitionId": "qd-user-ops-333" } ✓ Saved Saving query for Author... { "queryDefinitionId": "qd-author-ops-444" } ✓ Saved Saving query for Location... { "queryDefinitionId": "qd-location-ops-555" } ✓ Saved All model queries saved
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Model-specific queries: print(f"Individual Model Queries Saved:") print(f"") for model in [JobPost, Skills, User, Author, Location]: print(f" {model.__name__}:") print(f" - CREATE operations") print(f" - READ operations") print(f" - UPDATE operations") print(f" - DELETE operations") print(f"")
Example 5: Update Existing Query Definition

Updates an existing saved query with improved filters and additional fields.

#!/bin/bash
# Update existing query definition
# Improve with better filters

QUERY_ID="qd-abc123def456-error-analysis"

echo "Updating query definition: $QUERY_ID"

aws logs put-query-definition \
    --query-definition-id "$QUERY_ID" \
    --name "Django-Model-Errors-Enhanced" \
    --log-group-names "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" "/aws/django/anihpj-production" \
    --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message, level, model, request_id, user_id
        | filter level in ["ERROR", "CRITICAL"]
        | parse @message "(?<error_type>\\w+Error|\\w+Exception): (?<details>.*)" as error_type, details
        | stats count(*) as error_count, 
                earliest(@timestamp) as first_seen,
                latest(@timestamp) as last_seen
          by model, error_type
        | filter error_count > 0
        | sort error_count desc
        | limit 100'

echo "Query updated with enhanced filters"
Output: Updating query definition: qd-abc123def456-error-analysis { "queryDefinitionId": "qd-abc123def456-error-analysis" } Query updated with enhanced filters
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Updated query improvements: print(f"Enhanced Error Query:") print(f"") print(f"New features:") print(f" - Includes CRITICAL level") print(f" - Tracks first/last occurrence") print(f" - Extracts Django exceptions:") print(f" - DoesNotExist") print(f" - ValidationError") print(f" - IntegrityError") print(f" - PermissionDenied")
