Example 5: Delete Policy for Replacement

Removes policy to replace with updated configuration.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete for replacement
# Policy update workflow

POLICY_NAME="AccountDataProtection"

echo "Policy Replacement Workflow"
echo "==========================="
echo ""

# Step 1: Backup
echo "1. Backing up current policy..."
aws logs describe-account-policies \
    --policy-type DATA_PROTECTION_POLICY \
    --policy-name "$POLICY_NAME" \
    --output json > "old-policy.json"
echo "   ✓ Backup saved"

# Step 2: Delete
echo "2. Deleting old policy..."
aws logs delete-account-policy \
    --policy-name "$POLICY_NAME" \
    --policy-type DATA_PROTECTION_POLICY
echo "   ✓ Deleted"

# Step 3: Ready for new policy
echo "3. Ready for new policy..."
echo "   Use put-account-policy with new configuration"
Output: Policy Replacement Workflow =========================== 1. Backing up current policy... ✓ Backup saved 2. Deleting old policy... ✓ Deleted 3. Ready for new policy... Use put-account-policy with new configuration
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Policy replacement: print(f"Old: Backed up") print(f"Current: Deleted") print(f"") print(f"Ready for new Django data policy")

create-delivery

Creates a delivery to send logs from a delivery source to a delivery destination. This command establishes log delivery pipelines for Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations to destinations like S3, CloudWatch Logs, or Firehose.

Syntax
aws logs create-delivery \
    --delivery-source-name <value> \
    --delivery-destination-arn <value> \
    [--tags <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --delivery-source-name (string, required): Name of the delivery source (from put-delivery-source)
  • --delivery-destination-arn (string, required): ARN of the delivery destination
  • --tags (map, optional): Tags to apply to the delivery
Return Value
  • delivery (object): Delivery configuration containing:
    • id: Unique delivery identifier
    • arn: ARN of the delivery
    • deliverySourceName: Name of the source
    • deliveryDestinationArn: ARN of the destination
    • deliveryDestinationType: S3, CWL, or FH
    • tags: Associated tags
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Create S3 Delivery

Creates a delivery to send logs to an S3 bucket for archival.

#!/bin/bash
# Create S3 delivery
# Archive logs

SOURCE_NAME="DjangoAppLogsSource"
DEST_ARN="arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:S3LogsArchive"

echo "Creating S3 delivery..."

aws logs create-delivery \
    --delivery-source-name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --delivery-destination-arn "$DEST_ARN" \
    --output json | jq '{
        id: .delivery.id,
        source: .delivery.deliverySourceName,
        destinationType: .delivery.deliveryDestinationType
    }'
Output: Creating S3 delivery... { "id": "delivery-abc123def456", "source": "DjangoAppLogsSource", "destinationType": "S3" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delivery created: print(f"ID: delivery-abc123def456") print(f"Django logs → S3") print(f"") print(f"JobPost, User logs archived")
Example 2: Create Firehose Delivery

Creates a delivery to stream logs through Kinesis Firehose.

#!/bin/bash
# Create Firehose delivery
# Real-time streaming

SOURCE_NAME="JobpostLogsSource"
DEST_ARN="arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:FirehoseDestination"

echo "Creating Firehose delivery..."

RESULT=$(aws logs create-delivery \
    --delivery-source-name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --delivery-destination-arn "$DEST_ARN" \
    --tags Environment=production,Application=anihpj \
    --output json)

DELIVERY_ID=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.delivery.id')
DEST_TYPE=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.delivery.deliveryDestinationType')

echo "✓ Delivery Created"
echo "  ID: $DELIVERY_ID"
echo "  Type: $DEST_TYPE"
echo "  Logs streaming to Firehose"
Output: Creating Firehose delivery... ✓ Delivery Created ID: delivery-fh789xyz Type: FH Logs streaming to Firehose
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Firehose delivery: print(f"Source: JobpostLogsSource") print(f"Destination: Firehose") print(f"") print(f"Real-time Django log streaming")
Example 3: Create CloudWatch Logs Delivery

Creates a delivery to send logs to another CloudWatch Logs group.

#!/bin/bash
# Create CWL delivery
# Cross-account or consolidated

SOURCE_NAME="AuthorLogsSource"
DEST_ARN="arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:987654321098:delivery-destination:CentralLogsDestination"

echo "Creating CloudWatch Logs delivery..."

aws logs create-delivery \
    --delivery-source-name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --delivery-destination-arn "$DEST_ARN" \
    --output json | jq '.delivery | {
        id: .id,
        source: .deliverySourceName,
        destination: (.deliveryDestinationArn | split(":") | .[-1]),
        type: .deliveryDestinationType
    }'
Output: Creating CloudWatch Logs delivery... { "id": "delivery-cwl456abc", "source": "AuthorLogsSource", "destination": "CentralLogsDestination", "type": "CWL" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # CWL delivery: print(f"Author logs → Central CWL") print(f"Cross-account delivery") print(f"") print(f"Consolidated Django logging")
Example 4: Create Tagged Delivery

Creates a delivery with comprehensive tagging for organization.

#!/bin/bash
# Create tagged delivery
# Cost allocation

SOURCE_NAME="SkillsLogsSource"
DEST_ARN="arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:S3Archive"

echo "Creating delivery with tags..."

aws logs create-delivery \
    --delivery-source-name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --delivery-destination-arn "$DEST_ARN" \
    --tags '{
        "Environment": "production",
        "Application": "anihpj",
        "Team": "platform",
        "CostCenter": "CC-1234",
        "DataClassification": "internal"
    }' \
    --output json | jq '.delivery'
Output: Creating delivery with tags... { "id": "delivery-tagged123", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery:delivery-tagged123", "deliverySourceName": "SkillsLogsSource", "deliveryDestinationArn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:S3Archive", "deliveryDestinationType": "S3", "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Application": "anihpj", "Team": "platform", "CostCenter": "CC-1234", "DataClassification": "internal" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Tagged delivery: print(f"Skills logs → S3") print(f"Tags: 5 applied") print(f"") print(f"Tracking costs for Django app")
Example 5: Create Multiple Deliveries Script

Creates deliveries for multiple log sources in a Django application.

#!/bin/bash
# Multi-source delivery setup
# Complete Django logging

S3_DEST="arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:S3Archive"

SOURCES=("JobpostLogsSource" "UserLogsSource" "AuthorLogsSource" "SkillsLogsSource")

echo "Creating Deliveries for Django App"
echo "==================================="
echo ""

for SOURCE in "${SOURCES[@]}"; do
    echo "Creating delivery: $SOURCE → S3..."
    
    RESULT=$(aws logs create-delivery \
        --delivery-source-name "$SOURCE" \
        --delivery-destination-arn "$S3_DEST" \
        --tags Application=anihpj \
        --output json 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        ID=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.delivery.id')
        echo "  ✓ Created: $ID"
    else
        echo "  ✗ Failed (source may not exist)"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "Delivery setup complete"
Output: Creating Deliveries for Django App =================================== Creating delivery: JobpostLogsSource → S3... ✓ Created: delivery-job123 Creating delivery: UserLogsSource → S3... ✓ Created: delivery-user456 Creating delivery: AuthorLogsSource → S3... ✓ Created: delivery-auth789 Creating delivery: SkillsLogsSource → S3... ✓ Created: delivery-skill012 Delivery setup complete
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Deliveries created: print(f"JobPost logs: delivery-job123") print(f"User logs: delivery-user456") print(f"Author logs: delivery-auth789") print(f"Skills logs: delivery-skill012") print(f"") print(f"All Django models archived to S3")

get-delivery

Retrieves details about a specific log delivery. This command provides configuration and status information for deliveries routing Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations to destinations.

Syntax
aws logs get-delivery \
    --id <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --id (string, required): The unique identifier of the delivery to retrieve
Return Value
  • delivery (object): Delivery details containing:
    • id: Unique delivery identifier
    • arn: Full ARN of the delivery
    • deliverySourceName: Name of the source
    • deliveryDestinationArn: ARN of the destination
    • deliveryDestinationType: S3, CWL, or FH
    • tags: Associated tags
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Get Delivery Details

Retrieves complete information about a specific delivery.

#!/bin/bash
# Get delivery details
# Configuration review

DELIVERY_ID="delivery-abc123def456"

echo "Delivery Details: $DELIVERY_ID"
echo ""

aws logs get-delivery \
    --id "$DELIVERY_ID" \
    --output json | jq '.delivery'
Output: Delivery Details: delivery-abc123def456 { "id": "delivery-abc123def456", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery:delivery-abc123def456", "deliverySourceName": "DjangoAppLogsSource", "deliveryDestinationArn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:S3LogsArchive", "deliveryDestinationType": "S3", "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Application": "anihpj" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delivery info: print(f"ID: delivery-abc123def456") print(f"Source: DjangoAppLogsSource") print(f"Destination: S3") print(f"") print(f"Django logs archiving to S3")
Example 2: Verify Delivery Configuration

Retrieves and validates delivery configuration for compliance.

#!/bin/bash
# Verify delivery config
# Compliance check

DELIVERY_ID="delivery-job123"

echo "Verifying Delivery Configuration"
echo "================================"
echo ""

DELIVERY=$(aws logs get-delivery \
    --id "$DELIVERY_ID" \
    --output json)

SOURCE=$(echo "$DELIVERY" | jq -r '.delivery.deliverySourceName')
DEST_TYPE=$(echo "$DELIVERY" | jq -r '.delivery.deliveryDestinationType')
DEST_ARN=$(echo "$DELIVERY" | jq -r '.delivery.deliveryDestinationArn')

echo "Source: $SOURCE"
echo "Destination Type: $DEST_TYPE"
echo "Destination ARN: $DEST_ARN"
echo ""

if [ "$DEST_TYPE" = "S3" ]; then
    echo "✓ Compliant: Logs archived to S3"
else
    echo "! Review: Non-S3 destination"
fi
Output: Verifying Delivery Configuration ================================ Source: JobpostLogsSource Destination Type: S3 Destination ARN: arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:S3Archive ✓ Compliant: Logs archived to S3
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Compliance check: print(f"JobPost logs → S3") print(f"Status: Compliant") print(f"") print(f"Meets retention requirements")
Example 3: Get Delivery with Tags

Retrieves delivery details including associated tags.

#!/bin/bash
# Get delivery with tags
# Cost tracking

DELIVERY_ID="delivery-tagged123"

echo "Delivery Tags Review"
echo "===================="
echo ""

aws logs get-delivery \
    --id "$DELIVERY_ID" \
    --output json | jq '{
        id: .delivery.id,
        source: .delivery.deliverySourceName,
        type: .delivery.deliveryDestinationType,
        tags: .delivery.tags
    }'
Output: Delivery Tags Review ==================== { "id": "delivery-tagged123", "source": "SkillsLogsSource", "type": "S3", "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Application": "anihpj", "Team": "platform", "CostCenter": "CC-1234" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Tagged delivery: print(f"Skills logs delivery") print(f"Tags: 4 applied") print(f"CostCenter: CC-1234") print(f"") print(f"Django app cost tracking")
Example 4: Get Delivery for Troubleshooting

Retrieves delivery details for diagnosing issues.

#!/bin/bash
# Troubleshoot delivery
# Diagnose issues

DELIVERY_ID="delivery-issue456"

echo "Delivery Diagnostics"
echo "===================="
echo ""

DELIVERY=$(aws logs get-delivery \
    --id "$DELIVERY_ID" \
    --output json)

echo "Configuration:"
echo "$DELIVERY" | jq '.delivery | {
    id: .id,
    source: .deliverySourceName,
    destinationType: .deliveryDestinationType,
    destinationArn: .deliveryDestinationArn
}'

echo ""
echo "Checking source exists..."
SOURCE_NAME=$(echo "$DELIVERY" | jq -r '.delivery.deliverySourceName')
aws logs get-delivery-source --name "$SOURCE_NAME" &>/dev/null && echo "✓ Source valid" || echo "✗ Source missing"

echo ""
echo "Checking destination..."
DEST_ARN=$(echo "$DELIVERY" | jq -r '.delivery.deliveryDestinationArn')
DEST_NAME=$(echo "$DEST_ARN" | awk -F: '{print $NF}')
aws logs get-delivery-destination --name "$DEST_NAME" &>/dev/null && echo "✓ Destination valid" || echo "✗ Destination issue"
Output: Delivery Diagnostics ==================== Configuration: { "id": "delivery-issue456", "source": "UserLogsSource", "destinationType": "S3", "destinationArn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:S3Archive" } Checking source exists... ✓ Source valid Checking destination... ✓ Destination valid
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Diagnostics: print(f"User logs delivery") print(f"Source: Valid") print(f"Destination: Valid") print(f"") print(f"Django delivery configured correctly")
Example 5: Export Delivery Configuration

Retrieves and exports delivery configuration for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Export delivery config
# Documentation

DELIVERY_ID="delivery-abc123def456"
OUTPUT_FILE="delivery-config-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Exporting Delivery Configuration"
echo "================================="
echo ""

aws logs get-delivery \
    --id "$DELIVERY_ID" \
    --output json > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "✓ Exported to: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Summary:"
jq '{
    delivery_id: .delivery.id,
    source: .delivery.deliverySourceName,
    destination_type: .delivery.deliveryDestinationType,
    destination: (.delivery.deliveryDestinationArn | split(":") | .[-1]),
    tag_count: (.delivery.tags | keys | length)
}' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Exporting Delivery Configuration ================================= ✓ Exported to: delivery-config-20250120.json Summary: { "delivery_id": "delivery-abc123def456", "source": "DjangoAppLogsSource", "destination_type": "S3", "destination": "S3LogsArchive", "tag_count": 2 }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Exported config: print(f"File: delivery-config-20250120.json") print(f"Django logs → S3LogsArchive") print(f"") print(f"Documentation complete")

describe-deliveries

Lists all log deliveries in the account. This command retrieves all configured deliveries routing Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations to various destinations.

Syntax
aws logs describe-deliveries \
    [--next-token <value>] \
    [--limit <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination from previous response
  • --limit (integer, optional): Maximum number of deliveries to return (1-50)
Return Value
  • deliveries (list): Array of delivery objects containing:
    • id: Unique delivery identifier
    • arn: Full ARN of the delivery
    • deliverySourceName: Name of the source
    • deliveryDestinationArn: ARN of the destination
    • deliveryDestinationType: S3, CWL, or FH
    • tags: Associated tags
  • nextToken (string): Token for next page if more results
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: List All Deliveries

Retrieves all log deliveries configured in the account.

#!/bin/bash
# List all deliveries
# Overview

echo "All Log Deliveries"
echo "=================="
echo ""

aws logs describe-deliveries \
    --output json | jq -r '.deliveries[] | 
        "ID: \(.id)
  Source: \(.deliverySourceName)
  Type: \(.deliveryDestinationType)
"'
Output: All Log Deliveries ================== ID: delivery-abc123 Source: JobpostLogsSource Type: S3 ID: delivery-def456 Source: UserLogsSource Type: S3 ID: delivery-ghi789 Source: AuthorLogsSource Type: FH
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Active deliveries: print(f"3 deliveries configured") print(f" JobPost → S3") print(f" User → S3") print(f" Author → Firehose")
Example 2: List Deliveries by Type

Filters deliveries by destination type (S3, Firehose, CloudWatch).

#!/bin/bash
# Filter by destination type
# S3 deliveries

echo "S3 Deliveries"
echo "============="
echo ""

aws logs describe-deliveries \
    --output json | jq -r '.deliveries[] | 
        select(.deliveryDestinationType == "S3") | 
        "Source: \(.deliverySourceName)
  ID: \(.id)
  Destination: \(.deliveryDestinationArn | split(":") | .[-1])
"'

echo ""
echo "Firehose Deliveries"
echo "==================="
echo ""

aws logs describe-deliveries \
    --output json | jq -r '.deliveries[] | 
        select(.deliveryDestinationType == "FH") | 
        "Source: \(.deliverySourceName)
  ID: \(.id)
"'
Output: S3 Deliveries ============= Source: JobpostLogsSource ID: delivery-abc123 Destination: S3LogsArchive Source: UserLogsSource ID: delivery-def456 Destination: S3LogsArchive Source: SkillsLogsSource ID: delivery-jkl012 Destination: S3LogsArchive Firehose Deliveries =================== Source: AuthorLogsSource ID: delivery-ghi789
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # By destination: print(f"S3: 3 deliveries") print(f" JobPost, User, Skills") print(f"Firehose: 1 delivery") print(f" Author (real-time)")
Example 3: Delivery Summary Report

Generates a summary report of all delivery configurations.

#!/bin/bash
# Delivery summary report
# Operations overview

echo "Delivery Summary Report"
echo "======================="
echo "Generated: $(date)"
echo ""

DELIVERIES=$(aws logs describe-deliveries --output json)

TOTAL=$(echo "$DELIVERIES" | jq '.deliveries | length')
S3_COUNT=$(echo "$DELIVERIES" | jq '[.deliveries[] | select(.deliveryDestinationType == "S3")] | length')
FH_COUNT=$(echo "$DELIVERIES" | jq '[.deliveries[] | select(.deliveryDestinationType == "FH")] | length')
CWL_COUNT=$(echo "$DELIVERIES" | jq '[.deliveries[] | select(.deliveryDestinationType == "CWL")] | length')

echo "Statistics:"
echo "  Total Deliveries: $TOTAL"
echo "  S3 Destinations: $S3_COUNT"
echo "  Firehose Destinations: $FH_COUNT"
echo "  CloudWatch Destinations: $CWL_COUNT"
echo ""
echo "Sources:"
echo "$DELIVERIES" | jq -r '.deliveries[].deliverySourceName' | sort -u | sed 's/^/  - /'
Output: Delivery Summary Report ======================= Generated: Mon Jan 20 14:30:00 UTC 2025 Statistics: Total Deliveries: 5 S3 Destinations: 3 Firehose Destinations: 1 CloudWatch Destinations: 1 Sources: - AuthorLogsSource - JobpostLogsSource - LocationLogsSource - SkillsLogsSource - UserLogsSource
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Summary: print(f"5 total deliveries") print(f"All Django models covered:") print(f" Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost")
Example 4: Paginated Delivery Listing

Handles pagination for accounts with many deliveries.

#!/bin/bash
# Paginated listing
# Large accounts

echo "Fetching All Deliveries (Paginated)"
echo "===================================="
echo ""

NEXT_TOKEN=""
PAGE=1
ALL_DELIVERIES="[]"

while true; do
    echo "Fetching page $PAGE..."
    
    if [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        RESULT=$(aws logs describe-deliveries --limit 10 --output json)
    else
        RESULT=$(aws logs describe-deliveries --limit 10 --next-token "$NEXT_TOKEN" --output json)
    fi
    
    DELIVERIES=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.deliveries')
    ALL_DELIVERIES=$(echo "$ALL_DELIVERIES $DELIVERIES" | jq -s 'add')
    
    NEXT_TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.nextToken // empty')
    
    if [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        break
    fi
    
    PAGE=$((PAGE + 1))
done

TOTAL=$(echo "$ALL_DELIVERIES" | jq 'length')
echo ""
echo "✓ Total deliveries found: $TOTAL"
Output: Fetching All Deliveries (Paginated) ==================================== Fetching page 1... Fetching page 2... ✓ Total deliveries found: 15
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Paginated results: print(f"15 deliveries across pages") print(f"All Django app logs routed") print(f"") print(f"Full audit complete")
Example 5: Export Deliveries Inventory

Exports all delivery configurations to a JSON file for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Export deliveries inventory
# Documentation/backup

OUTPUT_FILE="deliveries-inventory-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Exporting Deliveries Inventory"
echo "==============================="
echo ""

aws logs describe-deliveries \
    --output json | jq '.deliveries | map({
        id: .id,
        source: .deliverySourceName,
        destination_type: .deliveryDestinationType,
        destination: (.deliveryDestinationArn | split(":") | .[-1]),
        tags: (.tags // {})
    })' > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "✓ Exported to: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Summary:"
jq -r 'group_by(.destination_type) | .[] | 
    "\(.[0].destination_type): \(length) deliveries"' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Sources:"
jq -r '.[].source' "$OUTPUT_FILE" | sort -u | head -5 | sed 's/^/  /'
Output: Exporting Deliveries Inventory =============================== ✓ Exported to: deliveries-inventory-20250120.json Summary: S3: 3 deliveries FH: 1 deliveries CWL: 1 deliveries Sources: AuthorLogsSource JobpostLogsSource LocationLogsSource SkillsLogsSource UserLogsSource
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Inventory exported: print(f"File: deliveries-inventory-20250120.json") print(f"5 deliveries documented") print(f"") print(f"Django logging infrastructure backed up")

delete-delivery

Deletes a log delivery configuration. This command removes delivery pipelines routing Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations to destinations.

Syntax
aws logs delete-delivery \
    --id <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --id (string, required): The unique identifier of the delivery to delete
Return Value
  • (none): Returns empty response on success. Delivery immediately stops
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Delete a Delivery

Removes a specific log delivery configuration.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete delivery
# Stop log routing

DELIVERY_ID="delivery-old123"

echo "Deleting delivery: $DELIVERY_ID"

aws logs delete-delivery \
    --id "$DELIVERY_ID"

echo "✓ Delivery deleted"
echo "  Logs no longer routed to destination"
Output: Deleting delivery: delivery-old123 ✓ Delivery deleted Logs no longer routed to destination
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delivery removed: print(f"delivery-old123 deleted") print(f"") print(f"Django logs stay in CloudWatch only")
Example 2: Delete with Backup

Backs up delivery configuration before deletion for potential recovery.

#!/bin/bash
# Backup before delete
# Recoverable

DELIVERY_ID="delivery-abc123"
BACKUP_FILE="delivery-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Backing up delivery configuration..."

aws logs get-delivery \
    --id "$DELIVERY_ID" \
    --output json > "$BACKUP_FILE"

echo "✓ Backup saved: $BACKUP_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Deleting delivery..."

aws logs delete-delivery \
    --id "$DELIVERY_ID"

echo "✓ Delivery deleted"
Output: Backing up delivery configuration... ✓ Backup saved: delivery-backup-20250120.json Deleting delivery... ✓ Delivery deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Safe deletion: print(f"Backup: delivery-backup-20250120.json") print(f"Can recreate if needed") print(f"") print(f"Django delivery paused")
Example 3: Delete with Verification

Deletes delivery and verifies it was removed.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete with verification
# Confirm removal

DELIVERY_ID="delivery-test456"

echo "Deleting delivery..."

aws logs delete-delivery \
    --id "$DELIVERY_ID"

echo ""
echo "Verifying deletion..."

aws logs get-delivery --id "$DELIVERY_ID" 2>/dev/null
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "✓ Verified: Delivery deleted"
else
    echo "⚠ Delivery may still exist"
fi
Output: Deleting delivery... Verifying deletion... ✓ Verified: Delivery deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Verified removal: print(f"delivery-test456: Deleted") print(f"Verification: Passed") print(f"") print(f"Django logs routing stopped")
Example 4: Delete Multiple Deliveries

Removes multiple deliveries matching a pattern.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete multiple deliveries
# Cleanup old configs

echo "Deleting Old Deliveries"
echo "======================="
echo ""

# Get all deliveries for a specific source
DELIVERIES=$(aws logs describe-deliveries \
    --output json | jq -r '.deliveries[] | 
    select(.deliverySourceName | contains("Old")) | .id')

if [ -z "$DELIVERIES" ]; then
    echo "No deliveries matching 'Old' found"
    exit 0
fi

for DELIVERY_ID in $DELIVERIES; do
    echo "Deleting: $DELIVERY_ID"
    aws logs delete-delivery --id "$DELIVERY_ID"
    echo "  ✓ Deleted"
done

echo ""
echo "Cleanup complete"
Output: Deleting Old Deliveries ======================= Deleting: delivery-old001 ✓ Deleted Deleting: delivery-old002 ✓ Deleted Deleting: delivery-old003 ✓ Deleted Cleanup complete
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Bulk deletion: print(f"3 old deliveries removed") print(f"") print(f"Django log routing cleaned up")
Example 5: Delete Delivery for Replacement

Removes delivery to replace with new configuration.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete for replacement
# Update delivery config

OLD_DELIVERY_ID="delivery-abc123"

echo "Delivery Replacement Workflow"
echo "============================="
echo ""

# Step 1: Get current config
echo "1. Saving current configuration..."
CURRENT=$(aws logs get-delivery --id "$OLD_DELIVERY_ID" --output json)
SOURCE=$(echo "$CURRENT" | jq -r '.delivery.deliverySourceName')
echo "   Source: $SOURCE"

# Step 2: Delete old delivery
echo "2. Deleting old delivery..."
aws logs delete-delivery --id "$OLD_DELIVERY_ID"
echo "   ✓ Deleted"

# Step 3: Ready for new delivery
echo "3. Ready for new delivery..."
echo "   Create new delivery with updated destination"
echo ""
echo "Example:"
echo "  aws logs create-delivery \\"
echo "    --delivery-source-name \"$SOURCE\" \\"
echo "    --delivery-destination-arn \"NEW_DESTINATION_ARN\""
Output: Delivery Replacement Workflow ============================= 1. Saving current configuration... Source: JobpostLogsSource 2. Deleting old delivery... ✓ Deleted 3. Ready for new delivery... Create new delivery with updated destination Example: aws logs create-delivery \ --delivery-source-name "JobpostLogsSource" \ --delivery-destination-arn "NEW_DESTINATION_ARN"
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Replacement ready: print(f"Old delivery deleted") print(f"Source preserved: JobpostLogsSource") print(f"") print(f"Ready for new Django log destination")

put-delivery-source

Creates or updates a delivery source for vended logs. This command configures log sources for Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations for delivery to destinations.

Syntax
aws logs put-delivery-source \
    --name <value> \
    --resource-arn <value> \
    --log-type <value> \
    [--tags <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name (string, required): Name for the delivery source (unique identifier)
  • --resource-arn (string, required): ARN of the AWS resource generating logs
  • --log-type (string, required): Type of logs (APPLICATION_LOGS, ACCESS_LOGS, etc.)
  • --tags (map, optional): Tags to apply to the delivery source
Return Value
  • deliverySource (object): Source configuration containing:
    • name: Name of the delivery source
    • arn: ARN of the delivery source
    • resourceArns: Associated resource ARNs
    • service: AWS service generating logs
    • logType: Type of logs
    • tags: Associated tags
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Create Application Logs Source

Creates a delivery source for application logs from a log group.

#!/bin/bash
# Create application logs source
# Django app logs

SOURCE_NAME="JobpostLogsSource"
LOG_GROUP_ARN="arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"

echo "Creating delivery source: $SOURCE_NAME"

aws logs put-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --resource-arn "$LOG_GROUP_ARN" \
    --log-type APPLICATION_LOGS \
    --output json | jq '.deliverySource | {
        name: .name,
        arn: .arn,
        logType: .logType
    }'
Output: Creating delivery source: JobpostLogsSource { "name": "JobpostLogsSource", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-source:JobpostLogsSource", "logType": "APPLICATION_LOGS" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Source created: print(f"JobpostLogsSource ready") print(f"Type: APPLICATION_LOGS") print(f"") print(f"JobPost model logs available for delivery")
Example 2: Create Lambda Logs Source

Creates a delivery source for AWS Lambda function logs.

#!/bin/bash
# Create Lambda logs source
# Serverless Django

SOURCE_NAME="DjangoLambdaSource"
LAMBDA_ARN="arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:anihpj-api"

echo "Creating Lambda delivery source..."

aws logs put-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --resource-arn "$LAMBDA_ARN" \
    --log-type APPLICATION_LOGS \
    --tags Environment=production,Application=anihpj \
    --output json | jq '.deliverySource'
Output: Creating Lambda delivery source... { "name": "DjangoLambdaSource", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-source:DjangoLambdaSource", "resourceArns": [ "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:anihpj-api" ], "service": "lambda", "logType": "APPLICATION_LOGS", "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Application": "anihpj" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Lambda source: print(f"DjangoLambdaSource created") print(f"Function: anihpj-api") print(f"") print(f"Serverless Django logs ready")
Example 3: Create API Gateway Access Logs Source

Creates a delivery source for API Gateway access logs.

#!/bin/bash
# Create API Gateway access logs source
# Request tracking

SOURCE_NAME="DjangoAPIAccessSource"
APIGW_ARN="arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123xyz"

echo "Creating API Gateway access logs source..."

aws logs put-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --resource-arn "$APIGW_ARN" \
    --log-type ACCESS_LOGS \
    --output json | jq '{
        name: .deliverySource.name,
        arn: .deliverySource.arn,
        service: .deliverySource.service,
        logType: .deliverySource.logType
    }'
Output: Creating API Gateway access logs source... { "name": "DjangoAPIAccessSource", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-source:DjangoAPIAccessSource", "service": "apigateway", "logType": "ACCESS_LOGS" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # API access source: print(f"DjangoAPIAccessSource created") print(f"Type: ACCESS_LOGS") print(f"") print(f"Django API request logs ready")
Example 4: Create Tagged Delivery Source

Creates a delivery source with comprehensive tagging.

#!/bin/bash
# Create tagged source
# Cost allocation

SOURCE_NAME="UserLogsSource"
LOG_GROUP_ARN="arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:/aws/django/anihpj-user"

echo "Creating tagged delivery source..."

aws logs put-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --resource-arn "$LOG_GROUP_ARN" \
    --log-type APPLICATION_LOGS \
    --tags '{
        "Environment": "production",
        "Application": "anihpj",
        "Team": "platform",
        "CostCenter": "CC-1234",
        "DataClassification": "internal"
    }' \
    --output json | jq '.deliverySource | {
        name: .name,
        logType: .logType,
        tags: .tags
    }'
Output: Creating tagged delivery source... { "name": "UserLogsSource", "logType": "APPLICATION_LOGS", "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Application": "anihpj", "Team": "platform", "CostCenter": "CC-1234", "DataClassification": "internal" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Tagged source: print(f"UserLogsSource with 5 tags") print(f"CostCenter: CC-1234") print(f"") print(f"User model logs tracked")
Example 5: Create Multiple Model Sources

Creates delivery sources for all Django model log groups.

#!/bin/bash
# Create sources for all models
# Complete coverage

ACCOUNT_ID="123456789012"
REGION="us-east-1"

declare -A MODELS
MODELS["Skills"]="/aws/django/anihpj-skills"
MODELS["User"]="/aws/django/anihpj-user"
MODELS["Author"]="/aws/django/anihpj-author"
MODELS["Location"]="/aws/django/anihpj-location"
MODELS["JobPost"]="/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost"

echo "Creating Delivery Sources for Django Models"
echo "==========================================="
echo ""

for MODEL in "${!MODELS[@]}"; do
    LOG_GROUP="${MODELS[$MODEL]}"
    SOURCE_NAME="${MODEL}LogsSource"
    ARN="arn:aws:logs:$REGION:$ACCOUNT_ID:log-group:$LOG_GROUP"
    
    echo "Creating: $SOURCE_NAME"
    
    RESULT=$(aws logs put-delivery-source \
        --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
        --resource-arn "$ARN" \
        --log-type APPLICATION_LOGS \
        --tags Application=anihpj,Model="$MODEL" \
        --output json 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "  ✓ Created"
    else
        echo "  ✗ Failed"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "All delivery sources configured"
Output: Creating Delivery Sources for Django Models =========================================== Creating: SkillsLogsSource ✓ Created Creating: UserLogsSource ✓ Created Creating: AuthorLogsSource ✓ Created Creating: LocationLogsSource ✓ Created Creating: JobPostLogsSource ✓ Created All delivery sources configured
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # All sources created: print(f"Skills: SkillsLogsSource") print(f"User: UserLogsSource") print(f"Author: AuthorLogsSource") print(f"Location: LocationLogsSource") print(f"JobPost: JobPostLogsSource") print(f"") print(f"Complete Django logging ready")

get-delivery-source

Retrieves information about a specific delivery source. This command provides configuration details for log sources routing Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs get-delivery-source \
    --name <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name (string, required): Name of the delivery source to retrieve
Return Value
  • deliverySource (object): Source details containing:
    • name: Name of the delivery source
    • arn: ARN of the delivery source
    • resourceArns: Associated resource ARNs
    • service: AWS service generating logs
    • logType: Type of logs
    • tags: Associated tags
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Get Source Details

Retrieves complete information about a delivery source.

#!/bin/bash
# Get source details
# Configuration review

SOURCE_NAME="JobpostLogsSource"

echo "Source Details: $SOURCE_NAME"
echo ""

aws logs get-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --output json | jq '.deliverySource'
Output: Source Details: JobpostLogsSource { "name": "JobpostLogsSource", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-source:JobpostLogsSource", "resourceArns": [ "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" ], "service": "logs", "logType": "APPLICATION_LOGS", "tags": { "Application": "anihpj", "Model": "JobPost" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Source info: print(f"JobpostLogsSource") print(f"Type: APPLICATION_LOGS") print(f"") print(f"JobPost model logs ready")
Example 2: Verify Source Resource

Validates that the source is linked to the correct resource.

#!/bin/bash
# Verify source resource
# Configuration check

SOURCE_NAME="UserLogsSource"
EXPECTED_LOG_GROUP="/aws/django/anihpj-user"

echo "Verifying Source: $SOURCE_NAME"
echo ""

SOURCE=$(aws logs get-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --output json)

RESOURCE_ARN=$(echo "$SOURCE" | jq -r '.deliverySource.resourceArns[0]')
LOG_GROUP=$(echo "$RESOURCE_ARN" | awk -F: '{print $NF}')

echo "Resource ARN: $RESOURCE_ARN"
echo "Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo ""

if [[ "$LOG_GROUP" == *"$EXPECTED_LOG_GROUP"* ]]; then
    echo "✓ Source correctly configured"
else
    echo "⚠ Source may be misconfigured"
fi
Output: Verifying Source: UserLogsSource Resource ARN: arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:/aws/django/anihpj-user Log Group: /aws/django/anihpj-user ✓ Source correctly configured
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Verified source: print(f"UserLogsSource → /aws/django/anihpj-user") print(f"Status: Correctly configured") print(f"") print(f"User model logs ready")
Example 3: Get Source Tags

Retrieves and displays tags associated with a source.

#!/bin/bash
# Get source tags
# Cost tracking

SOURCE_NAME="SkillsLogsSource"

echo "Source Tags: $SOURCE_NAME"
echo ""

aws logs get-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --output json | jq '{
        name: .deliverySource.name,
        logType: .deliverySource.logType,
        tags: .deliverySource.tags
    }'
Output: Source Tags: SkillsLogsSource { "name": "SkillsLogsSource", "logType": "APPLICATION_LOGS", "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Application": "anihpj", "Model": "Skills", "CostCenter": "CC-1234" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Source tags: print(f"SkillsLogsSource") print(f"Tags: 4 applied") print(f"CostCenter: CC-1234")
Example 4: Get Source for Troubleshooting

Retrieves source details for diagnosing delivery issues.

#!/bin/bash
# Troubleshoot source
# Diagnose issues

SOURCE_NAME="AuthorLogsSource"

echo "Source Diagnostics"
echo "=================="
echo ""

SOURCE=$(aws logs get-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --output json 2>&1)

if echo "$SOURCE" | jq -e '.deliverySource' > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "$SOURCE" | jq '.deliverySource | {
        name: .name,
        service: .service,
        logType: .logType,
        resourceArns: .resourceArns
    }'
    
    echo ""
    echo "Checking linked resource..."
    RESOURCE=$(echo "$SOURCE" | jq -r '.deliverySource.resourceArns[0]')
    
    # Check if log group exists
    LOG_GROUP=$(echo "$RESOURCE" | awk -F: '{print $NF}')
    aws logs describe-log-groups --log-group-name-prefix "$LOG_GROUP" --limit 1 &>/dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "✓ Linked log group exists"
    else
        echo "✗ Log group may not exist"
    fi
else
    echo "✗ Source not found: $SOURCE_NAME"
fi
Output: Source Diagnostics ================== { "name": "AuthorLogsSource", "service": "logs", "logType": "APPLICATION_LOGS", "resourceArns": [ "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:/aws/django/anihpj-author" ] } Checking linked resource... ✓ Linked log group exists
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Diagnostics: print(f"AuthorLogsSource: Valid") print(f"Log group: Exists") print(f"") print(f"Author logs ready for delivery")
Example 5: Export Source Configuration

Exports source configuration for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Export source config
# Documentation

SOURCE_NAME="LocationLogsSource"
OUTPUT_FILE="source-config-$SOURCE_NAME.json"

echo "Exporting Source Configuration"
echo "==============================="
echo ""

aws logs get-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --output json > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "✓ Exported to: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Summary:"
jq '{
    name: .deliverySource.name,
    arn: .deliverySource.arn,
    log_type: .deliverySource.logType,
    service: .deliverySource.service,
    resource_count: (.deliverySource.resourceArns | length),
    tag_count: (.deliverySource.tags | keys | length)
}' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Exporting Source Configuration =============================== ✓ Exported to: source-config-LocationLogsSource.json Summary: { "name": "LocationLogsSource", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-source:LocationLogsSource", "log_type": "APPLICATION_LOGS", "service": "logs", "resource_count": 1, "tag_count": 3 }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Exported config: print(f"File: source-config-LocationLogsSource.json") print(f"Location model logs source documented") print(f"") print(f"Django infrastructure backed up")

describe-delivery-sources

Lists all delivery sources in the account. This command retrieves all configured log sources routing Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs describe-delivery-sources \
    [--next-token <value>] \
    [--limit <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination from previous response
  • --limit (integer, optional): Maximum number of sources to return (1-50)
Return Value
  • deliverySources (list): Array of source objects containing:
    • name: Name of the delivery source
    • arn: ARN of the delivery source
    • resourceArns: Associated resource ARNs
    • service: AWS service generating logs
    • logType: Type of logs
    • tags: Associated tags
  • nextToken (string): Token for next page if more results
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: List All Sources

Retrieves all delivery sources configured in the account.

#!/bin/bash
# List all sources
# Overview

echo "All Delivery Sources"
echo "===================="
echo ""

aws logs describe-delivery-sources \
    --output json | jq -r '.deliverySources[] | 
        "Name: \(.name)
  Log Type: \(.logType)
  Service: \(.service)
"'
Output: All Delivery Sources ==================== Name: JobpostLogsSource Log Type: APPLICATION_LOGS Service: logs Name: UserLogsSource Log Type: APPLICATION_LOGS Service: logs Name: AuthorLogsSource Log Type: APPLICATION_LOGS Service: logs Name: SkillsLogsSource Log Type: APPLICATION_LOGS Service: logs Name: LocationLogsSource Log Type: APPLICATION_LOGS Service: logs
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # All sources: print(f"5 delivery sources") print(f"All Django models covered:") print(f" JobPost, User, Author, Skills, Location")
Example 2: List Sources by Service

Groups delivery sources by AWS service.

#!/bin/bash
# Group by service
# Service inventory

echo "Delivery Sources by Service"
echo "==========================="
echo ""

SOURCES=$(aws logs describe-delivery-sources --output json)

echo "CloudWatch Logs Sources:"
echo "$SOURCES" | jq -r '.deliverySources[] | 
    select(.service == "logs") | "  - \(.name)"'

echo ""
echo "Lambda Sources:"
echo "$SOURCES" | jq -r '.deliverySources[] | 
    select(.service == "lambda") | "  - \(.name)"'

echo ""
echo "API Gateway Sources:"
echo "$SOURCES" | jq -r '.deliverySources[] | 
    select(.service == "apigateway") | "  - \(.name)"'
Output: Delivery Sources by Service =========================== CloudWatch Logs Sources: - JobpostLogsSource - UserLogsSource - AuthorLogsSource - SkillsLogsSource - LocationLogsSource Lambda Sources: - DjangoLambdaSource API Gateway Sources: - DjangoAPIAccessSource
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # By service: print(f"CloudWatch Logs: 5 sources") print(f"Lambda: 1 source") print(f"API Gateway: 1 source") print(f"") print(f"All Django logs covered")
Example 3: Sources Summary Report

Generates a summary report of all delivery sources.

#!/bin/bash
# Sources summary report
# Operations overview

echo "Delivery Sources Summary"
echo "========================"
echo "Generated: $(date)"
echo ""

SOURCES=$(aws logs describe-delivery-sources --output json)

TOTAL=$(echo "$SOURCES" | jq '.deliverySources | length')
APP_LOGS=$(echo "$SOURCES" | jq '[.deliverySources[] | select(.logType == "APPLICATION_LOGS")] | length')
ACCESS_LOGS=$(echo "$SOURCES" | jq '[.deliverySources[] | select(.logType == "ACCESS_LOGS")] | length')

echo "Statistics:"
echo "  Total Sources: $TOTAL"
echo "  Application Logs: $APP_LOGS"
echo "  Access Logs: $ACCESS_LOGS"
echo ""
echo "Sources:"
echo "$SOURCES" | jq -r '.deliverySources[] | 
    "  \(.name): \(.logType)"'
Output: Delivery Sources Summary ======================== Generated: Mon Jan 20 14:30:00 UTC 2025 Statistics: Total Sources: 7 Application Logs: 6 Access Logs: 1 Sources: JobpostLogsSource: APPLICATION_LOGS UserLogsSource: APPLICATION_LOGS AuthorLogsSource: APPLICATION_LOGS SkillsLogsSource: APPLICATION_LOGS LocationLogsSource: APPLICATION_LOGS DjangoLambdaSource: APPLICATION_LOGS DjangoAPIAccessSource: ACCESS_LOGS
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Summary: print(f"7 total sources") print(f"6 application log sources") print(f"1 access log source") print(f"") print(f"Django logging infrastructure complete")
Example 4: Check Source Coverage

Verifies all Django model log groups have delivery sources.

#!/bin/bash
# Check source coverage
# Completeness audit

echo "Source Coverage Audit"
echo "====================="
echo ""

# Expected log groups
EXPECTED=("/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" "/aws/django/anihpj-user" "/aws/django/anihpj-author" "/aws/django/anihpj-skills" "/aws/django/anihpj-location")

SOURCES=$(aws logs describe-delivery-sources --output json)

for LOG_GROUP in "${EXPECTED[@]}"; do
    MODEL=$(echo "$LOG_GROUP" | awk -F'-' '{print $NF}')
    
    FOUND=$(echo "$SOURCES" | jq -r ".deliverySources[] | 
        select(.resourceArns[] | contains(\"$LOG_GROUP\")) | .name")
    
    if [ -n "$FOUND" ]; then
        echo "✓ $MODEL: $FOUND"
    else
        echo "✗ $MODEL: No source configured"
    fi
done
Output: Source Coverage Audit ===================== ✓ jobpost: JobpostLogsSource ✓ user: UserLogsSource ✓ author: AuthorLogsSource ✓ skills: SkillsLogsSource ✓ location: LocationLogsSource
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Coverage check: print(f"All models have sources:") print(f" JobPost ✓") print(f" User ✓") print(f" Author ✓") print(f" Skills ✓") print(f" Location ✓")
Example 5: Export Sources Inventory

Exports all delivery sources to a JSON file for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Export sources inventory
# Documentation/backup

OUTPUT_FILE="sources-inventory-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Exporting Delivery Sources"
echo "=========================="
echo ""

aws logs describe-delivery-sources \
    --output json | jq '.deliverySources | map({
        name: .name,
        arn: .arn,
        service: .service,
        log_type: .logType,
        resources: .resourceArns,
        tags: (.tags // {})
    })' > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "✓ Exported to: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Summary:"
jq -r 'group_by(.service) | .[] | 
    "\(.[0].service): \(length) sources"' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Total sources: $(jq 'length' "$OUTPUT_FILE")"
Output: Exporting Delivery Sources ========================== ✓ Exported to: sources-inventory-20250120.json Summary: logs: 5 sources lambda: 1 sources apigateway: 1 sources Total sources: 7
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Inventory exported: print(f"File: sources-inventory-20250120.json") print(f"7 sources documented") print(f"") print(f"Django logging infrastructure backed up")

delete-delivery-source

Deletes a delivery source. This command removes log sources used for routing Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs delete-delivery-source \
    --name <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name (string, required): Name of the delivery source to delete
Return Value
  • (none): Returns empty response on success. Source is immediately removed
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Delete Delivery Source

Removes a specific delivery source.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete delivery source
# Remove config

SOURCE_NAME="OldLogsSource"

echo "Deleting delivery source: $SOURCE_NAME"

aws logs delete-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME"

echo "✓ Delivery source deleted"
Output: Deleting delivery source: OldLogsSource ✓ Delivery source deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Source removed: print(f"OldLogsSource deleted") print(f"") print(f"Django log source cleaned up")
Example 2: Delete with Backup

Backs up source configuration before deletion.

#!/bin/bash
# Backup before delete
# Recoverable

SOURCE_NAME="DeprecatedSource"
BACKUP_FILE="source-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Backing up source configuration..."

aws logs get-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --output json > "$BACKUP_FILE"

echo "✓ Backup saved: $BACKUP_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Deleting source..."

aws logs delete-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME"

echo "✓ Source deleted"
Output: Backing up source configuration... ✓ Backup saved: source-backup-20250120.json Deleting source... ✓ Source deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Safe deletion: print(f"Backup: source-backup-20250120.json") print(f"Can recreate if needed") print(f"") print(f"Django source paused")
Example 3: Delete with Verification

Deletes source and verifies it was removed.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete with verification
# Confirm removal

SOURCE_NAME="TestSource"

echo "Deleting source..."

aws logs delete-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME"

echo ""
echo "Verifying deletion..."

aws logs get-delivery-source --name "$SOURCE_NAME" 2>/dev/null
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "✓ Verified: Source deleted"
else
    echo "⚠ Source may still exist"
fi
Output: Deleting source... Verifying deletion... ✓ Verified: Source deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Verified removal: print(f"TestSource: Deleted") print(f"Verification: Passed")
Example 4: Delete Unused Sources

Identifies and removes sources without active deliveries.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete unused sources
# Cleanup

echo "Finding Unused Delivery Sources"
echo "================================"
echo ""

# Get all sources
SOURCES=$(aws logs describe-delivery-sources --output json | jq -r '.deliverySources[].name')

# Get sources with active deliveries
ACTIVE=$(aws logs describe-deliveries --output json | jq -r '.deliveries[].deliverySourceName' | sort -u)

echo "Checking sources..."
for SOURCE in $SOURCES; do
    if echo "$ACTIVE" | grep -q "^$SOURCE$"; then
        echo "  $SOURCE: In use"
    else
        echo "  $SOURCE: Unused - Deleting..."
        aws logs delete-delivery-source --name "$SOURCE"
        echo "    ✓ Deleted"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "Cleanup complete"
Output: Finding Unused Delivery Sources ================================ Checking sources... JobpostLogsSource: In use UserLogsSource: In use OldSource1: Unused - Deleting... ✓ Deleted OldSource2: Unused - Deleting... ✓ Deleted AuthorLogsSource: In use Cleanup complete
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Cleanup: print(f"2 unused sources deleted") print(f"3 active sources preserved") print(f"") print(f"Django logging cleaned up")
Example 5: Delete Source for Replacement

Removes source to replace with new configuration.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete for replacement
# Update workflow

SOURCE_NAME="JobpostLogsSource"

echo "Source Replacement Workflow"
echo "==========================="
echo ""

# Step 1: Get current config
echo "1. Saving current configuration..."
OLD_CONFIG=$(aws logs get-delivery-source --name "$SOURCE_NAME" --output json)
RESOURCE_ARN=$(echo "$OLD_CONFIG" | jq -r '.deliverySource.resourceArns[0]')
echo "   Resource: $RESOURCE_ARN"

# Step 2: Delete existing source
echo "2. Deleting old source..."
aws logs delete-delivery-source --name "$SOURCE_NAME"
echo "   ✓ Deleted"

# Step 3: Create new source
echo "3. Creating new source with updated config..."
aws logs put-delivery-source \
    --name "$SOURCE_NAME" \
    --resource-arn "$RESOURCE_ARN" \
    --log-type APPLICATION_LOGS \
    --tags Version=2,Updated=true \
    --output json | jq '.deliverySource.name'
echo "   ✓ Created"

echo ""
echo "Source replaced successfully"
Output: Source Replacement Workflow =========================== 1. Saving current configuration... Resource: arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost 2. Deleting old source... ✓ Deleted 3. Creating new source with updated config... "JobpostLogsSource" ✓ Created Source replaced successfully
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Replacement: print(f"Old source deleted") print(f"New source created with updated tags") print(f"") print(f"JobPost logs source refreshed")

put-delivery-destination

Creates or updates a delivery destination (S3, Firehose, or CloudWatch Logs). This command configures destinations for Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs put-delivery-destination \
    --name <value> \
    --delivery-destination-configuration <value> \
    [--output-format <value>] \
    [--tags <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name (string, required): Name for the delivery destination
  • --delivery-destination-configuration (structure, required): Configuration containing destinationResourceArn
  • --output-format (string, optional): Output format (json, plain, w3c, raw, parquet)
  • --tags (map, optional): Tags to apply to the destination
Return Value
  • deliveryDestination (object): Destination details containing:
    • name: Name of the destination
    • arn: ARN of the delivery destination
    • deliveryDestinationType: S3, CWL, or FH
    • deliveryDestinationConfiguration: Resource configuration
    • tags: Associated tags
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Create S3 Destination

Creates a delivery destination for S3 bucket archival.

#!/bin/bash
# Create S3 destination
# Archive storage

DEST_NAME="S3LogsDestination"
S3_BUCKET_ARN="arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-logs-bucket"

echo "Creating S3 delivery destination..."

aws logs put-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME" \
    --delivery-destination-configuration "{
        \"destinationResourceArn\": \"$S3_BUCKET_ARN\"
    }" \
    --output-format json \
    --output json | jq '.deliveryDestination | {
        name: .name,
        type: .deliveryDestinationType,
        arn: .arn
    }'
Output: Creating S3 delivery destination... { "name": "S3LogsDestination", "type": "S3", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:S3LogsDestination" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # S3 destination: print(f"S3LogsDestination created") print(f"Type: S3") print(f"") print(f"Django logs archive ready")
Example 2: Create Firehose Destination

Creates a delivery destination for Kinesis Firehose streaming.

#!/bin/bash
# Create Firehose destination
# Real-time streaming

DEST_NAME="FirehoseDestination"
FIREHOSE_ARN="arn:aws:firehose:us-east-1:123456789012:deliverystream/django-logs-stream"

echo "Creating Firehose delivery destination..."

aws logs put-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME" \
    --delivery-destination-configuration "{
        \"destinationResourceArn\": \"$FIREHOSE_ARN\"
    }" \
    --tags Environment=production,Application=anihpj \
    --output json | jq '.deliveryDestination'
Output: Creating Firehose delivery destination... { "name": "FirehoseDestination", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:FirehoseDestination", "deliveryDestinationType": "FH", "deliveryDestinationConfiguration": { "destinationResourceArn": "arn:aws:firehose:us-east-1:123456789012:deliverystream/django-logs-stream" }, "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Application": "anihpj" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Firehose destination: print(f"FirehoseDestination created") print(f"Stream: django-logs-stream") print(f"") print(f"Real-time Django log streaming")
Example 3: Create CloudWatch Logs Destination

Creates a delivery destination for cross-account CloudWatch Logs.

#!/bin/bash
# Create CloudWatch Logs destination
# Cross-account

DEST_NAME="CentralLogsDestination"
CWL_ARN="arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:987654321098:log-group:/central/django-logs"

echo "Creating CloudWatch Logs destination..."

aws logs put-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME" \
    --delivery-destination-configuration "{
        \"destinationResourceArn\": \"$CWL_ARN\"
    }" \
    --output json | jq '{
        name: .deliveryDestination.name,
        type: .deliveryDestination.deliveryDestinationType,
        target: (.deliveryDestination.deliveryDestinationConfiguration.destinationResourceArn | split(":") | .[-1])
    }'
Output: Creating CloudWatch Logs destination... { "name": "CentralLogsDestination", "type": "CWL", "target": "/central/django-logs" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # CWL destination: print(f"CentralLogsDestination created") print(f"Target: /central/django-logs") print(f"") print(f"Cross-account Django log aggregation")
Example 4: Create S3 Destination with Parquet Format

Creates an S3 destination with Parquet output for analytics.

#!/bin/bash
# S3 with Parquet format
# Analytics optimized

DEST_NAME="S3AnalyticsDestination"
S3_BUCKET_ARN="arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-analytics-bucket"

echo "Creating S3 analytics destination..."

aws logs put-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME" \
    --delivery-destination-configuration "{
        \"destinationResourceArn\": \"$S3_BUCKET_ARN\"
    }" \
    --output-format parquet \
    --tags '{
        "Environment": "production",
        "Application": "anihpj",
        "Purpose": "analytics",
        "Format": "parquet"
    }' \
    --output json | jq '.deliveryDestination | {
        name: .name,
        type: .deliveryDestinationType,
        outputFormat: .outputFormat,
        tags: .tags
    }'
Output: Creating S3 analytics destination... { "name": "S3AnalyticsDestination", "type": "S3", "outputFormat": "parquet", "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Application": "anihpj", "Purpose": "analytics", "Format": "parquet" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Parquet destination: print(f"S3AnalyticsDestination") print(f"Format: Parquet") print(f"") print(f"Django logs ready for Athena queries")
Example 5: Create Multiple Destinations

Creates destinations for different use cases in Django application.

#!/bin/bash
# Create multiple destinations
# Complete logging setup

ACCOUNT_ID="123456789012"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "Creating Delivery Destinations"
echo "==============================="
echo ""

# Archive destination (S3 - JSON)
echo "1. Creating archive destination (S3/JSON)..."
aws logs put-delivery-destination \
    --name "S3ArchiveDestination" \
    --delivery-destination-configuration '{
        "destinationResourceArn": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-archive-bucket"
    }' \
    --output-format json \
    --tags Purpose=archive \
    --output json | jq -r '.deliveryDestination.name'

# Analytics destination (S3 - Parquet)
echo "2. Creating analytics destination (S3/Parquet)..."
aws logs put-delivery-destination \
    --name "S3AnalyticsDestination" \
    --delivery-destination-configuration '{
        "destinationResourceArn": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-analytics-bucket"
    }' \
    --output-format parquet \
    --tags Purpose=analytics \
    --output json | jq -r '.deliveryDestination.name'

# Real-time destination (Firehose)
echo "3. Creating real-time destination (Firehose)..."
aws logs put-delivery-destination \
    --name "FirehoseRealtime" \
    --delivery-destination-configuration '{
        "destinationResourceArn": "arn:aws:firehose:us-east-1:123456789012:deliverystream/realtime-logs"
    }' \
    --tags Purpose=realtime \
    --output json | jq -r '.deliveryDestination.name'

echo ""
echo "✓ All destinations created"
Output: Creating Delivery Destinations =============================== 1. Creating archive destination (S3/JSON)... S3ArchiveDestination 2. Creating analytics destination (S3/Parquet)... S3AnalyticsDestination 3. Creating real-time destination (Firehose)... FirehoseRealtime ✓ All destinations created
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # All destinations: print(f"Archive: S3ArchiveDestination (JSON)") print(f"Analytics: S3AnalyticsDestination (Parquet)") print(f"Real-time: FirehoseRealtime") print(f"") print(f"Complete Django log destinations")

get-delivery-destination

Retrieves information about a specific delivery destination. This command provides configuration details for destinations receiving Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs get-delivery-destination \
    --name <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name (string, required): Name of the delivery destination to retrieve
Return Value
  • deliveryDestination (object): Destination details containing:
    • name: Name of the destination
    • arn: ARN of the delivery destination
    • deliveryDestinationType: S3, CWL, or FH
    • deliveryDestinationConfiguration: Resource configuration
    • outputFormat: Output format (if configured)
    • tags: Associated tags
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Get Destination Details

Retrieves complete information about a delivery destination.

#!/bin/bash
# Get destination details
# Configuration review

DEST_NAME="S3LogsDestination"

echo "Destination Details: $DEST_NAME"
echo ""

aws logs get-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME" \
    --output json | jq '.deliveryDestination'
Output: Destination Details: S3LogsDestination { "name": "S3LogsDestination", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:S3LogsDestination", "deliveryDestinationType": "S3", "outputFormat": "json", "deliveryDestinationConfiguration": { "destinationResourceArn": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-logs-bucket" }, "tags": { "Environment": "production", "Application": "anihpj" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Destination info: print(f"S3LogsDestination") print(f"Type: S3, Format: JSON") print(f"Bucket: anihpj-logs-bucket") print(f"") print(f"Django logs archive destination")
Example 2: Verify S3 Bucket Configuration

Validates that the destination points to the correct S3 bucket.

#!/bin/bash
# Verify S3 bucket
# Configuration check

DEST_NAME="S3LogsDestination"
EXPECTED_BUCKET="anihpj-logs-bucket"

echo "Verifying Destination: $DEST_NAME"
echo ""

DEST=$(aws logs get-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME" \
    --output json)

BUCKET_ARN=$(echo "$DEST" | jq -r '.deliveryDestination.deliveryDestinationConfiguration.destinationResourceArn')
BUCKET_NAME=$(echo "$BUCKET_ARN" | awk -F::: '{print $2}')

echo "Configured Bucket: $BUCKET_NAME"
echo "Expected Bucket: $EXPECTED_BUCKET"
echo ""

if [ "$BUCKET_NAME" = "$EXPECTED_BUCKET" ]; then
    echo "✓ Destination correctly configured"
else
    echo "⚠ Bucket mismatch"
fi
Output: Verifying Destination: S3LogsDestination Configured Bucket: anihpj-logs-bucket Expected Bucket: anihpj-logs-bucket ✓ Destination correctly configured
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Verified: print(f"S3LogsDestination → anihpj-logs-bucket") print(f"Status: Correctly configured") print(f"") print(f"Django logs going to correct bucket")
Example 3: Check Output Format

Retrieves destination details including output format configuration.

#!/bin/bash
# Check output format
# Analytics review

DEST_NAME="S3AnalyticsDestination"

echo "Output Format Check: $DEST_NAME"
echo ""

aws logs get-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME" \
    --output json | jq '{
        name: .deliveryDestination.name,
        type: .deliveryDestination.deliveryDestinationType,
        format: (.deliveryDestination.outputFormat // "default"),
        bucket: (.deliveryDestination.deliveryDestinationConfiguration.destinationResourceArn | split(":::") | .[1])
    }'
Output: Output Format Check: S3AnalyticsDestination { "name": "S3AnalyticsDestination", "type": "S3", "format": "parquet", "bucket": "anihpj-analytics-bucket" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Format check: print(f"S3AnalyticsDestination") print(f"Format: Parquet") print(f"") print(f"Django logs ready for Athena")
Example 4: Get Destination for Troubleshooting

Retrieves destination details for diagnosing delivery issues.

#!/bin/bash
# Troubleshoot destination
# Diagnose issues

DEST_NAME="FirehoseDestination"

echo "Destination Diagnostics"
echo "======================="
echo ""

DEST=$(aws logs get-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME" \
    --output json 2>&1)

if echo "$DEST" | jq -e '.deliveryDestination' > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "$DEST" | jq '.deliveryDestination | {
        name: .name,
        type: .deliveryDestinationType,
        resource: .deliveryDestinationConfiguration.destinationResourceArn
    }'
    
    echo ""
    RESOURCE=$(echo "$DEST" | jq -r '.deliveryDestination.deliveryDestinationConfiguration.destinationResourceArn')
    
    if [[ "$RESOURCE" == *"firehose"* ]]; then
        STREAM_NAME=$(echo "$RESOURCE" | awk -F/ '{print $NF}')
        echo "Checking Firehose stream: $STREAM_NAME"
        aws firehose describe-delivery-stream --delivery-stream-name "$STREAM_NAME" &>/dev/null
        if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "✓ Firehose stream exists and accessible"
        else
            echo "✗ Cannot access Firehose stream"
        fi
    fi
else
    echo "✗ Destination not found: $DEST_NAME"
fi
Output: Destination Diagnostics ======================= { "name": "FirehoseDestination", "type": "FH", "resource": "arn:aws:firehose:us-east-1:123456789012:deliverystream/django-logs-stream" } Checking Firehose stream: django-logs-stream ✓ Firehose stream exists and accessible
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Diagnostics: print(f"FirehoseDestination: Valid") print(f"Stream: Accessible") print(f"") print(f"Django log streaming working")
Example 5: Export Destination Configuration

Exports destination configuration for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Export destination config
# Documentation

DEST_NAME="S3LogsDestination"
OUTPUT_FILE="destination-config-$DEST_NAME.json"

echo "Exporting Destination Configuration"
echo "===================================="
echo ""

aws logs get-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME" \
    --output json > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "✓ Exported to: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Summary:"
jq '{
    name: .deliveryDestination.name,
    arn: .deliveryDestination.arn,
    type: .deliveryDestination.deliveryDestinationType,
    format: (.deliveryDestination.outputFormat // "default"),
    target: (.deliveryDestination.deliveryDestinationConfiguration.destinationResourceArn | split(":::") | .[1] // .deliveryDestination.deliveryDestinationConfiguration.destinationResourceArn),
    tag_count: (.deliveryDestination.tags | keys | length)
}' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Exporting Destination Configuration ==================================== ✓ Exported to: destination-config-S3LogsDestination.json Summary: { "name": "S3LogsDestination", "arn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:delivery-destination:S3LogsDestination", "type": "S3", "format": "json", "target": "anihpj-logs-bucket", "tag_count": 2 }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Exported config: print(f"File: destination-config-S3LogsDestination.json") print(f"S3 destination documented") print(f"") print(f"Django logging infrastructure backed up")

describe-delivery-destinations

Lists all delivery destinations in the account. This command retrieves all configured destinations for Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs describe-delivery-destinations \
    [--next-token <value>] \
    [--limit <value>] \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --next-token (string, optional): Token for pagination from previous response
  • --limit (integer, optional): Maximum number of destinations to return (1-50)
Return Value
  • deliveryDestinations (list): Array of destination objects containing:
    • name: Name of the destination
    • arn: ARN of the delivery destination
    • deliveryDestinationType: S3, CWL, or FH
    • deliveryDestinationConfiguration: Resource configuration
    • outputFormat: Output format (if configured)
    • tags: Associated tags
  • nextToken (string): Token for next page if more results
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: List All Destinations

Retrieves all delivery destinations configured in the account.

#!/bin/bash
# List all destinations
# Overview

echo "All Delivery Destinations"
echo "========================="
echo ""

aws logs describe-delivery-destinations \
    --output json | jq -r '.deliveryDestinations[] | 
        "Name: \(.name)
  Type: \(.deliveryDestinationType)
  Format: \(.outputFormat // "default")
"'
Output: All Delivery Destinations ========================= Name: S3LogsDestination Type: S3 Format: json Name: S3AnalyticsDestination Type: S3 Format: parquet Name: FirehoseDestination Type: FH Format: default Name: CentralLogsDestination Type: CWL Format: default
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # All destinations: print(f"4 delivery destinations") print(f" S3 Archive (JSON)") print(f" S3 Analytics (Parquet)") print(f" Firehose (Real-time)") print(f" CloudWatch Logs (Central)")
Example 2: List Destinations by Type

Groups delivery destinations by type.

#!/bin/bash
# Group by destination type
# Service inventory

echo "Delivery Destinations by Type"
echo "=============================="
echo ""

DESTS=$(aws logs describe-delivery-destinations --output json)

echo "S3 Destinations:"
echo "$DESTS" | jq -r '.deliveryDestinations[] | 
    select(.deliveryDestinationType == "S3") | 
    "  - \(.name) (\(.outputFormat // "default"))"'

echo ""
echo "Firehose Destinations:"
echo "$DESTS" | jq -r '.deliveryDestinations[] | 
    select(.deliveryDestinationType == "FH") | 
    "  - \(.name)"'

echo ""
echo "CloudWatch Logs Destinations:"
echo "$DESTS" | jq -r '.deliveryDestinations[] | 
    select(.deliveryDestinationType == "CWL") | 
    "  - \(.name)"'
Output: Delivery Destinations by Type ============================== S3 Destinations: - S3LogsDestination (json) - S3AnalyticsDestination (parquet) - S3ArchiveDestination (json) Firehose Destinations: - FirehoseDestination - FirehoseRealtime CloudWatch Logs Destinations: - CentralLogsDestination
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # By type: print(f"S3: 3 destinations") print(f"Firehose: 2 destinations") print(f"CloudWatch Logs: 1 destination") print(f"") print(f"Django logs multi-destination")
Example 3: Destinations Summary Report

Generates a summary report of all delivery destinations.

#!/bin/bash
# Destinations summary report
# Operations overview

echo "Delivery Destinations Summary"
echo "============================="
echo "Generated: $(date)"
echo ""

DESTS=$(aws logs describe-delivery-destinations --output json)

TOTAL=$(echo "$DESTS" | jq '.deliveryDestinations | length')
S3_COUNT=$(echo "$DESTS" | jq '[.deliveryDestinations[] | select(.deliveryDestinationType == "S3")] | length')
FH_COUNT=$(echo "$DESTS" | jq '[.deliveryDestinations[] | select(.deliveryDestinationType == "FH")] | length')
CWL_COUNT=$(echo "$DESTS" | jq '[.deliveryDestinations[] | select(.deliveryDestinationType == "CWL")] | length')

echo "Statistics:"
echo "  Total Destinations: $TOTAL"
echo "  S3: $S3_COUNT"
echo "  Firehose: $FH_COUNT"
echo "  CloudWatch Logs: $CWL_COUNT"
echo ""
echo "Output Formats:"
echo "$DESTS" | jq -r '.deliveryDestinations | group_by(.outputFormat // "default") | .[] | 
    "  \(.[0].outputFormat // "default"): \(length)"'
Output: Delivery Destinations Summary ============================= Generated: Mon Jan 20 14:30:00 UTC 2025 Statistics: Total Destinations: 6 S3: 3 Firehose: 2 CloudWatch Logs: 1 Output Formats: json: 2 parquet: 1 default: 3
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Summary: print(f"6 total destinations") print(f"Multiple formats for analysis") print(f"") print(f"Django logs multi-format support")
Example 4: List Destinations with Resources

Shows destinations with their underlying resource ARNs.

#!/bin/bash
# Destinations with resources
# Infrastructure view

echo "Destinations and Resources"
echo "=========================="
echo ""

aws logs describe-delivery-destinations \
    --output json | jq -r '.deliveryDestinations[] | 
        "Name: \(.name)
  Type: \(.deliveryDestinationType)
  Resource: \(.deliveryDestinationConfiguration.destinationResourceArn | split(":") | .[-1])
  Tags: \(.tags | keys | length) tags
"'
Output: Destinations and Resources ========================== Name: S3LogsDestination Type: S3 Resource: anihpj-logs-bucket Tags: 2 tags Name: S3AnalyticsDestination Type: S3 Resource: anihpj-analytics-bucket Tags: 4 tags Name: FirehoseDestination Type: FH Resource: django-logs-stream Tags: 2 tags Name: CentralLogsDestination Type: CWL Resource: /central/django-logs Tags: 1 tags
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Resources: print(f"S3 buckets: 2") print(f"Firehose streams: 1") print(f"Log groups: 1") print(f"") print(f"Django logging infrastructure mapped")
Example 5: Export Destinations Inventory

Exports all delivery destinations to a JSON file for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Export destinations inventory
# Documentation/backup

OUTPUT_FILE="destinations-inventory-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Exporting Delivery Destinations"
echo "================================"
echo ""

aws logs describe-delivery-destinations \
    --output json | jq '.deliveryDestinations | map({
        name: .name,
        arn: .arn,
        type: .deliveryDestinationType,
        format: (.outputFormat // "default"),
        resource: (.deliveryDestinationConfiguration.destinationResourceArn | split(":") | .[-1]),
        tags: (.tags // {})
    })' > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "✓ Exported to: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Summary:"
jq -r 'group_by(.type) | .[] | 
    "\(.[0].type): \(length) destinations"' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Total destinations: $(jq 'length' "$OUTPUT_FILE")"
Output: Exporting Delivery Destinations ================================ ✓ Exported to: destinations-inventory-20250120.json Summary: S3: 3 destinations FH: 2 destinations CWL: 1 destinations Total destinations: 6
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Inventory exported: print(f"File: destinations-inventory-20250120.json") print(f"6 destinations documented") print(f"") print(f"Django logging infrastructure backed up")

delete-delivery-destination

Deletes a delivery destination. This command removes destination configurations used for Django application logs tracking Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model operations.

Syntax
aws logs delete-delivery-destination \
    --name <value> \
    [--cli-input-json <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name (string, required): Name of the delivery destination to delete
Return Value
  • (none): Returns empty response on success. Destination is immediately removed
5 Detailed CloudWatch Logs Insights Examples
Example 1: Delete Delivery Destination

Removes a specific delivery destination.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete delivery destination
# Remove config

DEST_NAME="OldDestination"

echo "Deleting delivery destination: $DEST_NAME"

aws logs delete-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME"

echo "✓ Delivery destination deleted"
Output: Deleting delivery destination: OldDestination ✓ Delivery destination deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Destination removed: print(f"OldDestination deleted") print(f"") print(f"Django log destination cleaned up")
Example 2: Delete with Backup

Backs up destination configuration before deletion.

#!/bin/bash
# Backup before delete
# Recoverable

DEST_NAME="DeprecatedS3Destination"
BACKUP_FILE="destination-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Backing up destination configuration..."

aws logs get-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME" \
    --output json > "$BACKUP_FILE"

echo "✓ Backup saved: $BACKUP_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Deleting destination..."

aws logs delete-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME"

echo "✓ Destination deleted"
Output: Backing up destination configuration... ✓ Backup saved: destination-backup-20250120.json Deleting destination... ✓ Destination deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Safe deletion: print(f"Backup: destination-backup-20250120.json") print(f"Can recreate if needed")
Example 3: Delete with Verification

Deletes destination and verifies it was removed.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete with verification
# Confirm removal

DEST_NAME="TestDestination"

echo "Deleting destination..."

aws logs delete-delivery-destination \
    --name "$DEST_NAME"

echo ""
echo "Verifying deletion..."

aws logs get-delivery-destination --name "$DEST_NAME" 2>/dev/null
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "✓ Verified: Destination deleted"
else
    echo "⚠ Destination may still exist"
fi
Output: Deleting destination... Verifying deletion... ✓ Verified: Destination deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Verified removal: print(f"TestDestination: Deleted") print(f"Verification: Passed")
Example 4: Delete Unused Destinations

Identifies and removes destinations without active deliveries.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete unused destinations
# Cleanup

echo "Finding Unused Delivery Destinations"
echo "====================================="
echo ""

# Get all destinations
DESTS=$(aws logs describe-delivery-destinations --output json | jq -r '.deliveryDestinations[].name')

# Get destinations with active deliveries
ACTIVE=$(aws logs describe-deliveries --output json | jq -r '.deliveries[].deliveryDestinationArn | split(":") | .[-1]' | sort -u)

echo "Checking destinations..."
for DEST in $DESTS; do
