aws xray create-sampling-rule \
    --sampling-rule '{
        "RuleName": "anihpj-prod-general",
        "Priority": 9000,
        "FixedRate": 0.05,
        "ReservoirSize": 1,
        "ServiceName": "anihpj-django",
        "ServiceType": "*",
        "Host": "*",
        "HTTPMethod": "*",
        "URLPath": "*",
        "Version": 1
    }'

echo "Production sampling strategy deployed"
Output: { "SamplingRuleRecord": { "SamplingRule": { "RuleName": "anihpj-prod-errors", "Priority": 100, "FixedRate": 1.0 } } } { "SamplingRuleRecord": { "SamplingRule": { "RuleName": "anihpj-prod-auth-actions", "Priority": 300, "FixedRate": 0.50 } } } { "SamplingRuleRecord": { "SamplingRule": { "RuleName": "anihpj-prod-general", "Priority": 9000, "FixedRate": 0.05 } } } Production sampling strategy deployed
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Tiered sampling strategy: # # Priority 100 (Errors): 100% of 5xx responses # - JobPost.DoesNotExist → 404 # - ValidationError → 400 # - DatabaseError → 500 # # Priority 300 (Auth actions): 50% of user mutations # - User.objects.create() # - JobPost.objects.filter(author__user=request.user) # # Priority 9000 (General): 5% of all other traffic # - JobPost.objects.all() # - Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains=q) # # Cost estimate: ~$15/month for 1M requests

delete-group

Deletes an X-Ray group that was previously created for filtering traces. Use this to clean up groups no longer needed for monitoring your Django application.

Syntax
aws xray delete-group \
    [--group-name <value>] \
    [--group-arn <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --group-name (string): The case-sensitive name of the group to delete
  • --group-arn (string): The ARN of the group to delete
Return Value
  • No output on success. The operation returns an empty response.
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: Delete group by name

Remove a trace filter group that is no longer needed for monitoring JobPost operations.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray delete-group \
    --group-name "anihpj-jobpost-errors"

echo "Group deleted successfully"
Output: Group deleted successfully
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Deleted group was filtering: # - JobPost.objects.get() failures # - JobPost creation validation errors # Now using CloudWatch for error monitoring instead
Example 2: Delete group by ARN

Remove a group using its Amazon Resource Name for precise deletion.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray delete-group \
    --group-arn "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-slow-skills"
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Removed slow Skills query monitoring group # Skills.objects performance now optimized # No longer need dedicated trace filtering
Example 3: Delete multiple groups in batch cleanup

Remove several obsolete trace groups as part of infrastructure cleanup.

#!/bin/bash
# Batch cleanup of obsolete X-Ray groups
GROUPS_TO_DELETE=(
    "anihpj-legacy-errors"
    "anihpj-temp-debug"
    "anihpj-staging-traces"
)

for group in "${GROUPS_TO_DELETE[@]}"; do
    echo "Deleting group: $group"
    aws xray delete-group --group-name "$group" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "  Deleted successfully" || \
        echo "  Group not found or already deleted"
done

echo "Batch cleanup complete"
Output: Deleting group: anihpj-legacy-errors Deleted successfully Deleting group: anihpj-temp-debug Deleted successfully Deleting group: anihpj-staging-traces Group not found or already deleted Batch cleanup complete
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Cleanup of obsolete monitoring groups: # - anihpj-legacy-errors: Old error tracking (replaced) # - anihpj-temp-debug: Temporary debugging session # - anihpj-staging-traces: Staging environment removed # # Active groups retained: # - anihpj-production-api (JobPost monitoring) # - anihpj-skills-performance (Skills M2M tracking)
Example 4: Delete group with verification

Safely delete a group after verifying it exists and confirming no active insights.

#!/bin/bash
GROUP_NAME="anihpj-author-traces"

# Check if group exists first
if aws xray get-group --group-name "$GROUP_NAME" &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Group found: $GROUP_NAME"
    
    # Check for active insights before deletion
    INSIGHTS=$(aws xray get-insight-summaries \
        --group-name "$GROUP_NAME" \
        --start-time $(date -d '7 days ago' +%s) \
        --end-time $(date +%s) \
        --query 'InsightSummaries | length(@)')
    
    if [ "$INSIGHTS" -gt 0 ]; then
        echo "Warning: $INSIGHTS active insights found"
        read -p "Delete anyway? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
        echo
        if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
            echo "Deletion cancelled"
            exit 0
        fi
    fi
    
    aws xray delete-group --group-name "$GROUP_NAME"
    echo "Group deleted: $GROUP_NAME"
else
    echo "Group not found: $GROUP_NAME"
fi
Output: Group found: anihpj-author-traces Warning: 2 active insights found Delete anyway? (y/n) y Group deleted: anihpj-author-traces
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Safe deletion of Author traces group: # - Verified group existence before deletion # - Checked for active insights (found 2) # - User confirmed deletion # # Group was monitoring: # - Author.objects.get() lookups # - Author.objects.filter(user=request.user) # - ForeignKey traversals from JobPost to Author
Example 5: Delete and recreate group with updated filter

Replace an existing group with an updated filter expression for improved trace filtering.

#!/bin/bash
GROUP_NAME="anihpj-jobpost-api"

# Delete existing group
echo "Deleting existing group..."
aws xray delete-group --group-name "$GROUP_NAME" 2>/dev/null

# Create new group with updated filter
echo "Creating updated group with new filter..."
aws xray create-group \
    --group-name "$GROUP_NAME" \
    --filter-expression 'service(id(name: "django-jobpost-api")) AND (responsetime > 0.5 OR error = true)' \
    --insights-configuration '{"InsightsEnabled": true, "NotificationsEnabled": true}'

echo "Group recreated with updated filter"
Output: Deleting existing group... Creating updated group with new filter... { "Group": { "GroupName": "anihpj-jobpost-api", "GroupARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-jobpost-api", "FilterExpression": "service(id(name: \"django-jobpost-api\")) AND (responsetime > 0.5 OR error = true)", "InsightsConfiguration": { "InsightsEnabled": true, "NotificationsEnabled": true } } } Group recreated with updated filter
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Updated filter now captures: # - Slow requests (>0.5s) - likely N+1 queries on: # JobPost.objects.all() without select_related # - All errors including: # JobPost.DoesNotExist # Skills.ValidationError # # New filter more targeted than previous "all traces" # Reduces noise while capturing important events

delete-resource-policy

Deletes a resource policy from X-Ray. Resource policies control cross-account access to your trace data.

Syntax
aws xray delete-resource-policy \
    --policy-name <value> \
    [--policy-revision-id <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --policy-name (string, required): The name of the resource policy to delete
  • --policy-revision-id (string): The revision ID of the policy for optimistic concurrency
Return Value
  • No output on success.
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: Delete cross-account access policy

Remove a policy that allowed another AWS account to access Django application traces.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray delete-resource-policy \
    --policy-name "anihpj-cross-account-traces"

echo "Resource policy deleted"
Output: Resource policy deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Removed cross-account trace access # External monitoring team no longer has access # JobPost, Skills, Author traces now private
Example 2: Delete policy with revision ID for concurrency

Safely delete a policy using revision ID to prevent conflicts in multi-team environments.

#!/bin/bash
# Get current policy revision first
POLICY_INFO=$(aws xray list-resource-policies \
    --query "ResourcePolicies[?PolicyName=='anihpj-vendor-access']" \
    --output json)

REVISION_ID=$(echo "$POLICY_INFO" | jq -r '.[0].PolicyRevisionId')

# Delete with revision ID for optimistic concurrency
aws xray delete-resource-policy \
    --policy-name "anihpj-vendor-access" \
    --policy-revision-id "$REVISION_ID"

echo "Policy deleted with revision check"
Output: Policy deleted with revision check
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Removed vendor access to traces: # - Third-party monitoring tool access revoked # - JobPost performance data no longer shared # - Revision ID prevents race conditions # # Useful when multiple admins manage policies
Example 3: Delete multiple resource policies during offboarding

Remove all cross-account policies when decommissioning external access.

#!/bin/bash
# List all resource policies
POLICIES=$(aws xray list-resource-policies \
    --query 'ResourcePolicies[*].PolicyName' \
    --output text)

echo "Found policies: $POLICIES"

# Delete each policy that matches pattern
for policy in $POLICIES; do
    if [[ "$policy" == anihpj-external-* ]]; then
        echo "Deleting external policy: $policy"
        aws xray delete-resource-policy --policy-name "$policy"
    fi
done

echo "External access policies removed"
Output: Found policies: anihpj-external-dev anihpj-external-qa anihpj-internal-monitoring Deleting external policy: anihpj-external-dev Deleting external policy: anihpj-external-qa External access policies removed
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Offboarding external teams from trace access: # - Dev team access (anihpj-external-dev) removed # - QA team access (anihpj-external-qa) removed # - Internal monitoring retained # # Traces for JobPost, Skills, Author, Location # now only accessible to production team
Example 4: Delete policy and audit the action

Remove a resource policy with audit logging for compliance tracking.

#!/bin/bash
POLICY_NAME="anihpj-partner-access"
AUDIT_LOG="/var/log/xray-policy-audit.log"
TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
USER=$(aws sts get-caller-identity --query 'Arn' --output text)

# Get policy details before deletion
POLICY_DETAILS=$(aws xray list-resource-policies \
    --query "ResourcePolicies[?PolicyName=='$POLICY_NAME']" \
    --output json)

# Delete the policy
aws xray delete-resource-policy --policy-name "$POLICY_NAME"

# Log the deletion for audit
echo "{
    \"timestamp\": \"$TIMESTAMP\",
    \"action\": \"delete-resource-policy\",
    \"user\": \"$USER\",
    \"policy_name\": \"$POLICY_NAME\",
    \"policy_details\": $POLICY_DETAILS,
    \"reason\": \"partnership_ended\"
}" >> "$AUDIT_LOG"

echo "Policy deleted and audit logged"
Output: Policy deleted and audit logged
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Audit trail for policy deletion: # { # "timestamp": "2025-01-18T12:00:00Z", # "action": "delete-resource-policy", # "user": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/admin", # "policy_name": "anihpj-partner-access", # "reason": "partnership_ended" # } # # Compliance requirement satisfied for: # - SOC 2 access control logging # - GDPR data access tracking
Example 5: Delete and replace policy with updated permissions

Remove an outdated policy and create a new one with refined permissions for trace access.

#!/bin/bash
POLICY_NAME="anihpj-readonly-traces"
EXTERNAL_ACCOUNT="987654321098"

# Delete existing broad policy
echo "Removing old policy..."
aws xray delete-resource-policy --policy-name "$POLICY_NAME"

# Create new policy with restricted access
echo "Creating refined policy..."
aws xray put-resource-policy \
    --policy-name "$POLICY_NAME" \
    --policy-document "{
        \"Version\": \"2012-10-17\",
        \"Statement\": [
            {
                \"Sid\": \"LimitedTraceAccess\",
                \"Effect\": \"Allow\",
                \"Principal\": {
                    \"AWS\": \"arn:aws:iam::${EXTERNAL_ACCOUNT}:root\"
                },
                \"Action\": [
                    \"xray:GetTraceSummaries\",
                    \"xray:BatchGetTraces\"
                ],
                \"Resource\": \"*\",
                \"Condition\": {
                    \"StringEquals\": {
                        \"xray:GroupName\": \"anihpj-production-api\"
                    }
                }
            }
        ]
    }"

echo "Policy updated with restricted permissions"
Output: Removing old policy... Creating refined policy... { "ResourcePolicy": { "PolicyName": "anihpj-readonly-traces", "PolicyDocument": "...", "PolicyRevisionId": "abc123..." } } Policy updated with restricted permissions
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Refined cross-account access: # # OLD policy allowed: # - Full trace access to all groups # - Write operations on traces # # NEW policy restricts to: # - Read-only access (GetTraceSummaries, BatchGetTraces) # - Only anihpj-production-api group # - Only JobPost/Skills/Author production traces # # Principle of least privilege applied

delete-sampling-rule

Deletes a sampling rule from X-Ray. Use this to remove sampling configurations that are no longer needed for your Django application tracing.

Syntax
aws xray delete-sampling-rule \
    [--rule-name <value>] \
    [--rule-arn <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --rule-name (string): The name of the sampling rule to delete
  • --rule-arn (string): The ARN of the sampling rule to delete
Return Value
  • SamplingRuleRecord (object): The deleted sampling rule record
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: Delete sampling rule by name

Remove a sampling rule that was used for Django API endpoint tracing.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray delete-sampling-rule \
    --rule-name "anihpj-api-sampling"
Output: { "SamplingRuleRecord": { "SamplingRule": { "RuleName": "anihpj-api-sampling", "RuleARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:sampling-rule/anihpj-api-sampling" }, "CreatedAt": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z", "ModifiedAt": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Removed 10% API sampling rule # Now using default sampling for: # - JobPost API endpoints # - Skills CRUD operations # - Author and Location queries
Example 2: Delete sampling rule by ARN

Remove a sampling rule using its Amazon Resource Name for precise deletion.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray delete-sampling-rule \
    --rule-arn "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:sampling-rule/anihpj-error-traces"
Output: { "SamplingRuleRecord": { "SamplingRule": { "RuleName": "anihpj-error-traces", "RuleARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:sampling-rule/anihpj-error-traces", "Priority": 100, "FixedRate": 1.0 }, "CreatedAt": "2025-01-10T08:00:00Z", "ModifiedAt": "2025-01-10T08:00:00Z" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Removed 100% error sampling rule # Error traces now sampled at default rate # Was capturing all: # - JobPost.DoesNotExist exceptions # - Skills validation errors
Example 3: Delete multiple obsolete sampling rules

Clean up old sampling rules that are no longer needed for Django application tracing.

#!/bin/bash
# Get all sampling rules and delete obsolete ones
OBSOLETE_RULES=(
    "anihpj-legacy-sampling"
    "anihpj-debug-high-rate"
    "anihpj-staging-rules"
)

for rule in "${OBSOLETE_RULES[@]}"; do
    echo "Deleting rule: $rule"
    RESULT=$(aws xray delete-sampling-rule --rule-name "$rule" 2>&1)
    if echo "$RESULT" | grep -q "SamplingRuleRecord"; then
        echo "  ✓ Deleted successfully"
    else
        echo "  ⚠ Rule not found or already deleted"
    fi
done

echo "Cleanup complete"
Output: Deleting rule: anihpj-legacy-sampling ✓ Deleted successfully Deleting rule: anihpj-debug-high-rate ✓ Deleted successfully Deleting rule: anihpj-staging-rules ⚠ Rule not found or already deleted Cleanup complete
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Removed obsolete sampling rules: # - anihpj-legacy-sampling: Old 50% rule (replaced) # - anihpj-debug-high-rate: Temp debugging (100%) # - anihpj-staging-rules: Staging env removed # # Active rules retained: # - anihpj-production-api (10% sampling) # - anihpj-errors (100% for 5xx)
Example 4: Delete and recreate sampling rule with updated settings

Replace a sampling rule with updated configuration for Django application monitoring.

#!/bin/bash
RULE_NAME="anihpj-jobpost-sampling"

# Delete existing rule
echo "Removing old sampling rule..."
aws xray delete-sampling-rule --rule-name "$RULE_NAME"

# Create updated rule with lower rate (cost optimization)
echo "Creating updated rule with optimized settings..."
aws xray create-sampling-rule \
    --sampling-rule "{
        \"RuleName\": \"$RULE_NAME\",
        \"Priority\": 500,
        \"FixedRate\": 0.05,
        \"ReservoirSize\": 2,
        \"ServiceName\": \"anihpj-django\",
        \"ServiceType\": \"*\",
        \"Host\": \"*\",
        \"HTTPMethod\": \"*\",
        \"URLPath\": \"/api/jobposts/*\",
        \"Version\": 1
    }"

echo "Sampling rule updated: 10% → 5%"
Output: Removing old sampling rule... { "SamplingRuleRecord": { "SamplingRule": {"RuleName": "anihpj-jobpost-sampling", "FixedRate": 0.10} } } Creating updated rule with optimized settings... { "SamplingRuleRecord": { "SamplingRule": {"RuleName": "anihpj-jobpost-sampling", "FixedRate": 0.05} } } Sampling rule updated: 10% → 5%
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Sampling rate optimization: # OLD: 10% of JobPost requests traced # NEW: 5% of JobPost requests traced # # Cost savings: ~50% reduction in trace volume # Still captures sufficient data for: # - Performance trends on JobPost.objects.all() # - Skills M2M relationship timing # - Author/Location lookup patterns
Example 5: Conditional deletion based on sampling rate

Delete high-rate sampling rules that exceed cost thresholds for Django application.

#!/bin/bash
MAX_RATE=0.20  # Delete rules with rate > 20%

# Get all sampling rules
RULES=$(aws xray get-sampling-rules --query 'SamplingRuleRecords[*].SamplingRule' --output json)

# Find and delete high-rate rules
echo "$RULES" | jq -c '.[]' | while read -r rule; do
    RULE_NAME=$(echo "$rule" | jq -r '.RuleName')
    FIXED_RATE=$(echo "$rule" | jq -r '.FixedRate')
    
    # Skip default rule
    if [ "$RULE_NAME" == "Default" ]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Check if rate exceeds threshold
    if (( $(echo "$FIXED_RATE > $MAX_RATE" | bc -l) )); then
        echo "High rate rule found: $RULE_NAME (${FIXED_RATE})"
        echo "  Deleting for cost optimization..."
        aws xray delete-sampling-rule --rule-name "$RULE_NAME"
    fi
done

echo "Cost optimization complete"
Output: High rate rule found: anihpj-debug-all (1.0) Deleting for cost optimization... { "SamplingRuleRecord": {"SamplingRule": {"RuleName": "anihpj-debug-all"}} } High rate rule found: anihpj-staging (0.50) Deleting for cost optimization... { "SamplingRuleRecord": {"SamplingRule": {"RuleName": "anihpj-staging"}} } Cost optimization complete
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Cost optimization removed high-rate rules: # - anihpj-debug-all (100%): Debugging complete # - anihpj-staging (50%): Staging not needed # # Retained rules under 20% threshold: # - anihpj-production (5%): Normal monitoring # - anihpj-errors (100%): Exception - keep all errors # # Estimated monthly savings: ~$50-100

get-encryption-config

Retrieves the current encryption configuration for X-Ray trace data. Shows whether traces from your Django application are encrypted with AWS managed keys or customer managed KMS keys.

Syntax
aws xray get-encryption-config
Parameters Description
  • No parameters required.
Return Value
  • EncryptionConfig (object): Contains KeyId (KMS key ARN if using CMK), Status (UPDATING or ACTIVE), and Type (NONE or KMS)
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: Check current encryption settings

Verify how trace data from the anihpj Django application is encrypted.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-encryption-config
Output: { "EncryptionConfig": { "KeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012", "Status": "ACTIVE", "Type": "KMS" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Trace data encryption status: # - Type: KMS (customer managed key) # - Status: ACTIVE # # Encrypted traces include: # - JobPost.objects query patterns # - User authentication traces # - Skills validation operations
Example 2: Verify compliance encryption

Check encryption for compliance requirements and audit trace security.

#!/bin/bash
# Get encryption config and validate
ENCRYPTION=$(aws xray get-encryption-config --query 'EncryptionConfig.Type' --output text)

if [ "$ENCRYPTION" == "KMS" ]; then
    echo "✓ Traces encrypted with KMS - Compliance OK"
    aws xray get-encryption-config --query 'EncryptionConfig.KeyId' --output text
else
    echo "⚠ Traces using default encryption"
fi
Output: ✓ Traces encrypted with KMS - Compliance OK arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Compliance verification for: # - PII in User model traces # - Sensitive JobPost data # - Author contact information # - Location geographic data # KMS encryption ensures GDPR/HIPAA compliance
Example 3: Generate encryption audit report

Create a detailed encryption configuration report for security audits.

#!/bin/bash
# Generate encryption audit report
REPORT_FILE="xray-encryption-audit-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Generating X-Ray encryption audit report..."

# Get encryption config
ENCRYPTION_CONFIG=$(aws xray get-encryption-config)

# Get KMS key details if using CMK
KEY_ID=$(echo "$ENCRYPTION_CONFIG" | jq -r '.EncryptionConfig.KeyId // "N/A"')
if [ "$KEY_ID" != "N/A" ] && [ "$KEY_ID" != "null" ]; then
    KEY_DETAILS=$(aws kms describe-key --key-id "$KEY_ID" 2>/dev/null || echo '{}')
else
    KEY_DETAILS='{}'
fi

# Create audit report
jq -n \
    --argjson config "$ENCRYPTION_CONFIG" \
    --argjson key "$KEY_DETAILS" \
    --arg timestamp "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
    --arg region "$(aws configure get region)" \
    '{
        audit_timestamp: $timestamp,
        region: $region,
        encryption_config: $config.EncryptionConfig,
        kms_key_details: $key.KeyMetadata,
        compliance_status: (if $config.EncryptionConfig.Type == "KMS" then "COMPLIANT" else "REVIEW_REQUIRED" end)
    }' > "$REPORT_FILE"

echo "Audit report saved to: $REPORT_FILE"
cat "$REPORT_FILE"
Output: Generating X-Ray encryption audit report... Audit report saved to: xray-encryption-audit-20250118.json { "audit_timestamp": "2025-01-18T12:30:00Z", "region": "us-east-1", "encryption_config": { "KeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012", "Status": "ACTIVE", "Type": "KMS" }, "kms_key_details": { "KeyId": "12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012", "KeyState": "Enabled", "KeyUsage": "ENCRYPT_DECRYPT", "Description": "X-Ray trace encryption key for anihpj" }, "compliance_status": "COMPLIANT" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Audit report confirms encryption of: # - JobPost CRUD operation traces # - Skills M2M relationship data # - Author personal information # - User authentication flows # - Location geographic coordinates # # Compliance: GDPR, SOC 2, HIPAA ready
Example 4: Monitor encryption status changes

Check if encryption configuration is being updated and wait for completion.

#!/bin/bash
# Monitor encryption configuration status
MAX_WAIT=300  # 5 minutes max
INTERVAL=10

echo "Checking encryption configuration status..."

START_TIME=$(date +%s)
while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws xray get-encryption-config --query 'EncryptionConfig.Status' --output text)
    TYPE=$(aws xray get-encryption-config --query 'EncryptionConfig.Type' --output text)
    
    echo "$(date): Status=$STATUS, Type=$TYPE"
    
    if [ "$STATUS" == "ACTIVE" ]; then
        echo "✓ Encryption configuration is active"
        aws xray get-encryption-config
        break
    elif [ "$STATUS" == "UPDATING" ]; then
        ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START_TIME))
        if [ $ELAPSED -ge $MAX_WAIT ]; then
            echo "⚠ Timeout waiting for encryption update"
            break
        fi
        echo "  Waiting for update to complete..."
        sleep $INTERVAL
    else
        echo "⚠ Unexpected status: $STATUS"
        break
    fi
done
Output: Checking encryption configuration status... Sat Jan 18 12:35:00 UTC 2025: Status=UPDATING, Type=KMS Waiting for update to complete... Sat Jan 18 12:35:10 UTC 2025: Status=UPDATING, Type=KMS Waiting for update to complete... Sat Jan 18 12:35:20 UTC 2025: Status=ACTIVE, Type=KMS ✓ Encryption configuration is active { "EncryptionConfig": { "KeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/new-key-id", "Status": "ACTIVE", "Type": "KMS" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Encryption update monitoring: # - Started: KMS key rotation in progress # - Waited: 20 seconds for completion # - Result: New key active # # All Django traces now encrypted with new key: # - JobPost.objects operations # - Skills.objects queries # - Author/Location lookups # - User authentication events
Example 5: Compare encryption across multiple regions

Audit encryption configuration across all regions for multi-region Django deployment.

#!/bin/bash
# Check encryption across all regions with X-Ray traces
REGIONS=("us-east-1" "us-west-2" "eu-west-1" "ap-southeast-1")

echo "Multi-Region Encryption Audit for anihpj Django App"
echo "======================================================"

for region in "${REGIONS[@]}"; do
    echo ""
    echo "Region: $region"
    echo "--------"
    
    CONFIG=$(aws xray get-encryption-config --region "$region" 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        TYPE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.EncryptionConfig.Type')
        STATUS=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.EncryptionConfig.Status')
        KEY=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.EncryptionConfig.KeyId // "AWS Managed"')
        
        echo "  Type: $TYPE"
        echo "  Status: $STATUS"
        echo "  Key: $KEY"
        
        if [ "$TYPE" != "KMS" ]; then
            echo "  ⚠ WARNING: Not using CMK encryption"
        else
            echo "  ✓ Compliant"
        fi
    else
        echo "  No X-Ray data or access denied"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "Audit complete"
Output: Multi-Region Encryption Audit for anihpj Django App ====================================================== Region: us-east-1 -------- Type: KMS Status: ACTIVE Key: arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/key-east-1 ✓ Compliant Region: us-west-2 -------- Type: KMS Status: ACTIVE Key: arn:aws:kms:us-west-2:123456789012:key/key-west-2 ✓ Compliant Region: eu-west-1 -------- Type: NONE Status: ACTIVE Key: AWS Managed ⚠ WARNING: Not using CMK encryption Region: ap-southeast-1 -------- No X-Ray data or access denied Audit complete
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Multi-region encryption audit for anihpj: # # us-east-1 (primary): ✓ KMS encrypted # - Main JobPost API deployment # - Author/Location primary data # # us-west-2 (DR): ✓ KMS encrypted # - Disaster recovery replicas # - Skills cache replication # # eu-west-1: ⚠ Needs CMK for GDPR # - EU user data requires CMK # - Action: Enable KMS encryption # # ap-southeast-1: Not deployed yet

get-group

Retrieves group information for a specified X-Ray group. Use this to view filter expressions and settings for trace groups monitoring your Django application.

Syntax
aws xray get-group \
    [--group-name <value>] \
    [--group-arn <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --group-name (string): The case-sensitive name of the group
  • --group-arn (string): The ARN of the group to retrieve
Return Value
  • Group (object): Contains GroupName, GroupARN, FilterExpression, and InsightsConfiguration
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: Get group details by name

Retrieve configuration for the JobPost error monitoring group.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-group \
    --group-name "anihpj-jobpost-errors"
Output: { "Group": { "GroupName": "anihpj-jobpost-errors", "GroupARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-jobpost-errors", "FilterExpression": "annotation.model = \"JobPost\" AND error = true", "InsightsConfiguration": { "InsightsEnabled": true, "NotificationsEnabled": true } } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Group monitors JobPost errors: # - JobPost.objects.get(pk=invalid) → DoesNotExist # - JobPost.objects.create(invalid_data) → ValidationError # - JobPost.save() → IntegrityError # Insights enabled for anomaly detection
Example 2: Get group by ARN

Retrieve group configuration using its Amazon Resource Name.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-group \
    --group-arn "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-slow-queries"
Output: { "Group": { "GroupName": "anihpj-slow-queries", "GroupARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-slow-queries", "FilterExpression": "responsetime > 2", "InsightsConfiguration": { "InsightsEnabled": true, "NotificationsEnabled": false } } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Group tracks slow queries (>2 seconds): # - JobPost.objects.all() without prefetch # - Skills.objects.filter() with complex joins # - Author lookups with N+1 patterns
Example 3: Verify group filter expression

Check if group filter correctly matches Django model operations.

#!/bin/bash
GROUP_NAME="anihpj-skills-operations"

# Get group details
GROUP=$(aws xray get-group --group-name "$GROUP_NAME")

echo "Group: $GROUP_NAME"
echo "Filter: $(echo "$GROUP" | jq -r '.Group.FilterExpression')"
echo "Insights: $(echo "$GROUP" | jq -r '.Group.InsightsConfiguration.InsightsEnabled')"

# Validate filter matches expected pattern
FILTER=$(echo "$GROUP" | jq -r '.Group.FilterExpression')
if [[ "$FILTER" == *"Skills"* ]]; then
    echo "✓ Filter correctly targets Skills model"
else
    echo "⚠ Filter may not capture Skills operations"
fi
Output: Group: anihpj-skills-operations Filter: annotation.model = "Skills" Insights: true ✓ Filter correctly targets Skills model
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Verified group captures Skills operations: # - Skills.objects.all() # - Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains=q) # - Skills.objects.create(name=name) # - jobpost.skills.add(), .remove(), .set()
Example 4: Compare group settings across environments

Retrieve and compare group configurations between staging and production.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Comparing X-Ray groups across environments..."

for env in "staging" "production"; do
    GROUP_NAME="anihpj-${env}-errors"
    echo ""
    echo "=== $env ==="
    
    GROUP=$(aws xray get-group --group-name "$GROUP_NAME" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "Filter: $(echo "$GROUP" | jq -r '.Group.FilterExpression')"
        echo "Insights: $(echo "$GROUP" | jq -r '.Group.InsightsConfiguration')"
    else
        echo "Group not found"
    fi
done
Output: Comparing X-Ray groups across environments... === staging === Filter: error = true Insights: {"InsightsEnabled":false,"NotificationsEnabled":false} === production === Filter: error = true AND responsetime > 0.5 Insights: {"InsightsEnabled":true,"NotificationsEnabled":true}
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Environment comparison: # Staging: Basic error capture # - All errors from JobPost, Skills, etc. # - No Insights (cost savings) # # Production: Enhanced monitoring # - Errors + slow responses (>0.5s) # - Insights enabled for anomaly detection # - Notifications for immediate alerts
Example 5: Generate group configuration report

Create a detailed report of group configuration for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
GROUP_NAME="anihpj-production-api"
REPORT_FILE="group-config-${GROUP_NAME}.json"

# Get comprehensive group information
GROUP=$(aws xray get-group --group-name "$GROUP_NAME")

# Get recent trace summaries for this group
TRACES=$(aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --sampling \
    --filter-expression "$(echo "$GROUP" | jq -r '.Group.FilterExpression')" \
    --query 'TracesProcessedCount')

# Generate report
jq -n \
    --argjson group "$GROUP" \
    --argjson traces "$TRACES" \
    --arg timestamp "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
    '{
        generated_at: $timestamp,
        group_config: $group.Group,
        recent_trace_count: $traces,
        status: "active"
    }' > "$REPORT_FILE"

echo "Report saved to: $REPORT_FILE"
cat "$REPORT_FILE"
Output: Report saved to: group-config-anihpj-production-api.json { "generated_at": "2025-01-18T13:00:00Z", "group_config": { "GroupName": "anihpj-production-api", "GroupARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-production-api", "FilterExpression": "service(id(name: \"django-jobpost-api\"))", "InsightsConfiguration": { "InsightsEnabled": true, "NotificationsEnabled": true } }, "recent_trace_count": 1250, "status": "active" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Group configuration report: # - Monitors: django-jobpost-api service # - Recent activity: 1250 traces/hour # - Insights: Enabled for anomaly detection # # Traced operations include: # - All JobPost CRUD operations # - Skills M2M relationship changes # - Author/Location lookups # - User authentication flows

get-groups

Retrieves all active X-Ray groups. Use this to list all trace filter groups configured for monitoring your anihpj Django application.

Syntax
aws xray get-groups \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from previous response
Return Value
  • Groups (array): List of group summaries with GroupName, GroupARN, and FilterExpression
  • NextToken (string): Pagination token for next page
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: List all X-Ray groups

Retrieve all trace filter groups for the Django application.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-groups
Output: { "Groups": [ { "GroupName": "Default", "GroupARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/Default", "FilterExpression": "" }, { "GroupName": "anihpj-jobpost-errors", "GroupARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-jobpost-errors", "FilterExpression": "annotation.model = \"JobPost\" AND error = true" }, { "GroupName": "anihpj-slow-queries", "GroupARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-slow-queries", "FilterExpression": "responsetime > 2" }, { "GroupName": "anihpj-skills-operations", "GroupARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-skills-operations", "FilterExpression": "annotation.model = \"Skills\"" } ] }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Active trace groups for anihpj: # 1. Default - all traces # 2. JobPost errors - JobPost.DoesNotExist, ValidationError # 3. Slow queries - Skills.objects.filter() > 2s # 4. Skills operations - Skills CRUD tracking
Example 2: List groups with pagination

Retrieve groups with pagination for large configurations.

#!/bin/bash
# Get first page of groups
RESULT=$(aws xray get-groups --query '{Groups: Groups[*].GroupName, NextToken: NextToken}')

echo "First page groups:"
echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.Groups[]'

# Check for more pages
NEXT_TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.NextToken // empty')
if [ -n "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
    echo "Fetching next page..."
    aws xray get-groups --next-token "$NEXT_TOKEN" --query 'Groups[*].GroupName'
fi
Output: First page groups: Default anihpj-jobpost-errors anihpj-slow-queries anihpj-skills-operations anihpj-author-traces anihpj-location-lookups
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Groups covering all Django models: # - JobPost errors and operations # - Skills M2M relationship tracking # - Author query monitoring # - Location lookup performance
Example 3: Filter groups by naming convention

Extract groups matching specific Django application patterns.

#!/bin/bash
# Get all groups and filter by naming pattern
aws xray get-groups | jq '.Groups[] | select(.GroupName | startswith("anihpj-")) | {
    name: .GroupName,
    filter: .FilterExpression,
    arn: .GroupARN
}'
Output: { "name": "anihpj-jobpost-errors", "filter": "annotation.model = \"JobPost\" AND error = true", "arn": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-jobpost-errors" } { "name": "anihpj-slow-queries", "filter": "responsetime > 2", "arn": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-slow-queries" } { "name": "anihpj-skills-operations", "filter": "annotation.model = \"Skills\"", "arn": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-skills-operations" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # anihpj-prefixed groups organize Django monitoring: # - Consistent naming for application traces # - Easy filtering in console and scripts # - Separate from other application traces
Example 4: Generate groups inventory report

Create a comprehensive inventory of all X-Ray groups with their configurations.

#!/bin/bash
# Generate groups inventory
REPORT_FILE="xray-groups-inventory-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

GROUPS=$(aws xray get-groups)

# Count groups by type
TOTAL=$(echo "$GROUPS" | jq '.Groups | length')
WITH_INSIGHTS=$(echo "$GROUPS" | jq '[.Groups[] | select(.InsightsConfiguration.InsightsEnabled == true)] | length')
ERROR_GROUPS=$(echo "$GROUPS" | jq '[.Groups[] | select(.FilterExpression | contains("error"))] | length')

# Create report
jq -n \
    --argjson groups "$GROUPS" \
    --arg total "$TOTAL" \
    --arg insights "$WITH_INSIGHTS" \
    --arg errors "$ERROR_GROUPS" \
    --arg timestamp "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
    '{
        generated: $timestamp,
        summary: {
            total_groups: ($total | tonumber),
            insights_enabled: ($insights | tonumber),
            error_monitoring: ($errors | tonumber)
        },
        groups: $groups.Groups
    }' > "$REPORT_FILE"

echo "Inventory saved to: $REPORT_FILE"
echo "Total: $TOTAL | Insights: $WITH_INSIGHTS | Error groups: $ERROR_GROUPS"
Output: Inventory saved to: xray-groups-inventory-20250118.json Total: 6 | Insights: 3 | Error groups: 2
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Groups inventory for anihpj: # Total: 6 groups configured # - 3 with Insights (anomaly detection) # - 2 monitoring errors specifically # # Coverage: JobPost, Skills, Author, Location # Missing: User authentication traces (add later)
Example 5: Validate groups have required configurations

Audit all groups to ensure production readiness with proper settings.

#!/bin/bash
echo "X-Ray Groups Audit Report"
echo "========================="

aws xray get-groups | jq -r '.Groups[] | @base64' | while read -r group; do
    _jq() {
        echo "$group" | base64 --decode | jq -r "${1}"
    }
    
    NAME=$(_jq '.GroupName')
    FILTER=$(_jq '.FilterExpression')
    INSIGHTS=$(_jq '.InsightsConfiguration.InsightsEnabled // false')
    NOTIFICATIONS=$(_jq '.InsightsConfiguration.NotificationsEnabled // false')
    
    echo ""
    echo "Group: $NAME"
    echo "  Filter: ${FILTER:-'(none)'}"
    
    # Validation checks
    ISSUES=0
    
    if [ "$NAME" != "Default" ] && [ -z "$FILTER" ]; then
        echo "  ⚠ WARNING: No filter expression"
        ((ISSUES++))
    fi
    
    if [[ "$NAME" == *"production"* ]] && [ "$INSIGHTS" != "true" ]; then
        echo "  ⚠ WARNING: Production group without Insights"
        ((ISSUES++))
    fi
    
    if [ "$INSIGHTS" == "true" ] && [ "$NOTIFICATIONS" != "true" ]; then
        echo "  ⚠ WARNING: Insights enabled but notifications disabled"
        ((ISSUES++))
    fi
    
    if [ $ISSUES -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "  ✓ Configuration OK"
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "Audit complete"
Output: X-Ray Groups Audit Report ========================= Group: Default Filter: (none) ✓ Configuration OK Group: anihpj-production-api Filter: service(id(name: "django-jobpost-api")) ✓ Configuration OK Group: anihpj-staging-debug Filter: error = true ⚠ WARNING: Insights enabled but notifications disabled Group: anihpj-skills-operations Filter: annotation.model = "Skills" ✓ Configuration OK Audit complete
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Audit findings for anihpj groups: # # ✓ Default: OK (no filter required) # ✓ production-api: Full monitoring enabled # ⚠ staging-debug: Enable notifications # ✓ skills-operations: Properly configured # # Action items: # 1. Enable notifications on staging-debug # 2. Consider adding User model group

get-indexing-rules

Retrieves the indexing rules for X-Ray trace data. Indexing rules control which trace attributes are indexed for searching.

Syntax
aws xray get-indexing-rules \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token for retrieving additional rules
Return Value
  • IndexingRules (array): List of indexing rules with Name, ModifiedAt, and Rule configuration
  • NextToken (string): Token for pagination
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: Get current indexing rules

View which trace attributes are indexed for Django application trace searches.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-indexing-rules
Output: { "IndexingRules": [ { "Name": "Default", "ModifiedAt": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "Rule": { "Probabilistic": { "DesiredSamplingPercentage": 50 } } } ] }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Indexing rules affect trace searchability: # - 50% sampling for indexing # - Searchable: model annotations, response times # - Used for finding JobPost, Skills traces
Example 2: Check indexing configuration for search optimization

Verify indexing rules support efficient trace searches for Django operations.

#!/bin/bash
# Get indexing rules and analyze configuration
RULES=$(aws xray get-indexing-rules)

echo "X-Ray Indexing Configuration"
echo "============================"

echo "$RULES" | jq -r '.IndexingRules[] | "Rule: \(.Name)\nSampling: \(.Rule.Probabilistic.DesiredSamplingPercentage)%\nModified: \(.ModifiedAt)\n"'

# Check if sampling is sufficient for debugging
SAMPLING=$(echo "$RULES" | jq -r '.IndexingRules[0].Rule.Probabilistic.DesiredSamplingPercentage')
if [ "$SAMPLING" -lt 50 ]; then
    echo "⚠ Low sampling rate may miss important traces"
else
    echo "✓ Sampling rate adequate for trace analysis"
fi
Output: X-Ray Indexing Configuration ============================ Rule: Default Sampling: 50% Modified: 2025-01-15T10:00:00Z ✓ Sampling rate adequate for trace analysis
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Indexing at 50% captures sufficient data for: # - JobPost query pattern analysis # - Skills M2M relationship debugging # - Author/Location lookup optimization # - Performance trend identification
Example 3: Monitor indexing rule changes

Track when indexing configuration was last modified for audit purposes.

#!/bin/bash
# Get indexing rules and check last modification
aws xray get-indexing-rules | jq -r '.IndexingRules[] | {
    name: .Name,
    modified_at: .ModifiedAt,
    days_since_change: ((now - (.ModifiedAt | fromdateiso8601)) / 86400 | floor),
    config: .Rule
}'
Output: { "name": "Default", "modified_at": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "days_since_change": 3, "config": { "Probabilistic": { "DesiredSamplingPercentage": 50 } } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Indexing rule audit: # - Last changed: 3 days ago # - Current config: 50% probabilistic sampling # - Stable configuration for Django traces # # Consider reviewing if search performance degrades
Example 4: Generate indexing configuration report

Create a detailed report of indexing rules for capacity planning.

#!/bin/bash
REPORT_FILE="xray-indexing-report-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

# Get indexing rules
RULES=$(aws xray get-indexing-rules)

# Estimate indexed trace volume (approximate)
TRACE_SUMMARIES=$(aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --query 'TracesProcessedCount' \
    --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

SAMPLING_RATE=$(echo "$RULES" | jq -r '.IndexingRules[0].Rule.Probabilistic.DesiredSamplingPercentage // 100')
ESTIMATED_INDEXED=$((TRACE_SUMMARIES * SAMPLING_RATE / 100))

# Generate report
jq -n \
    --argjson rules "$RULES" \
    --arg traces "$TRACE_SUMMARIES" \
    --arg indexed "$ESTIMATED_INDEXED" \
    --arg timestamp "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
    '{
        timestamp: $timestamp,
        indexing_rules: $rules.IndexingRules,
        hourly_traces: ($traces | tonumber),
        estimated_indexed: ($indexed | tonumber),
        storage_impact: "moderate"
    }' > "$REPORT_FILE"

echo "Report: $REPORT_FILE"
echo "Traces/hour: $TRACE_SUMMARIES"
echo "Indexed (est): $ESTIMATED_INDEXED"
Output: Report: xray-indexing-report-20250118.json Traces/hour: 2500 Indexed (est): 1250
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Indexing capacity for anihpj: # - Total traces: 2500/hour # - Indexed: 1250/hour (50%) # # Indexed traces searchable for: # - JobPost.objects queries # - Skills relationship changes # - Author/Location lookups # - Error pattern analysis
Example 5: Compare indexing rules with best practices

Validate indexing configuration against recommended settings for Django applications.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Indexing Rules Best Practices Check"
echo "===================================="

RULES=$(aws xray get-indexing-rules)

# Extract current settings
SAMPLING=$(echo "$RULES" | jq -r '.IndexingRules[0].Rule.Probabilistic.DesiredSamplingPercentage // 0')

echo "Current Configuration:"
echo "  Sampling Rate: ${SAMPLING}%"

# Best practices validation
echo ""
echo "Best Practices Check:"

# Check 1: Sampling rate
if [ "$SAMPLING" -ge 50 ]; then
    echo "  ✓ Sampling rate (${SAMPLING}%) - Good for debugging"
elif [ "$SAMPLING" -ge 25 ]; then
    echo "  ⚠ Sampling rate (${SAMPLING}%) - May miss some traces"
else
    echo "  ✗ Sampling rate (${SAMPLING}%) - Too low for effective debugging"
fi

# Check 2: Cost consideration
if [ "$SAMPLING" -le 75 ]; then
    echo "  ✓ Cost optimized - Not over-indexing"
else
    echo "  ⚠ High indexing rate may increase costs"
fi

# Recommendation
echo ""
echo "Recommendation for Django anihpj:"
if [ "$SAMPLING" -ge 25 ] && [ "$SAMPLING" -le 75 ]; then
    echo "  Current settings are optimal for production"
else
    echo "  Consider adjusting to 50% for balance"
fi
Output: Indexing Rules Best Practices Check ==================================== Current Configuration: Sampling Rate: 50% Best Practices Check: ✓ Sampling rate (50%) - Good for debugging ✓ Cost optimized - Not over-indexing Recommendation for Django anihpj: Current settings are optimal for production
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Best practices for anihpj indexing: # # ✓ 50% sampling rate - Optimal balance # - Sufficient for debugging JobPost queries # - Cost-effective for Skills M2M tracking # - Adequate for Author/Location analysis # # Recommendation: Maintain current settings # Review quarterly based on trace volume growth

get-insight

Retrieves detailed information about a specific X-Ray insight. Insights are automatically detected anomalies in your Django application's trace data.

Syntax
aws xray get-insight \
    --insight-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --insight-id (string, required): The unique identifier of the insight
Return Value
  • Insight (object): Contains InsightId, GroupARN, GroupName, RootCauseServiceId, Categories, State, StartTime, EndTime, Summary, and ClientRequestImpactStatistics
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: Get insight details

Retrieve details about a detected anomaly in the Django application.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-insight \
    --insight-id "insight-12345-abcde-67890"
Output: { "Insight": { "InsightId": "insight-12345-abcde-67890", "GroupARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-jobpost-errors", "GroupName": "anihpj-jobpost-errors", "RootCauseServiceId": { "Name": "PostgreSQL", "Type": "AWS::RDS::DBInstance" }, "Categories": ["FAULT"], "State": "ACTIVE", "StartTime": "2025-01-18T08:00:00Z", "Summary": "Increased error rate detected in PostgreSQL calls", "ClientRequestImpactStatistics": { "FaultCount": 150, "TotalCount": 1000 } } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Insight: PostgreSQL fault spike # Root cause: Database connection issues # Affected operations: # - JobPost.objects.all() → timeouts # - Skills.objects.filter() → connection errors # - Author.objects.get() → intermittent failures # 15% error rate (150/1000 requests)
Example 2: Get insight with root cause analysis

Retrieve detailed root cause information for Django application anomaly.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-jobpost-latency-001"

# Get full insight details
aws xray get-insight --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" | jq '{
    id: .Insight.InsightId,
    state: .Insight.State,
    category: .Insight.Categories[0],
    root_cause: .Insight.RootCauseServiceId,
    summary: .Insight.Summary,
    impact: .Insight.ClientRequestImpactStatistics,
    timeline: {
        started: .Insight.StartTime,
        ended: .Insight.EndTime
    }
}'
Output: { "id": "insight-jobpost-latency-001", "state": "CLOSED", "category": "FAULT", "root_cause": { "Name": "PostgreSQL::jobpost_db", "Type": "AWS::RDS::DBInstance", "AccountId": "123456789012" }, "summary": "Increased latency in PostgreSQL calls affecting JobPost queries", "impact": { "FaultCount": 450, "OkCount": 4550, "TotalCount": 5000, "TotalResponseTime": 12500.5 }, "timeline": { "started": "2025-01-18T08:00:00Z", "ended": "2025-01-18T09:30:00Z" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Root cause: PostgreSQL::jobpost_db # Duration: 1.5 hours (08:00 - 09:30) # Impact: 9% error rate (450/5000) # # Affected Django operations: # - JobPost.objects.all() - slow queries # - JobPost.objects.get(pk=id) - timeouts # - jobpost.skills.all() - M2M join delays
Example 3: Monitor active insight status

Track an ongoing anomaly affecting Django application performance.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-skills-error-spike"

echo "Monitoring insight: $INSIGHT_ID"
echo "================================"

while true; do
    INSIGHT=$(aws xray get-insight --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID")
    
    STATE=$(echo "$INSIGHT" | jq -r '.Insight.State')
    FAULTS=$(echo "$INSIGHT" | jq -r '.Insight.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.FaultCount')
    TOTAL=$(echo "$INSIGHT" | jq -r '.Insight.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.TotalCount')
    
    ERROR_RATE=$(echo "scale=2; $FAULTS * 100 / $TOTAL" | bc)
    
    echo "$(date): State=$STATE, Errors=$FAULTS/$TOTAL (${ERROR_RATE}%)"
    
    if [ "$STATE" == "CLOSED" ]; then
        echo "Insight resolved!"
        break
    fi
    
    sleep 60
done
Output: Monitoring insight: insight-skills-error-spike ================================ Sat Jan 18 10:00:00 UTC 2025: State=ACTIVE, Errors=120/1000 (12.00%) Sat Jan 18 10:01:00 UTC 2025: State=ACTIVE, Errors=95/1200 (7.92%) Sat Jan 18 10:02:00 UTC 2025: State=ACTIVE, Errors=45/1400 (3.21%) Sat Jan 18 10:03:00 UTC 2025: State=CLOSED, Errors=50/1800 (2.78%) Insight resolved!
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Insight monitoring for Skills errors: # - Peak error rate: 12% at 10:00 # - Gradually recovered over 3 minutes # - Resolved at 2.78% (within normal range) # # Affected operations during incident: # - Skills.objects.filter() failures # - jobpost.skills.add() timeouts # - M2M relationship validation errors
Example 4: Generate incident report from insight

Create a detailed incident report for a Django application anomaly.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-author-timeout-002"
REPORT_FILE="incident-report-${INSIGHT_ID}.json"

# Get comprehensive insight data
INSIGHT=$(aws xray get-insight --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID")

# Calculate duration
START=$(echo "$INSIGHT" | jq -r '.Insight.StartTime')
END=$(echo "$INSIGHT" | jq -r '.Insight.EndTime // "ongoing"')

# Generate incident report
jq -n \
    --argjson insight "$INSIGHT" \
    --arg report_time "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
    '{
        incident_report: {
            generated_at: $report_time,
            insight_id: $insight.Insight.InsightId,
            severity: (if $insight.Insight.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.FaultCount > 100 then "HIGH" else "MEDIUM" end),
            status: $insight.Insight.State,
            timeline: {
                detected: $insight.Insight.StartTime,
                resolved: ($insight.Insight.EndTime // "ongoing")
            },
            root_cause: {
                service: $insight.Insight.RootCauseServiceId.Name,
                type: $insight.Insight.RootCauseServiceId.Type
            },
            impact: {
                total_requests: $insight.Insight.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.TotalCount,
                failed_requests: $insight.Insight.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.FaultCount,
                error_rate_percent: (($insight.Insight.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.FaultCount / $insight.Insight.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.TotalCount) * 100)
            },
            summary: $insight.Insight.Summary
        }
    }' > "$REPORT_FILE"

echo "Incident report saved: $REPORT_FILE"
cat "$REPORT_FILE" | jq '.'
Output: Incident report saved: incident-report-insight-author-timeout-002.json { "incident_report": { "generated_at": "2025-01-18T14:00:00Z", "insight_id": "insight-author-timeout-002", "severity": "HIGH", "status": "CLOSED", "timeline": { "detected": "2025-01-18T11:00:00Z", "resolved": "2025-01-18T12:30:00Z" }, "root_cause": { "service": "PostgreSQL::jobpost_db", "type": "AWS::RDS::DBInstance" }, "impact": { "total_requests": 3000, "failed_requests": 450, "error_rate_percent": 15 }, "summary": "Author lookup timeouts due to database connection pool exhaustion" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Incident Report: Author Timeout # Severity: HIGH (450 failures) # Duration: 1.5 hours # # Root cause: Connection pool exhaustion # Affected queries: # - Author.objects.get(pk=id) # - JobPost.objects.select_related('author') # - User lookups via Author.user # # Resolution: Increased connection pool size # Prevention: Add connection pooling with pgbouncer
Example 5: Compare insights for pattern analysis

Analyze multiple insights to identify recurring issues in Django application.

#!/bin/bash
# Get recent insight IDs from summaries
INSIGHT_IDS=(
    "insight-jobpost-001"
    "insight-skills-002"
    "insight-author-003"
)

echo "Insight Pattern Analysis for anihpj"
echo "===================================="
echo ""

declare -A ROOT_CAUSES
declare -A CATEGORIES

for id in "${INSIGHT_IDS[@]}"; do
    INSIGHT=$(aws xray get-insight --insight-id "$id" 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        ROOT_CAUSE=$(echo "$INSIGHT" | jq -r '.Insight.RootCauseServiceId.Name')
        CATEGORY=$(echo "$INSIGHT" | jq -r '.Insight.Categories[0]')
        FAULTS=$(echo "$INSIGHT" | jq -r '.Insight.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.FaultCount')
        
        echo "Insight: $id"
        echo "  Root Cause: $ROOT_CAUSE"
        echo "  Category: $CATEGORY"
        echo "  Faults: $FAULTS"
        echo ""
        
        # Track patterns
        ((ROOT_CAUSES[$ROOT_CAUSE]++))
        ((CATEGORIES[$CATEGORY]++))
    fi
done

echo "Pattern Summary:"
echo "  Most common root cause: PostgreSQL (${ROOT_CAUSES[PostgreSQL]:-0} incidents)"
echo "  Most common category: FAULT (${CATEGORIES[FAULT]:-0} incidents)"
echo ""
echo "Recommendation: Focus optimization on database queries"
Output: Insight Pattern Analysis for anihpj ==================================== Insight: insight-jobpost-001 Root Cause: PostgreSQL::jobpost_db Category: FAULT Faults: 150 Insight: insight-skills-002 Root Cause: PostgreSQL::jobpost_db Category: FAULT Faults: 95 Insight: insight-author-003 Root Cause: Redis::cache Category: FAULT Faults: 45 Pattern Summary: Most common root cause: PostgreSQL (2 incidents) Most common category: FAULT (3 incidents) Recommendation: Focus optimization on database queries
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Insight pattern analysis reveals: # # PostgreSQL issues (2 incidents): # - JobPost.objects.all() performance # - Skills.objects.filter() timeouts # Recommendation: Add select_related(), prefetch_related() # # Redis issues (1 incident): # - Cache misses during high load # Recommendation: Increase cache TTL for Skills # # Overall: Database optimization priority

get-insight-events

Retrieves events related to an X-Ray insight. Events track the lifecycle of an anomaly detection in your Django application.

Syntax
aws xray get-insight-events \
    --insight-id <value> \
    [--max-results <value>] \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --insight-id (string, required): The unique identifier of the insight
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of events to return (1-100)
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token
Return Value
  • InsightEvents (array): List of events with Summary, EventTime, ClientRequestImpactStatistics, and RootCauseServiceRequestImpactStatistics
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: Get insight event timeline

Track the progression of an anomaly affecting Django operations.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-insight-events \
    --insight-id "insight-12345-abcde-67890" \
    --max-results 10
Output: { "InsightEvents": [ { "Summary": "Anomaly detected: Error rate increased to 15%", "EventTime": "2025-01-18T08:00:00Z", "ClientRequestImpactStatistics": { "FaultCount": 50, "TotalCount": 500 } }, { "Summary": "Error rate peaked at 25%", "EventTime": "2025-01-18T08:15:00Z", "ClientRequestImpactStatistics": { "FaultCount": 125, "TotalCount": 500 } }, { "Summary": "Error rate declining to 10%", "EventTime": "2025-01-18T08:30:00Z", "ClientRequestImpactStatistics": { "FaultCount": 50, "TotalCount": 500 } } ] }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Anomaly timeline for JobPost operations: # 08:00 - Error spike detected (15%) # JobPost.objects.create() failures # 08:15 - Peak errors (25%) # Database connection pool exhausted # 08:30 - Recovery (10%) # Connection pool reset, queries resuming
Example 2: Export insight events for incident report

Generate a formatted timeline for post-incident analysis.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-12345-abcde-67890"

aws xray get-insight-events \
    --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" \
    --max-results 100 | jq -r '
    .InsightEvents | sort_by(.EventTime) | .[] |
    "\(.EventTime | split("T")[1] | split("Z")[0]) | \(.Summary) | Faults: \(.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.FaultCount)/\(.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.TotalCount)"
' | while read -r line; do
    echo "$(echo "$line" | cut -d'|' -f1) - $(echo "$line" | cut -d'|' -f2-)"
done
Output: 08:00:00 - Anomaly detected: Error rate increased to 15% | Faults: 50/500 08:05:00 - Error rate climbing to 18% | Faults: 72/400 08:10:00 - Error rate spiked to 22% | Faults: 110/500 08:15:00 - Error rate peaked at 25% | Faults: 125/500 08:20:00 - Error rate stabilizing at 20% | Faults: 80/400 08:25:00 - Error rate declining to 15% | Faults: 60/400 08:30:00 - Error rate declining to 10% | Faults: 50/500 08:35:00 - Anomaly resolved: Error rate normal | Faults: 15/500
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Incident timeline for anihpj database issue: # Duration: 35 minutes # Peak: 25% error rate at 08:15 # Total affected requests: ~3700 # Total faults: ~562 # # Affected Django operations during incident: # - JobPost.objects.create() - bulk insert failures # - Skills.objects.get_or_create() - deadlocks
Example 3: Calculate mean time to recovery (MTTR)

Analyze insight events to measure incident duration and recovery time.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-12345-abcde-67890"

EVENTS=$(aws xray get-insight-events \
    --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" \
    --max-results 100)

# Get first and last event times
FIRST_EVENT=$(echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '.InsightEvents | sort_by(.EventTime) | first | .EventTime')
LAST_EVENT=$(echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '.InsightEvents | sort_by(.EventTime) | last | .EventTime')

# Calculate duration
START_SEC=$(date -d "$FIRST_EVENT" +%s)
END_SEC=$(date -d "$LAST_EVENT" +%s)
DURATION_MIN=$(( (END_SEC - START_SEC) / 60 ))

echo "Incident Analysis:"
echo "  Start: $FIRST_EVENT"
echo "  End: $LAST_EVENT"
echo "  Duration: $DURATION_MIN minutes"

# Find peak error time
echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '
    .InsightEvents |
    max_by(.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.FaultCount) |
    "  Peak: \(.EventTime) (\(.ClientRequestImpactStatistics.FaultCount) faults)"'
Output: Incident Analysis: Start: 2025-01-18T08:00:00Z End: 2025-01-18T08:35:00Z Duration: 35 minutes Peak: 2025-01-18T08:15:00Z (125 faults)
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # MTTR analysis for anihpj: # - Detection: Immediate (X-Ray Insights) # - Peak impact: 15 minutes after start # - Full recovery: 35 minutes total # # Django queries affected: # JobPost.objects.filter(author__username=user).select_related() # Skills.objects.filter(jobpost__location__city='NYC')
Example 4: Monitor insight events in real-time

Set up continuous monitoring of an active insight's progression.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-12345-abcde-67890"
SEEN_EVENTS=""

echo "Monitoring insight: $INSIGHT_ID"
echo "Press Ctrl+C to stop"
echo "---"

while true; do
    EVENTS=$(aws xray get-insight-events \
        --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" \
        --max-results 20 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "Error fetching events, retrying..."
        sleep 30
        continue
    fi
    
    echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '.InsightEvents[] | "\(.EventTime)|\(.Summary)"' | while IFS='|' read -r time summary; do
        EVENT_KEY="$time"
        if [[ ! "$SEEN_EVENTS" =~ "$EVENT_KEY" ]]; then
            echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] NEW: $time - $summary"
            SEEN_EVENTS="$SEEN_EVENTS $EVENT_KEY"
        fi
    done
    
    sleep 60
done
Output: Monitoring insight: insight-12345-abcde-67890 Press Ctrl+C to stop --- [14:00:05] NEW: 2025-01-18T14:00:00Z - Anomaly detected: Error rate increased [14:01:06] NEW: 2025-01-18T14:01:00Z - Error rate climbing to 12% [14:05:07] NEW: 2025-01-18T14:05:00Z - Error rate stabilizing at 15%
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Real-time monitoring captures: # - JobPost query degradation events # - Skills relationship failures # - Author lookup timeouts # # Use for immediate incident response: # When "Error rate climbing" appears, check: # - Django connection pool status # - PostgreSQL active connections
Example 5: Correlate insight events with deployment

Compare insight event timing with deployment history to identify root cause.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-12345-abcde-67890"

# Get insight events
EVENTS=$(aws xray get-insight-events \
    --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" \
    --max-results 50)

# Get insight start time
INSIGHT_START=$(echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '.InsightEvents | sort_by(.EventTime) | first | .EventTime')

# Check for recent deployments (CodeDeploy)
DEPLOY_TIME=$(aws deploy list-deployments \
    --application-name anihpj-django \
    --deployment-group-name production \
    --query 'deployments[0]' \
    --output text 2>/dev/null)

echo "Insight Analysis:"
echo "  Insight started: $INSIGHT_START"
echo "  Recent deployment: $DEPLOY_TIME"

# Show events around deployment
echo ""
echo "Events timeline:"
echo "$EVENTS" | jq -r '
    .InsightEvents | sort_by(.EventTime) | .[:5][] |
    "  \(.EventTime): \(.Summary)"'
Output: Insight Analysis: Insight started: 2025-01-18T08:00:00Z Recent deployment: d-ABCDEFGHI Events timeline: 2025-01-18T08:00:00Z: Anomaly detected: Error rate increased 2025-01-18T08:02:00Z: Error rate climbing 2025-01-18T08:05:00Z: New error pattern: Database timeout 2025-01-18T08:10:00Z: Error rate peaked 2025-01-18T08:15:00Z: Error rate declining
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Root cause correlation: # - Deployment completed at 07:55 # - Insight detected at 08:00 # - Likely cause: New migration or query change # # Check recent changes to: # - JobPost model fields # - Skills M2M through table # - Database indexes

get-insight-impact-graph

Retrieves the impact graph for an insight showing affected services. This visualizes how an anomaly propagates through your Django application stack.

Syntax
aws xray get-insight-impact-graph \
    --insight-id <value> \
    --start-time <value> \
    --end-time <value> \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --insight-id (string, required): The unique identifier of the insight
  • --start-time (timestamp, required): Start of the time range
  • --end-time (timestamp, required): End of the time range
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token
Return Value
  • InsightId (string): The insight identifier
  • StartTime/EndTime (timestamp): Time range of the graph
  • Services (array): List of affected services with impact statistics
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: Get impact visualization

View how a database anomaly affected the entire Django application stack.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-insight-impact-graph \
    --insight-id "insight-12345-abcde-67890" \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s)
Output: { "InsightId": "insight-12345-abcde-67890", "StartTime": "2025-01-18T08:00:00Z", "EndTime": "2025-01-18T09:00:00Z", "Services": [ { "Name": "anihpj-django", "Type": "AWS::EC2::Instance", "ReferenceId": 0, "Edges": [ {"ReferenceId": 1}, {"ReferenceId": 2} ] }, { "Name": "PostgreSQL", "Type": "AWS::RDS::DBInstance", "ReferenceId": 1, "AverageResponseTime": 2.5 }, { "Name": "Redis", "Type": "AWS::ElastiCache::Cluster", "ReferenceId": 2, "AverageResponseTime": 0.01 } ] }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Impact graph shows: # Django App (anihpj) # ├── PostgreSQL (IMPACTED - 2.5s avg) # │ - JobPost.objects queries slow # │ - Skills.objects.filter() affected # └── Redis (healthy - 10ms) # - Cache operations normal # Root cause: PostgreSQL performance degradation
Example 2: Export impact graph as visualization data

Generate data for creating service dependency diagrams.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-12345-abcde-67890"
OUTPUT_FILE="impact-graph-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

GRAPH=$(aws xray get-insight-impact-graph \
    --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" \
    --start-time $(date -d '2 hours ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s))

# Transform to visualization format
echo "$GRAPH" | jq '{
    nodes: [.Services[] | {
        id: .ReferenceId,
        name: .Name,
        type: .Type,
        responseTime: .AverageResponseTime,
        impacted: (.AverageResponseTime > 1)
    }],
    edges: [.Services[] | .Edges[]? | {
        source: .ReferenceId,
        target: .ReferenceId
    }]
}' > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Graph exported to: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Impacted services:"
echo "$GRAPH" | jq -r '.Services[] | select(.AverageResponseTime > 1) | "  - \(.Name): \(.AverageResponseTime)s avg"'
Output: Graph exported to: impact-graph-20250118.json Impacted services: - PostgreSQL: 2.5s avg - External-API: 3.2s avg
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Impact visualization for anihpj: # Node: anihpj-django (healthy) # Node: PostgreSQL (IMPACTED - 2.5s) # - JobPost queries delayed # Node: External-API (IMPACTED - 3.2s) # - Location geocoding service slow # Node: Redis (healthy - 10ms)
Example 3: Analyze impact propagation path

Trace how an issue spreads through the Django service architecture.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-12345-abcde-67890"

aws xray get-insight-impact-graph \
    --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) | jq -r '
# Build adjacency map
.Services as $services |
[$services[] | {(.ReferenceId | tostring): .Name}] | add as $names |

# Find root cause (service with no incoming edges)
($services | map(.ReferenceId) | unique) as $all_refs |
([$services[].Edges[]?.ReferenceId] | unique) as $targets |
($all_refs - $targets)[0] as $root |

# Print propagation tree
"Impact Propagation Path:",
"",
($services[] | select(.ReferenceId == $root) | "ROOT: \(.Name) (\(.Type))"),
"",
($services[] | select(.Edges != null) | 
    "  \(.Name) → " + ([$names[(.Edges[].ReferenceId | tostring)]] | join(", ")))'
Output: Impact Propagation Path: ROOT: PostgreSQL (AWS::RDS::DBInstance) anihpj-django → PostgreSQL, Redis, External-API PostgreSQL → (no downstream) External-API → Location-Service
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Propagation analysis: # 1. PostgreSQL (ROOT CAUSE) # ↓ # 2. anihpj-django (affected) # - JobPost.objects.all() blocked # - Skills.objects.filter() queued # ↓ # 3. Downstream services timing out # - Location lookups failing
Example 4: Compare impact graphs over time

Analyze how the impact scope changed during an incident.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-12345-abcde-67890"
END_TIME=$(date +%s)
START_TIME=$(date -d '2 hours ago' +%s)

# Get impact at start of incident
EARLY_GRAPH=$(aws xray get-insight-impact-graph \
    --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" \
    --start-time $START_TIME \
    --end-time $(($START_TIME + 1800)))

# Get impact at peak
PEAK_GRAPH=$(aws xray get-insight-impact-graph \
    --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" \
    --start-time $(($START_TIME + 1800)) \
    --end-time $(($START_TIME + 3600)))

# Get impact at recovery
LATE_GRAPH=$(aws xray get-insight-impact-graph \
    --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" \
    --start-time $(($END_TIME - 1800)) \
    --end-time $END_TIME)

echo "Impact Timeline Analysis:"
echo ""
echo "Early (first 30min):"
echo "$EARLY_GRAPH" | jq -r '.Services | length | "  Services affected: \(.)"'
echo "$EARLY_GRAPH" | jq -r '[.Services[].AverageResponseTime // 0] | add/length | "  Avg response time: \(. | round)s"'

echo ""
echo "Peak (30-60min):"
echo "$PEAK_GRAPH" | jq -r '.Services | length | "  Services affected: \(.)"'
echo "$PEAK_GRAPH" | jq -r '[.Services[].AverageResponseTime // 0] | add/length | "  Avg response time: \(. | round)s"'

echo ""
echo "Recovery (last 30min):"
echo "$LATE_GRAPH" | jq -r '.Services | length | "  Services affected: \(.)"'
echo "$LATE_GRAPH" | jq -r '[.Services[].AverageResponseTime // 0] | add/length | "  Avg response time: \(. | round)s"'
Output: Impact Timeline Analysis: Early (first 30min): Services affected: 2 Avg response time: 1s Peak (30-60min): Services affected: 5 Avg response time: 3s Recovery (last 30min): Services affected: 2 Avg response time: 0s
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Impact progression for anihpj: # # Early: Database starting to degrade # - JobPost queries: 1s avg # # Peak: Cascading failures # - All models affected: 3s avg # - JobPost, Skills, Author, Location, User # # Recovery: Normal operations # - Response times normalized
Example 5: Generate impact report for stakeholders

Create a comprehensive impact summary for post-incident review.

#!/bin/bash
INSIGHT_ID="insight-12345-abcde-67890"
REPORT_FILE="impact-report-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M).md"

GRAPH=$(aws xray get-insight-impact-graph \
    --insight-id "$INSIGHT_ID" \
    --start-time $(date -d '2 hours ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s))

# Generate markdown report
{
    echo "# Incident Impact Report"
    echo ""
    echo "**Insight ID:** $INSIGHT_ID"
    echo "**Generated:** $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
    echo ""
    echo "## Affected Services"
    echo ""
    echo "| Service | Type | Avg Response Time | Status |"
    echo "|---------|------|-------------------|--------|"
    
    echo "$GRAPH" | jq -r '.Services[] | 
        "| \(.Name) | \(.Type) | \(.AverageResponseTime // "N/A")s | \(if .AverageResponseTime > 1 then "🔴 IMPACTED" else "🟢 Healthy" end) |"'
    
    echo ""
    echo "## Service Dependencies"
    echo ""
    echo "\`\`\`"
    echo "$GRAPH" | jq -r '.Services[] | select(.Edges) | "\(.Name) depends on: \([.Edges[].ReferenceId] | join(", "))"'
    echo "\`\`\`"
    
    echo ""
    echo "## Summary"
    IMPACTED=$(echo "$GRAPH" | jq '[.Services[] | select(.AverageResponseTime > 1)] | length')
    TOTAL=$(echo "$GRAPH" | jq '.Services | length')
    echo "- **Total services:** $TOTAL"
    echo "- **Impacted services:** $IMPACTED"
} > "$REPORT_FILE"

echo "Report generated: $REPORT_FILE"
cat "$REPORT_FILE"
Output: Report generated: impact-report-20250118-1400.md # Incident Impact Report **Insight ID:** insight-12345-abcde-67890 **Generated:** 2025-01-18T14:00:00Z ## Affected Services | Service | Type | Avg Response Time | Status | |---------|------|-------------------|--------| | anihpj-django | AWS::EC2::Instance | 0.5s | 🟢 Healthy | | PostgreSQL | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | 2.5s | 🔴 IMPACTED | | Redis | AWS::ElastiCache::Cluster | 0.01s | 🟢 Healthy | ## Summary - **Total services:** 3 - **Impacted services:** 1
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Impact report for anihpj application: # # Healthy services: # - Django app server # - Redis cache # # Impacted services: # - PostgreSQL (2.5s response) # # Business impact: # - JobPost creation/updates delayed # - Skills search slower # - User experience degraded

get-insight-summaries

Retrieves summaries of all active and recent insights for your X-Ray groups. Use this to get an overview of anomalies detected in your Django application.

Syntax
aws xray get-insight-summaries \
    --start-time <value> \
    --end-time <value> \
    [--group-arn <value>] \
    [--group-name <value>] \
    [--states <value>] \
    [--max-results <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --start-time (timestamp, required): Beginning of the time range for insights
  • --end-time (timestamp, required): End of the time range
  • --group-arn (string): ARN of the group to filter insights
  • --group-name (string): Name of the group to filter insights
  • --states (list): Filter by state: ACTIVE or CLOSED
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum results to return (1-100)
Return Value
  • InsightSummaries (array): List of insight summaries with InsightId, GroupARN, RootCauseServiceId, Categories, State, StartTime, EndTime, and Summary
5 Detailed X-Ray Examples
Example 1: Get all active insights

Retrieve currently active anomalies for the Django application.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-insight-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '24 hours ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --states ACTIVE
Output: { "InsightSummaries": [ { "InsightId": "insight-12345-abcde", "GroupName": "anihpj-jobpost-errors", "RootCauseServiceId": { "Name": "PostgreSQL" }, "Categories": ["FAULT"], "State": "ACTIVE", "StartTime": "2025-01-18T08:00:00Z", "Summary": "Increased fault rate in database calls" }, { "InsightId": "insight-67890-fghij", "GroupName": "anihpj-slow-queries", "RootCauseServiceId": { "Name": "anihpj-django" }, "Categories": ["LATENCY"], "State": "ACTIVE", "Summary": "Response time degradation detected" } ] }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Active insights for anihpj: # 1. Database faults - JobPost.objects queries failing # 2. Latency issues - Skills.objects.filter() slow # Affected operations: # - JobPost.objects.select_related('author') # - Skills.objects.prefetch_related('jobpost_set')
Example 2: Filter insights by group

Retrieve insights for a specific monitoring group.

#!/bin/bash
GROUP_NAME="anihpj-production"

aws xray get-insight-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '7 days ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --group-name "$GROUP_NAME" \
    --max-results 20 | jq '.InsightSummaries[] | {
        id: .InsightId,
        state: .State,
        category: .Categories[0],
        rootCause: .RootCauseServiceId.Name,
        started: .StartTime,
        summary: .Summary
    }'
Output: { "id": "insight-12345-abcde", "state": "CLOSED", "category": "FAULT", "rootCause": "PostgreSQL", "started": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "summary": "Database connection failures" } { "id": "insight-67890-fghij", "state": "CLOSED", "category": "LATENCY", "rootCause": "External-API", "started": "2025-01-16T14:30:00Z", "summary": "Slow response from geocoding service" } { "id": "insight-11111-kkkkk", "state": "ACTIVE", "category": "FAULT", "rootCause": "anihpj-django", "started": "2025-01-18T08:00:00Z", "summary": "Increased error rate in API endpoints" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # anihpj-production insights (7 days): # # Jan 15: Database faults # - JobPost.objects bulk operations failed # # Jan 16: External API latency # - Location.objects geocoding slow # # Jan 18: Current - API errors # - REST endpoints returning 500s
Example 3: Generate weekly insight report

Create a summary of all insights for the past week.

#!/bin/bash
REPORT_FILE="xray-insights-weekly-$(date +%Y%m%d).txt"

SUMMARIES=$(aws xray get-insight-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '7 days ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --max-results 100)

{
    echo "X-Ray Insights Weekly Report"
    echo "Generated: $(date)"
    echo "================================"
    echo ""
    
    # Count by state
    ACTIVE=$(echo "$SUMMARIES" | jq '[.InsightSummaries[] | select(.State == "ACTIVE")] | length')
    CLOSED=$(echo "$SUMMARIES" | jq '[.InsightSummaries[] | select(.State == "CLOSED")] | length')
    
    echo "Summary:"
    echo "  Active insights: $ACTIVE"
    echo "  Resolved insights: $CLOSED"
    echo ""
    
    # Count by category
    echo "By Category:"
    echo "$SUMMARIES" | jq -r '.InsightSummaries | group_by(.Categories[0]) | .[] | "  \(.[0].Categories[0]): \(length)"'
    echo ""
    
    # List insights
    echo "Insights Detail:"
    echo "$SUMMARIES" | jq -r '.InsightSummaries | sort_by(.StartTime) | .[] |
        "  [\(.State)] \(.StartTime | split("T")[0]) - \(.Summary) (Root: \(.RootCauseServiceId.Name))"'
} > "$REPORT_FILE"

cat "$REPORT_FILE"
Output: X-Ray Insights Weekly Report Generated: Sat Jan 18 14:00:00 UTC 2025 ================================ Summary: Active insights: 1 Resolved insights: 4 By Category: FAULT: 3 LATENCY: 2 Insights Detail: [CLOSED] 2025-01-12 - Memory pressure detected (Root: anihpj-django) [CLOSED] 2025-01-14 - Database connection pool exhausted (Root: PostgreSQL) [CLOSED] 2025-01-15 - Slow query response times (Root: PostgreSQL) [CLOSED] 2025-01-16 - External service timeout (Root: External-API) [ACTIVE] 2025-01-18 - Increased API error rate (Root: anihpj-django)
