        "RestoreTestingSelectionName": "dynamodb-cache-tables",
        "ProtectedResourceType": "DynamoDB",
        "ProtectedResourceArns": [
            "arn:aws:dynamodb:us-east-1:123456789012:table/jobpost-cache",
            "arn:aws:dynamodb:us-east-1:123456789012:table/skills-cache"
        ],
        "IamRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSBackupRestoreTestRole"
    }'
Output: { "RestoreTestingSelectionName": "dynamodb-cache-tables", "RestoreTestingPlanName": "anihpj-monthly-dr-drill" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills # DynamoDB cache restore testing # Validates JobPost and Skills query cache recovery
Example 4: Select all tagged resources

Test restore for all backup-tagged resources.

aws backup create-restore-testing-selection \
    --restore-testing-plan-name "anihpj-quarterly-compliance" \
    --restore-testing-selection '{
        "RestoreTestingSelectionName": "all-tagged-resources",
        "ProtectedResourceType": "EC2",
        "ProtectedResourceConditions": {
            "StringEquals": [{"Key": "BackupTest", "Value": "true"}]
        },
        "IamRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSBackupRestoreTestRole",
        "RestoreMetadataOverrides": {
            "InstanceType": "t2.micro"
        }
    }'
Output: { "RestoreTestingSelectionName": "all-tagged-resources", "RestoreTestingPlanName": "anihpj-quarterly-compliance" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Author, Location # EC2 restore testing for Django application servers # Validates entire application stack recovery
Example 5: Select with validation window

Test restore with specific validation parameters.

aws backup create-restore-testing-selection \
    --restore-testing-plan-name "anihpj-cross-region-test" \
    --restore-testing-selection '{
        "RestoreTestingSelectionName": "dr-validation",
        "ProtectedResourceType": "RDS",
        "ProtectedResourceArns": ["arn:aws:rds:us-west-2:123456789012:db:jobpost-dr"],
        "IamRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSBackupRestoreTestRole",
        "ValidationWindowHours": 2
    }'
Output: { "RestoreTestingSelectionName": "dr-validation", "RestoreTestingPlanName": "anihpj-cross-region-test" }
Django Context: # DR restore validation in us-west-2 # 2-hour window to verify JobPost data integrity # Tests all models: Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost

create-tiering-configuration

Creates a tiering configuration to automatically move recovery points to cold storage.

Syntax
aws backup create-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name <value> \
    --tiering-policy <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the backup vault
  • --tiering-policy (JSON): Policy defining when to tier to cold storage
Return Value
  • Returns backup vault name and tiering policy
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Tier to cold storage after 30 days

Reduce storage costs by moving old backups to cold tier.

aws backup create-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-prod-vault" \
    --tiering-policy '{
        "DaysAfterCreation": 30,
        "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 30
    }'
Output: { "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-prod-vault", "TieringPolicy": { "DaysAfterCreation": 30, "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 30 } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # JobPost backups older than 30 days move to cold storage # Reduces costs while maintaining long-term retention
Example 2: Tier compliance data after 90 days

Long-term archival for compliance requirements.

aws backup create-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-compliance-vault" \
    --tiering-policy '{
        "DaysAfterCreation": 90,
        "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 90
    }'
Output: { "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-compliance-vault", "TieringPolicy": { "DaysAfterCreation": 90, "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 90 } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Author # User and Author compliance data archived after 90 days # Meets regulatory retention with cost optimization
Example 3: Quick tiering for development backups

Tier development backups after 7 days.

aws backup create-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-dev-vault" \
    --tiering-policy '{
        "DaysAfterCreation": 7,
        "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 7
    }'
Output: { "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-dev-vault", "TieringPolicy": { "DaysAfterCreation": 7, "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 7 } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, Location # Dev environment: Skills and Location test data tiered quickly # Minimizes storage costs for non-critical backups
Example 4: Aggressive tiering for large databases

Tier large RDS backups after 14 days.

aws backup create-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-rds-vault" \
    --tiering-policy '{
        "DaysAfterCreation": 14,
        "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 14
    }'
Output: { "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-rds-vault", "TieringPolicy": { "DaysAfterCreation": 14, "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 14 } }
Django Context: # RDS backups (JobPost database) tiered after 2 weeks # Recent 14 days in hot storage for quick recovery # Older backups in cold storage for cost savings
Example 5: Conservative tiering for critical data

Keep critical backups in hot storage longer.

aws backup create-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-critical-vault" \
    --tiering-policy '{
        "DaysAfterCreation": 180,
        "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 180
    }'
Output: { "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-critical-vault", "TieringPolicy": { "DaysAfterCreation": 180, "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 180 } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Critical data: 6 months in hot storage for fast recovery # All models protected with premium recovery capabilities

delete-backup-plan

Deletes a backup plan and its associated backup rules.

Syntax
aws backup delete-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-plan-id (string): ID of the backup plan to delete
Return Value
  • Returns backup plan ID, ARN, and deletion date
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Delete obsolete development plan

Remove unused development backup plan.

aws backup delete-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan-id "plan-dev-old-12345"
Output: { "BackupPlanId": "plan-dev-old-12345", "BackupPlanArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:backup-plan:plan-dev-old-12345", "DeletionDate": "2025-01-19T11:00:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Removed old dev backup plan for Skills test data # No longer needed after migration to new plan
Example 2: Delete expired project plan

Remove backup plan for completed project.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-project-xyz-67890"

# Delete the backup plan
aws backup delete-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID"

echo "Deleted backup plan: $PLAN_ID"
Output: { "BackupPlanId": "plan-project-xyz-67890", "DeletionDate": "2025-01-19T11:05:00Z" } Deleted backup plan: plan-project-xyz-67890
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Author # Removed backup plan for completed JobPost migration project # Author data now covered by production plan
Example 3: Delete test backup plan

Clean up after backup testing.

aws backup delete-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan-id "plan-test-backup-config"
Output: { "BackupPlanId": "plan-test-backup-config", "DeletionDate": "2025-01-19T11:10:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Location # Deleted test backup plan used for Location data testing # Production plan now active
Example 4: Bulk delete old plans

Remove multiple deprecated backup plans.

#!/bin/bash
OLD_PLANS=("plan-2023-q1" "plan-2023-q2" "plan-2023-q3")

for plan_id in "${OLD_PLANS[@]}"; do
    aws backup delete-backup-plan \
        --backup-plan-id "$plan_id"
    echo "Deleted: $plan_id"
    sleep 2
done
Output: Deleted: plan-2023-q1 Deleted: plan-2023-q2 Deleted: plan-2023-q3
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User # Removed 2023 quarterly backup plans # User data now on current 2025 backup strategy
Example 5: Delete plan with confirmation

Delete plan with pre-check validation.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-legacy-backup"

# Check plan details before deleting
echo "Checking plan details..."
aws backup get-backup-plan --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID"

# Confirm deletion
read -p "Delete plan $PLAN_ID? (yes/no): " confirm
if [ "$confirm" == "yes" ]; then
    aws backup delete-backup-plan --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID"
    echo "Plan deleted"
else
    echo "Deletion cancelled"
fi
Output: Checking plan details... { "BackupPlan": { "BackupPlanName": "legacy-backup", "Rules": [...] } } Delete plan plan-legacy-backup? (yes/no): yes Plan deleted
Django Context: # Safe deletion with confirmation for legacy plan # Ensured no active backups for JobPost, Skills, Author, Location, User

delete-backup-selection

Deletes a resource selection from a backup plan.

Syntax
aws backup delete-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id <value> \
    --selection-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-plan-id (string): ID of the backup plan
  • --selection-id (string): ID of the selection to delete
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Delete RDS selection

Remove RDS database from backup plan.

aws backup delete-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id "plan-12345-abcde" \
    --selection-id "sel-rds-67890"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Selection deleted successfully
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills # Removed old RDS selection from backup plan # JobPost and Skills now use updated selection criteria
Example 2: Delete tag-based selection

Remove tag-based resource selection.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-prod-backup"
SELECTION_ID="sel-old-tags-123"

aws backup delete-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --selection-id "$SELECTION_ID"

echo "Selection $SELECTION_ID removed from plan $PLAN_ID"
Output: Selection sel-old-tags-123 removed from plan plan-prod-backup
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Removed deprecated tag selection # Author and Location resources now use new tagging scheme
Example 3: Delete EBS volume selection

Remove specific EBS volumes from backup.

aws backup delete-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id "plan-ebs-backup" \
    --selection-id "sel-ebs-volumes-456"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User # Removed old EBS selection for User data volumes # Volumes migrated to new storage tier
Example 4: Clean up multiple selections

Remove several obsolete selections.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-legacy-12345"
SELECTIONS=("sel-001" "sel-002" "sel-003")

for sel_id in "${SELECTIONS[@]}"; do
    aws backup delete-backup-selection \
        --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
        --selection-id "$sel_id"
    echo "Deleted selection: $sel_id"
done
Output: Deleted selection: sel-001 Deleted selection: sel-002 Deleted selection: sel-003
Django Context: # Cleaned up legacy selections # JobPost, Skills, Author, Location, User now in consolidated selection
Example 5: Delete with verification

Verify selection before deletion.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-production"
SELECTION_ID="sel-verify-delete"

# Get selection details
echo "Selection details:"
aws backup get-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --selection-id "$SELECTION_ID"

# Delete
aws backup delete-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --selection-id "$SELECTION_ID"
Output: Selection details: { "SelectionName": "verify-delete", "ProtectedResourceTypes": ["RDS"] } HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Verified and deleted selection after JobPost migration complete

delete-backup-vault

Deletes an empty backup vault.

Syntax
aws backup delete-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the vault to delete
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Delete empty test vault

Remove unused test backup vault.

aws backup delete-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-test-vault"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Vault deleted successfully
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Deleted test vault after Skills module testing complete # Production vault now handles all backups
Example 2: Delete old project vault

Clean up vault after project completion.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-project-2024"

# Verify vault is empty
RECOVERY_POINTS=$(aws backup list-recovery-points-by-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME" \
    --query 'length(RecoveryPoints)')

if [ "$RECOVERY_POINTS" -eq 0 ]; then
    aws backup delete-backup-vault --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME"
    echo "Vault $VAULT_NAME deleted"
else
    echo "Vault not empty, cannot delete"
fi
Output: Vault anihpj-project-2024 deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Author # Deleted 2024 project vault after JobPost migration # All Author data migrated to new vault structure
Example 3: Delete deprecated vault

Remove vault from old backup strategy.

aws backup delete-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-deprecated-vault"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Location, User # Removed deprecated vault # Location and User data now in consolidated vault
Example 4: Clean up regional vaults

Delete empty vaults across regions.

#!/bin/bash
REGIONS=("us-east-1" "us-west-2" "eu-west-1")
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-temp-vault"

for region in "${REGIONS[@]}"; do
    echo "Checking $region..."
    aws backup delete-backup-vault \
        --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME" \
        --region "$region" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "Deleted in $region" || \
        echo "Not found or not empty in $region"
done
Output: Checking us-east-1... Deleted in us-east-1 Checking us-west-2... Not found or not empty in us-west-2 Checking eu-west-1... Deleted in eu-west-1
Django Context: # Cleaned up temporary vaults across regions # JobPost data consolidated in primary region vault
Example 5: Delete with access policy cleanup

Remove vault and its access policies.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-legacy-vault"

# Delete access policy first
aws backup delete-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME" 2>/dev/null

# Delete notifications
aws backup delete-backup-vault-notifications \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME" 2>/dev/null

# Delete vault
aws backup delete-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME"

echo "Vault and policies deleted"
Output: Vault and policies deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, Author, Location # Complete cleanup: vault, policies, notifications # Skills, Author, Location data migrated to new infrastructure

delete-backup-vault-access-policy

Deletes the access policy for a backup vault.

Syntax
aws backup delete-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the vault
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Remove cross-account access

Delete policy allowing cross-account restore.

aws backup delete-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-prod-vault"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Access policy deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Removed cross-account access policy # JobPost vault now restricted to primary account only
Example 2: Reset vault permissions

Delete existing policy to reconfigure access.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-compliance-vault"

# Delete old policy
aws backup delete-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME"

echo "Policy deleted, ready for new configuration"
Output: Policy deleted, ready for new configuration
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Author # Reset access policy for User and Author compliance vault # Preparing for updated security requirements
Example 3: Remove temporary access

Delete policy after audit completion.

aws backup delete-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-audit-vault"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, Location # Removed temporary audit access # Skills and Location data vault now fully restricted
Example 4: Clean up multiple vault policies

Remove policies from several vaults.

#!/bin/bash
VAULTS=("anihpj-dev-vault" "anihpj-test-vault" "anihpj-staging-vault")

for vault in "${VAULTS[@]}"; do
    aws backup delete-backup-vault-access-policy \
        --backup-vault-name "$vault" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "Deleted policy for $vault" || \
        echo "No policy or error for $vault"
done
Output: Deleted policy for anihpj-dev-vault No policy or error for anihpj-test-vault Deleted policy for anihpj-staging-vault
Django Context: # Cleaned up non-production vault policies # JobPost development environments now using default permissions
Example 5: Remove policy after migration

Delete policy post data migration.

#!/bin/bash
OLD_VAULT="anihpj-old-region-vault"

# Verify no recent backups
RECENT=$(aws backup list-recovery-points-by-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "$OLD_VAULT" \
    --by-created-after "$(date -d '30 days ago' -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
    --query 'length(RecoveryPoints)')

if [ "$RECENT" -eq 0 ]; then
    aws backup delete-backup-vault-access-policy \
        --backup-vault-name "$OLD_VAULT"
    echo "Policy deleted - no recent backups"
fi
Output: Policy deleted - no recent backups
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # All models migrated to new region # Old vault policy removed safely

delete-backup-vault-lock-configuration

Deletes the vault lock configuration from a backup vault.

Syntax
aws backup delete-backup-vault-lock-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the vault
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Remove test vault lock

Delete lock configuration after testing.

aws backup delete-backup-vault-lock-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-test-lock-vault"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Vault lock configuration deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Removed test lock configuration # Skills test data vault now unlocked for cleanup
Example 2: Reset vault lock settings

Delete to reconfigure retention settings.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-reconfigure-vault"

# Delete existing lock
aws backup delete-backup-vault-lock-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME"

echo "Lock removed, ready for new configuration"
Output: Lock removed, ready for new configuration
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Author # Reset lock to update JobPost and Author retention policies
Example 3: Remove dev environment lock

Delete lock from development vault.

aws backup delete-backup-vault-lock-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-dev-vault"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Location, User # Dev vault unlocked for flexible testing # Location and User test data management enabled
Example 4: Delete lock after compliance period

Remove lock once retention period satisfied.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-compliance-2024"

# Check if compliance period ended
CURRENT_DATE=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
COMPLIANCE_END="2025-01-01"

if [[ "$CURRENT_DATE" > "$COMPLIANCE_END" ]]; then
    aws backup delete-backup-vault-lock-configuration \
        --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME"
    echo "Compliance period ended, lock removed"
fi
Output: Compliance period ended, lock removed
Django Context: # 2024 compliance period completed # JobPost historical data now accessible for cleanup
Example 5: Bulk remove locks from old vaults

Clean up lock configurations from legacy vaults.

#!/bin/bash
VAULTS=("vault-2022" "vault-2023-q1" "vault-2023-q2")

for vault in "${VAULTS[@]}"; do
    aws backup delete-backup-vault-lock-configuration \
        --backup-vault-name "anihpj-$vault" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "Lock removed from anihpj-$vault" || \
        echo "No lock on anihpj-$vault"
done
Output: Lock removed from anihpj-vault-2022 Lock removed from anihpj-vault-2023-q1 No lock on anihpj-vault-2023-q2
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, Author, Location # Legacy vault locks removed # Historical Skills, Author, Location data ready for archival

delete-backup-vault-notifications

Deletes backup vault SNS notification configuration.

Syntax
aws backup delete-backup-vault-notifications \
    --backup-vault-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the vault
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Disable vault notifications

Stop receiving SNS alerts for vault events.

aws backup delete-backup-vault-notifications \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-prod-vault"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Notifications disabled
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Disabled SNS notifications for JobPost vault # Transitioning to CloudWatch Events for monitoring
Example 2: Remove test notifications

Clean up test notification configuration.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-test-notifications"

aws backup delete-backup-vault-notifications \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME"

echo "Test notifications removed from $VAULT_NAME"
Output: Test notifications removed from anihpj-test-notifications
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User # Test notification setup removed # Skills and User vault notifications now centralized
Example 3: Disable dev vault alerts

Stop alerts from development vault.

aws backup delete-backup-vault-notifications \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-dev-vault"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Dev vault notifications disabled to reduce noise # Author and Location dev backups no longer trigger alerts
Example 4: Clean up multiple vault notifications

Remove notifications from several vaults.

#!/bin/bash
VAULTS=("anihpj-legacy-vault" "anihpj-old-vault" "anihpj-archive-vault")

for vault in "${VAULTS[@]}"; do
    aws backup delete-backup-vault-notifications \
        --backup-vault-name "$vault" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "Notifications removed from $vault" || \
        echo "No notifications configured for $vault"
done
Output: Notifications removed from anihpj-legacy-vault No notifications configured for anihpj-old-vault Notifications removed from anihpj-archive-vault
Django Context: # Legacy vault notifications cleaned up # Consolidated JobPost monitoring in primary vault
Example 5: Reset notification configuration

Delete old notifications before reconfiguring.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-reconfigure-notifications"

# Delete existing notifications
aws backup delete-backup-vault-notifications \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME"

echo "Ready to configure new notification settings"
# Now you can put new notifications with different SNS topic
Output: Ready to configure new notification settings
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Reset notifications for all model vaults # Preparing for updated alerting strategy

delete-framework

Deletes a compliance framework.

Syntax
aws backup delete-framework \
    --framework-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --framework-name (string): Name of the framework to delete
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Delete test framework

Remove framework after testing complete.

aws backup delete-framework \
    --framework-name "anihpj-test-framework"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Framework deleted successfully
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Test compliance framework removed # Skills backup compliance now uses production framework
Example 2: Delete deprecated compliance framework

Remove old framework after migration.

#!/bin/bash
OLD_FRAMEWORK="anihpj-gdpr-v1"

# Delete the old framework
aws backup delete-framework \
    --framework-name "$OLD_FRAMEWORK"

echo "Deprecated framework $OLD_FRAMEWORK removed"
Output: Deprecated framework anihpj-gdpr-v1 removed
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Author # Old GDPR framework removed # User and Author compliance now on updated framework v2
Example 3: Clean up project framework

Delete framework after project completion.

aws backup delete-framework \
    --framework-name "anihpj-project-compliance-2024"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Location # 2024 project framework removed # JobPost and Location now under standard compliance
Example 4: Bulk delete old frameworks

Remove multiple legacy frameworks.

#!/bin/bash
FRAMEWORKS=("framework-2022" "framework-2023-q1" "framework-pilot")

for framework in "${FRAMEWORKS[@]}"; do
    aws backup delete-framework \
        --framework-name "anihpj-$framework" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "Deleted: anihpj-$framework" || \
        echo "Not found: anihpj-$framework"
done
Output: Deleted: anihpj-framework-2022 Deleted: anihpj-framework-2023-q1 Not found: anihpj-framework-pilot
Django Context: # Legacy compliance frameworks cleaned up # All JobPost models now on current 2025 framework
Example 5: Delete with report verification

Verify no active reports before deletion.

#!/bin/bash
FRAMEWORK_NAME="anihpj-audit-framework"

# Check if framework has active reports
REPORTS=$(aws backup list-report-plans \
    --query "ReportPlans[?contains(ReportSetting.FrameworkArns, '$FRAMEWORK_NAME')] | length(@)")

if [ "$REPORTS" -eq 0 ]; then
    aws backup delete-framework --framework-name "$FRAMEWORK_NAME"
    echo "Framework deleted - no active reports"
else
    echo "Cannot delete - $REPORTS report(s) still use this framework"
fi
Output: Framework deleted - no active reports
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, Author, Location # Audit framework safely removed # Skills, Author, Location compliance validated before deletion

delete-recovery-point

Deletes a specific recovery point from a backup vault.

Syntax
aws backup delete-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name <value> \
    --recovery-point-arn <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the vault
  • --recovery-point-arn (string): ARN of the recovery point to delete
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Delete specific recovery point

Remove individual backup after migration.

aws backup delete-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-prod-vault" \
    --recovery-point-arn "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-abc123"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Recovery point deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Deleted obsolete JobPost recovery point after successful migration
Example 2: Delete old test recovery point

Clean up test backup data.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-test-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-test-xyz"

aws backup delete-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN"

echo "Test recovery point deleted"
Output: Test recovery point deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User # Removed test recovery point for Skills and User data
Example 3: Delete corrupted recovery point

Remove failed or corrupted backup.

aws backup delete-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-prod-vault" \
    --recovery-point-arn "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-failed-001"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Removed corrupted backup # Author and Location data integrity maintained with good backups
Example 4: Clean up old recovery points by date

Delete recovery points older than retention period.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-dev-vault"
CUTOFF_DATE="2024-06-01T00:00:00Z"

# List old recovery points
OLD_RPS=$(aws backup list-recovery-points-by-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --by-created-before "$CUTOFF_DATE" \
    --query 'RecoveryPoints[].RecoveryPointArn' \
    --output text)

# Delete each old recovery point
for rp_arn in $OLD_RPS; do
    aws backup delete-recovery-point \
        --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
        --recovery-point-arn "$rp_arn"
    echo "Deleted: $rp_arn"
done
Output: Deleted: arn:aws:backup:...:recovery-point:rp-old-001 Deleted: arn:aws:backup:...:recovery-point:rp-old-002 Deleted: arn:aws:backup:...:recovery-point:rp-old-003
Django Context: # Cleaned up dev backups older than June 2024 # Freed storage for JobPost development vault
Example 5: Delete with safety check

Verify recovery point before deletion.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-compliance-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-check-delete"

# Describe recovery point first
echo "Recovery Point Details:"
aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN"

read -p "Delete this recovery point? (yes/no): " confirm
if [ "$confirm" == "yes" ]; then
    aws backup delete-recovery-point \
        --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
        --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN"
    echo "Deleted"
else
    echo "Cancelled"
fi
Output: Recovery Point Details: { "RecoveryPointArn": "...", "ResourceType": "RDS", "CreationDate": "2024-12-15T..." } Delete this recovery point? (yes/no): yes Deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Safe deletion with verification # Ensured no active dependencies before removing backup

delete-report-plan

Deletes a backup report plan.

Syntax
aws backup delete-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --report-plan-name (string): Name of the report plan to delete
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Delete test report plan

Remove report plan after testing.

aws backup delete-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name "anihpj-test-report"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Report plan deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Test report plan removed # Skills backup reporting now uses production plan
Example 2: Delete old compliance report

Remove outdated compliance reporting.

#!/bin/bash
OLD_REPORT="anihpj-2024-compliance-report"

aws backup delete-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name "$OLD_REPORT"

echo "Old report plan $OLD_REPORT deleted"
Output: Old report plan anihpj-2024-compliance-report deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Author # 2024 compliance report plan removed # User and Author data now on 2025 reporting
Example 3: Delete duplicate report plan

Clean up accidentally created duplicate.

aws backup delete-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name "anihpj-daily-compliance-duplicate"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Location # Duplicate report plan removed # JobPost and Location reporting consolidated
Example 4: Bulk delete old report plans

Remove multiple legacy report plans.

#!/bin/bash
REPORTS=("report-2022" "report-2023-q1" "report-pilot")

for report in "${REPORTS[@]}"; do
    aws backup delete-report-plan \
        --report-plan-name "anihpj-$report" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "Deleted: anihpj-$report" || \
        echo "Not found: anihpj-$report"
done
Output: Deleted: anihpj-report-2022 Deleted: anihpj-report-2023-q1 Not found: anihpj-report-pilot
Django Context: # Legacy report plans cleaned up # All JobPost backup reports now on current system
Example 5: Delete with S3 cleanup

Remove report plan and clean S3 bucket.

#!/bin/bash
REPORT_NAME="anihpj-old-reports"
S3_BUCKET="anihpj-backup-reports"
S3_PREFIX="old-reports/"

# Delete report plan
aws backup delete-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name "$REPORT_NAME"

# Clean up old reports in S3
aws s3 rm "s3://$S3_BUCKET/$S3_PREFIX" --recursive

echo "Report plan and S3 data cleaned up"
Output: delete: s3://anihpj-backup-reports/old-reports/report-001.csv delete: s3://anihpj-backup-reports/old-reports/report-002.csv Report plan and S3 data cleaned up
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, Author, Location # Complete cleanup: report plan and historical data # Skills, Author, Location reporting migrated to new system

Syntax

    --restore-testing-plan-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --restore-testing-plan-name (string): Name of the restore testing plan
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion

Remove multiple legacy testing plans.

#!/bin/bash
PLANS=("test-2024-q1" "test-2024-q2" "test-2024-q3" "test-2024-q4")

for plan in "${PLANS[@]}"; do
    aws backup delete-restore-testing-plan \

Output: Deleted: anihpj-test-2024-q1 Deleted: anihpj-test-2024-q2 Deleted: anihpj-test-2024-q3 Deleted: anihpj-test-2024-q4

Example 5: Delete with selection cleanup

Remove test plan and associated selections.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_NAME="anihpj-comprehensive-test"

# List selections first
echo "Associated selections:"
aws backup list-restore-testing-selections \
    --restore-testing-plan-name "$PLAN_NAME"

# Delete plan (selections deleted automatically)
aws backup delete-restore-testing-plan \
    --restore-testing-plan-name "$PLAN_NAME"

echo "Plan and selections deleted"
Output: Associated selections: { "RestoreTestingSelections": [ {"SelectionName": "rds-selection"}, {"SelectionName": "ebs-selection"} ] } Plan and selections deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Complete test plan cleanup with all selections # All model restore testing migrated to new framework

delete-restore-testing-selection

Deletes a resource selection from a restore testing plan.

    --restore-testing-plan-name <value> \

Parameters Description

  • --restore-testing-selection-name (string): Name of the selection to delete
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion

    --restore-testing-selection-name "jobpost-rds-selection"

Clean up testing selection after validation.

    --restore-testing-selection-name "test-selection-001"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK

for selection in "${SELECTIONS[@]}"; do

Verify selection before deletion.

#!/bin/bash

# Get selection details

    --restore-testing-plan-name "$PLAN" \
    --restore-testing-selection-name "$SELECTION"

    --restore-testing-plan-name "$PLAN" \

Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User, Author, Location # Verified and deleted selection safely # All models now in consolidated testing selection

delete-tiering-configuration

Deletes the tiering configuration from a backup vault.

Syntax
aws backup delete-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the vault
Return Value
  • Returns HTTP 200 status on successful deletion
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Remove tiering from test vault

Delete tiering configuration after testing.

aws backup delete-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-test-tiering-vault"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK Tiering configuration deleted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Removed test tiering configuration # Skills vault will use default storage tier
Example 2: Reset tiering policy

Delete to reconfigure tiering rules.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-reconfigure-tiering"

# Delete existing tiering
aws backup delete-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT"

echo "Tiering removed, ready for new policy"
Output: Tiering removed, ready for new policy
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Author # Reset tiering for JobPost and Author vault # Preparing for updated cost optimization strategy
Example 3: Remove aggressive tiering

Delete fast tiering for critical vault.

aws backup delete-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-critical-vault"
Output: HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Location # Removed aggressive tiering from critical vault # User and Location backups now in hot storage longer
Example 4: Bulk remove tiering from vaults

Clean up tiering from multiple vaults.

#!/bin/bash
VAULTS=("dev-vault" "test-vault" "staging-vault")

for vault in "${VAULTS[@]}"; do
    aws backup delete-tiering-configuration \
        --backup-vault-name "anihpj-$vault" 2>/dev/null && \
        echo "Tiering removed from anihpj-$vault" || \
        echo "No tiering on anihpj-$vault"
done
Output: Tiering removed from anihpj-dev-vault No tiering on anihpj-test-vault Tiering removed from anihpj-staging-vault
Django Context: # Non-production tiering configurations removed # JobPost dev/test environments now using standard storage
Example 5: Delete tiering with verification

Verify tiering details before removal.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-verify-tiering"

# Check current tiering
echo "Current tiering configuration:"
aws backup describe-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --query 'TieringPolicy'

# Delete tiering
aws backup delete-tiering-configuration \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT"

echo "Tiering configuration removed"
Output: Current tiering configuration: { "DaysAfterCreation": 30, "MoveToColdStorageAfter": 30 } Tiering configuration removed
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, Author, Location # Verified and removed 30-day tiering # Skills, Author, Location backups remain in hot storage

describe-backup-job

Retrieves detailed information about a specific backup job.

Syntax
aws backup describe-backup-job \
    --backup-job-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-job-id (string): ID of the backup job
Return Value
  • Returns complete backup job details including status, progress, and metadata
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Check backup job status

Monitor ongoing JobPost database backup.

aws backup describe-backup-job \
    --backup-job-id "job-abc123-def456"
Output: { "BackupJobId": "job-abc123-def456", "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-prod-vault", "State": "RUNNING", "PercentDone": "45%", "ResourceType": "RDS", "ResourceArn": "arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:jobpost-prod", "CreationDate": "2025-01-19T05:00:00Z", "BytesTransferred": 536870912, "ExpectedCompletionDate": "2025-01-19T05:30:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User # Backup job 45% complete # JobPost database (Skills, User tables) being backed up # ~512MB transferred so far
Example 2: Verify completed backup

Check backup job completion details.

#!/bin/bash
JOB_ID="job-completed-xyz789"

aws backup describe-backup-job \
    --backup-job-id "$JOB_ID" \
    --query '{JobID:BackupJobId, Status:State, Size:BackupSizeInBytes, Created:CreationDate, Completed:CompletionDate}'
Output: { "JobID": "job-completed-xyz789", "Status": "COMPLETED", "Size": 2147483648, "Created": "2025-01-19T03:00:00Z", "Completed": "2025-01-19T03:35:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Backup completed successfully # Author and Location data: 2GB backup in 35 minutes
Example 3: Monitor backup progress

Poll backup job status until completion.

#!/bin/bash
JOB_ID="job-monitor-progress"

while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws backup describe-backup-job \
        --backup-job-id "$JOB_ID" \
        --query 'State' --output text)
    
    PERCENT=$(aws backup describe-backup-job \
        --backup-job-id "$JOB_ID" \
        --query 'PercentDone' --output text)
    
    echo "Status: $STATUS - Progress: $PERCENT"
    
    [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETED" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ] && break
    sleep 30
done
Output: Status: RUNNING - Progress: 25% Status: RUNNING - Progress: 50% Status: RUNNING - Progress: 75% Status: COMPLETED - Progress: 100%
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Real-time monitoring of JobPost backup # Tracked progress from 0% to 100% completion
Example 4: Check failed backup details

Investigate backup failure causes.

#!/bin/bash
JOB_ID="job-failed-investigation"

aws backup describe-backup-job \
    --backup-job-id "$JOB_ID" \
    --query '{Status:State, Message:StatusMessage, StartTime:CreationDate, ResourceType:ResourceType}'
Output: { "Status": "FAILED", "Message": "Insufficient permissions to access RDS instance", "StartTime": "2025-01-19T04:00:00Z", "ResourceType": "RDS" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Skills # Backup failed: permission issue # User and Skills database backup requires IAM role update
Example 5: Get recovery point ARN

Extract recovery point from completed backup.

#!/bin/bash
JOB_ID="job-get-recovery-point"

# Get recovery point ARN
RECOVERY_POINT=$(aws backup describe-backup-job \
    --backup-job-id "$JOB_ID" \
    --query 'RecoveryPointArn' --output text)

echo "Recovery Point: $RECOVERY_POINT"

# Can now use this ARN for restore operations
aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-prod-vault" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RECOVERY_POINT"
Output: Recovery Point: arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-xyz789abc { "RecoveryPointArn": "arn:aws:backup:...:rp-xyz789abc", "Status": "COMPLETED", "BackupSizeInBytes": 1073741824 }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Retrieved recovery point ARN from backup job # All models backed up: 1GB snapshot ready for restore

describe-backup-vault

Retrieves detailed information about a backup vault.

Syntax
aws backup describe-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the backup vault
Return Value
  • Returns vault details including ARN, creation date, encryption, and statistics
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Get production vault details

View JobPost production vault configuration.

aws backup describe-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-prod-vault"
Output: { "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-prod-vault", "BackupVaultArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:backup-vault:anihpj-prod-vault", "EncryptionKeyArn": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/mrk-abc123", "CreationDate": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z", "NumberOfRecoveryPoints": 45, "Locked": false }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User # Production vault contains 45 recovery points # JobPost, Skills, and User data encrypted with KMS
Example 2: Check vault statistics

Extract key metrics from vault.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-compliance-vault"

aws backup describe-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --query '{Name:BackupVaultName, RecoveryPoints:NumberOfRecoveryPoints, Locked:Locked, Created:CreationDate}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-compliance-vault", "RecoveryPoints": 120, "Locked": true, "Created": "2023-06-01T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Compliance vault: 120 recovery points # Author and Location data retention locked for compliance
Example 3: Verify vault encryption

Check encryption configuration.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-encrypted-vault"

ENC_KEY=$(aws backup describe-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --query 'EncryptionKeyArn' --output text)

echo "Encryption Key: $ENC_KEY"

# Verify KMS key details
aws kms describe-key --key-id "$ENC_KEY"
Output: Encryption Key: arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/mrk-xyz789 { "KeyMetadata": { "KeyId": "mrk-xyz789", "KeyState": "Enabled", "Description": "JobPost backup encryption key" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Vault encrypted with dedicated KMS key # JobPost sensitive data protected at rest
Example 4: List vault access policy

Check who can access vault.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-shared-vault"

# Get vault details
aws backup describe-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT"

# Get access policy
aws backup get-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT"
Output: { "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-shared-vault", "BackupVaultArn": "arn:aws:backup:...:anihpj-shared-vault", "NumberOfRecoveryPoints": 30 } { "Policy": "{\"Version\":\"2012-10-17\",\"Statement\":[...]}" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User # Shared vault with cross-account access # Skills and User data accessible to DR account
Example 5: Monitor all vaults

Check status of multiple vaults.

#!/bin/bash
VAULTS=("anihpj-prod-vault" "anihpj-dr-vault" "anihpj-compliance-vault")

echo "Vault Status Report"
echo "===================="

for vault in "${VAULTS[@]}"; do
    POINTS=$(aws backup describe-backup-vault \
        --backup-vault-name "$vault" \
        --query 'NumberOfRecoveryPoints' --output text)
    
    LOCKED=$(aws backup describe-backup-vault \
        --backup-vault-name "$vault" \
        --query 'Locked' --output text)
    
    echo "$vault: $POINTS recovery points (Locked: $LOCKED)"
done
Output: Vault Status Report ==================== anihpj-prod-vault: 45 recovery points (Locked: false) anihpj-dr-vault: 30 recovery points (Locked: false) anihpj-compliance-vault: 120 recovery points (Locked: true)
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Multi-vault monitoring dashboard # All Django models protected across prod, DR, and compliance vaults

describe-copy-job

Retrieves information about a backup copy job.

Syntax
aws backup describe-copy-job \
    --copy-job-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --copy-job-id (string): ID of the copy job
Return Value
  • Returns copy job details including source, destination, and status
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Check cross-region copy status

Monitor JobPost backup copy to DR region.

aws backup describe-copy-job \
    --copy-job-id "copy-job-abc123"
Output: { "CopyJobId": "copy-job-abc123", "State": "RUNNING", "SourceBackupVaultArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:...:backup-vault:anihpj-prod-vault", "DestinationBackupVaultArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-west-2:...:backup-vault:anihpj-dr-vault", "ResourceType": "RDS", "CreationDate": "2025-01-19T06:00:00Z", "PercentDone": "60%" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Cross-region copy 60% complete # JobPost database copying from us-east-1 to us-west-2
Example 2: Verify completed copy

Check copy job completion details.

#!/bin/bash
COPY_JOB_ID="copy-job-completed-xyz"

aws backup describe-copy-job \
    --copy-job-id "$COPY_JOB_ID" \
    --query '{JobID:CopyJobId, Status:State, SourceRegion:SourceBackupVaultArn, DestRegion:DestinationBackupVaultArn, Completed:CompletionDate}'
Output: { "JobID": "copy-job-completed-xyz", "Status": "COMPLETED", "SourceRegion": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:...", "DestRegion": "arn:aws:backup:eu-west-1:...", "Completed": "2025-01-19T07:30:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, Author # Copy completed: Skills and Author data now in EU region # GDPR compliance with EU data residency
Example 3: Monitor copy progress

Poll copy job until completion.

#!/bin/bash
COPY_JOB_ID="copy-job-monitor"

while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws backup describe-copy-job \
        --copy-job-id "$COPY_JOB_ID" \
        --query 'State' --output text)
    
    PERCENT=$(aws backup describe-copy-job \
        --copy-job-id "$COPY_JOB_ID" \
        --query 'PercentDone' --output text)
    
    echo "Copy Status: $STATUS - Progress: $PERCENT"
    
    [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETED" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ] && break
    sleep 60
done
Output: Copy Status: RUNNING - Progress: 20% Copy Status: RUNNING - Progress: 50% Copy Status: RUNNING - Progress: 80% Copy Status: COMPLETED - Progress: 100%
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Location # Real-time copy monitoring # User and Location data transfer tracked to completion
Example 4: Check failed copy details

Investigate copy failure.

#!/bin/bash
COPY_JOB_ID="copy-job-failed"

aws backup describe-copy-job \
    --copy-job-id "$COPY_JOB_ID" \
    --query '{Status:State, Message:StatusMessage, Source:SourceBackupVaultArn, Destination:DestinationBackupVaultArn}'
Output: { "Status": "FAILED", "Message": "Destination vault does not exist", "Source": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:...:anihpj-prod-vault", "Destination": "arn:aws:backup:ap-south-1:...:anihpj-missing-vault" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Copy failed: destination vault missing # JobPost DR copy requires vault creation in ap-south-1
Example 5: Extract destination recovery point

Get recovery point ARN in destination region.

#!/bin/bash
COPY_JOB_ID="copy-job-get-rp"

# Get destination recovery point
DEST_RP=$(aws backup describe-copy-job \
    --copy-job-id "$COPY_JOB_ID" \
    --query 'DestinationRecoveryPointArn' --output text)

echo "Destination Recovery Point: $DEST_RP"

# Can now use this for DR restore operations
Output: Destination Recovery Point: arn:aws:backup:us-west-2:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-dr-copy-abc
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # DR recovery point available in us-west-2 # All models ready for disaster recovery restore

describe-framework

Retrieves details about a compliance framework.

Syntax
aws backup describe-framework \
    --framework-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --framework-name (string): Name of the framework
Return Value
  • Returns framework configuration including controls and compliance status
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Check GDPR framework status

View GDPR compliance framework configuration.

aws backup describe-framework \
    --framework-name "anihpj-gdpr-compliance"
Output: { "FrameworkName": "anihpj-gdpr-compliance", "FrameworkArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:framework:anihpj-gdpr-compliance", "FrameworkDescription": "GDPR compliance for JobPost user data", "FrameworkControls": [ { "ControlName": "BACKUP_RECOVERY_POINT_ENCRYPTED", "ControlScope": {"ComplianceResourceTypes": ["RDS"]} }, { "ControlName": "BACKUP_RECOVERY_POINT_MINIMUM_RETENTION_CHECK", "ControlInputParameters": [{"ParameterName": "requiredRetentionDays", "ParameterValue": "90"}] } ], "DeploymentStatus": "ACTIVE" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Author # GDPR framework active # User and Author data: encryption enforced, 90-day minimum retention
Example 2: Verify framework controls

Extract specific control configuration.

#!/bin/bash
FRAMEWORK="anihpj-encryption-standard"

aws backup describe-framework \
    --framework-name "$FRAMEWORK" \
    --query 'FrameworkControls[*].{Control:ControlName, Scope:ControlScope}'
Output: [ { "Control": "BACKUP_RECOVERY_POINT_ENCRYPTED", "Scope": null }, { "Control": "BACKUP_COPY_ENCRYPTED", "Scope": null } ]
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills # Encryption framework applies to all resources # JobPost and Skills backups and copies must be encrypted
Example 3: Check deployment status

Verify framework is active and deployed.

#!/bin/bash
FRAMEWORK="anihpj-daily-backup-check"

STATUS=$(aws backup describe-framework \
    --framework-name "$FRAMEWORK" \
    --query 'DeploymentStatus' --output text)

echo "Framework Status: $STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" == "ACTIVE" ]; then
    echo "Framework is monitoring backups"
else
    echo "Framework deployment issue!"
fi
Output: Framework Status: ACTIVE Framework is monitoring backups
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Location # Daily backup framework active # Location data backup compliance monitored continuously
Example 4: List framework compliance scope

Check which resources are covered.

#!/bin/bash
FRAMEWORK="anihpj-audit-framework"

aws backup describe-framework \
    --framework-name "$FRAMEWORK" \
    --query '{Name:FrameworkName, Controls:length(FrameworkControls), Status:DeploymentStatus}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-audit-framework", "Controls": 3, "Status": "ACTIVE" }
Django Context: # Audit framework with 3 active controls # Monitors JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location backups
Example 5: Export framework configuration

Save framework details for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
FRAMEWORK="anihpj-dr-framework"
OUTPUT_FILE="framework-config.json"

aws backup describe-framework \
    --framework-name "$FRAMEWORK" > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Framework configuration saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"

# View summary
jq '{Name: .FrameworkName, Description: .FrameworkDescription, Controls: [.FrameworkControls[].ControlName]}' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Framework configuration saved to framework-config.json { "Name": "anihpj-dr-framework", "Description": "Cross-region DR compliance", "Controls": [ "BACKUP_RECOVERY_POINT_MINIMUM_RETENTION_CHECK", "BACKUP_COPY_ENCRYPTED" ] }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # DR framework configuration exported # All models: 365-day retention + encrypted copies enforced

describe-global-settings

Retrieves global AWS Backup settings for the account.

Syntax
aws backup describe-global-settings
Parameters Description
  • No parameters required
Return Value
  • Returns global settings including resource type opt-in status
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Check global backup configuration

View account-level backup settings.

aws backup describe-global-settings
Output: { "GlobalSettings": { "isCrossAccountBackupEnabled": "true" }, "LastUpdateTime": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Cross-account backup enabled # JobPost data can be backed up to partner accounts
Example 2: Verify resource type support

Check which AWS services are enabled for backup.

#!/bin/bash
aws backup describe-global-settings \
    --query 'GlobalSettings' --output json | jq .
Output: { "isCrossAccountBackupEnabled": "true" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User # Global settings verified for Skills and User RDS backups
Example 3: Check last update time

See when global settings were last modified.

#!/bin/bash
LAST_UPDATE=$(aws backup describe-global-settings \
    --query 'LastUpdateTime' --output text)

echo "Global settings last updated: $LAST_UPDATE"
Output: Global settings last updated: 2025-01-15T10:00:00Z
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Settings last changed Jan 15, 2025 # Author and Location backup configuration stable since then
Example 4: Export settings for audit

Save global settings to file for compliance documentation.

#!/bin/bash
OUTPUT_FILE="backup-global-settings-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws backup describe-global-settings > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Global settings saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Global settings saved to backup-global-settings-20250119.json
Django Context: # Compliance documentation generated # JobPost backup global settings archived for audit trail
Example 5: Compare settings across accounts

Check settings in multiple AWS accounts.

#!/bin/bash
PROFILES=("prod" "dr" "dev")

echo "Cross-Account Backup Status:"
for profile in "${PROFILES[@]}"; do
    STATUS=$(aws backup describe-global-settings \
        --profile "$profile" \
        --query 'GlobalSettings.isCrossAccountBackupEnabled' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null)
    echo "$profile: $STATUS"
done
Output: Cross-Account Backup Status: prod: true dr: true dev: false
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Multi-account analysis complete # Prod and DR: cross-account enabled for all models # Dev: local backups only

describe-protected-resource

Retrieves information about a protected resource.

Syntax
aws backup describe-protected-resource \
    --resource-arn <value>
Parameters Description
  • --resource-arn (string): ARN of the resource
Return Value
  • Returns protection details including last backup time and resource type
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Check RDS protection status

Verify JobPost database is protected.

aws backup describe-protected-resource \
    --resource-arn "arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:jobpost-prod"
Output: { "ResourceArn": "arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:jobpost-prod", "ResourceType": "RDS", "LastBackupTime": "2025-01-19T05:00:00Z", "ResourceName": "jobpost-prod" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User # JobPost database protected # Last backup: Jan 19, 2025 at 5:00 AM # Contains: JobPost, Skills, User tables
Example 2: Verify EBS volume protection

Check Django application volume backup status.

#!/bin/bash
VOLUME_ARN="arn:aws:ec2:us-east-1:123456789012:volume/vol-django-app"

aws backup describe-protected-resource \
    --resource-arn "$VOLUME_ARN" \
    --query '{Resource:ResourceName, Type:ResourceType, LastBackup:LastBackupTime}'
Output: { "Resource": "vol-django-app", "Type": "EBS", "LastBackup": "2025-01-19T04:00:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Django app volume protected # Contains Author profile images and Location static data
Example 3: Check when resource was last backed up

Calculate time since last backup.

#!/bin/bash
RESOURCE_ARN="arn:aws:dynamodb:us-east-1:123456789012:table/jobpost-cache"

LAST_BACKUP=$(aws backup describe-protected-resource \
    --resource-arn "$RESOURCE_ARN" \
    --query 'LastBackupTime' --output text)

echo "Last backup: $LAST_BACKUP"
echo "Hours since backup: $(( ( $(date +%s) - $(date -d "$LAST_BACKUP" +%s) ) / 3600 ))"
Output: Last backup: 2025-01-19T02:00:00Z Hours since backup: 10
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # DynamoDB cache table for Skills queries # Last backed up 10 hours ago
Example 4: Verify S3 bucket protection

Check media bucket backup configuration.

#!/bin/bash
BUCKET_ARN="arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-media"

aws backup describe-protected-resource \
    --resource-arn "$BUCKET_ARN"
Output: { "ResourceArn": "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-media", "ResourceType": "S3", "LastBackupTime": "2025-01-19T03:00:00Z", "ResourceName": "anihpj-media" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Author # S3 media bucket protected # Contains JobPost attachments and Author profile pictures
Example 5: Monitor multiple resources

Check protection status of all critical resources.

#!/bin/bash
RESOURCES=(
    "arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:jobpost-prod"
    "arn:aws:ec2:us-east-1:123456789012:volume/vol-app"
    "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-media"
)

echo "Protection Status Report"
echo "======================="

for arn in "${RESOURCES[@]}"; do
    LAST_BACKUP=$(aws backup describe-protected-resource \
        --resource-arn "$arn" \
        --query 'LastBackupTime' --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "NOT_PROTECTED")
    
    RESOURCE_TYPE=$(aws backup describe-protected-resource \
        --resource-arn "$arn" \
        --query 'ResourceType' --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "UNKNOWN")
    
    echo "$RESOURCE_TYPE: Last backup at $LAST_BACKUP"
done
Output: Protection Status Report ======================= RDS: Last backup at 2025-01-19T05:00:00Z EBS: Last backup at 2025-01-19T04:00:00Z S3: Last backup at 2025-01-19T03:00:00Z
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # All critical resources protected: # - RDS (JobPost, User, Skills) # - EBS (Django app) # - S3 (Author, Location media)

describe-recovery-point

Retrieves detailed information about a recovery point.

Syntax
aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name <value> \
    --recovery-point-arn <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the backup vault
  • --recovery-point-arn (string): ARN of the recovery point
Return Value
  • Returns recovery point details including status, size, and lifecycle
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Get recovery point details

View complete information about JobPost backup.

aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-prod-vault" \
    --recovery-point-arn "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-abc123"
Output: { "RecoveryPointArn": "arn:aws:backup:...:rp-abc123", "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-prod-vault", "ResourceType": "RDS", "ResourceArn": "arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:jobpost-prod", "CreationDate": "2025-01-19T05:00:00Z", "Status": "COMPLETED", "BackupSizeInBytes": 2147483648, "IsEncrypted": true, "StorageClass": "WARM", "Lifecycle": { "MoveToColdStorageAt": "2025-02-18T05:00:00Z", "DeleteAt": "2025-04-19T05:00:00Z" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User # Recovery point details: # - JobPost database snapshot: 2GB # - Encrypted, warm storage # - Moves to cold storage: Feb 18 # - Expires: Apr 19 (90-day retention)
Example 2: Check recovery point size

Get backup size for capacity planning.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-prod-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-xyz789"

SIZE_BYTES=$(aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN" \
    --query 'BackupSizeInBytes' --output text)

SIZE_GB=$(echo "scale=2; $SIZE_BYTES / 1073741824" | bc)
echo "Backup size: ${SIZE_GB}GB"
Output: Backup size: 2.00GB
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Author and Location data backup: 2GB # Capacity planning for storage costs
Example 3: Verify recovery point lifecycle

Check tiering and retention dates.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-compliance-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-lifecycle"

aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN" \
    --query '{Created:CreationDate, ColdStorage:Lifecycle.MoveToColdStorageAt, Expires:Lifecycle.DeleteAt}'
Output: { "Created": "2025-01-19T05:00:00Z", "ColdStorage": "2025-04-19T05:00:00Z", "Expires": "2026-01-19T05:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Compliance backup lifecycle: # - Created: Jan 19, 2025 # - Cold storage: Apr 19, 2025 (90 days) # - Expires: Jan 19, 2026 (1 year) # JobPost compliance data retention
Example 4: Check recovery point encryption

Verify backup is encrypted.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-encrypted-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-check-enc"

ENCRYPTED=$(aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN" \
    --query 'IsEncrypted' --output text)

if [ "$ENCRYPTED" == "True" ]; then
    echo "✓ Recovery point is encrypted"
else
    echo "✗ WARNING: Recovery point is NOT encrypted!"
fi
Output: ✓ Recovery point is encrypted
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User # User data backup verified as encrypted # Meets security compliance requirements
Example 5: Export recovery point metadata

Save backup details for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-prod-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-export"
OUTPUT_FILE="recovery-point-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).json"

aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN" > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Recovery point metadata saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"

# Extract key info
jq '{Type: .ResourceType, Size: .BackupSizeInBytes, Created: .CreationDate, Status: .Status}' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Recovery point metadata saved to recovery-point-20250119-120000.json { "Type": "RDS", "Size": 2147483648, "Created": "2025-01-19T05:00:00Z", "Status": "COMPLETED" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Complete recovery point metadata archived # All Django models: 2GB RDS snapshot documented

describe-region-settings

Retrieves AWS Backup settings specific to a region.

Syntax
aws backup describe-region-settings \
    --region <value>
Parameters Description
  • --region (string): AWS region (optional, defaults to configured region)
Return Value
  • Returns region-specific backup configuration and resource type opt-in status
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Check region backup settings

View backup configuration for us-east-1.

aws backup describe-region-settings --region us-east-1
Output: { "ResourceTypeOptInPreference": { "RDS": true, "EBS": true, "S3": true, "DynamoDB": true, "EFS": false } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills # us-east-1 backup settings: # - RDS enabled (JobPost, Skills database) # - EBS enabled (Django app volumes) # - S3 enabled (media files) # - DynamoDB enabled (cache)
Example 2: Compare multi-region settings

Check backup settings across regions.

#!/bin/bash
REGIONS=("us-east-1" "us-west-2" "eu-west-1")

echo "Backup Configuration by Region"
echo "=============================="

for region in "${REGIONS[@]}"; do
    echo -e "\n$region:"
    aws backup describe-region-settings \
        --region "$region" \
        --query 'ResourceTypeOptInPreference' \
        --output json | jq -r 'to_entries | .[] | "  \(.key): \(.value)"'
done
Output: Backup Configuration by Region ============================== us-east-1: RDS: true EBS: true S3: true DynamoDB: true us-west-2: RDS: true EBS: true S3: true eu-west-1: RDS: true EBS: false
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Author # Multi-region backup status: # - us-east-1: Full backup (User, Author RDS + volumes + S3) # - us-west-2: RDS + S3 only # - eu-west-1: RDS only (GDPR data)
Example 3: Verify S3 backup enabled

Check if S3 backup is configured in region.

#!/bin/bash
REGION="us-east-1"

S3_ENABLED=$(aws backup describe-region-settings \
    --region "$REGION" \
    --query 'ResourceTypeOptInPreference.S3' \
    --output text)

if [ "$S3_ENABLED" == "True" ]; then
    echo "✓ S3 backup is enabled in $REGION"
else
    echo "✗ S3 backup is NOT enabled in $REGION"
fi
Output: ✓ S3 backup is enabled in us-east-1
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Author # S3 backup enabled in us-east-1 # JobPost attachments and Author profile images protected
Example 4: Export region settings for audit

Save region configuration for compliance documentation.

#!/bin/bash
REGION="us-east-1"
OUTPUT_FILE="backup-region-settings-${REGION}-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws backup describe-region-settings \
    --region "$REGION" > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Region settings saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"

# Display enabled services
echo -e "\nEnabled backup services:"
jq -r '.ResourceTypeOptInPreference | to_entries | .[] | select(.value == true) | .key' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Region settings saved to backup-region-settings-us-east-1-20250119.json Enabled backup services: RDS EBS S3 DynamoDB
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, Location # us-east-1 backup audit complete: # - RDS: Skills, Location tables # - S3: Location static media # - DynamoDB: Skills query cache
Example 5: Monitor DR region configuration

Verify disaster recovery region backup settings.

#!/bin/bash
PRIMARY_REGION="us-east-1"
DR_REGION="us-west-2"

echo "Comparing Primary vs DR Region Backup Settings"
echo "=============================================="

echo -e "\nPrimary Region ($PRIMARY_REGION):"
aws backup describe-region-settings --region "$PRIMARY_REGION" \
    --query 'ResourceTypeOptInPreference'

echo -e "\nDR Region ($DR_REGION):"
aws backup describe-region-settings --region "$DR_REGION" \
    --query 'ResourceTypeOptInPreference'
Output: Comparing Primary vs DR Region Backup Settings ============================================== Primary Region (us-east-1): { "RDS": true, "EBS": true, "S3": true, "DynamoDB": true } DR Region (us-west-2): { "RDS": true, "EBS": true, "S3": true, "DynamoDB": false }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # DR region comparison: # - Both regions: RDS (all models), EBS, S3 enabled # - Primary only: DynamoDB cache (acceptable for DR) # - Critical data replicated to us-west-2

describe-report-job

Retrieves information about a backup report generation job.

Syntax
aws backup describe-report-job \
    --report-job-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --report-job-id (string): Unique identifier for the report job
Return Value
  • Returns report job details including status, creation time, and output location
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Check report job status

View status of compliance report generation.

aws backup describe-report-job \
    --report-job-id "report-123abc"
Output: { "ReportJob": { "ReportJobId": "report-123abc", "ReportPlanArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:report-plan:compliance-report", "CreationTime": "2025-01-19T06:00:00Z", "CompletionTime": "2025-01-19T06:15:00Z", "Status": "COMPLETED", "ReportDestination": { "S3BucketName": "anihpj-backup-reports", "S3Keys": ["reports/compliance-20250119.csv"] } } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User # Compliance report completed in 15 minutes # JobPost, Skills, User backup compliance exported to S3
Example 2: Monitor report generation progress

Check if report is still generating.

#!/bin/bash
REPORT_JOB_ID="report-xyz789"

STATUS=$(aws backup describe-report-job \
    --report-job-id "$REPORT_JOB_ID" \
    --query 'ReportJob.Status' --output text)

echo "Report job $REPORT_JOB_ID status: $STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETED" ]; then
    S3_KEY=$(aws backup describe-report-job \
        --report-job-id "$REPORT_JOB_ID" \
        --query 'ReportJob.ReportDestination.S3Keys[0]' --output text)
    echo "Report available at: s3://anihpj-backup-reports/$S3_KEY"
fi
Output: Report job report-xyz789 status: COMPLETED Report available at: s3://anihpj-backup-reports/reports/compliance-20250119.csv
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Report generation complete # Author and Location backup compliance data available
Example 3: Calculate report generation time

Measure how long report took to generate.

#!/bin/bash
REPORT_JOB_ID="report-timing"

CREATED=$(aws backup describe-report-job \
    --report-job-id "$REPORT_JOB_ID" \
    --query 'ReportJob.CreationTime' --output text)

COMPLETED=$(aws backup describe-report-job \
    --report-job-id "$REPORT_JOB_ID" \
    --query 'ReportJob.CompletionTime' --output text)

DURATION=$(( ( $(date -d "$COMPLETED" +%s) - $(date -d "$CREATED" +%s) ) / 60 ))
echo "Report generation time: $DURATION minutes"
Output: Report generation time: 12 minutes
Django Context: # Performance metric: 12 minutes to generate compliance report # All JobPost models: backup status and recovery point details
Example 4: Download completed report

Retrieve report file from S3.

#!/bin/bash
REPORT_JOB_ID="report-download"

# Get report details
REPORT_INFO=$(aws backup describe-report-job --report-job-id "$REPORT_JOB_ID")

BUCKET=$(echo "$REPORT_INFO" | jq -r '.ReportJob.ReportDestination.S3BucketName')
S3_KEY=$(echo "$REPORT_INFO" | jq -r '.ReportJob.ReportDestination.S3Keys[0]')

# Download report
LOCAL_FILE="compliance-report-$(date +%Y%m%d).csv"
aws s3 cp "s3://${BUCKET}/${S3_KEY}" "$LOCAL_FILE"

echo "Report downloaded to $LOCAL_FILE"
echo "Total rows: $(wc -l < "$LOCAL_FILE")"
Output: Report downloaded to compliance-report-20250119.csv Total rows: 487
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Compliance report downloaded: 487 backup items # Coverage: JobPost (120), User (85), Skills (145), Author (87), Location (50)
Example 5: Monitor failed report jobs

Check for report generation failures.

#!/bin/bash
REPORT_JOB_ID="report-check-failed"

REPORT_JOB=$(aws backup describe-report-job --report-job-id "$REPORT_JOB_ID")

STATUS=$(echo "$REPORT_JOB" | jq -r '.ReportJob.Status')

if [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo "✗ Report job failed"
    echo "Created: $(echo "$REPORT_JOB" | jq -r '.ReportJob.CreationTime')"
    echo "Check CloudWatch Logs for details"
else
    echo "✓ Report job status: $STATUS"
fi
Output: ✓ Report job status: COMPLETED
Django Context: # Report job successful # All Django models backup compliance verified

describe-report-plan

Retrieves information about a backup report plan.

Syntax
aws backup describe-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --report-plan-name (string): Name of the report plan
Return Value
  • Returns report plan details including schedule, format, and destination
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Get report plan details

View configuration of daily compliance report.

aws backup describe-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name "anihpj-daily-compliance"
Output: { "ReportPlan": { "ReportPlanArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:report-plan:anihpj-daily-compliance", "ReportPlanName": "anihpj-daily-compliance", "ReportDeliveryChannel": { "S3BucketName": "anihpj-backup-reports", "Formats": ["CSV"] }, "ReportSetting": { "ReportTemplate": "BACKUP_JOB_REPORT" }, "CreationTime": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User # Daily compliance report plan configured: # - Template: Backup job report # - Format: CSV # - Destination: S3 bucket # - Covers: JobPost, Skills, User backups
Example 2: Check report schedule

View when reports are generated.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_NAME="anihpj-weekly-summary"

aws backup describe-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name "$PLAN_NAME" \
    --query 'ReportPlan.{Name:ReportPlanName, Template:ReportSetting.ReportTemplate, Created:CreationTime}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-weekly-summary", "Template": "RESTORE_JOB_REPORT", "Created": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Weekly restore job summary report # Tracks Author and Location data restoration activities
Example 3: Verify report destination

Check where reports are delivered.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_NAME="anihpj-compliance-monthly"

BUCKET=$(aws backup describe-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name "$PLAN_NAME" \
    --query 'ReportPlan.ReportDeliveryChannel.S3BucketName' \
    --output text)

echo "Reports delivered to: s3://$BUCKET/"

# Check if bucket exists
if aws s3 ls "s3://$BUCKET/" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "✓ S3 bucket accessible"
else
    echo "✗ WARNING: S3 bucket not accessible"
fi
Output: Reports delivered to: s3://anihpj-backup-reports/ ✓ S3 bucket accessible
Django Context: # Monthly compliance reports accessible # JobPost backup compliance documentation archived
Example 4: List report formats

Check available report output formats.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_NAME="anihpj-audit-report"

FORMATS=$(aws backup describe-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name "$PLAN_NAME" \
    --query 'ReportPlan.ReportDeliveryChannel.Formats[]' \
    --output text)

echo "Available formats: $FORMATS"
Output: Available formats: CSV JSON
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Audit reports available in CSV and JSON # Skills table backup auditing in multiple formats
Example 5: Export report plan configuration

Save report plan for documentation or replication.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_NAME="anihpj-daily-compliance"
OUTPUT_FILE="report-plan-${PLAN_NAME}-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws backup describe-report-plan \
    --report-plan-name "$PLAN_NAME" > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Report plan saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"

# Display key configuration
jq '{Template: .ReportPlan.ReportSetting.ReportTemplate, Bucket: .ReportPlan.ReportDeliveryChannel.S3BucketName, Formats: .ReportPlan.ReportDeliveryChannel.Formats}' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Report plan saved to report-plan-anihpj-daily-compliance-20250119.json { "Template": "BACKUP_JOB_REPORT", "Bucket": "anihpj-backup-reports", "Formats": [ "CSV" ] }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Report plan configuration archived # All models: backup job reporting configured and documented

describe-restore-job

Retrieves information about a restore job.

Syntax
aws backup describe-restore-job \
    --restore-job-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --restore-job-id (string): Unique identifier for the restore job
Return Value
  • Returns restore job details including status, resource type, and completion time
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Check restore job status

Monitor JobPost database restoration.

aws backup describe-restore-job \
    --restore-job-id "restore-abc123"
Output: { "RestoreJobId": "restore-abc123", "RecoveryPointArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-xyz", "CreationDate": "2025-01-19T08:00:00Z", "CompletionDate": "2025-01-19T08:45:00Z", "Status": "COMPLETED", "ResourceType": "RDS", "CreatedResourceArn": "arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:jobpost-restored" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User # JobPost database restore completed in 45 minutes # Restored database: jobpost-restored # Contains: JobPost, Skills, User tables
Example 2: Monitor restore progress

Check if restore is still in progress.

#!/bin/bash
RESTORE_JOB_ID="restore-in-progress"

STATUS=$(aws backup describe-restore-job \
    --restore-job-id "$RESTORE_JOB_ID" \
    --query 'Status' --output text)

echo "Restore job status: $STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" == "RUNNING" ]; then
    CREATED=$(aws backup describe-restore-job \
        --restore-job-id "$RESTORE_JOB_ID" \
        --query 'CreationDate' --output text)
    echo "Started at: $CREATED"
    echo "Still in progress..."
elif [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETED" ]; then
    echo "✓ Restore completed successfully"
fi
Output: Restore job status: COMPLETED ✓ Restore completed successfully
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Author and Location data restoration complete # Ready for validation and production cutover
Example 3: Calculate restore duration

Measure recovery time objective (RTO).

#!/bin/bash
RESTORE_JOB_ID="restore-timing"

CREATED=$(aws backup describe-restore-job \
    --restore-job-id "$RESTORE_JOB_ID" \
    --query 'CreationDate' --output text)

COMPLETED=$(aws backup describe-restore-job \
    --restore-job-id "$RESTORE_JOB_ID" \
    --query 'CompletionDate' --output text)

DURATION=$(( ( $(date -d "$COMPLETED" +%s) - $(date -d "$CREATED" +%s) ) / 60 ))
echo "Restore duration: $DURATION minutes"
echo "RTO: $(( DURATION / 60 )) hours $(( DURATION % 60 )) minutes"
Output: Restore duration: 52 minutes RTO: 0 hours 52 minutes
Django Context: # RTO measured: 52 minutes for full JobPost database # Meets 1-hour RTO requirement for production restoration
Example 4: Get restored resource ARN

Retrieve ARN of newly restored resource.

#!/bin/bash
RESTORE_JOB_ID="restore-get-arn"

RESOURCE_ARN=$(aws backup describe-restore-job \
    --restore-job-id "$RESTORE_JOB_ID" \
    --query 'CreatedResourceArn' --output text)

RESOURCE_TYPE=$(aws backup describe-restore-job \
    --restore-job-id "$RESTORE_JOB_ID" \
    --query 'ResourceType' --output text)

echo "Restored $RESOURCE_TYPE: $RESOURCE_ARN"
Output: Restored RDS: arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:jobpost-dr
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # DR database restored: jobpost-dr # Skills table ready for DR environment testing
Example 5: Export restore job details

Document restoration for compliance and audit.

#!/bin/bash
RESTORE_JOB_ID="restore-audit"
OUTPUT_FILE="restore-job-${RESTORE_JOB_ID}-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws backup describe-restore-job \
    --restore-job-id "$RESTORE_JOB_ID" > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Restore job details saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"

# Extract key metrics
jq '{JobId: .RestoreJobId, Status: .Status, ResourceType: .ResourceType, Duration: (.CompletionDate | fromdate) - (.CreationDate | fromdate) | (. / 60 | floor), RestoredResource: .CreatedResourceArn}' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Restore job details saved to restore-job-restore-audit-20250119.json { "JobId": "restore-audit", "Status": "COMPLETED", "ResourceType": "RDS", "Duration": 48, "RestoredResource": "arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:jobpost-restored" }
