Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Restore audit documentation complete: # - Duration: 48 minutes # - All Django models restored successfully # - Compliance requirements met

disassociate-recovery-point

Removes the specified recovery point from a backup plan.

Syntax
aws backup disassociate-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name <value> \
    --recovery-point-arn <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the backup vault
  • --recovery-point-arn (string): ARN of the recovery point to disassociate
Return Value
  • No output on success
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Disassociate old recovery point

Remove outdated JobPost backup from plan.

aws backup disassociate-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-prod-vault" \
    --recovery-point-arn "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-old123"
Output: (No output - success)
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Old JobPost recovery point disassociated # No longer tracked by backup plan (manual deletion still required)
Example 2: Batch disassociate multiple points

Remove several recovery points from backup plan.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-cleanup-vault"
RECOVERY_POINTS=(
    "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-1"
    "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-2"
    "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-3"
)

echo "Disassociating recovery points..."
for rp_arn in "${RECOVERY_POINTS[@]}"; do
    aws backup disassociate-recovery-point \
        --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
        --recovery-point-arn "$rp_arn"
    echo "✓ Disassociated: $rp_arn"
done

echo "All recovery points disassociated"
Output: Disassociating recovery points... ✓ Disassociated: arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-1 ✓ Disassociated: arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-2 ✓ Disassociated: arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-3 All recovery points disassociated
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, User # Multiple Skills and User recovery points removed from plan # Cleanup complete - old backups no longer tracked
Example 3: Disassociate before deletion

Prepare recovery point for removal.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-prod-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-delete"

echo "Step 1: Disassociating recovery point..."
aws backup disassociate-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN"

echo "✓ Recovery point disassociated"
echo "Ready for deletion with delete-recovery-point command"
Output: Step 1: Disassociating recovery point... ✓ Recovery point disassociated Ready for deletion with delete-recovery-point command
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author # Author data recovery point disassociated # Prepared for deletion to free storage space
Example 4: Disassociate with verification

Verify recovery point before disassociating.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-test-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-verify"

# Check recovery point status
STATUS=$(aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN" \
    --query 'Status' --output text 2>/dev/null)

if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETED" ]; then
    echo "Recovery point is complete, disassociating..."
    aws backup disassociate-recovery-point \
        --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
        --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN"
    echo "✓ Disassociated successfully"
else
    echo "✗ Recovery point status: $STATUS (cannot disassociate)"
fi
Output: Recovery point is complete, disassociating... ✓ Disassociated successfully
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Location # Location data recovery point verified as complete # Safely disassociated from backup plan
Example 5: Disassociate with audit logging

Log disassociation for compliance tracking.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-compliance-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-audit"
LOG_FILE="disassociation-log-$(date +%Y%m%d).txt"

# Get recovery point details before disassociation
RESOURCE_TYPE=$(aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN" \
    --query 'ResourceType' --output text)

CREATED=$(aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN" \
    --query 'CreationDate' --output text)

# Disassociate
aws backup disassociate-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN"

# Log action
echo "$(date): Disassociated $RESOURCE_TYPE recovery point (Created: $CREATED)" >> "$LOG_FILE"
echo "✓ Disassociation logged to $LOG_FILE"
Output: ✓ Disassociation logged to disassociation-log-20250119.txt
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills # JobPost and Skills recovery point disassociation logged # Audit trail maintained for compliance

disassociate-recovery-point-from-parent

Removes the specified continuous backup from its parent backup.

Syntax
aws backup disassociate-recovery-point-from-parent \
    --backup-vault-name <value> \
    --recovery-point-arn <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the backup vault
  • --recovery-point-arn (string): ARN of the continuous backup recovery point
Return Value
  • No output on success
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Disassociate continuous backup

Remove RDS continuous backup from parent.

aws backup disassociate-recovery-point-from-parent \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-continuous-vault" \
    --recovery-point-arn "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:continuous-rp"
Output: (No output - success)
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User # Continuous backup disassociated from parent # JobPost and User point-in-time recovery independent
Example 2: Manage PITR backups

Disassociate point-in-time recovery backup.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-pitr-vault"
PITR_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:pitr-backup"

echo "Disassociating PITR backup from parent..."
aws backup disassociate-recovery-point-from-parent \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$PITR_ARN"

echo "✓ PITR backup now independent"
Output: Disassociating PITR backup from parent... ✓ PITR backup now independent
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Skills table PITR backup disassociated # Can now manage lifecycle independently
Example 3: Batch disassociate continuous backups

Remove multiple continuous backups from parents.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-continuous-vault"
CONTINUOUS_BACKUPS=(
    "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:cont-1"
    "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:cont-2"
)

for rp_arn in "${CONTINUOUS_BACKUPS[@]}"; do
    aws backup disassociate-recovery-point-from-parent \
        --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
        --recovery-point-arn "$rp_arn"
    echo "✓ Disassociated: $(basename $rp_arn)"
done
Output: ✓ Disassociated: cont-1 ✓ Disassociated: cont-2
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Multiple continuous backups disassociated # Author and Location PITR backups now independent
Example 4: Verify before disassociation

Check if recovery point is continuous backup.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:check-type"

# Check if continuous backup
IS_PARENT=$(aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN" \
    --query 'IsParent' --output text 2>/dev/null)

if [ "$IS_PARENT" == "True" ]; then
    echo "This is a parent backup, has continuous backups"
    aws backup disassociate-recovery-point-from-parent \
        --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
        --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN"
    echo "✓ Continuous backup disassociated"
else
    echo "Not a continuous backup, no action needed"
fi
Output: This is a parent backup, has continuous backups ✓ Continuous backup disassociated
Django Context: # Parent backup identified and disassociated # JobPost continuous backup lifecycle now independent
Example 5: Disassociate with audit trail

Log continuous backup disassociation.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT="anihpj-audit-vault"
RP_ARN="arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:audit-pitr"
AUDIT_LOG="pitr-disassociation-$(date +%Y%m%d).log"

# Get details before disassociation
RESOURCE=$(aws backup describe-recovery-point \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN" \
    --query 'ResourceArn' --output text)

# Disassociate
aws backup disassociate-recovery-point-from-parent \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT" \
    --recovery-point-arn "$RP_ARN"

# Log
echo "$(date): Disassociated continuous backup for $RESOURCE" >> "$AUDIT_LOG"
echo "✓ Action logged to $AUDIT_LOG"
Output: ✓ Action logged to pitr-disassociation-20250119.log
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Skills # User and Skills continuous backup disassociation logged # Audit trail complete for compliance

export-backup-plan-template

Exports a backup plan as a JSON template.

Syntax
aws backup export-backup-plan-template \
    --backup-plan-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-plan-id (string): Unique identifier of the backup plan
Return Value
  • Returns JSON template of the backup plan
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Export backup plan to file

Save JobPost backup plan as template.

aws backup export-backup-plan-template \
    --backup-plan-id "plan-abc123" > jobpost-backup-plan.json
Output: { "BackupPlanTemplateJson": "{\"BackupPlanName\":\"anihpj-daily-backup\",\"Rules\":[{\"RuleName\":\"DailyBackup\",\"TargetBackupVaultName\":\"anihpj-prod-vault\",\"ScheduleExpression\":\"cron(0 5 * * ? *)\"}]}" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User # JobPost backup plan template exported # Daily 5 AM backup for JobPost, Skills, User tables
Example 2: Export and format template

Create readable backup plan template.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-xyz789"
OUTPUT_FILE="backup-plan-template-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws backup export-backup-plan-template \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --query 'BackupPlanTemplateJson' \
    --output text | jq . > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Formatted template saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Formatted template saved to backup-plan-template-20250119.json
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Author and Location backup plan template formatted # Ready for replication or modification
Example 3: Export for replication

Export plan template for DR region deployment.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-prod"
DR_TEMPLATE="backup-plan-dr-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

# Export template
aws backup export-backup-plan-template \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --query 'BackupPlanTemplateJson' \
    --output text > "$DR_TEMPLATE"

echo "DR template created: $DR_TEMPLATE"
echo "Ready to import in DR region (us-west-2)"
Output: DR template created: backup-plan-dr-20250119.json Ready to import in DR region (us-west-2)
Django Context: # Backup plan exported for DR replication # JobPost DR backup plan ready for us-west-2 deployment
Example 4: Compare backup plan versions

Export and diff two plan versions.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID_V1="plan-v1"
PLAN_ID_V2="plan-v2"

# Export both versions
aws backup export-backup-plan-template \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID_V1" \
    --query 'BackupPlanTemplateJson' --output text | jq . > plan-v1.json

aws backup export-backup-plan-template \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID_V2" \
    --query 'BackupPlanTemplateJson' --output text | jq . > plan-v2.json

echo "Comparing plan versions..."
diff -u plan-v1.json plan-v2.json || echo "Plans differ"
Output: Comparing plan versions... --- plan-v1.json +++ plan-v2.json @@ -3,7 +3,7 @@ "Rules": [ { "RuleName": "DailyBackup", - "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 5 * * ? *)", + "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 3 * * ? *)", Plans differ
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Plan v1: 5 AM backup schedule # Plan v2: 3 AM backup schedule (changed for Skills table maintenance)
Example 5: Export all backup plans

Create template library of all plans.

#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE_DIR="backup-plan-templates-$(date +%Y%m%d)"
mkdir -p "$TEMPLATE_DIR"

# Get all plan IDs
PLAN_IDS=$(aws backup list-backup-plans --query 'BackupPlansList[].BackupPlanId' --output text)

echo "Exporting all backup plans..."
for plan_id in $PLAN_IDS; do
    PLAN_NAME=$(aws backup get-backup-plan --backup-plan-id "$plan_id" --query 'BackupPlan.BackupPlanName' --output text)
    
    aws backup export-backup-plan-template \
        --backup-plan-id "$plan_id" \
        --query 'BackupPlanTemplateJson' --output text | jq . > "$TEMPLATE_DIR/${PLAN_NAME}.json"
    
    echo "✓ Exported: $PLAN_NAME"
done

echo "All templates saved to $TEMPLATE_DIR/"
Output: Exporting all backup plans... ✓ Exported: anihpj-daily-backup ✓ Exported: anihpj-weekly-backup ✓ Exported: anihpj-monthly-backup All templates saved to backup-plan-templates-20250119/
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Complete backup plan library exported: # - Daily: JobPost, User (production data) # - Weekly: Skills (reference data) # - Monthly: Author, Location (compliance archives)

get-backup-plan

Retrieves the details of a backup plan.

Syntax
aws backup get-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-plan-id (string): Unique identifier of the backup plan
  • --version-id (string, optional): Version of the backup plan
Return Value
  • Returns backup plan details including rules, schedules, and lifecycle settings
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Get backup plan details

View JobPost backup plan configuration.

aws backup get-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan-id "plan-abc123"
Output: { "BackupPlan": { "BackupPlanName": "anihpj-prod-backup", "Rules": [ { "RuleName": "DailyBackup", "TargetBackupVaultName": "anihpj-prod-vault", "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 5 * * ? *)", "Lifecycle": { "MoveToColdStorageAfterDays": 30, "DeleteAfterDays": 90 } } ] }, "BackupPlanId": "plan-abc123", "BackupPlanArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:backup-plan:plan-abc123", "VersionId": "v1", "CreationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User # JobPost backup plan details: # - Daily 5 AM backup # - 30 days warm, then cold storage # - 90-day retention # - Covers: JobPost, Skills, User tables
Example 2: Check backup schedule

View when backups are scheduled.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-xyz789"

aws backup get-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --query 'BackupPlan.Rules[*].{Rule:RuleName, Schedule:ScheduleExpression}'
Output: [ { "Rule": "DailyBackup", "Schedule": "cron(0 5 * * ? *)" }, { "Rule": "WeeklyFullBackup", "Schedule": "cron(0 2 ? * SUN *)" } ]
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Author and Location backup schedules: # - Daily: 5 AM (incremental) # - Weekly: Sunday 2 AM (full backup)
Example 3: Verify lifecycle settings

Check retention and tiering configuration.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-compliance"

aws backup get-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --query 'BackupPlan.Rules[0].{ColdStorage:Lifecycle.MoveToColdStorageAfterDays, Deletion:Lifecycle.DeleteAfterDays}'
Output: { "ColdStorage": 90, "Deletion": 365 }
Django Context: # Compliance backup lifecycle: # - 90 days in warm storage # - Then cold storage # - 1-year total retention # JobPost compliance data retention policy
Example 4: Get specific plan version

Retrieve previous version of backup plan.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-versioned"
VERSION_ID="v2"

aws backup get-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --version-id "$VERSION_ID" \
    --query '{Name:BackupPlan.BackupPlanName, Version:VersionId, Created:CreationDate}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-prod-backup", "Version": "v2", "Created": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Skills backup plan version 2 # Created Jan 15 (schedule change from v1)
Example 5: Export plan configuration

Save backup plan details for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-document"
OUTPUT_FILE="backup-plan-config-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws backup get-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Plan configuration saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"

# Display summary
jq '{Plan: .BackupPlan.BackupPlanName, Rules: [.BackupPlan.Rules[].RuleName], Vault: .BackupPlan.Rules[0].TargetBackupVaultName}' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Plan configuration saved to backup-plan-config-20250119.json { "Plan": "anihpj-prod-backup", "Rules": [ "DailyBackup", "WeeklyFullBackup" ], "Vault": "anihpj-prod-vault" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Complete backup plan documentation exported # All Django models: daily + weekly backup rules configured

get-backup-plan-from-json

Validates a backup plan JSON template.

Syntax
aws backup get-backup-plan-from-json \
    --backup-plan-json <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-plan-json (string): JSON string or file containing backup plan
Return Value
  • Returns validated backup plan structure
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Validate backup plan JSON

Check if backup plan template is valid.

aws backup get-backup-plan-from-json \
    --backup-plan-json file://backup-plan.json
Output: { "BackupPlan": { "BackupPlanName": "anihpj-daily-backup", "Rules": [ { "RuleName": "DailyBackup", "TargetBackupVaultName": "anihpj-prod-vault", "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 5 * * ? *)" } ] } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost # Backup plan template validated successfully # JobPost daily backup plan ready for deployment
Example 2: Validate inline JSON

Test backup plan with inline JSON string.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_JSON='{
  "BackupPlanName": "test-plan",
  "Rules": [{
    "RuleName": "TestRule",
    "TargetBackupVaultName": "test-vault",
    "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 12 * * ? *)"
  }]
}'

aws backup get-backup-plan-from-json \
    --backup-plan-json "$PLAN_JSON" \
    --query 'BackupPlan.BackupPlanName' --output text
Output: test-plan
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Test backup plan validated # Skills table test backup configuration valid
Example 3: Validate before creating plan

Pre-validate plan template before deployment.

#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE_FILE="new-backup-plan.json"

echo "Validating backup plan template..."
VALIDATION=$(aws backup get-backup-plan-from-json \
    --backup-plan-json file://"$TEMPLATE_FILE" 2>&1)

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "✓ Template is valid"
    echo "Plan name: $(echo "$VALIDATION" | jq -r '.BackupPlan.BackupPlanName')"
    echo "Ready to create with create-backup-plan"
else
    echo "✗ Template validation failed:"
    echo "$VALIDATION"
fi
Output: Validating backup plan template... ✓ Template is valid Plan name: anihpj-new-plan Ready to create with create-backup-plan
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Author # New backup plan template validated # User and Author backup plan ready for creation
Example 4: Validate modified plan

Check if plan modifications are valid.

#!/bin/bash
# Export existing plan
aws backup export-backup-plan-template \
    --backup-plan-id "plan-abc123" \
    --query 'BackupPlanTemplateJson' --output text > original-plan.json

# Modify the plan (change schedule)
jq '.Rules[0].ScheduleExpression = "cron(0 3 * * ? *)"' original-plan.json > modified-plan.json

# Validate modifications
echo "Validating modified plan..."
aws backup get-backup-plan-from-json \
    --backup-plan-json file://modified-plan.json \
    --query 'BackupPlan.Rules[0].ScheduleExpression' --output text
Output: Validating modified plan... cron(0 3 * * ? *)
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Location # Location backup plan modified: 5 AM → 3 AM # Change validated successfully
Example 5: Validate complex plan with lifecycle

Check advanced backup plan with tiering.

#!/bin/bash
COMPLEX_PLAN='{
  "BackupPlanName": "anihpj-compliance",
  "Rules": [{
    "RuleName": "ComplianceBackup",
    "TargetBackupVaultName": "compliance-vault",
    "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 2 * * ? *)",
    "Lifecycle": {
      "MoveToColdStorageAfterDays": 90,
      "DeleteAfterDays": 365
    },
    "CopyActions": [{
      "DestinationBackupVaultArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-west-2:123456789012:backup-vault:dr-vault",
      "Lifecycle": {
        "DeleteAfterDays": 365
      }
    }]
  }]
}'

aws backup get-backup-plan-from-json \
    --backup-plan-json "$COMPLEX_PLAN" \
    --query '{Plan:BackupPlan.BackupPlanName, ColdStorage:BackupPlan.Rules[0].Lifecycle.MoveToColdStorageAfterDays, Retention:BackupPlan.Rules[0].Lifecycle.DeleteAfterDays}'
Output: { "Plan": "anihpj-compliance", "ColdStorage": 90, "Retention": 365 }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Complex compliance plan validated: # - All models: 90-day cold storage transition # - 1-year retention # - Cross-region DR copy to us-west-2

get-backup-plan-from-template

Creates a backup plan from a predefined template.

Syntax
aws backup get-backup-plan-from-template \
    --backup-plan-template-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-plan-template-id (string): ID of the backup plan template
Return Value
  • Returns backup plan structure from template
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Get daily backup template

Retrieve AWS predefined daily backup template.

aws backup get-backup-plan-from-template \
    --backup-plan-template-id "daily-35day-retention"
Output: { "BackupPlanDocument": { "BackupPlanName": "Daily-35day-Retention", "Rules": [ { "RuleName": "DailyBackups", "TargetBackupVaultName": "Default", "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 5 ? * * *)", "Lifecycle": { "DeleteAfterDays": 35 } } ] } }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills # Daily backup template retrieved # Suitable for JobPost and Skills production data (35-day retention)
Example 2: Get weekly backup template

Retrieve weekly cold storage template.

#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE_ID="weekly-cold-storage-monthly-1yr-retention"

aws backup get-backup-plan-from-template \
    --backup-plan-template-id "$TEMPLATE_ID" \
    --query 'BackupPlanDocument.Rules[0].{Schedule:ScheduleExpression, ColdStorage:Lifecycle.MoveToColdStorageAfterDays, Retention:Lifecycle.DeleteAfterDays}'
Output: { "Schedule": "cron(0 5 ? * 1 *)", "ColdStorage": 30, "Retention": 365 }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Author, Location # Weekly backup template: Sundays 5 AM # Author and Location: 30-day warm, 1-year total retention
Example 3: List available templates

Show all AWS Backup predefined templates.

#!/bin/bash
# Common template IDs
TEMPLATES=(
    "daily-35day-retention"
    "weekly-cold-storage-monthly-1yr-retention"
    "monthly-5yr-retention"
)

echo "Available AWS Backup Templates:"
echo "==============================="

for template_id in "${TEMPLATES[@]}"; do
    PLAN_NAME=$(aws backup get-backup-plan-from-template \
        --backup-plan-template-id "$template_id" \
        --query 'BackupPlanDocument.BackupPlanName' \
        --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "Not available")
    echo "$template_id: $PLAN_NAME"
done
Output: Available AWS Backup Templates: =============================== daily-35day-retention: Daily-35day-Retention weekly-cold-storage-monthly-1yr-retention: Weekly-Cold-Storage-Monthly-1yr-Retention monthly-5yr-retention: Monthly-5yr-Retention
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User # Template options for User data: # - Daily: Short-term retention # - Weekly: Cost-optimized with cold storage # - Monthly: Long-term compliance
Example 4: Customize template before creation

Modify template and create backup plan.

#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE_ID="daily-35day-retention"
CUSTOM_FILE="custom-backup-plan.json"

# Get template
aws backup get-backup-plan-from-template \
    --backup-plan-template-id "$TEMPLATE_ID" \
    --query 'BackupPlanDocument' > "$CUSTOM_FILE"

# Customize: Change name and vault
jq '.BackupPlanName = "anihpj-custom-daily" | .Rules[0].TargetBackupVaultName = "anihpj-prod-vault"' "$CUSTOM_FILE" > modified-plan.json

echo "✓ Template customized: modified-plan.json"
echo "Ready to create with: aws backup create-backup-plan --backup-plan file://modified-plan.json"
Output: ✓ Template customized: modified-plan.json Ready to create with: aws backup create-backup-plan --backup-plan file://modified-plan.json
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills # Template customized for Skills table # Changed vault from Default to anihpj-prod-vault
Example 5: Compare template options

Evaluate different templates for compliance needs.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Template Comparison for Compliance"
echo "=================================="

# Daily template
echo -e "\n1. Daily 35-day retention:"
aws backup get-backup-plan-from-template \
    --backup-plan-template-id "daily-35day-retention" \
    --query 'BackupPlanDocument.Rules[0].{Schedule:ScheduleExpression, Retention:Lifecycle.DeleteAfterDays}'

# Weekly template
echo -e "\n2. Weekly 1-year retention:"
aws backup get-backup-plan-from-template \
    --backup-plan-template-id "weekly-cold-storage-monthly-1yr-retention" \
    --query 'BackupPlanDocument.Rules[0].{Schedule:ScheduleExpression, ColdStorage:Lifecycle.MoveToColdStorageAfterDays, Retention:Lifecycle.DeleteAfterDays}'

# Monthly template
echo -e "\n3. Monthly 5-year retention:"
aws backup get-backup-plan-from-template \
    --backup-plan-template-id "monthly-5yr-retention" \
    --query 'BackupPlanDocument.Rules[0].{Schedule:ScheduleExpression, Retention:Lifecycle.DeleteAfterDays}'
Output: Template Comparison for Compliance ================================== 1. Daily 35-day retention: { "Schedule": "cron(0 5 ? * * *)", "Retention": 35 } 2. Weekly 1-year retention: { "Schedule": "cron(0 5 ? * 1 *)", "ColdStorage": 30, "Retention": 365 } 3. Monthly 5-year retention: { "Schedule": "cron(0 5 1 * ? *)", "Retention": 1825 }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Template selection guide: # - Daily: JobPost, User (active data, 35 days) # - Weekly: Skills (reference data, 1 year) # - Monthly: Author, Location (compliance, 5 years)

get-backup-selection

Retrieves the details of a backup selection.

Syntax
aws backup get-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id <value> \
    --selection-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-plan-id (string): Unique identifier of the backup plan
  • --selection-id (string): Unique identifier of the backup selection
Return Value
  • Returns backup selection details including resources and IAM role
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Get backup selection details

View resources assigned to backup plan.

aws backup get-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id "plan-abc123" \
    --selection-id "selection-xyz789"
Output: { "BackupSelection": { "SelectionName": "anihpj-rds-selection", "IamRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSBackupRole", "Resources": [ "arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:jobpost-prod" ] }, "SelectionId": "selection-xyz789", "BackupPlanId": "plan-abc123", "CreationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, User # Backup selection: anihpj-rds-selection # Protects: jobpost-prod database # Contains: JobPost, Skills, User tables
Example 2: List selected resources

View all resources in backup selection.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-prod"
SELECTION_ID="selection-all-resources"

aws backup get-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --selection-id "$SELECTION_ID" \
    --query 'BackupSelection.Resources[]' --output text
Output: arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:jobpost-prod arn:aws:ec2:us-east-1:123456789012:volume/vol-django-app arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-media
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Author, Location # Protected resources: # - RDS: JobPost database # - EBS: Django application volume # - S3: Author and Location media files
Example 3: Check IAM role

Verify backup selection IAM permissions.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-security-check"
SELECTION_ID="selection-check"

ROLE_ARN=$(aws backup get-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --selection-id "$SELECTION_ID" \
    --query 'BackupSelection.IamRoleArn' --output text)

echo "IAM Role: $ROLE_ARN"

# Check if role exists
ROLE_NAME=$(basename "$ROLE_ARN")
if aws iam get-role --role-name "$ROLE_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "✓ IAM role exists and accessible"
else
    echo "✗ WARNING: IAM role not found or inaccessible"
fi
Output: IAM Role: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSBackupRole ✓ IAM role exists and accessible
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User # IAM role verification successful # User data backup permissions validated
Example 4: Export selection configuration

Save backup selection for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-document"
SELECTION_ID="selection-export"
OUTPUT_FILE="backup-selection-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws backup get-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --selection-id "$SELECTION_ID" > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Selection saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"

# Display summary
jq '{Name: .BackupSelection.SelectionName, Resources: (.BackupSelection.Resources | length), CreatedAt: .CreationDate}' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Selection saved to backup-selection-20250119.json { "Name": "anihpj-prod-selection", "Resources": 5, "CreatedAt": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Skills, Author # Backup selection documented: 5 resources # Includes Skills database and Author media storage
Example 5: Verify tag-based selection

Check if selection uses tags to identify resources.

#!/bin/bash
PLAN_ID="plan-tags"
SELECTION_ID="selection-by-tags"

SELECTION=$(aws backup get-backup-selection \
    --backup-plan-id "$PLAN_ID" \
    --selection-id "$SELECTION_ID")

# Check for tag-based selection
TAGS=$(echo "$SELECTION" | jq -r '.BackupSelection.ListOfTags // empty')

if [ -n "$TAGS" ]; then
    echo "Tag-based selection:"
    echo "$TAGS" | jq .
else
    echo "Resource-based selection (no tags)"
    echo "$SELECTION" | jq -r '.BackupSelection.Resources[]'
fi
Output: Tag-based selection: [ { "ConditionType": "STRINGEQUALS", "ConditionKey": "Environment", "ConditionValue": "Production" }, { "ConditionType": "STRINGEQUALS", "ConditionKey": "Application", "ConditionValue": "JobPost" } ]
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Tag-based backup selection: # - Environment: Production # - Application: JobPost # Automatically includes all tagged resources (JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location)

get-backup-vault-access-policy

Retrieves the access policy for a backup vault.

Syntax
aws backup get-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name of the backup vault
Return Value
  • Returns IAM policy document for the backup vault
5 Detailed AWS Backup Examples
Example 1: Get vault access policy

View backup vault permissions.

aws backup get-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-prod-vault"
Output: { "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-prod-vault", "BackupVaultArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:backup-vault:anihpj-prod-vault", "Policy": "{\"Version\":\"2012-10-17\",\"Statement\":[{\"Effect\":\"Allow\",\"Principal\":{\"AWS\":\"arn:aws:iam::123456789012:root\"},\"Action\":[\"backup:CopyIntoBackupVault\"],\"Resource\":\"*\"}]}" }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills # anihpj-prod-vault access policy retrieved # Allows cross-account backup copy for JobPost and Skills data
Example 2: View formatted policy

Display vault policy in readable format.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-compliance-vault"

aws backup get-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME" \
    --query 'Policy' --output text | jq .
Output: { "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": { "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::987654321098:root" }, "Action": [ "backup:CopyIntoBackupVault", "backup:DescribeRecoveryPoint" ], "Resource": "*" } ] }
Django Context: from jobpost.models import User, Author # Compliance vault policy allows: # - Partner account (987654321098) can copy backups # - Partner can view recovery points # User and Author data sharing for compliance audit
Example 3: Check cross-account access

Verify if vault allows DR account access.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-prod-vault"
DR_ACCOUNT="111222333444"

POLICY=$(aws backup get-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME" \
    --query 'Policy' --output text)

if echo "$POLICY" | jq -e ".Statement[].Principal.AWS | contains(\"$DR_ACCOUNT\")" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "✓ DR account $DR_ACCOUNT has access to vault"
else
    echo "✗ DR account $DR_ACCOUNT does NOT have access"
fi
Output: ✓ DR account 111222333444 has access to vault
Django Context: from jobpost.models import Location # DR account access verified # Location data can be copied to DR account for disaster recovery
Example 4: Export policy for audit

Save vault access policy for compliance documentation.

#!/bin/bash
VAULT_NAME="anihpj-audit-vault"
OUTPUT_FILE="vault-policy-${VAULT_NAME}-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws backup get-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "$VAULT_NAME" \
    --query 'Policy' --output text | jq . > "$OUTPUT_FILE"

echo "Policy saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"

# Display principals with access
echo -e "\nAccounts with access:"
jq -r '.Statement[].Principal.AWS' "$OUTPUT_FILE"
Output: Policy saved to vault-policy-anihpj-audit-vault-20250119.json Accounts with access: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:root arn:aws:iam::987654321098:root
Django Context: # Vault access policy documented # Primary account (123456789012) and audit account (987654321098) # JobPost data accessible for compliance review
Example 5: Compare vault policies

Check access differences between prod and DR vaults.

#!/bin/bash
PROD_VAULT="anihpj-prod-vault"
DR_VAULT="anihpj-dr-vault"

echo "Production Vault Policy:"
aws backup get-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "$PROD_VAULT" \
    --query 'Policy' --output text | jq -r '.Statement[].Action[]'

echo -e "\nDR Vault Policy:"
aws backup get-backup-vault-access-policy \
    --backup-vault-name "$DR_VAULT" \
    --query 'Policy' --output text | jq -r '.Statement[].Action[]'
Output: Production Vault Policy: backup:CopyIntoBackupVault backup:DescribeRecoveryPoint DR Vault Policy: backup:CopyIntoBackupVault backup:StartRestoreJob backup:DescribeRecoveryPoint
Django Context: from jobpost.models import JobPost, User, Skills, Author, Location # Policy comparison: # - Prod vault: Copy + view only # - DR vault: Copy + view + restore (full DR capabilities) # All Django models protected with appropriate permissions

Amazon S3 Glacier

Long-term archival storage for compliance and backup

📋 S3 Glacier Overview

Amazon S3 Glacier is a secure, durable, and extremely low-cost cloud storage service for data archiving and long-term backup. Use it to archive old JobPost data, compliance records, and historical Django application logs at minimal cost.

Core S3 Glacier Operations

create-vault - Create archive vault
list-vaults - List all vaults
upload-archive - Upload data
initiate-job - Start retrieval
get-job-output - Download data
delete-archive - Remove archive

create-vault

Creates a new vault for storing archives.

Syntax
aws glacier create-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name <value>
Parameters
  • --account-id (string): AWS account ID (use - for current)
  • --vault-name (string): Name for the vault
Example 1: Create archive vault

Create vault for jobpost historical data.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier create-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive"
Output: { "location": "/123456789012/vaults/anihpj-jobpost-archive" }
Django Context: # Glacier vault for archiving: # - Old JobPost.objects (published > 1 year ago) # - Historical Skills.objects data # - Compliance records for Author.objects
Example 2: Create compliance vault

Vault for regulatory compliance data.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier create-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-compliance-archive"
Output: { "location": "/123456789012/vaults/anihpj-compliance-archive" }
Django Context: # Compliance vault storing: # - User.objects audit logs # - JobPost change history # - Author data retention records

upload-archive

Uploads an archive to a vault.

Syntax
aws glacier upload-archive \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name <value> \
    --body <file-path> \
    [--archive-description <value>]
Example 1: Archive old job posts

Upload exported JobPost data to Glacier.

#!/bin/bash
# Export old job posts to JSON
python manage.py dumpdata jobpost.JobPost --indent 2 > jobposts-archive-2024.json

# Compress the export
gzip jobposts-archive-2024.json

# Upload to Glacier
aws glacier upload-archive \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive" \
    --body jobposts-archive-2024.json.gz \
    --archive-description "JobPost data export - 2024 annual archive"
Output: { "location": "/123456789012/vaults/anihpj-jobpost-archive/archives/archive-id-abc123", "checksum": "sha256-hash-value", "archiveId": "archive-id-abc123" }
Django Context: # Archived: JobPost.objects.filter(created_at__year=2024) # Includes: title, author, location, skills, status # Size: Compressed JSON export # Cost: ~$0.004/GB/month
Example 2: Archive database backup

Upload PostgreSQL dump to Glacier.

#!/bin/bash
# Create database dump
pg_dump -h localhost -U postgres jobpost_db > db-backup-20250111.sql

# Compress
gzip db-backup-20250111.sql

# Upload to Glacier
aws glacier upload-archive \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive" \
    --body db-backup-20250111.sql.gz \
    --archive-description "Full PostgreSQL backup - jobpost_db - 2025-01-11"
Output: { "archiveId": "archive-db-xyz789", "checksum": "sha256-checksum-value" }
Django Context: # Full database backup containing: # - jobpost_jobpost (JobPost model) # - jobpost_skills (Skills model) # - jobpost_author (Author model) # - jobpost_location (Location model) # - auth_user (User model)
Example 3: Archive audit logs

Store compliance audit logs.

#!/bin/bash
# Archive monthly audit logs
aws glacier upload-archive \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-compliance-archive" \
    --body /var/log/django/audit-2024-12.log.gz \
    --archive-description "Django audit logs - December 2024"
Output: { "archiveId": "archive-audit-dec2024" }
Django Context: # Audit logs containing: # - User.objects login/logout events # - JobPost.objects create/update/delete # - Author.objects modifications # - Admin actions on all models

initiate-job

Initiates a retrieval job to download archived data.

Syntax
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name <value> \
    --job-parameters <value>
Example 1: Standard retrieval (3-5 hours)

Initiate standard retrieval of archived data.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive" \
    --job-parameters '{
        "Type": "archive-retrieval",
        "ArchiveId": "archive-id-abc123",
        "Tier": "Standard",
        "Description": "Retrieve 2024 JobPost archive"
    }'
Output: { "location": "/123456789012/vaults/anihpj-jobpost-archive/jobs/job-id-12345", "jobId": "job-id-12345" }
Django Context: # Retrieving: JobPost.objects 2024 archive # Wait time: 3-5 hours (Standard tier) # Cost: $0.01/GB retrieval # Use for: Historical analysis
Example 2: Expedited retrieval (1-5 minutes)

Urgent retrieval of critical data.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive" \
    --job-parameters '{
        "Type": "archive-retrieval",
        "ArchiveId": "archive-db-xyz789",
        "Tier": "Expedited",
        "Description": "URGENT: Retrieve database backup for recovery"
    }'
Output: { "jobId": "expedited-job-urgent-001" }
Django Context: # URGENT retrieval: Full database backup # Wait time: 1-5 minutes (Expedited tier) # Cost: $0.03/GB retrieval # Use for: Disaster recovery
Example 3: Bulk retrieval (5-12 hours)

Cost-effective retrieval of large archives.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-compliance-archive" \
    --job-parameters '{
        "Type": "archive-retrieval",
        "ArchiveId": "archive-audit-dec2024",
        "Tier": "Bulk",
        "Description": "Audit log retrieval for compliance review"
    }'
Output: { "jobId": "bulk-job-audit-001" }
Django Context: # Bulk retrieval: Audit logs # Wait time: 5-12 hours (Bulk tier) # Cost: $0.0025/GB (cheapest) # Use for: Non-urgent compliance review

get-job-output

Downloads the output of a completed retrieval job.

Syntax
aws glacier get-job-output \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name <value> \
    --job-id <value> \
    <output-file>
Example 1: Download retrieved archive

Download completed job output.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier get-job-output \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive" \
    --job-id "job-id-12345" \
    retrieved-jobposts-2024.json.gz

# Decompress
gunzip retrieved-jobposts-2024.json.gz

# Import back to Django
python manage.py loaddata retrieved-jobposts-2024.json
Output: { "status": 200, "contentType": "application/octet-stream", "archiveDescription": "JobPost data export - 2024 annual archive" }
Django Context: # Downloaded and restored: # - Historical JobPost.objects from 2024 # - Associated Skills and Locations # - Ready for analysis or migration

list-vaults

Lists all vaults in the account.

Syntax
aws glacier list-vaults \
    --account-id -
Example 1: List all archive vaults

View all Glacier vaults.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier list-vaults --account-id -
Output: { "VaultList": [ { "VaultName": "anihpj-jobpost-archive", "CreationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "NumberOfArchives": 12, "SizeInBytes": 5368709120 }, { "VaultName": "anihpj-compliance-archive", "CreationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "NumberOfArchives": 24, "SizeInBytes": 1073741824 } ] }
Django Context: # Glacier vaults summary: # - jobpost-archive: 12 archives, 5GB # Contains: JobPost, Skills, Author data # - compliance-archive: 24 archives, 1GB # Contains: Audit logs, compliance records

✅ Testing with pytest

pytest integration for testing Django applications and AWS CLI commands. This section covers unit tests, integration tests, and fixtures for testing the jobpost application models with AWS services.

pytest for Django and AWS

Testing Django applications with AWS service integrations

📋 pytest Testing Overview

pytest is a powerful testing framework for Python that makes it easy to write and run tests for your Django jobpost application. Combined with moto for AWS mocking and pytest-django, you can test AWS CLI integrations, model operations, and API endpoints.

Testing Patterns

pytest-django - Django integration
moto - AWS service mocking
fixtures - Test data setup
markers - Test categorization
conftest.py - Shared fixtures
coverage - Code coverage

conftest.py Setup

Configure shared fixtures for jobpost model testing.

Syntax
# conftest.py
import pytest
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from jobpost.models import Skills, Author, Location, JobPost

@pytest.fixture
def user(db):
    return User.objects.create_user(username='testuser', password='testpass')

@pytest.fixture
def skills(db):
    return [
        Skills.objects.create(name='Python', level='Advanced'),
        Skills.objects.create(name='Django', level='Advanced'),
        Skills.objects.create(name='AWS', level='Intermediate')
    ]

@pytest.fixture
def location(db):
    return Location.objects.create(
        city='San Francisco', state='CA', country='USA', zip_code='94105'
    )

@pytest.fixture
def author(db, user, location):
    return Author.objects.create(user=user, bio='Test author', location=location)

@pytest.fixture
def jobpost(db, author, location, skills):
    job = JobPost.objects.create(
        title='Senior Python Developer',
        author=author,
        location=location,
        status='published'
    )
    job.required_skills.set(skills[:2])
    return job
Example 1: Test JobPost creation

Test creating job posts with all relationships.

# tests/test_models.py
import pytest
from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, Author, Location

@pytest.mark.django_db
class TestJobPostModel:
    def test_create_jobpost(self, author, location, skills):
        """Test JobPost.objects.create() with relationships"""
        job = JobPost.objects.create(
            title='DevOps Engineer',
            author=author,
            location=location,
            description='Looking for experienced DevOps engineer',
            status='draft'
        )
        job.required_skills.add(skills[2])  # AWS skill
        
        assert job.pk is not None
        assert job.title == 'DevOps Engineer'
        assert job.author == author
        assert job.location == location
        assert job.required_skills.count() == 1
        assert skills[2] in job.required_skills.all()
    
    def test_jobpost_str_representation(self, jobpost):
        """Test JobPost.__str__() method"""
        assert str(jobpost) == 'Senior Python Developer'
    
    def test_jobpost_published_filter(self, db, author, location):
        """Test filtering published job posts"""
        # Create multiple jobs with different statuses
        JobPost.objects.create(title='Published Job', author=author, 
                              location=location, status='published')
        JobPost.objects.create(title='Draft Job', author=author, 
                              location=location, status='draft')
        
        published = JobPost.objects.filter(status='published')
        assert published.count() >= 1
        assert all(j.status == 'published' for j in published)
Output: $ pytest tests/test_models.py -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_create_jobpost PASSED tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_jobpost_str_representation PASSED tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_jobpost_published_filter PASSED ========================= 3 passed in 0.45s =========================
Django Context: # Tests verify: # - JobPost.objects.create() works correctly # - Author and Location relationships intact # - Skills many-to-many relationship works # - Status filtering works as expected
Example 2: Test Skills model

Test Skills model operations.

# tests/test_skills.py
import pytest
from jobpost.models import Skills

@pytest.mark.django_db
class TestSkillsModel:
    def test_create_skill(self, db):
        """Test Skills.objects.create()"""
        skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Kubernetes', level='Intermediate')
        
        assert skill.pk is not None
        assert skill.name == 'Kubernetes'
        assert skill.level == 'Intermediate'
    
    def test_unique_skill_name(self, db):
        """Test skill name uniqueness (if applicable)"""
        Skills.objects.create(name='Docker', level='Advanced')
        # Depending on model constraints, this might raise IntegrityError
        skill2 = Skills.objects.create(name='Docker', level='Beginner')
        assert Skills.objects.filter(name='Docker').count() >= 1
    
    def test_skills_ordering(self, skills):
        """Test default ordering of skills"""
        all_skills = Skills.objects.all()
        assert all_skills.count() == 3
        # Verify skills exist
        skill_names = list(all_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))
        assert 'Python' in skill_names
        assert 'Django' in skill_names
        assert 'AWS' in skill_names
Output: $ pytest tests/test_skills.py -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_create_skill PASSED tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_unique_skill_name PASSED tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_skills_ordering PASSED ========================= 3 passed in 0.32s =========================
Django Context: # Tests verify: # - Skills.objects.create() creates properly # - Model constraints work correctly # - QuerySet operations return expected results
Example 3: Mock AWS S3 with moto

Test S3 integration for static file uploads.

# tests/test_aws_integration.py
import pytest
import boto3
from moto import mock_aws

@pytest.fixture
def aws_credentials():
    """Mock AWS Credentials for moto"""
    import os
    os.environ['AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID'] = 'testing'
    os.environ['AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY'] = 'testing'
    os.environ['AWS_SECURITY_TOKEN'] = 'testing'
    os.environ['AWS_SESSION_TOKEN'] = 'testing'
    os.environ['AWS_DEFAULT_REGION'] = 'us-east-1'

@pytest.fixture
def s3_client(aws_credentials):
    with mock_aws():
        yield boto3.client('s3', region_name='us-east-1')

@mock_aws
class TestS3Integration:
    def test_create_bucket_for_static_files(self, aws_credentials):
        """Test creating S3 bucket for Django static files"""
        s3 = boto3.client('s3', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Create bucket for static files
        s3.create_bucket(Bucket='anihpj-static-files')
        
        # Verify bucket exists
        response = s3.list_buckets()
        bucket_names = [b['Name'] for b in response['Buckets']]
        assert 'anihpj-static-files' in bucket_names
    
    def test_upload_jobpost_media(self, aws_credentials):
        """Test uploading media files for JobPost"""
        s3 = boto3.client('s3', region_name='us-east-1')
        s3.create_bucket(Bucket='anihpj-media')
        
        # Upload test file
        s3.put_object(
            Bucket='anihpj-media',
            Key='jobposts/12345/image.jpg',
            Body=b'fake image content'
        )
        
        # Verify upload
        response = s3.get_object(
            Bucket='anihpj-media',
            Key='jobposts/12345/image.jpg'
        )
        assert response['Body'].read() == b'fake image content'
Output: $ pytest tests/test_aws_integration.py -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_aws_integration.py::TestS3Integration::test_create_bucket_for_static_files PASSED tests/test_aws_integration.py::TestS3Integration::test_upload_jobpost_media PASSED ========================= 2 passed in 0.89s =========================
Django Context: # Tests verify: # - S3 bucket creation for Django static files # - Media file upload for JobPost images # - AWS integration works correctly (mocked)
Example 4: Mock SQS for async processing

Test SQS integration for job notifications.

# tests/test_sqs_integration.py
import pytest
import boto3
import json
from moto import mock_aws

@mock_aws
class TestSQSIntegration:
    def test_send_jobpost_notification(self):
        """Test sending JobPost creation notification to SQS"""
        sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Create queue
        response = sqs.create_queue(QueueName='anihpj-jobpost-notifications')
        queue_url = response['QueueUrl']
        
        # Send notification
        message = {
            'event': 'jobpost_created',
            'jobpost_id': 12345,
            'author_id': 67,
            'title': 'Senior Python Developer'
        }
        sqs.send_message(
            QueueUrl=queue_url,
            MessageBody=json.dumps(message)
        )
        
        # Receive and verify
        response = sqs.receive_message(QueueUrl=queue_url)
        received = json.loads(response['Messages'][0]['Body'])
        
        assert received['event'] == 'jobpost_created'
        assert received['jobpost_id'] == 12345
    
    def test_batch_send_skill_updates(self):
        """Test batch sending skill update notifications"""
        sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')
        response = sqs.create_queue(QueueName='anihpj-skill-updates')
        queue_url = response['QueueUrl']
        
        # Batch send skill updates
        entries = [
            {'Id': '1', 'MessageBody': json.dumps({'skill': 'Python', 'action': 'add'})},
            {'Id': '2', 'MessageBody': json.dumps({'skill': 'Django', 'action': 'add'})},
            {'Id': '3', 'MessageBody': json.dumps({'skill': 'AWS', 'action': 'update'})}
        ]
        sqs.send_message_batch(QueueUrl=queue_url, Entries=entries)
        
        # Verify messages
        messages = []
        for _ in range(3):
            resp = sqs.receive_message(QueueUrl=queue_url, MaxNumberOfMessages=3)
            if 'Messages' in resp:
                messages.extend(resp['Messages'])
        
        assert len(messages) >= 3
Output: $ pytest tests/test_sqs_integration.py -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_sqs_integration.py::TestSQSIntegration::test_send_jobpost_notification PASSED tests/test_sqs_integration.py::TestSQSIntegration::test_batch_send_skill_updates PASSED ========================= 2 passed in 0.67s =========================
Django Context: # Tests verify: # - SQS queue creation works # - JobPost notifications sent correctly # - Skills.objects updates trigger messages # - Batch processing works
Example 5: Integration test with DynamoDB

Test DynamoDB for session storage.

# tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py
import pytest
import boto3
from moto import mock_aws

@mock_aws
class TestDynamoDBIntegration:
    def test_create_session_table(self):
        """Test creating DynamoDB table for Django sessions"""
        dynamodb = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Create table
        dynamodb.create_table(
            TableName='anihpj-sessions',
            KeySchema=[
                {'AttributeName': 'session_key', 'KeyType': 'HASH'}
            ],
            AttributeDefinitions=[
                {'AttributeName': 'session_key', 'AttributeType': 'S'}
            ],
            BillingMode='PAY_PER_REQUEST'
        )
        
        # Verify table exists
        response = dynamodb.list_tables()
        assert 'anihpj-sessions' in response['TableNames']
    
    def test_store_user_session(self):
        """Test storing user session data"""
        dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        client = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Create table
        client.create_table(
            TableName='anihpj-sessions',
            KeySchema=[
                {'AttributeName': 'session_key', 'KeyType': 'HASH'}
            ],
            AttributeDefinitions=[
                {'AttributeName': 'session_key', 'AttributeType': 'S'}
            ],
            BillingMode='PAY_PER_REQUEST'
        )
        
        table = dynamodb.Table('anihpj-sessions')
        
        # Store session
        table.put_item(Item={
            'session_key': 'abc123xyz',
            'user_id': 42,
            'author_id': 67,
            'last_viewed_jobs': [12345, 12346, 12347]
        })
        
        # Retrieve session
        response = table.get_item(Key={'session_key': 'abc123xyz'})
        item = response['Item']
        
        assert item['user_id'] == 42
        assert item['author_id'] == 67
        assert len(item['last_viewed_jobs']) == 3
Output: $ pytest tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py::TestDynamoDBIntegration::test_create_session_table PASSED tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py::TestDynamoDBIntegration::test_store_user_session PASSED ========================= 2 passed in 0.54s =========================
Django Context: # Tests verify: # - DynamoDB table creation for sessions # - User session storage works # - Author data linked in sessions # - JobPost view history tracked

Running Tests

Commands to run pytest with different options.

Common pytest Commands
# Run all tests
pytest

# Run with verbose output
pytest -v

# Run specific test file
pytest tests/test_models.py

# Run specific test class
pytest tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel

# Run specific test method
pytest tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_create_jobpost

# Run with coverage report
pytest --cov=jobpost --cov-report=html

# Run tests matching a pattern
pytest -k "jobpost or skills"

# Run only Django database tests
pytest -m django_db

# Run tests in parallel
pytest -n auto

# Run with AWS mocking
pytest tests/test_aws_integration.py -v
Example: Full test suite execution

Run complete test suite with coverage.

#!/bin/bash
# Run full test suite for anihpj jobpost app
pytest tests/ \
    --cov=jobpost \
    --cov-report=html \
    --cov-report=term-missing \
    -v \
    --tb=short
Output: $ pytest tests/ --cov=jobpost --cov-report=term-missing -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_create_jobpost PASSED tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_jobpost_str_representation PASSED tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_jobpost_published_filter PASSED tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_create_skill PASSED tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_unique_skill_name PASSED tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_skills_ordering PASSED tests/test_aws_integration.py::TestS3Integration::test_create_bucket PASSED tests/test_aws_integration.py::TestS3Integration::test_upload_jobpost_media PASSED tests/test_sqs_integration.py::TestSQSIntegration::test_send_jobpost_notification PASSED tests/test_sqs_integration.py::TestSQSIntegration::test_batch_send_skill_updates PASSED tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py::TestDynamoDBIntegration::test_create_session_table PASSED tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py::TestDynamoDBIntegration::test_store_user_session PASSED ---------- coverage: jobpost ---------- Name Stmts Miss Cover Missing ----------------------------------------------------- jobpost/__init__.py 0 0 100% jobpost/models.py 45 3 93% 78-80 jobpost/views.py 62 8 87% 45-52 jobpost/admin.py 12 0 100% ----------------------------------------------------- TOTAL 119 11 91% ======================== 12 passed in 2.34s =========================
Django Context: # Test coverage summary: # - models.py: 93% coverage (JobPost, Skills, Author, Location) # - views.py: 87% coverage (API endpoints) # - admin.py: 100% coverage (admin configuration) # - Overall: 91% coverage # All AWS integrations tested with moto mocking
